case involving header guard branches: suitably clever compilers can
notice that a branch is a header guard, and can then subsequently ignore
a `#include` for the file being guarded. It is for this reason that
`wasTaken()` always holds on header guard branches, but `wasNotToken()`
rarely holds on header guard branches.

predicate.name: wasTaken
predicate.comments:
Holds if at least one translation unit evaluated this directive's
condition and subsequently took the branch.

predicate.name: wasNotTaken
predicate.comments:
Holds if at least one translation unit evaluated this directive's
condition but then didn't take the branch.

If `#else` is the next matching directive, then this means that the
`#else` was taken instead.

predicate.name: wasPredictable
predicate.comments:
Holds if this directive was either taken by all translation units
which evaluated it, or was not taken by any translation unit which
evaluated it.

class.comments:
A C/C++ preprocessor `#if` directive. For example there is a
`PreprocessorIf` on the first line of the following code:
```
#if defined(MYDEFINE)
// ...
#endif
```
For the related notion of a directive which causes branching (which
includes `#if`, plus also `#ifdef`, `#ifndef`, `#elif`, `#elifdef`,
and `#elifndef`), see `PreprocessorBranch`.

A C/C++ preprocessor `#ifdef` directive. For example there is a
`PreprocessorIfdef` on the first line of the following code:
```
#ifdef MYDEFINE
// ...
#endif
```
The syntax `#ifdef X` is shorthand for `#if defined(X)`.

A C/C++ preprocessor `#ifndef` directive. For example there is a
`PreprocessorIfndef` on the first line of the following code:
```
#ifndef MYDEFINE
// ...
#endif
```
The syntax `#ifndef X` is shorthand for `#if !defined(X)`.

A C/C++ preprocessor `#else` directive. For example there is a
`PreprocessorElse` on the fifth line of the following code:
```
#ifdef MYDEFINE1
// ...
#elif MYDEFINE2
// ...
#else
// ...
#endif
```

A C/C++ preprocessor `#elif` directive. For example there is a
`PreprocessorElif` on the third line of the following code:
```
#ifdef MYDEFINE1
// ...
#elif MYDEFINE2
// ...
#else
// ...
#endif
```

A C/C++ preprocessor `#elifdef` directive. For example there is a
`PreprocessorElifdef` on the third line of the following code:
```
#ifdef MYDEFINE1
// ...
#elifdef MYDEFINE2
// ...
#else
// ...
#endif
```

A C/C++ preprocessor `#elifndef` directive. For example there is a
`PreprocessorElifndef` on the third line of the following code:
```
#ifdef MYDEFINE1
// ...
#elifndef MYDEFINE2
// ...
#else
// ...
#endif
```

A C/C++ preprocessor `#endif` directive. For example there is a
`PreprocessorEndif` on the third line of the following code:
```
#ifdef MYDEFINE
// ...
#endif
```

A C/C++ preprocessor `#warning` directive. For example:
```
#warning "This configuration is not supported."
```

A C/C++ preprocessor `#error` directive. For example:
```
#error "This configuration is not implemented."
```

A C/C++ preprocessor `#undef` directive. For example there is a
`PreprocessorUndef` on the second line of the following code:
```
#ifdef MYMACRO
#undef MYMACRO
#endif
```

method.name: getName
method.comments: Gets the name of the macro that is undefined.

class.comments:
A C/C++ preprocessor `#pragma` directive. For example:
```
#pragma once
```

method.name: toString
method.body: if exists(this.getHead()) then result = "#pragma " + this.getHead() else result = "#pragma"

class.comments:
A C/C++ preprocessor `#line` directive. For example:
```
#line 1 "source.c"
```
A `Declaration` extended to add methods for generating strings useful only for dumps and debugging.

method.name: getIdentityString
method.comments:
Gets a string that uniquely identifies this declaration, suitable for use when debugging queries. Only holds for
functions, user-defined types, global and namespace-scope variables, and member variables.

This operation is very expensive, and should not be used in production queries. Consider using
`hasQualifiedName()` for identifying known declarations in production queries.

method.name: getTemplateArgumentsString
method.body:
if exists(this.getATemplateArgument())
    then
      result =
        "<" +
          strictconcat(int i |
            exists(this.getTemplateArgument(i))
          |
            getTemplateArgumentString(this, i), ", " order by i
          ) + ">"
    else result = ""

class.comments: A `Type` extended to add methods for generating strings useful only for dumps and debugging.

method.name: getTypeIdentityString
method.comments:
Gets a string that uniquely identifies this type, suitable for use when debugging queries. All typedefs and
decltypes are expanded, and all symbol names are fully qualified.

This operation is very expensive, and should not be used in production queries.

method.name: getTypeSpecifier
method.comments:
Gets the "type specifier" part of this type's name. This is generally the "leaf" type from which the type was
constructed.

Examples:
- `int` -> `int`
- `int*` -> `int`
- `int (*&)(float, double) const` -> `int`

This predicate is intended to be used only by the implementation of `getTypeIdentityString`.

method.name: getDeclaratorPrefix
method.comments:
Gets the portion of this type's declarator that comes before the declarator for any derived type.

This predicate is intended to be used only by the implementation of `getTypeIdentityString`.

method.name: getDeclaratorSuffixBeforeQualifiers
method.comments:
Gets the portion of this type's declarator that comes after the declarator for any derived type, but before any
qualifiers on the current type.

This predicate is intended to be used only by the implementation of `getTypeIdentityString`.

method.name: getDeclaratorSuffix
method.comments:
Gets the portion of this type's declarator that comes after the declarator for any derived type and after any
qualifiers on the current type.

This predicate is intended to be used only by the implementation of `getTypeIdentityString`.

method.name: getDeclaratorPrefix
getDeclaratorToken
method.comments: Gets the token used when declaring this kind of type (e.g. `*`, `&`, `&&`)/

predicate.name: PointerIshDumpType
predicate.body:
this instanceof PointerType or
    this instanceof ReferenceType

method.name: getDeclaratorPrefix
method.body:
exists(string declarator, string parenDeclarator, Type baseType |
      declarator = this.getClass().(DumpType).getTypeIdentityString() + "::*" and
      result = this.getBaseType().(DumpType).getDeclaratorPrefix() + " " + parenDeclarator and
      baseType = this.getBaseType().getUnspecifiedType() and
      if baseType instanceof ArrayType or baseType instanceof RoutineType
      then parenDeclarator = "(" + declarator
      else parenDeclarator = declarator
    )

method.name: getDeclaratorSuffixBeforeQualifiers
getDeclaratorSuffixBeforeQualifiers
getDeclaratorToken
method.comments: Gets the token used when declaring this kind of type (e.g. `*`, `&`, `^`)/

method.name: getDeclaratorSuffixBeforeQualifiers
method.body:
result =
      "(" +
        concat(int i |
          exists(this.getParameterType(i))
        |
          getParameterTypeString(this.getParameterType(i)), ", " order by i
        ) + ")"

method.name: getDeclaratorPrefix
getDeclaratorSuffixBeforeQualifiers
method.body:
exists(string baseSuffix |
      baseSuffix = this.getBaseType().(DumpType).getDeclaratorSuffixBeforeQualifiers() and
      if this.getBaseType().getUnspecifiedType() instanceof RoutineType
      then result = baseSuffix + " " + this.getSpecifierString()
      else result = baseSuffix
    )

method.name: getIdentityString
getIdentityString
method.body:
exists(DumpType type |
      (this instanceof MemberVariable or this instanceof GlobalOrNamespaceVariable) and
      type = this.getType() and
      result =
        type.getTypeSpecifier() + type.getDeclaratorPrefix() + " " + getScopePrefix(this) +
          this.getName() + this.getTemplateArgumentsString() +
          type.getDeclaratorSuffixBeforeQualifiers() + type.getDeclaratorSuffix()
    )

class.comments: The query can extend this class to control which declarations are printed.

method.name: toString
method.comments: Gets a textual representation of this `PrintASTConfiguration`.

predicate.name: shouldPrintDeclaration
predicate.comments:
Holds if the AST for `decl` should be printed. By default, holds for all
functions and global and namespace variables. Currently, does not support any
other declaration types.

class.comments: A node in the output tree.

method.name: toString
method.comments: Gets a textual representation of this node in the PrintAST output tree.

method.name: getChildInternal
method.comments:
Gets the child node at index `childIndex`. Child indices must be unique,
but need not be contiguous.

method.name: getChild
method.comments:
Gets the child node at index `childIndex`.
Adds edges to fully converted expressions, that are not part of the
regular parent/child relation traversal.

method.name: getAChild
method.comments: Gets the children of this node.

method.name: getParent
method.comments: Gets the parent of this node, if any.

Gets the value of the property of this node, where the name of the property
is `key`.

method.name: getChildAccessorPredicate
method.comments:
Gets the QL predicate that can be used to access the child at `childIndex`.
May not always return a QL predicate, see for example `FunctionNode`.

method.name: getChildAccessorPredicateInternal
method.comments:
Gets the QL predicate that can be used to access the child at `childIndex`.
INTERNAL DO NOT USE: Does not contain accessors for the synthesized nodes for conversions.

predicate.name: shouldPrint
predicate.comments:
Holds if this node should be printed in the output. By default, all nodes
within functions and global and namespace variables are printed, but the query
can override `PrintASTConfiguration.shouldPrintDeclaration` to filter the output.

class.comments: Class that restricts the elements that we compute `qlClass` for.

predicate.name: PrintableElement
predicate.body:
exists(TAstNode(this))
    or
    exists(TDeclarationEntryNode(_, this))
    or
    this instanceof Type

class.comments: A node representing an AST node.

method.name: getAst
method.comments: Gets the AST represented by this node.

class.comments: A node representing an AST node other than a `DeclarationEntry`.
A node representing an `Expr`.

method.name: getChildInternal
method.body:
result.(AstNode).getAst() = expr.getChild(childIndex)
    or
    childIndex = max(int index | exists(expr.getChild(index)) or index = 0) + 1 and
    result.(AstNode).getAst() = expr.(ConditionDeclExpr).getInitializingExpr()
    or
    exists(int destructorIndex |
      result.(AstNode).getAst() = expr.getImplicitDestructorCall(destructorIndex) and
      childIndex = destructorIndex + max(int index | exists(expr.getChild(index)) or index = 0) + 2
    )

method.name: getProperty
method.body:
result = super.getProperty(key)
    or
    key = "Value" and
    result = qlClass(expr) + this.getValue()
    or
    key = "Type" and
    result = qlClass(expr.getType()) + expr.getType().toString()
    or
    key = "ValueCategory" and
    result = expr.getValueCategoryString()

method.name: getValue
method.comments: Gets the value of this expression, if it is a constant.

class.comments: A node representing a `StringLiteral`.
A node representing a `ConceptIdExpr`.

    or

    result.(ConceptIdExprArgumentsNode).getConceptIdExpr() = expr

method.name: getChildInternal
method.body:
childIndex = 0 and
    result.getAst() = conv.getExpr() and
    conv.getExpr() instanceof Conversion
    or
    result.getAst() = expr.getImplicitDestructorCall(childIndex - 1)

method.name: getProperty
method.body:

    or
    key = "Conversion" and
    result = "[" + qlConversion(cast) + "] " + cast.getSemanticConversionString()

method.name: getChildInternal

result = super.getChildInternal(childIndex - count(generic.getAChild()))
    or
    result.getAst() = generic.getChild(childIndex)

class.comments: A node representing a `StmtExpr`.
A node representing a `RequiresExpr`

method.body:
result = super.getChildInternal(childIndex)
    or
    childIndex = -1 and
    result.(RequiresExprParametersNode).getRequiresExpr() = expr

class.comments: A node representing a `DeclarationEntry`.

method.name: getProperty
method.body:
result = BaseAstNode.super.getProperty(key)
    or
    key = "Type" and
    result = qlClass(ast.getType()) + ast.getType().toString()

class.comments: A node representing a `VariableDeclarationEntry`.
A node representing a `Stmt`.

method.name: getChildInternal
method.body:
exists(Locatable child |
      child = stmt.getChild(childIndex) and
      (
        result.getAst() = child.(Expr) or
        result.getAst() = child.(Stmt)
      )
    )
    or
    exists(int destructorIndex |
      result.getAst() = stmt.getImplicitDestructorCall(destructorIndex) and
      childIndex = destructorIndex + max(int index | exists(stmt.getChild(index)) or index = 0) + 1
    )

class.comments: A node representing a child of a `Stmt` that is itself a `Stmt`.

method.name: getChildInternal
method.body:
result = super.getChildInternal(childIndex)
    or
    exists(int destructorIndex |
      result.getAst() = childStmt.getImplicitDestructorCall(destructorIndex) and
      childIndex =
        destructorIndex + max(int index | exists(childStmt.getChild(index)) or index = 0) + 1
    )

predicate.name: ChildStmtNode
predicate.body: exists(Stmt parent | parent.getAChild() = childStmt and childStmt = ast)

class.comments: A node representing a `DeclStmt`.

method.name: getChildInternal
method.body:
exists(DeclarationEntry entry |
      declStmt.getDeclarationEntry(childIndex) = entry and
      result = TDeclarationEntryNode(declStmt, entry)
    )

class.comments: A node representing a `Handler`.

method.name: getChildInternal
method.body:
result = super.getChildInternal(childIndex)
    or
    childIndex = -1 and
    result.getAst() = handler.getParameter()

class.comments: A node representing a `Parameter`.

method.name: getProperty
method.body:
result = super.getProperty(key)
    or
    key = "Type" and
    result = qlClass(param.getType()) + param.getType().toString()

class.comments: A node representing an `Initializer`.
A node representing the arguments of a `ConceptIdExpr`.

method.name: getChildInternal
method.body:
exists(Locatable arg | arg = concept.getTemplateArgument(childIndex) |
      result.(ConceptIdExprTypeArgumentNode).isArgumentNode(arg, concept, childIndex)
      or
      result.(ExprNode).getAst() = arg
    )

method.name: getConceptIdExpr
method.comments: Gets the `ConceptIdExpr` for which this node represents the parameters.

class.comments: A node representing a type argument of a `ConceptIdExpr`.

predicate.name: isArgumentNode
predicate.body: type = t and concept = c and index = i
predicate.comments: Holds if `t` is the `i`th template argument of `c`.

method.comments: Gets the `Function` for which this node represents the parameters.

class.comments: A node representing the parameters of a `RequiresExpr`.

method.comments: Gets the `RequiresExpr` for which this node represents the parameters.

method.comments: Gets the `Constructor` for which this node represents the initializer list.

method.name: getDestructor
method.comments: Gets the `Destructor` for which this node represents the destruction list.

method.body:
result = super.getProperty(key)
    or
    key = "semmle.order" and result = this.getOrder().toString()

A node representing a `Function`.

method.name: getChildInternal
method.body:
childIndex = 0 and
    result.(FunctionParametersNode).getFunction() = func
    or
    childIndex = 1 and
    result.(ConstructorInitializersNode).getConstructor() = func
    or
    childIndex = 2 and

childIndex = 0 and result = "<params>"
    or
    childIndex = 1 and result = "<initializations>"
    or

    or

class.comments:
A C/C++ specifier: `friend`, `auto`, `register`, `static`, `extern`,
`mutable`, `inline`, `virtual`, or `explicit`.

method.name: getLocation
method.comments: Gets a dummy location for the specifier.

method.name: getName
method.comments: Gets the name of this specifier.

predicate.name: hasName
predicate.comments: Holds if the name of this specifier is `name`.

class.comments: A C/C++ function specifier: `inline`, `virtual`, or `explicit`.

A C/C++ storage class specifier: `auto`, `register`, `static`, `extern`,
or `mutable`.
A C++ access specifier: `public`, `protected`, or `private`.

method.name: accessInDirectDerived
method.comments:
Gets the visibility of a field with access specifier `this` if it is
directly inherited with access specifier `baseAccess`. For example:

```
class A { protected int f; };
class B : private A {};
```

In this example, `this` is `protected`, `baseAccess` is `private`, and
`result` is `private` because the visibility of field `f` in class `B`
is `private`.

This method encodes the rules of N4140 11.2/1, tabulated here:

```
`this`           | `baseAccess`           | `result`
(access in base) | (base class specifier) | (access in derived)
----------------------------------------------------------
private          | private                | N/A
private          | protected              | N/A
private          | public                 | N/A
protected        | private                | private
protected        | protected              | protected
protected        | public                 | protected
public           | private                | private
public           | protected              | protected
public           | public                 | public
```

class.comments:
A C/C++ calling convention specifier: `cdecl`, `fastcall`, `stdcall`, `thiscall`,
`vectorcall`, or `clrcall`.

An attribute introduced by GNU's `__attribute__((name))` syntax,
Microsoft's `__declspec(name)` syntax, Microsoft's `[name]` syntax, the
C++11 standard `[[name]]` syntax, or the C++11 `alignas` syntax.

method.name: getName
method.comments:
Gets the name of this attribute.

As examples, this is "noreturn" for `__attribute__((__noreturn__))`,
"fallthrough" for `[[clang::fallthrough]]`, and "dllimport" for
`__declspec(dllimport)`.

Note that the name does not include the namespace. For example, the
name of `[[clang::fallthrough]]` is "fallthrough".

method.name: getArgument
method.comments: Gets the `i`th argument of the attribute.

method.name: getAnArgument
method.comments: Gets an argument of the attribute.

predicate.name: hasName
predicate.comments: Holds if the name of this attribute is `name`.

class.comments:
An attribute introduced by GNU's `__attribute__((name))` syntax, for
example: `__attribute__((__noreturn__))`.

An attribute introduced by the C++11 standard `[[name]]` syntax, for
example: `[[clang::fallthrough]]`.

method.name: getNamespace
method.comments:
Gets the namespace of this attribute.

As examples, this is "" for `[[carries_dependency]]`, and "clang" for
`[[clang::fallthrough]]`.

predicate.name: hasQualifiedName
predicate.comments: Holds if this attribute has the given namespace and name.

class.comments:
An attribute introduced by Microsoft's `__declspec(name)` syntax.  For
example the attribute on the following declaration:
```
__declspec(dllimport) void myFunction();
```
An attribute introduced by Microsoft's "[name]" syntax, for example "[SA_Pre(Deref=1,Access=SA_Read)]".

A GNU `format` attribute of the form `__attribute__((format(archetype, format-index, first-arg)))`
that declares a function to accept a `printf` style format string.  For example the attribute
on the following declaration:
```
int myPrintf(const char *format, ...) __attribute__((format(printf, 1, 2)));
```

method.name: getArchetype
method.comments:
Gets the archetype of this format attribute, for example
`"printf"`.

method.name: getFormatIndex
method.comments:
Gets the (0-based) index of the format string,
according to this attribute.

method.name: getFirstFormatArgIndex
method.body:
exists(int val |
      val = this.getArgument(2).getValueInt() and
      result = val - this.firstArgumentNumber() and
      not val = 0 // indicates a `vprintf` style format function with arguments not directly available.
    )
method.comments:
Gets the (0-based) index of the first format argument (if any),
according to this attribute.

class.comments:
An argument to an `Attribute`. For example the argument "dllimport" on the
attribute in the following code:
```
__declspec(dllimport) void myFunction();
```

method.name: getName
method.comments:
Gets the name of this argument, if it is a named argument. Named
arguments are a Microsoft feature, so only a `MicrosoftAttribute` can
have a named argument.

method.name: getValueText
method.comments:
Gets the text for the value of this argument, if its value is
a constant or a token.

method.name: getValueInt
method.comments: Gets the value of this argument, if its value is integral.

method.name: getValueType
method.comments: Gets the value of this argument, if its value is a type.

method.name: getValueConstant
method.comments: Gets the value of this argument, if its value is a constant.

method.name: getValueExpr
method.comments: Gets the value of this argument, if its value is an expression.

method.name: getAttribute
method.comments: Gets the attribute to which this is an argument.

method.name: getIndex
method.comments:
Gets the zero-based index of this argument in the containing
attribute's argument list.

method.name: toString
method.body:
if underlyingElement(this) instanceof @attribute_arg_empty
    then result = "empty argument"
    else
      exists(string prefix, string tail |
        (if exists(this.getName()) then prefix = this.getName() + "=" else prefix = "") and
        (
          if underlyingElement(this) instanceof @attribute_arg_type
          then tail = this.getValueType().getName()
          else
            if underlyingElement(this) instanceof @attribute_arg_constant_expr
            then tail = this.getValueConstant().toString()
            else
              if underlyingElement(this) instanceof @attribute_arg_expr
              then tail = this.getValueExpr().toString()
              else tail = this.getValueText()
        ) and
        result = prefix + tail
      )

method.name: getAPrimaryQlClass
method.body: not this instanceof LocalUnion and result = "LocalStruct"

method.name: getAPrimaryQlClass
method.body: not this instanceof NestedUnion and result = "NestedStruct"

predicate.name: isPrivate
predicate.comments: Holds if this member is private.

predicate.name: isProtected
predicate.comments: Holds if this member is protected.

predicate.name: isPublic
predicate.comments: Holds if this member is public.

class.comments:
A C++ template parameter.

In the example below, `T`, `TT`, and `I` are template parameters:
```
template <class T, template<typename> TT, int I>
class C { };
```

A C++ non-type template parameter.

In the example below, `I` is a non-type template parameter:
```
template <int I>
class C { };
```

A C++ `typename` (or `class`) template parameter.

DEPRECATED: Use `TypeTemplateParameter` instead.

A C++ `typename` (or `class`) template parameter.

In the example below, `T` is a template parameter:
```
template <class T>
class C { };
```

method.name: getTypeConstraint
method.comments: Get the type constraint of this type template parameter.

class.comments:
A C++ template template parameter.

In the example below, `T` is a template template parameter (although its name
may be omitted):
```
template <template <typename T> class Container, class Elem>
void foo(const Container<Elem> &value) { }
```

method.name: getAnInstantiation
method.comments:
Gets a class instantiated from this template template parameter.

For example for `Container<T>` in the following code, the result is
`Container<Elem>`:
```
template <template <typename T> class Container, class Elem>
void foo(const Container<Elem> &value) { }
```

class.comments:
A type representing the use of the C++11 `auto` keyword.
```
auto val = some_typed_expr();
```

A class that is an instantiation of a template template parameter.  For example,
in the following code there is a `Container<Elem>` instantiation:
```
template <template <typename T> class Container, class Elem>
void foo(const Container<Elem> &value) { }
```
For the `Container` template itself, see `TemplateTemplateParameter`.

method.name: getTemplate
method.comments:
Gets the template template parameter from which this instantiation was instantiated.

For example for `Container<Elem>` in the following code, the result is
`Container<T>`:
```
template <template <typename T> class Container, class Elem>
void foo(const Container<Elem> &value) { }
```

class.comments: The `gtest/gtest.h` file.
A test using the Google Test library.
The `boost/test` directory.
A test using the Boost Test library.
The `cppunit` directory.
A class from the `cppunit` directory.
A test using the CppUnit library.

predicate.name: CppUnitTest

class.comments: A file that contains one or more test cases.

predicate.name: TestFile
predicate.body:
exists(GoogleTest test | test.getFile() = this) or
    exists(BoostTest test | test.getFile() = this) or
    exists(CppUnitTest test | test.getFile() = this)

class.comments:
A C/C++ type.

This QL class represents the root of the C/C++ type hierarchy.

method.name: getName
method.comments: Gets the name of this type.

method.name: getASpecifier
method.comments:
Gets a specifier of this type, recursively looking through `typedef` and
`decltype`. For example, in the context of `typedef const int *restrict t`,
the type `volatile t` has specifiers `volatile` and `restrict` but not
`const` since the `const` is attached to the type being pointed to rather
than the pointer itself.

method.name: getAnAttribute
method.comments: Gets an attribute of this type.

method.name: internal_getAnAdditionalSpecifier
method.comments:
Internal -- should be `protected` when QL supports such a flag. Subtypes
override this to recursively get specifiers that are not attached directly
to this `@type` in the database but arise through type aliases such as
`typedef` and `decltype`.

method.name: getUnderlyingType
method.comments:
Gets this type after typedefs have been resolved.

The result of this predicate will be the type itself, except in the case of a TypedefType, a Decltype,
or a TypeofType, in which case the result will be type which results from (possibly recursively)
resolving typedefs.

method.name: getUnspecifiedType
method.comments:
Gets this type after specifiers have been deeply stripped and typedefs have been resolved.

For example, starting with `const i64* const` in the context of `typedef long long i64;`, this predicate will return `long long*`.

method.name: stripTopLevelSpecifiers
method.comments:
Gets this type after any top-level specifiers and typedefs have been stripped.

For example, starting with `const i64* const`, this predicate will return `const i64*`.

method.name: getSize
method.body:
builtintypes(underlyingElement(this), _, _, result, _, _) or
    pointerishsize(underlyingElement(this), result, _) or
    usertypesize(underlyingElement(this), result, _)
method.comments: Gets the size of this type in bytes.

method.name: getAlignment
method.body:
builtintypes(underlyingElement(this), _, _, _, _, result) or
    pointerishsize(underlyingElement(this), _, result) or
    usertypesize(underlyingElement(this), _, result)
method.comments: Gets the alignment of this type in bytes.

method.name: getPointerIndirectionLevel
method.comments: Gets the pointer indirection level of this type.

method.name: explain
method.comments:
Gets a detailed string representation explaining the AST of this type
(with all specifiers and nested constructs such as pointers). This is
intended to help debug queries and is a very expensive operation; not
to be used in production queries.

An example output is "const {pointer to {const {char}}}".

method.name: getATypeNameUse
method.comments:
Gets as many places as possible where this type is used by name in the source after macros have been replaced
(in particular, therefore, this will find type name uses caused by macros). Note that all type name uses within
instantiations are currently excluded - this is too draconian in the absence of indexing prototype instantiations
of functions, and is likely to improve in the future. At present, the method takes the conservative approach of
giving valid type name uses, but not necessarily *all* type name uses.

method.name: resolveTypedefs
method.comments:
Gets this type with any typedefs resolved. For example, given
`typedef C T`, this would resolve `const T&` to `const C&`.
Note that this will only work if the resolved type actually appears
on its own elsewhere in the program.

method.name: stripType
method.comments:
Gets the type stripped of pointers, references and cv-qualifiers, and resolving typedefs.
For example, given `typedef const C& T`, `stripType` returns `C`.

predicate.name: hasName
predicate.comments: Holds if this type is called `name`.

predicate.name: hasSpecifier
predicate.comments:
Holds if this declaration has a specifier called `name`, recursively looking
through `typedef` and `decltype`. For example, in the context of
`typedef const int *restrict t`, the type `volatile t` has specifiers
`volatile` and `restrict` but not `const` since the `const` is attached to
the type being pointed to rather than the pointer itself.

predicate.name: isConst
predicate.comments: Holds if this type is const.

predicate.name: isVolatile
predicate.comments: Holds if this type is volatile.

predicate.name: refersTo
predicate.comments:
Holds if this type refers to type `t` (by default,
a type always refers to itself).

predicate.name: refersToDirectly
predicate.comments: Holds if this type refers to type `t` directly.

predicate.name: isDeeplyConst
predicate.comments:
Holds if this type is constant and only contains constant types.
For instance, a `char *const` is a constant type, but not deeply constant,
because while the pointer can't be modified the character can. The type
`const char *const*` is a deeply constant type though - both the pointer
and what it points to are immutable.

predicate.name: isDeeplyConstBelow
predicate.comments:
Holds if this type is constant and only contains constant types, excluding
the type itself. It is implied by Type.isDeeplyConst() and is just used to
implement that predicate.
For example, `const char *const` is deeply constant and deeply constant below,
but `const char *` is only deeply constant below (the pointer can be changed,
but not the underlying char). `char *const` is neither (it is just `const`).

predicate.name: involvesReference
predicate.comments: Holds if this type involves a reference.

predicate.name: involvesTemplateParameter
predicate.comments: Holds if this type involves a template parameter.

class.comments:
An erroneous type.  This type has no corresponding C/C++ syntax.

`ErroneousType` is the type of `ErrorExpr`, which in turn refers to an illegal
language construct.  In the example below, a temporary (`0`) cannot be bound
to an lvalue reference (`int &`):
```
int &intref = 0;
```

The Arm scalable vector count type.

In the following example, `a` is declared using the scalable vector
count type:
```
svcount_t a;
```

The C/C++ arithmetic types. See 4.1.1.

This includes primitive types on which arithmetic, bitwise or logical
operations may be performed.  Examples of arithmetic types include
`char`, `int`, `float`, and `bool`.

A C/C++ integral or `enum` type.

The definition of "integral type" in the C++ standard excludes `enum` types,
but because an `enum` type holds a value of its underlying integral type,
it is often useful to have a common category that includes both integral
and `enum` types.

In the following example, `a`, `b` and `c` are all declared with an
integral or `enum` type:
```
unsigned long a;
enum e1 { val1, val2 } b;
enum class e2: short { val3, val4 } c;
```

method.name: getUnsigned
method.body:
exists(int unsignedKind |
      integralTypeMapping(kind, _, unsignedKind, _) and
      builtintypes(unresolveElement(result), _, unsignedKind, _, _, _)
    )
method.comments:
Gets the unsigned type corresponding to this integral type.  For
example on a `short`, this would give the type `unsigned short`.

method.name: getCanonicalArithmeticType
method.body:
exists(int canonicalKind |
      integralTypeMapping(kind, canonicalKind, _, _) and
      builtintypes(unresolveElement(result), _, canonicalKind, _, _, _)
    )
method.comments:
Gets the canonical type corresponding to this integral type.

For a plain type, this gives the same type (e.g. `short` -> `short`).
For an explicitly unsigned type, this gives the same type (e.g. `unsigned short` -> `unsigned short`).
For an explicitly signed type, this gives the plain version of that type (e.g. `signed short` -> `short`), except
that `signed char` -> `signed char`.

predicate.name: isSigned
predicate.comments: Holds if this integral type is signed.

predicate.name: isUnsigned
predicate.comments: Holds if this integral type is unsigned.

predicate.name: isExplicitlySigned
predicate.body:
builtintypes(underlyingElement(this), _, 7, _, _, _) or
    builtintypes(underlyingElement(this), _, 10, _, _, _) or
    builtintypes(underlyingElement(this), _, 13, _, _, _) or
    builtintypes(underlyingElement(this), _, 16, _, _, _) or
    builtintypes(underlyingElement(this), _, 19, _, _, _) or
    builtintypes(underlyingElement(this), _, 37, _, _, _)
predicate.comments: Holds if this integral type is explicitly signed.

predicate.name: isExplicitlyUnsigned
predicate.body:
builtintypes(underlyingElement(this), _, 6, _, _, _) or
    builtintypes(underlyingElement(this), _, 9, _, _, _) or
    builtintypes(underlyingElement(this), _, 12, _, _, _) or
    builtintypes(underlyingElement(this), _, 15, _, _, _) or
    builtintypes(underlyingElement(this), _, 18, _, _, _) or
    builtintypes(underlyingElement(this), _, 36, _, _, _)
predicate.comments: Holds if this integral type is explicitly unsigned.

predicate.name: isImplicitlySigned
predicate.body:
builtintypes(underlyingElement(this), _, 5, _, -1, _) or
    builtintypes(underlyingElement(this), _, 8, _, -1, _) or
    builtintypes(underlyingElement(this), _, 11, _, -1, _) or
    builtintypes(underlyingElement(this), _, 14, _, -1, _) or
    builtintypes(underlyingElement(this), _, 17, _, -1, _) or
    builtintypes(underlyingElement(this), _, 35, _, -1, _)
predicate.comments: Holds if this integral type is implicitly signed.

class.comments:
The C/C++ boolean type. See 4.2.  This is the C `_Bool` type
or the C++ `bool` type.  For example:
```
extern bool a, b;   // C++
_Bool c, d;         // C
```

The C/C++ `char` type (which is distinct from `signed char` and
`unsigned char`).  For example:
```
char a, b;
```

The C/C++ `unsigned char` type (which is distinct from plain `char`
even when `char` is `unsigned` by default).
```
unsigned char e, f;
```

The C/C++ `signed char` type (which is distinct from plain `char`
even when `char` is `signed` by default).
```
signed char c, d;
```

The C/C++ short types. See 4.3.  This includes `short`, `signed short`
and `unsigned short`.
```
signed short ss;
```

The C/C++ integer types. See 4.4.  This includes `int`, `signed int`
and `unsigned int`.
```
unsigned int ui;
```

The C/C++ long types. See 4.4.  This includes `long`, `signed long`
and `unsigned long`.
```
long l;
```

The C/C++ long long types. See 4.4.  This includes `long long`, `signed long long`
and `unsigned long long`.
```
signed long long sll;
```

The GNU C __int128 primitive types.  They are not part of standard C/C++.

This includes `__int128`, `signed __int128` and `unsigned __int128`.
```
unsigned __int128 ui128;
```

The type domain of a floating-point type. One of `RealDomain`, `ComplexDomain`, or
`ImaginaryDomain`.

method.name: toString
method.comments: Gets a textual representation of this type domain.

class.comments: The type domain of a floating-point type that represents a real number.
The type domain of a floating-point type that represents a complex number.
The type domain of a floating-point type that represents an imaginary number.

The C/C++ floating point types. See 4.5.  This includes `float`, `double` and `long double`, the
fixed-size floating-point types like `_Float32`, the extended-precision floating-point types like
`_Float64x`, and the decimal floating-point types like `_Decimal32`. It also includes the complex
and imaginary versions of all of these types.

method.name: getBase
method.comments: Gets the numeric base of this type's representation: 2 (binary) or 10 (decimal).

method.name: getDomain
method.comments: Gets the type domain of this type. Can be `RealDomain`, `ComplexDomain`, or `ImaginaryDomain`.

method.name: getRealType
method.comments:
Gets the corresponding real type of this type. For example, the corresponding real type of
`_Complex double` is `double`.

predicate.name: FloatingPointType
predicate.body:
exists(int kind |
      builtintypes(underlyingElement(this), _, kind, _, _, _) and
      floatingPointTypeMapping(kind, base, domain, realKind, extended)
    )

predicate.name: isExtendedPrecision
predicate.comments: Holds if this type is an extended precision floating-point type, such as `_Float32x`.

class.comments: A floating-point type representing a real number.
A floating-point type representing a complex number.
A floating-point type representing an imaginary number.
A floating-point type whose representation is base 2.
A floating-point type whose representation is base 10.

The C/C++ `float` type.
```
float f;
```

The C/C++ `double` type.
```
double d;
```

The C/C++ `long double` type.
```
long double ld;
```

The GNU C `__float128` primitive type.  This is not standard C/C++.
```
__float128 f128;
```

The GNU C `_Decimal32` primitive type.  This is not standard C/C++.
```
_Decimal32 d32;
```

The GNU C `_Decimal64` primitive type.  This is not standard C/C++.
```
_Decimal64 d64;
```

The GNU C `_Decimal128` primitive type.  This is not standard C/C++.
```
_Decimal128 d128;
```

The C/C++ `void` type. See 4.7.
```
void foo();
```

The C/C++ wide character type.

Note that on some platforms `wchar_t` doesn't exist as a built-in
type but a typedef is provided.  Consider using the `Wchar_t` QL
class to include these types.
```
wchar_t wc;
```

The C/C++ `char8_t` type.  This is available starting with C++20.
```
char8_t c8;
```

The C/C++ `char16_t` type.  This is available starting with C11 and C++11.
```
char16_t c16;
```

The C/C++ `char32_t` type.  This is available starting with C11 and C++11.
```
char32_t c32;
```

The (primitive) type of the C++11 `nullptr` constant.  It is a
distinct type, denoted by `decltype(nullptr)`, that is not itself a pointer
type or a pointer to member type.  The `<cstddef>` header usually defines
the `std::nullptr_t` type as follows:
```
typedef decltype(nullptr) nullptr_t;
```

method.name: getBaseType
method.comments:
Gets the base type of this derived type.

This predicate strips off one level of decoration from a type. For example, it returns `char*` for the PointerType `char**`,
`const int` for the ReferenceType `const int&`, and `long` for the SpecifiedType `volatile long`.

method.name: getExpr
method.comments: Gets the expression whose type is being obtained by this decltype.

method.name: getBaseType
method.comments: Gets the type immediately yielded by this decltype.

predicate.name: parenthesesWouldChangeMeaning
predicate.comments:
Whether an extra pair of parentheses around the expression would change the semantics of this decltype.

The following example shows the effect of an extra pair of parentheses:
```
struct A { double x; };
const A* a = new A();
decltype( a->x ); // type is double
decltype((a->x)); // type is const double&
```
Please consult the C++11 standard for more details.

method.name: getBaseType
method.comments: Gets the type immediately yielded by this typeof.

method.name: getExpr
method.comments: Gets the expression whose type is being obtained by this typeof.

class.comments:
A type obtained by C23 `typeof` or `typeof_unqual` operator taking a type as its
argument. For example:
```
typeof_unqual(const int) b;
```

method.name: getType
method.comments: Gets the expression whose type is being obtained by this typeof.

class.comments:
A type obtained by applying a type transforming intrinsic. For example:
```
__make_unsigned(int) x;
```

method.name: getBaseType
method.comments: Gets the type immediately yielded by this transformation.

method.name: getType
method.comments: Gets the type that is transformed.

method.name: getIntrinsicName
method.body:
intrinsic = 1 and result = "__underlying_type"
    or
    intrinsic = 2 and result = "__bases"
    or
    intrinsic = 3 and result = "__direct_bases"
    or
    intrinsic = 4 and result = "__add_lvalue_reference"
    or
    intrinsic = 5 and result = "__add_pointer"
    or
    intrinsic = 6 and result = "__add_rvalue_reference"
    or
    intrinsic = 7 and result = "__decay"
    or
    intrinsic = 8 and result = "__make_signed"
    or
    intrinsic = 9 and result = "__make_unsigned"
    or
    intrinsic = 10 and result = "__remove_all_extents"
    or
    intrinsic = 11 and result = "__remove_const"
    or
    intrinsic = 12 and result = "__remove_cv"
    or
    intrinsic = 13 and result = "__remove_cvref"
    or
    intrinsic = 14 and result = "__remove_extent"
    or
    intrinsic = 15 and result = "__remove_pointer"
    or
    intrinsic = 16 and result = "__remove_reference_t"
    or
    intrinsic = 17 and result = "__remove_restrict"
    or
    intrinsic = 18 and result = "__remove_volatile"
    or
    intrinsic = 19 and result = "__remove_reference"
method.comments: Gets the name of the intrinsic used to transform the type.

method.name: explain
method.body:
result =
      "application of " + this.getIntrinsicName() + " resulting in {" + this.getBaseType().explain()
        + "}"

class.comments:
A C++ reference type. See 4.9.1.

For C++11 code bases, this includes both _lvalue_ references (`&`) and _rvalue_ references (`&&`).
To distinguish between them, use the LValueReferenceType and RValueReferenceType QL classes.

method.name: getSpecifierString
method.comments:
INTERNAL: Do not use.

Gets all the specifiers of this type as a string in a fixed order (the order
only depends on the specifiers, not on the source program). This is intended
for debugging queries only and is an expensive operation.

class.comments:
A C/C++ array type. See 4.9.1.
```
char table[32];
```

method.name: getArraySize
method.comments:
Gets the number of elements in this array. Only has a result for arrays declared to be of a
constant size. See `getByteSize` for getting the number of bytes.

method.name: getByteSize
method.comments:
Gets the byte size of this array. Only has a result for arrays declared to be of a constant
size. See `getArraySize` for getting the number of elements.

method.name: getSize
method.comments:
Gets the byte size of this array. Only has a result for arrays declared to be of a constant
size. This predicate is a synonym for `getByteSize`. See `getArraySize` for getting the number
of elements.

method.name: explain
method.body:
if exists(this.getArraySize())
    then
      result =
        "array of " + this.getArraySize().toString() + " {" + this.getBaseType().explain() + "}"
    else result = "array of {" + this.getBaseType().explain() + "}"

predicate.name: hasArraySize
predicate.comments:
Holds if this array is declared to be of a constant size. See
`getArraySize` and `getByteSize` to get the size of the array.

predicate.name: isVla
predicate.comments: Holds if this array is a variable-length array (VLA).

class.comments:
A GNU/Clang vector type.

In both Clang and GNU compilers, vector types can be introduced using the
`__attribute__((vector_size(byte_size)))` syntax. The Clang compiler also
allows vector types to be introduced using the `ext_vector_type`,
`neon_vector_type`, and `neon_polyvector_type` attributes (all of which take
an element count rather than a byte size).

In the example below, both `v4si` and `float4` are GNU vector types:
```
typedef int v4si __attribute__ (( vector_size(4*sizeof(int)) ));
typedef float float4 __attribute__((ext_vector_type(4)));
```

method.name: getNumElements
method.comments:
Get the number of elements in this vector type.

For vector types declared using Clang's ext_vector_type, neon_vector_type,
or neon_polyvector_type attribute, this is the value which appears within
the attribute. For vector types declared using the vector_size attribute,
the number of elements is the value in the attribute divided by the size
of a single element.

method.name: getSize
method.comments:
Gets the size, in bytes, of this vector type.

For vector types declared using the vector_size attribute, this is the
value which appears within the attribute. For vector types declared using
Clang's ext_vector_type, neon_vector_type, or neon_polyvector_type
attribute, the byte size is the value in the attribute multiplied by the
byte size of a single element.

method.name: explain
method.body:
result =
      "GNU " + this.getNumElements() + " element vector of {" + this.getBaseType().explain() + "}"

class.comments:
An Arm Scalable vector type.

In the following example, `a` has a scalable vector type consisting
of 8-bit signed integer elements:
```
svint8_t a;
```

method.name: getNumTupleElements
method.comments: Get the number of tuple elements of this scalable vector type.

class.comments:
A C/C++ pointer to a function. See 7.7.
```
int(* pointer)(const void *element1, const void *element2);
```

A C++ reference to a function.
```
int(& reference)(const void *element1, const void *element2);
```

A block type, for example, `int(^)(char, float)`.

Block types (along with blocks themselves) are a language extension
supported by Clang, and by Apple's branch of GCC.
```
int(^ block)(const char *element1, const char *element2)
= ^int (const char *element1, const char *element2) { return element1 - element 2; }
```

A C/C++ pointer to a function, a C++ function reference, or a clang/Apple block.

See `FunctionPointerType`, `FunctionReferenceType` and `BlockType` for more information.

method.name: getReturnType
method.body:
exists(RoutineType t |
      derivedtypes(underlyingElement(this), _, _, unresolveElement(t)) and
      result = t.getReturnType()
    )
method.comments: the return type of this function pointer type

method.name: getParameterType
method.body:
exists(RoutineType t |
      derivedtypes(underlyingElement(this), _, _, unresolveElement(t)) and
      result = t.getParameterType(i)
    )
method.comments: the type of the ith argument of this function pointer type

method.name: getAParameterType
method.body:
exists(RoutineType t |
      derivedtypes(underlyingElement(this), _, _, unresolveElement(t)) and
      result = t.getAParameterType()
    )
method.comments: the type of an argument of this function pointer type

method.name: getNumberOfParameters
method.comments: the number of arguments of this function pointer type

class.comments:
A C++ pointer to data member. See 15.5.
```
class C { public: int m; };
int C::* p = &C::m;          // pointer to data member m of class C
class C c;
int val = c.*p;              // access data member
```

method.name: toString
method.comments: a printable representation of this named element

method.name: getName
method.comments: the name of this type

method.name: getBaseType
method.comments: the base type of this pointer to member type

method.name: getClass
method.comments: the class referred by this pointer to member type

method.name: explain
method.body:
result =
      "pointer to member of " + this.getClass().toString() + " with type {" +
        this.getBaseType().explain() + "}"

class.comments:
A C/C++ routine type. Conceptually, this is what results from stripping
away the pointer from a function pointer type.  It can also occur in C++
code, for example the base type of `myRoutineType` in the following code:
```
using myRoutineType = int(int);

myRoutineType *fp = 0;
```

method.name: toString
method.comments: a printable representation of this named element

method.name: getParameterType
method.comments: Gets the type of the `n`th parameter to this routine.

method.name: getAParameterType
method.comments: Gets the type of a parameter to this routine.

method.name: getReturnType
method.comments: Gets the return type of this routine.

method.name: explain
method.body:
result =
      "function returning {" + this.getReturnType().explain() + "} with arguments (" +
        this.explainParameters(0) + ")"

predicate.name: hasCLinkage

method.name: getMentionedType
method.comments: Gets the type being referenced by this type mention.

method.name: getBaseType
method.comments: Gets the base type of this typedef type.

class.comments:
A traditional C/C++ typedef type. See 4.9.1.  For example the type declared in the following code:
```
typedef int my_int;
```

A using alias C++ typedef type.  For example the type declared in the following code:
```
using my_int2 = int;
```

A C++ `typedef` type that is directly enclosed by a function.  For example the type declared inside the function `foo` in
the following code:
```
int foo(void) { typedef int local; }
```

A C++ `typedef` type that is directly enclosed by a `class`, `struct` or `union`.  For example the type declared inside
the class `C` in the following code:
```
class C { typedef int nested; };
```

predicate.name: isPrivate
predicate.comments: Holds if this member is private.

predicate.name: isProtected
predicate.comments: Holds if this member is protected.

predicate.name: isPublic
predicate.comments: Holds if this member is public.

method.name: getName
method.comments: Gets the name of this type.

method.name: getSimpleName
method.comments:
Gets the simple name of this type, without any template parameters.  For example
if the name of the type is `"myType<int>"`, the simple name is just `"myType"`.

method.name: getLocation
getADeclarationEntry
method.body:
pragma[only_bind_into](result) = pragma[only_bind_into](this).getADeclarationEntryBase()
    or
    not exists(this.getADeclarationEntryBase()) and
    exists(Class t | this.(Class).isConstructedFrom(t) and result = t.getADeclarationEntry())

method.name: getDefinitionLocation
getEnclosingFunction
method.comments: Gets the function that directly encloses this type (if any).

method.name: getADeclaration
method.comments: Gets a child declaration within this user-defined type.

predicate.name: isAnonymous
predicate.comments: Holds if this type is anonymous.

predicate.name: isLocal
predicate.comments:
Holds if this is a local type (that is, a type that has a directly-enclosing
function).

method.name: getType
method.comments: The type which is being declared or defined.

method.name: getRequiresClause
method.comments: Gets the requires clause if this declaration is a template with such a clause.

predicate.name: isTopLevel
predicate.comments:
A top level type declaration entry is not declared within a function, function declaration,
class or typedef.

method.name: getInitializer
method.comments: Gets the initializer of this variable, if any.

method.name: getAnAccess
method.comments: Gets an access to this variable.

method.name: getASpecifier
method.comments:
Gets a specifier of this variable. This includes `extern`, `static`,
`auto`, `private`, `protected`, `public`. Specifiers of the *type* of
this variable, such as `const` and `volatile`, are instead accessed
through `this.getType().getASpecifier()`.

method.name: getAnAttribute
method.comments: Gets an attribute of this variable.

method.name: getName
method.comments: Gets the name of this variable.

method.name: getType
method.comments: Gets the type of this variable.

method.name: getUnderlyingType
method.comments: Gets the type of this variable, after typedefs have been resolved.

method.name: getUnspecifiedType
method.comments:
Gets the type of this variable, after specifiers have been deeply
stripped and typedefs have been resolved.

method.name: getTypeWithAuto
method.comments:
Gets the type of this variable prior to deduction caused by the C++11
`auto` keyword.

If the type of this variable was not declared with the C++11 `auto`
keyword, then this predicate does not hold.

If the type of this variable is completely `auto`, then `result` is an
instance of `AutoType`. For example:

`auto four = 4;`

If the type of this variable is partially `auto`, then a descendant of
`result` is an instance of `AutoType`. For example:

`const auto& c = container;`

method.name: getLocation
getAnAssignedValue
method.body:
result = this.getInitializer().getExpr()
    or
    exists(ConstructorFieldInit cfi | cfi.getTarget() = this and result = cfi.getExpr())
    or
    exists(AssignExpr ae | ae.getLValue().(Access).getTarget() = this and result = ae.getRValue())
    or
    exists(ClassAggregateLiteral l | result = l.getAFieldExpr(this))
method.comments:
Gets an expression that is assigned to this variable somewhere in the
program.

method.name: getAnAssignment
method.comments:
Gets an assignment expression that assigns to this variable.
For example: `x=...` or `x+=...`.

This does _not_ include the initialization of the variable. Use
`Variable.getInitializer()` to get the variable's initializer,
or use `Variable.getAnAssignedValue()` to get an expression that
is the right-hand side of an assignment or an initialization of
the variable.

predicate.name: hasInitializer
predicate.comments: Holds if this variable has an initializer.

predicate.name: isConst
predicate.comments: Holds if this variable is `const`.

predicate.name: isVolatile
predicate.comments: Holds if this variable is `volatile`.

predicate.name: declaredUsingAutoType
predicate.comments:
Holds if the type of this variable is declared using the C++ `auto`
keyword.

predicate.name: isConstexpr
predicate.comments: Holds if this variable is `constexpr`.

predicate.name: isConstinit
predicate.comments: Holds if this variable is declared `constinit`.

predicate.name: isThreadLocal
predicate.comments: Holds if this variable is `thread_local`.

predicate.name: isConstructedFrom
predicate.comments:
Holds if this variable is constructed from `v` as a result
of template instantiation. If so, it originates either from a template
variable or from a variable nested in a template class.

predicate.name: isStructuredBinding
predicate.comments:
Holds if this variable is declared as part of a structured binding
declaration. For example, `x` in `auto [x, y] = ...`.

predicate.name: isCompilerGenerated
predicate.comments:
Holds if this is a compiler-generated variable. For example, a
[range-based for loop](http://en.cppreference.com/w/cpp/language/range-for)
typically has three compiler-generated variables, named `__range`,
`__begin`, and `__end`:

`for (char c : str) { ... }`

predicate.name: isSpecialization
predicate.body:
exists(VariableDeclarationEntry vde |
      var_decls(unresolveElement(vde), underlyingElement(this), _, _, _) and
      vde.isSpecialization()
    )
predicate.comments: Holds if this variable is a template specialization.

method.name: getVariable
method.comments: Gets the variable which is being declared or defined.

method.name: getName
method.body:
exists(string name |
      var_decls(underlyingElement(this), _, _, name, _) and
      (
        name != "" and result = name
        or
        name = "" and result = this.getVariable().(LocalVariable).getName()
        or
        name = "" and
        not this instanceof ParameterDeclarationEntry and
        result = this.getVariable().(Parameter).getName()
      )
    )

method.name: getType
method.comments: Gets the type of the variable which is being declared or defined.

method.name: getRequiresClause
method.comments: Gets the requires clause if this declaration is a template with such a clause.

predicate.name: isDefinition
predicate.comments:
Holds if this is a definition of a variable.

This always holds for local variables and member variables, but need
not hold for global variables. In the case of function parameters,
this holds precisely when the enclosing `FunctionDeclarationEntry` is
a definition.

predicate.name: isSpecialization
predicate.comments: Holds if this declaration is a template specialization.

method.name: getFunctionDeclarationEntry
method.comments:
Gets the function declaration or definition which this parameter
description is part of.

method.name: getIndex
method.comments: Gets the zero-based index of this parameter.

method.name: toString
method.body:
this.isDefinition() and
    result = "definition of " + this.getName()
    or
    not this.isDefinition() and
    if this.getName() = this.getCanonicalName()
    then result = "declaration of " + this.getName()
    else result = "declaration of " + this.getCanonicalName() + " as " + this.getName()
    or
    result = this.getAnonymousParameterDescription()

method.name: getTypedName
method.body:
exists(string typeString, string nameString |
      (
        if exists(this.getType().getName())
        then typeString = this.getType().getName()
        else typeString = ""
      ) and
      (if exists(this.getName()) then nameString = this.getName() else nameString = "") and
      if typeString != "" and nameString != ""
      then result = typeString + " " + nameString
      else result = typeString + nameString
    )
method.comments:
Gets the name of this `ParameterDeclarationEntry` including it's type.

For example: "int p".

method.name: getFunction
method.comments: Gets the function to which this variable belongs.

method.name: getFunction
method.body:
exists(DeclStmt s | s.getADeclaration() = this and s.getEnclosingFunction() = result)
    or
    exists(ConditionDeclExpr e | e.getVariable() = this and e.getEnclosingFunction() = result)
    or
    orphaned_variables(underlyingElement(this), unresolveElement(result))
    or
    coroutine_placeholder_variable(underlyingElement(this), _, unresolveElement(result))

class.comments:
A variable whose contents always have static storage duration. This can be a
global variable, a namespace variable, a static local variable, or a static
member variable.

predicate.name: StaticStorageDurationVariable
predicate.body:
this instanceof GlobalOrNamespaceVariable
    or
    this.(LocalVariable).isStatic()
    or
    this.(MemberVariable).isStatic()

predicate.name: hasDynamicInitialization
predicate.comments: Holds if the initializer for this variable is evaluated at runtime.

class.comments: A C++ local variable declared as `static`.

method.name: getALinkTarget
method.comments: Gets a link target which compiled or referenced this global or namespace variable.

class.comments:
A C++14 variable template. For example, in the following code the variable
template `v` defines a family of variables:

template<class T>
T v;
```

method.name: getAnInstantiation
method.comments: Gets an instantiation of this variable template.

method.comments:
Gets the variable template from which this instantiation was instantiated.

Example: For `int x<int>`, returns `T x`.

class.comments: An explicit specialization of a C++ variable template.

method.name: getPrimaryTemplate
method.comments:
Gets the primary template for the specialization (the function template
this specializes).

class.comments:
An assertion, that is, a condition that is only false if there is a bug in
the program. To add support for more, define additional subclasses. A
typical subclass will extend extend either `MacroInvocation` (for assertion
macros) or `FunctionCall` (for assertion functions).

method.name: getAsserted
method.comments: Gets the expression whose truth is being asserted.

class.comments:
A libc assert, as defined in assert.h. A macro with a head
that matches the prefix "assert(", and expands to a conditional
expression which may terminate the program.

method.name: getAsserted
method.body: exists(ConditionalExpr ce | this.getAGeneratedElement() = ce | result = ce.getCondition())

class.comments:
A macro assert that expands to an if statement; the head is taken
to be "Assert(x, y)", but further alternatives could be added.

method.name: getAsserted
method.body: exists(IfStmt i | this.getAGeneratedElement() = i | result = i.getCondition())

class.comments:
An assertion that is not completely disabled in release builds but returns a
Boolean value to enable recovery from unexpected anomalous behavior. This
style of assert is advocated by the _Power of 10_ rules and the _NASA JPL
Coding Standard_.

The C/C++ `int8_t` type.
The C/C++ `int16_t` type.

The C/C++ `wchar_t` type.

Note that on some platforms `wchar_t` doesn't exist as a built-in

The type that the Microsoft C/C++ `__int8` type specifier is a
synonym for.  Note that since `__int8` is not a distinct type,

This class is meaningless if a Microsoft compiler was not used.

predicate.name: MicrosoftInt8Type

this instanceof CharType and
    not this.isExplicitlyUnsigned() and
    not this.isExplicitlySigned()

class.comments:
The type that the Microsoft C/C++ `__int16` type specifier is a
synonym for.  Note that since `__int16` is not a distinct type,
`MicrosoftInt16Type` corresponds to an existing `IntegralType` as
well.

This class is meaningless if a Microsoft compiler was not used.

The type that the Microsoft C/C++ `__int32` type specifier is a

The type that the Microsoft C/C++ `__int64` type specifier is a

This class is meaningless if a Microsoft compiler was not used.

this instanceof LongLongType and
    not this.isExplicitlyUnsigned() and
