class.comments:
The `__builtin_va_list` type, used to provide variadic functionality.

This is a complement to the `__builtin_va_start`, `__builtin_va_end`,
`__builtin_va_copy` and `__builtin_va_arg` expressions.
A type that is used to represent time in a 'packed' form, such as an integer.

A type that is used to represent times and dates in an 'unpacked' form, that is,
with separate fields for day, month, year etc.
A `FieldAccess` that would represent an access to a field on a `struct`.

predicate.name: DateStructFieldAccess
predicate.body:
exists(Field f, StructLikeClass struct |
      f.getAnAccess() = this and
      struct.getAField() = f
    )

class.comments: A `FieldAccess` where access is to a day of the month field of the `struct`.
A `FieldAccess` where access is to a month field of the `struct`.
A `FieldAccess` where access is to a year field of the `struct`.
A `DayFieldAccess` for the `SYSTEMTIME` struct.
A `MonthFieldAccess` for the `SYSTEMTIME` struct.
A `YearFieldAccess` for the `SYSTEMTIME` struct.
A `DayFieldAccess` for `struct tm`.
A `MonthFieldAccess` for `struct tm`.
A `YearFieldAccess` for `struct tm`.

Options that control the dependencies generated by
this library.

predicate.name: preferTemplateDeps
predicate.comments:
Holds if dependencies should only be generated in templates rather than
in both templates and instantiations, where possible.  This is expensive
to compute, but tends to produce dependencies that are easier to read.

class.comments:
An Element that can be the source of a transitive dependency.  This is any
Element that is not in a template instantiation, plus declarations of template
specializations (even though they are technically in an instantiation) because
we need to generate (at least) a dependency from them to the general declaration.

predicate.name: DependsSource
predicate.body:
not this.isFromTemplateInstantiation(_) or
    // allow DeclarationEntrys of template specializations
    this.(DeclarationEntry).getDeclaration().(Function).isConstructedFrom(_) or
    this.(DeclarationEntry).getDeclaration().(Class).isConstructedFrom(_)

class.comments:
A program element which can be the target of inter-function or inter-file dependencies.

This is the union of Declaration, DeclarationEntry and Macro, minus various kinds of declaration:
FriendDecl is not included, as a friend declaration cannot be the target of a dependency (nor, as it happens, can they be a source).
TemplateParameter and related UserTypes are not included, as they are intrinsic sub-components of their associated template.
Template instantiations are excluded, as the template itself is more useful as a dependency target.
Stack variables and local types are excluded, as they are lexically tied to their enclosing function, and intra-function dependencies
can only be inter-file dependencies in pathological cases.
Builtin functions and macros are excluded, as dependencies on them do not translate to inter-file dependencies (note that static functions
and declarations within anonymous namespaces cannot be excluded for this reason, as the declaration can be in a header).
DeclarationEntrys are only needed if they're not definitions, for the definition to declaration dependency.

method.name: getADependentElement
method.comments:
Gets an element which depends upon this symbol.

To a first approximation, dependent elements can be thought of as occurrences of the symbol's name: instances of `VariableAccess`
for `Variable` symbols, instances of `MacroInvocation` for `Macro` symbols, and so on.

category:
1 - C/C++ compile-time dependency
2 - C/C++ link-time dependency (or transitive dependency with a link-time component)

predicate.name: Symbol
predicate.body:
(
      exists(EnumConstant ec | this = ec and not ec.getDeclaringEnum() instanceof LocalEnum)
      or
      this instanceof Macro and this.getFile().getAbsolutePath() != ""
      or
      this instanceof DeclarationEntry and
      not this.(VariableDeclarationEntry).getVariable() instanceof LocalScopeVariable and
      not this.(FunctionDeclarationEntry).getFunction() instanceof BuiltInFunction and
      not this.(TypeDeclarationEntry).getType() instanceof LocalEnum and
      not this.(TypeDeclarationEntry).getType() instanceof LocalClass and
      not this.(TypeDeclarationEntry).getType() instanceof LocalTypedefType and
      not this.(TypeDeclarationEntry).getType() instanceof TypeTemplateParameter
      or
      this instanceof NamespaceDeclarationEntry
    )

class.comments: An expression that reads from an environment variable.

method.name: getEnvironmentVariable
method.body:
// Conveniently, it's always the first argument to the call
    this.(Call).getArgument(0).(TextLiteral).getValue() = result
method.comments: The name of the environment variable.

method.name: getSourceDescription
method.comments:
A very short description of the source, suitable for use in
an error message.

class.comments: A macro defining NULL.
A use of the NULL macro.
A C++ class or structure which (possibly by inheritance) has at least one virtual method.

predicate.name: PolymorphicClass
predicate.body: exists(MemberFunction f | this.getABaseClass*() = f.getDeclaringType() and f.isVirtual())

class.comments: A reason for a specific buffer write size estimate.

method.name: toString
method.comments: Returns the name of the concrete class.

method.name: getDescription
method.comments: Returns a human readable representation of this reason.

method.name: combineWith
method.body:
exists(int grade, int otherGrade |
      gradeToReason(grade, this) and gradeToReason(otherGrade, other)
    |
      if otherGrade < grade then result = other else result = this
    )
method.comments:
Combine estimate reasons. Used to give a reason for the size of a format string
conversion given reasons coming from its individual specifiers.

class.comments:
No particular reason given. This is currently used for backward compatibility so that
classes derived from BufferWrite and overriding `getMaxData/0` still work with the
queries as intended.

The estimation comes from rough bounds just based on the type (e.g.
`0 <= x < 2^32` for an unsigned 32 bit integer).

method.name: getDescription
method.body: result = "based on flow analysis of value bounds with a widening approximation"

method.name: getDescription
method.body: result = "based on flow analysis of value bounds"

class.comments:
A function that can be identified as a `printf` style formatting
function by its use of the GNU `format` attribute.

method.name: getFormatParameterIndex

predicate.name: AttributeFormattingFunction
predicate.body:
exists(PrintfFormatAttribute printf_attrib |
      printf_attrib = this.getAnAttribute() and
      exists(printf_attrib.getFirstFormatArgIndex()) // exclude `vprintf` style format functions
    )

class.comments:
A function not in the standard library which takes a `printf`-like formatting
string and a variable number of arguments.

method.name: getOutputParameterIndex
method.body:
callsVariadicFormatter(this, "f", _, result) and isStream = true
    or
    callsVariadicFormatter(this, "s", _, result) and isStream = false

class.comments: A call to one of the formatting functions.

method.name: getTarget
method.comments: Gets the formatting function being called.

method.name: getArgument
method.comments:
Gets the `i`th argument for this call.

The range of `i` is from `0` to `getNumberOfArguments() - 1`.

method.name: getNumberOfArguments
method.comments:
Gets the number of actual parameters in this call; use
`getArgument(i)` with `i` between `0` and `result - 1` to
retrieve actuals.

method.name: getFormatParameterIndex
method.comments: Gets the index at which the format string occurs in the argument list.

method.name: getFormat
method.comments: Gets the format expression used in this call.

method.name: getFormatArgument
method.body:
exists(int i |
      result = this.getArgument(i) and
      n >= 0 and
      n = i - this.getTarget().getFirstFormatArgumentIndex()
    )
method.comments: Gets the nth argument to the format (including width and precision arguments).

method.name: getConversionArgument
method.body:
exists(FormatLiteral fl |
      fl = this.getFormat() and
      (
        result = this.getFormatArgument(fl.getParameterFieldValue(n))
        or
        result = this.getFormatArgument(fl.getFormatArgumentIndexFor(n, 2)) and
        not exists(fl.getParameterFieldValue(n))
      )
    )
method.comments: Gets the argument corresponding to the nth conversion specifier.

method.name: getMinFieldWidthArgument
method.body:
exists(FormatLiteral fl |
      fl = this.getFormat() and
      (
        result = this.getFormatArgument(fl.getMinFieldWidthParameterFieldValue(n))
        or
        result = this.getFormatArgument(fl.getFormatArgumentIndexFor(n, 0)) and
        not exists(fl.getMinFieldWidthParameterFieldValue(n))
      )
    )
method.comments:
Gets the argument corresponding to the nth conversion specifier's
minimum field width (has no result if that conversion specifier has
an explicit minimum field width).

method.name: getPrecisionArgument
method.body:
exists(FormatLiteral fl |
      fl = this.getFormat() and
      (
        result = this.getFormatArgument(fl.getPrecisionParameterFieldValue(n))
        or
        result = this.getFormatArgument(fl.getFormatArgumentIndexFor(n, 1)) and
        not exists(fl.getPrecisionParameterFieldValue(n))
      )
    )
method.comments:
Gets the argument corresponding to the nth conversion specifier's
precision (has no result if that conversion specifier has an explicit
precision).

method.name: getNumFormatArgument
method.comments:
Gets the number of arguments to this call that are parameters to the
format string.

method.name: getOutputArgument
method.comments:
Gets the argument, if any, to which the output is written. If `isStream` is
`true`, the output argument is a stream (that is, this call behaves like
`fprintf`). If `isStream` is `false`, the output argument is a buffer (that
is, this call behaves like `sprintf`)

class.comments: A class to represent format strings that occur as arguments to invocations of formatting functions.

method.name: getUse
method.comments: Gets the function call where this format string is used.

method.name: getDefaultCharType
method.comments:
Gets the default character type expected for `%s` by this format literal.  Typically
`char` or `wchar_t`.

method.name: getNonDefaultCharType
method.comments:
Gets the non-default character type expected for `%S` by this format literal.  Typically
`wchar_t` or `char`.  On some snapshots there may be multiple results where we can't tell
which is correct for a particular function.

method.name: getWideCharType
method.comments:
Gets the wide character type for this format literal.  This is usually `wchar_t`.  On some
snapshots there may be multiple results where we can't tell which is correct for a
particular function.

method.name: getFormat
method.comments:
Gets the format string, with '%%' and '%@' replaced by '_' (to avoid processing
them as format specifiers).

method.name: getNumConvSpec
method.comments: Gets the number of conversion specifiers (not counting `%%`)

method.name: getConvSpecOffset
method.comments:
Gets the position in the string at which the nth conversion specifier
starts.

method.name: getConvSpecRegexp
method.comments: Gets a regular expression used for matching a whole conversion specifier.

method.name: getConvSpec
method.body:
exists(int offset, string fmt, string rst, string regexp |
      offset = this.getConvSpecOffset(n) and
      fmt = this.getFormat() and
      rst = fmt.substring(offset, fmt.length()) and
      regexp = this.getConvSpecRegexp() and
      result = rst.regexpCapture(regexp, 1)
    )
method.comments: Gets the nth conversion specifier (including the initial `%`).

method.name: getParameterField
method.comments: Gets the parameter field of the nth conversion specifier (for example, `1$`).

method.name: getParameterFieldValue
method.comments:
Gets the parameter field of the nth conversion specifier (if it has one) as a
zero-based number.

method.name: getFlags
method.comments: Gets the flags of the nth conversion specifier.

method.name: getMinFieldWidthOpt
method.comments:
Gets the minimum field width of the nth conversion specifier
(empty string if none is given).

method.name: getMinFieldWidth
method.comments: Gets the minimum field width of the nth conversion specifier.

method.name: getMinFieldWidthParameterFieldValue
method.comments:
Gets the zero-based parameter number of the minimum field width of the nth
conversion specifier, if it is implicit and uses a parameter field (such as `*1$`).

method.name: getPrecisionOpt
method.comments: Gets the precision of the nth conversion specifier (empty string if none is given).

method.name: getPrecision
method.comments: Gets the precision of the nth conversion specifier.

method.name: getPrecisionParameterFieldValue
method.comments:
Gets the zero-based parameter number of the precision of the nth conversion
specifier, if it is implicit and uses a parameter field (such as `*1$`).

method.name: getLength
method.comments: Gets the length flag of the nth conversion specifier.

method.name: getConversionChar
method.comments: Gets the conversion character of the nth conversion specifier.

method.name: getIntegralConversion
method.body:
exists(string len |
      len = this.getLength(n) and
      (
        len = "hh" and result instanceof IntType
        or
        len = "h" and result instanceof IntType
        or
        len = "l" and result = this.getLongType()
        or
        len = ["ll", "L", "q"] and
        result instanceof LongLongType
        or
        len = "j" and result = this.getIntmax_t()
        or
        len = ["z", "Z"] and
        (result = this.getSize_t() or result = this.getSsize_t())
        or
        len = "t" and result = this.getPtrdiff_t()
        or
        len = "I" and
        (result = this.getSize_t() or result = this.getPtrdiff_t())
        or
        len = "I32" and
        exists(MicrosoftInt32Type t | t.getUnsigned() = result.(IntegralType).getUnsigned())
        or
        len = "I64" and
        exists(MicrosoftInt64Type t | t.getUnsigned() = result.(IntegralType).getUnsigned())
        or
        len = "" and result instanceof IntType
      )
    )
method.comments:
Gets the family of integral types required by the nth conversion
specifier's length flag.

method.name: getIntegralDisplayType
method.body:
exists(string len |
      len = this.getLength(n) and
      (
        len = "hh" and result instanceof CharType
        or
        len = "h" and result instanceof ShortType
        or
        len = "l" and result = this.getLongType()
        or
        len = ["ll", "L", "q"] and
        result instanceof LongLongType
        or
        len = "j" and result = this.getIntmax_t()
        or
        len = ["z", "Z"] and
        (result = this.getSize_t() or result = this.getSsize_t())
        or
        len = "t" and result = this.getPtrdiff_t()
        or
        len = "I" and
        (result = this.getSize_t() or result = this.getPtrdiff_t())
        or
        len = "I32" and
        exists(MicrosoftInt32Type t | t.getUnsigned() = result.(IntegralType).getUnsigned())
        or
        len = "I64" and
        exists(MicrosoftInt64Type t | t.getUnsigned() = result.(IntegralType).getUnsigned())
        or
        len = "" and result instanceof IntType
      )
    )
method.comments:
Gets the family of integral types output / displayed by the nth
conversion specifier's length flag.

method.name: getFloatingPointConversion
method.body:
exists(string len |
      len = this.getLength(n) and
      if len = ["L", "ll"] then result instanceof LongDoubleType else result instanceof DoubleType
    )
method.comments:
Gets the family of floating point types required by the nth conversion
specifier's length flag.

method.name: getStorePointerConversion
method.body:
exists(IntegralType base |
      exists(string len | len = this.getLength(n) |
        len = "hh" and base instanceof CharType
        or
        len = "h" and base instanceof ShortType
        or
        len = "l" and base = this.getLongType()
        or
        len = ["ll", "L"] and
        base instanceof LongLongType
        or
        len = "q" and base instanceof LongLongType
      ) and
      base.isSigned() and
      base = result.getBaseType()
    )
method.comments:
Gets the family of pointer types required by the nth conversion
specifier's length flag.

method.name: getConversionType
method.body:
result = this.getConversionType1(n) or
    result = this.getConversionType1b(n) or
    result = this.getConversionType2(n) or
    result = this.getConversionType3(n) or
    result = this.getConversionType4(n) or
    result = this.getConversionType6(n) or
    result = this.getConversionType7(n) or
    result = this.getConversionType8(n) or
    result = this.getConversionType9(n) or
    result = this.getConversionType10(n)
method.comments: Gets the argument type required by the nth conversion specifier.

method.name: getConversionTypeAlternate
method.body:
exists(string len, string conv |
      this.parseConvSpec(n, _, _, _, _, _, len, conv) and
      (len != "l" and len != "w" and len != "h") and
      this.getUse().getTarget().getFormatCharType().getSize() > 1 and // wide function
      (
        conv = "c" and
        result = this.getNonDefaultCharType()
        or
        conv = "C" and
        result = this.getDefaultCharType()
        or
        conv = "s" and
        result.(PointerType).getBaseType() = this.getNonDefaultCharType()
        or
        conv = "S" and
        result.(PointerType).getBaseType() = this.getDefaultCharType()
      )
    )
method.comments:
Gets an alternate argument type that would be required by the nth
conversion specifier on a Microsoft or non-Microsoft platform, opposite
to that of the snapshot. This may be useful for answering 'what might
happen' questions.

method.name: getFormatArgumentIndexFor
method.body:
this.hasFormatArgumentIndexFor(n, mode) and
    (3 * n) + mode =
      rank[result + 1](int n2, int mode2 |
        this.hasFormatArgumentIndexFor(n2, mode2)
      |
        (3 * n2) + mode2
      )
method.comments:
Gets the computed format argument index for the nth conversion specifier of this
format string (if `mode = 2`), it's minimum field width (if `mode = 0`) or it's
precision (if `mode = 1`).  Has no result if that element is not present.  Does
not account for positional arguments (`$`).

method.name: getNumArgNeeded
method.body:
exists(this.getConvSpecOffset(n)) and
    exists(this.getConversionChar(n)) and
    result = count(int mode | this.hasFormatArgumentIndexFor(n, mode))
method.comments:
Gets the number of arguments required by the nth conversion specifier
of this format string.

method.name: getNumArgNeeded
method.comments: Gets the number of arguments needed by this format string.

method.name: getMaxConvertedLength
method.comments:
Gets the maximum length of the string that can be produced by the nth
conversion specifier of this format string; has no result if this cannot
be determined.

method.name: getMaxConvertedLength
getMaxConvertedLengthLimited
method.comments:
Gets the maximum length of the string that can be produced by the nth
conversion specifier of this format string, except that float to string
conversions are assumed to be 8 characters.  This is helpful for
determining whether a buffer overflow is caused by long float to string
conversions.

method.name: getMaxConvertedLengthLimited
method.comments:
Gets the maximum length of the string that can be produced by the nth
conversion specifier of this format string, specifying the reason for the
estimation, except that float to string conversions are assumed to be 8
characters.  This is helpful for determining whether a buffer overflow is
caused by long float to string conversions.

method.name: getConstantPart
method.body:
if n = 0
    then result = this.getFormat().substring(0, this.getConvSpecOffset(0))
    else
      result =
        this.getFormat()
            .substring(this.getConvSpecOffset(n - 1) + this.getConvSpec(n - 1).length(),
              this.getConvSpecOffset(n))
method.comments:
Gets the constant part of the format string just before the nth
conversion specifier.  If `n` is zero, this will be the constant prefix
before the 0th specifier, otherwise it is the string between the end
of the n-1th specifier and the nth specifier.  Has no result if `n`
is negative or not strictly less than the number of conversion
specifiers.

method.name: getConstantSuffix
method.body:
exists(int n |
      n = this.getNumConvSpec() and
      (
        if n > 0
        then
          result =
            this.getFormat()
                .substring(this.getConvSpecOffset(n - 1) + this.getConvSpec(n - 1).length(),
                  this.getFormat().length())
        else result = this.getFormat()
      )
    )
method.comments:
Gets the constant part of the format string after the last conversion
specifier.

method.name: getMaxConvertedLength
method.comments:
Gets the maximum length of the string that can be produced by this format
string.  Has no result if this cannot be determined.

method.name: getMaxConvertedLengthLimited
method.comments:
Gets the maximum length of the string that can be produced by this format
string, except that float to string conversions are assumed to be 8
characters.  This is helpful for determining whether a buffer overflow
is caused by long float to string conversions.

method.name: getMaxConvertedLengthWithReason
method.comments:
Gets the maximum length of the string that can be produced by this format
string, specifying the reason for the estimate. Has no result if no estimate
can be found.

method.name: getMaxConvertedLengthLimitedWithReason
method.comments:
Gets the maximum length of the string that can be produced by this format
string, specifying the reason for the estimate, except that float to string
conversions are assumed to be 8 characters.  This is helpful for determining
whether a buffer overflow is caused by long float to string conversions.

predicate.name: isMicrosoft
predicate.comments:
Holds if this `FormatLiteral` is in a context that supports
Microsoft rules and extensions.

predicate.name: parseConvSpec
predicate.body:
exists(int offset, string fmt, string rst, string regexp |
      offset = this.getConvSpecOffset(n) and
      fmt = this.getFormat() and
      rst = fmt.substring(offset, fmt.length()) and
      regexp = this.getConvSpecRegexp() and
      (
        spec = rst.regexpCapture(regexp, 1) and
        params = rst.regexpCapture(regexp, 2) and
        flags = rst.regexpCapture(regexp, 3) and
        width = rst.regexpCapture(regexp, 4) and
        prec = rst.regexpCapture(regexp, 5) and
        len = rst.regexpCapture(regexp, 6) and
        conv = rst.regexpCapture(regexp, 7)
        or
        spec = rst.regexpCapture(regexp, 1) and
        not exists(rst.regexpCapture(regexp, 2)) and
        params = "" and
        flags = "" and
        width = "" and
        prec = "" and
        len = "" and
        conv = "%"
      )
    )
predicate.comments:
Holds if the arguments are a parsing of a conversion specifier to this
format string, where `n` is which conversion specifier to parse, `spec` is
the whole conversion specifier, `params` is the argument to be converted
in case it's not positional, `flags` contains additional format flags,
`width` is the maximum width option of this input, `len` is the length
flag of this input, and `conv` is the conversion character of this input.

Each parameter is the empty string if no value is given by the conversion
specifier.

predicate.name: hasAlternateFlag
predicate.comments: Holds if the nth conversion specifier has alternate flag ("#").

predicate.name: isZeroPadded
predicate.comments: Holds if the nth conversion specifier has zero padding flag ("0").

predicate.name: isLeftAdjusted
predicate.comments:
Holds if the nth conversion specifier has flag left adjustment flag
("-").  Note that this overrides the zero padding flag.

predicate.name: hasBlank
predicate.comments: Holds if the nth conversion specifier has the blank flag (" ").

predicate.name: hasSign
predicate.comments: Holds if the nth conversion specifier has the explicit sign flag ("+").

predicate.name: hasThousandsGrouping
predicate.comments: Holds if the nth conversion specifier has the thousands grouping flag ("'").

predicate.name: hasAlternativeDigits
predicate.comments: Holds if the nth conversion specifier has the alternative digits flag ("I").

predicate.name: hasMinFieldWidth
predicate.comments: Holds if the nth conversion specifier has a minimum field width.

predicate.name: hasExplicitMinFieldWidth
predicate.comments:
Holds if the nth conversion specifier has an explicitly given minimum
field width.

predicate.name: hasImplicitMinFieldWidth
predicate.comments:
Holds if the nth conversion specifier has an implicitly given minimum
field width (either "*" or "*i$" for some number i).

predicate.name: hasPrecision
predicate.comments: Holds if the nth conversion specifier has a precision.

predicate.name: hasExplicitPrecision
predicate.comments: Holds if the nth conversion specifier has an explicitly given precision.

predicate.name: hasImplicitPrecision
predicate.comments:
Holds if the nth conversion specifier has an implicitly given precision
(either "*" or "*i$" for some number i).

predicate.name: specsAreKnown
predicate.comments:
Holds if all conversion specifiers of this format string have been
parsed by the library (this does not hold if one or more unrecognized
format specifiers are present in the format string).

Gets the position at which the input string or stream parameter occurs,
if this function does not read from standard input.

method.name: getFormatParameterIndex

predicate.name: Scanf
predicate.body:
this.hasGlobalOrStdOrBslName("scanf") or // scanf(format, args...)
    this.hasGlobalOrStdOrBslName("wscanf") or // wscanf(format, args...)

predicate.name: Fscanf

this.hasGlobalName("_snscanf") or // _snscanf(src, max_amount, format, args...)

method.name: getScanfFunction
method.comments: Gets the `scanf`-like function that is called.

method.name: getInputParameterIndex
method.comments:
Gets the position at which the input string or stream parameter occurs,
if this function call does not read from standard input.

method.name: getFormatParameterIndex
method.comments: Gets the position at which the format parameter occurs.

method.name: getFormat
method.comments: Gets the format expression used in this call.

method.name: getOutputArgument
method.comments:
Gets the output argument at position `n` in the vararg list of this call.

The range of `n` is from `0` to `this.getNumberOfOutputArguments() - 1`.

method.name: getAnOutputArgument
method.comments: Gets an output argument given to this call in vararg position.

method.name: getNumberOfOutputArguments

predicate.name: isWideCharDefault
predicate.comments:
Holds if the default meaning of `%s` is a `wchar_t*` string
(rather than a `char*`).

class.comments: A class to represent format strings that occur as arguments to invocations of `scanf` functions.

method.name: getUse
method.comments: the function call where this format string is used

method.name: getFormat
method.comments:
Gets the format string itself, transformed as follows:
- '%%' is replaced with '_'
(this avoids accidentally processing them as format specifiers)
- '%*' is replaced with '_'
(%*any is matched but not assigned to an argument)

method.name: getNumConvSpec
method.comments: Gets the number of conversion specifiers (not counting `%%` and `%*`...).

method.name: getConvSpecOffset
method.body:
n = 0 and result = this.getFormat().indexOf("%", 0, 0)
    or
    n > 0 and
    exists(int p |
      n = p + 1 and result = this.getFormat().indexOf("%", 0, this.getConvSpecOffset(p) + 2)
    )
method.comments: Gets the position in the string at which the nth conversion specifier starts.

method.name: getConvSpecRegexp
method.comments: Gets the regular expression used for matching a whole conversion specifier.

method.name: getMaxWidthOpt
method.comments: Gets the maximum width option of the nth input (empty string if none is given).

method.name: getMaxWidth
method.comments: Gets the maximum width of the nth input.

method.name: getLength
method.comments: Gets the length flag of the nth conversion specifier.

method.name: getConversionChar
method.comments: Gets the conversion character of the nth conversion specifier.

method.name: getMaxConvertedLength
method.comments:
Gets the maximum length of the string that can be produced by the nth
conversion specifier of this format string; fails if no estimate is
possible (or implemented).

Holds if the arguments are a parsing of a conversion specifier to this

Each parameter is the empty string if no value is given by the conversion

class.comments: An analyzed null terminated string.

method.name: getMaxLength
method.comments:
Gets the maximum length (including null) this string can be, when this
can be calculated.

predicate.name: AnalyzedString
predicate.body:
this.getUnspecifiedType() instanceof ArrayType or
    this.getUnspecifiedType() instanceof PointerType

class.comments: A call to a strlen like function.

method.name: getStringExpr
method.comments: The string argument passed into this strlen-like call.

predicate.name: StrlenCall
predicate.body:
this.getTarget().hasGlobalOrStdName("strlen") or
    this.getTarget().hasGlobalOrStdName("wcslen") or
    this.getTarget().hasGlobalName("_mbslen") or
    this.getTarget().hasGlobalName("_mbslen_l") or
    this.getTarget().hasGlobalName("_mbstrlen") or
    this.getTarget().hasGlobalName("_mbstrlen_l")

class.comments:
A call that performs a string concatenation. A string can be either a C
string (i.e., a value of type `char*`), or a C++ string (i.e., a value of
type `std::string`).

method.name: getAnOperand
method.comments:
Gets an operand of this concatenation (one of the string operands being
concatenated).
Will not return out param for sprintf-like functions, but will consider the format string
to be part of the operands.

method.name: getResultNode
method.body:
if this.getTarget() instanceof StrcatFunction
    then
      result.asDefiningArgument() =
        this.getArgument(this.getTarget().(StrcatFunction).getParamDest())
      or
      // Hardcoding it is also the return
      result.asExpr() = this.(Call)
    else
      if this.getTarget() instanceof StrlcatFunction
      then (
        result.asDefiningArgument() =
          this.getArgument(this.getTarget().(StrlcatFunction).getParamDest())
      ) else
        if this instanceof FormattingFunctionCall
        then result.asDefiningArgument() = this.(FormattingFunctionCall).getOutputArgument(_)
        else result.asExpr() = this.(Call)
method.comments: Gets the data flow node representing the concatenation result.

predicate.name: StringConcatenation
predicate.body:
this instanceof FormattingFunctionCall
    or
    this.getTarget() instanceof StrcatFunction
    or
    this.getTarget() instanceof StrlcatFunction
    or
    // operator+ and ostream (<<) concat
    exists(Call call, Operator op |
      call.getTarget() = op and
      op.hasQualifiedName(["std", "bsl"], ["operator+", "operator<<"]) and
      op.getType()
          .stripType()
          .(UserType)
          .hasQualifiedName(["std", "bsl"], ["basic_string", "basic_ostream"]) and
      this = call
    )

class.comments:
A class that is either a `struct` or just has getters and setters
for its members. In either case it just stores data and has no
real encapsulation.

method.name: getASetter
method.comments:
Gets a setter function in this class, setting the given variable.
This is a function whose name begins "set"... that assigns to this variable and no other
member variable of the class. In addition, it takes a single parameter of
type the type of the corresponding member variable.

method.name: getAGetter
method.comments:
Gets a getter function in this class, getting the given variable.
This is a function whose name begins "get"... that reads this variable and no other
member variable of the class. In addition, its return type is the type
of the corresponding member variable.

predicate.name: StructLikeClass
predicate.body:
this instanceof Struct
    or
    forall(MemberFunction f | f = this.getAMemberFunction() |
      exists(MemberVariable v | setter(v, f, this) or getter(v, f, this))
      or
      f instanceof Constructor
      or
      f instanceof Destructor
      or
      // Allow the copy and move assignment ops - still struct-like
      f instanceof CopyAssignmentOperator
      or
      f instanceof MoveAssignmentOperator
    )

class.comments:
A type that acts as a mutex.  This class is extended below and and may
be extended in `Options.qll`.

method.name: getLockAccess
method.comments: Gets a call that locks or tries to lock any mutex of this type.

method.name: getMustlockAccess
method.comments: Gets a call that always locks any mutex of this type.

method.name: getTrylockAccess
method.comments:
Gets a call that tries to lock any mutex of this type,
by may return without success.

method.name: getUnlockAccess
method.comments: Gets a call that unlocks any mutex of this type.

predicate.name: mustlockAccess
predicate.comments:
Holds if `fc` is a call that always locks mutex `arg`
of this type.

predicate.name: trylockAccess
predicate.comments:
Holds if `fc` is a call that tries to lock mutex `arg`
of this type, but may return without success.

predicate.name: unlockAccess
predicate.comments: Holds if `fc` is a call that unlocks mutex `arg` of this type.

predicate.name: lockAccess
predicate.comments:
Holds if `fc` is n call that locks or tries to lock mutex
`arg` of this type.

class.comments:
A class or struct type that has both a lock and an unlock function
candidate, and is therefore a mutex.

This excludes types like `std::weak_ptr` which has a lock
method, but not an unlock method, and is not a mutex.)
An expression that is used to qualify some other expression.

predicate.name: Qualifier
predicate.body:
exists(VariableAccess a | a.getQualifier() = this) or
    exists(Call c | c.getQualifier() = this) or
    exists(VacuousDestructorCall v | v.getQualifier() = this)

class.comments:
An expression that occurs in a void context, i.e. either as the toplevel expression of
an expression statement or on the left hand side of the comma operator.

Expressions that are explicitly cast to void are not considered to be in void context.

A basic block in the C/C++ control-flow graph.

A basic block is a simple sequence of control-flow nodes,
connected to each other and nothing else:

```
A - B - C - D  ABCD is a basic block
```

Any incoming or outgoing edges break the block into two:

```
A - B > C - D  AB is a basic block and CD is a basic block (C has two incoming edges)


A - B < C - D  AB is a basic block and CD is a basic block (B has two outgoing edges)
```

method.name: getNode
method.comments: Gets the `ControlFlowNode` at position `pos` in this basic block.

method.name: getANode
method.comments: Gets a `ControlFlowNode` in this basic block.

method.name: getASuccessor
method.comments: Gets a `BasicBlock` that is a direct successor of this basic block.

method.name: getAPredecessor
method.comments: Gets a `BasicBlock` that is a direct predecessor of this basic block.

method.name: getATrueSuccessor
method.comments:
Gets a `BasicBlock` such that the control-flow edge `(this, result)` may be taken
when the outgoing edge of this basic block is an expression that is true.

method.name: getAFalseSuccessor
method.comments:
Gets a `BasicBlock` such that the control-flow edge `(this, result)` may be taken
when the outgoing edge of this basic block is an expression that is false.

method.name: getEnd
method.comments: Gets the final `ControlFlowNode` of this basic block.

method.name: getStart
method.comments: Gets the first `ControlFlowNode` of this basic block.

method.name: length
method.comments: Gets the number of `ControlFlowNode`s in this basic block.

method.name: getEnclosingFunction
method.comments: Gets the function containing this basic block.

predicate.name: contains
predicate.comments: Holds if this basic block contains `node`.

predicate.name: hasLocationInfo
predicate.body: this.hasLocationInfoInternal(filepath, startline, startcolumn, filepath, endline, endcolumn)
predicate.comments:
Holds if this element is at the specified location.
The location spans column `startcolumn` of line `startline` to
column `endcolumn` of line `endline` in file `filepath`.
For more information, see
[Locations](https://codeql.github.com/docs/writing-codeql-queries/providing-locations-in-codeql-queries/).

Yields no result if this basic block spans multiple source files.

predicate.name: inLoop
predicate.comments:
Holds if this basic block is in a loop of the control-flow graph. This
includes loops created by `goto` statements. This predicate may not hold
even if this basic block is syntactically inside a `while` loop if the
necessary back edges are unreachable.

predicate.name: isReachable
predicate.body:
exists(Function f | f.getBlock() = this)
    or
    exists(TryStmt t, BasicBlock tryblock |
      // a `Handler` precedes the `CatchBlock`, and is always the beginning
      // of a new `BasicBlock` (see `primitive_basic_block_entry_node`).
      this.(Handler).getTryStmt() = t and
      tryblock.isReachable() and
      tryblock.contains(t)
    )
    or
    exists(BasicBlock pred | pred.getASuccessor() = this and pred.isReachable())
predicate.comments:
Holds if control flow may reach this basic block from a function entry
point or any handler of a reachable `try` statement.

predicate.name: isUnreachable
predicate.comments: Means `not isReachable()`.

class.comments: An entry point of a function.
A basic block whose last node is the exit point of a function.

A control-flow node is either a statement or an expression; in addition,
functions are control-flow nodes representing the exit point of the
function. The graph represents one possible evaluation order out of all the
ones the compiler might have picked.

Control-flow nodes have successors and predecessors at the expression level,
so control flow is accurately represented in expressions as well as between
statements. Statements and initializers precede their contained expressions,
and expressions deeper in the tree precede those higher up; for example, the
statement `x = y + 1` gets a control-flow graph that looks like

```
ExprStmt -> y -> 1 -> (+) -> x -> (=)
```

The first control-flow node in a function is the body of the function (a
block), and the last is the function itself, which is used to represent the
exit point.

Each `throw` expression or `Handler` has a path (along any necessary
destructor calls) to its nearest enclosing `Handler` within the same
function, or to the exit point of the function if there is no such
`Handler`. There are no edges from function calls to `Handler`s.

method.name: getASuccessor
method.comments: Gets a direct successor of this control-flow node, if any.

method.name: getAPredecessor
method.comments: Gets a direct predecessor of this control-flow node, if any.

method.name: getControlFlowScope
method.comments: Gets the function containing this control-flow node.

method.name: getEnclosingStmt
method.comments: Gets the smallest statement containing this control-flow node.

method.name: getATrueSuccessor
method.comments:
Gets a node such that the control-flow edge `(this, result)` may be
taken when this expression is true.

method.name: getAFalseSuccessor
method.comments:
Gets a node such that the control-flow edge `(this, result)` may be
taken when this expression is false.

method.name: getBasicBlock
method.comments: Gets the `BasicBlock` containing this control-flow node.

predicate.name: isCondition
predicate.comments:
Holds if this node is the top-level expression of a conditional statement,
meaning that `this.getATrueSuccessor()` or `this.getAFalseSuccessor()`
will have a result.

class.comments:
An element that is convertible to `ControlFlowNode`. This class is similar
to `ControlFlowNode` except that is has no member predicates apart from
`toString`.

This class can be used as base class for classes that want to inherit the
extent of `ControlFlowNode` without inheriting its public member predicates.

An abstract class that can be extended to add additional edges to the
control-flow graph. Instances of this class correspond to the source nodes
of such edges, and the predicate `getAnEdgeTarget` should be overridden to
produce the target nodes of each source.

Changing the control-flow graph in some queries and not others can be
expensive in execution time and disk space. Most cached predicates in the
library depend on the control-flow graph, so these predicates will be
computed and cached for each variation of the control-flow graph
that is used.

Edges added by this class will still be removed by the library if they
appear to be unreachable. See the documentation on `ControlFlowNode` for
more information about the control-flow graph.

method.name: getAnEdgeTarget
method.comments: Gets a target node of this edge, where the source node is `this`.

class.comments:
INTERNAL: Do not use.
A string that identifies a data flow analysis along with a set of member
predicates that implement this analysis.

predicate.name: isDefault
predicate.comments: Holds if this annotation is the default annotation.

predicate.name: generatedOn
predicate.comments: Holds if this annotation is generated when analyzing expression `e`.

predicate.name: generatedBy
predicate.comments:
Holds if this annotation is generated for the variable `v` when
the control-flow edge `(src, dest)` is taken.

predicate.name: killedBy
predicate.comments:
Holds if this annotation is removed for the variable `v` when
the control-flow edge `(src, dest)` is taken.

predicate.name: marks
predicate.body:
this.generatedOn(e) and reachable(e)
    or
    this.marks(e.(AssignExpr).getRValue())
    or
    exists(LocalScopeVariable v | this.marks(v, e) and e = v.getAnAccess())
predicate.comments: Holds if expression `e` is given this annotation.

predicate.name: marks
predicate.body:
v.getAnAccess().getEnclosingFunction().getBlock() = n and
    this.isDefault()
    or
    this.marks(n.(Initializer).getExpr()) and
    v.getInitializer() = n
    or
    exists(ControlFlowNode pred |
      this.generatedBy(v, pred, n) and
      not this.killedBy(v, pred, n) and
      reachable(pred)
    )
    or
    exists(ControlFlowNode pred |
      this.assignedBy(v, pred, n) and
      not this.killedBy(v, pred, n) and
      reachable(pred)
    )
    or
    exists(ControlFlowNode pred |
      this.preservedBy(v, pred, n) and
      not this.killedBy(v, pred, n) and
      reachable(pred)
    )
predicate.comments: Holds if the variable `v` accessed in control-flow node `n` is given this annotation.

predicate.name: preservedBy
predicate.body:
this.marks(v, src) and
    src.getASuccessor() = dest and
    not v.getInitializer() = dest and
    not v.getAnAssignment() = src
predicate.comments:
Holds if the variable `v` preserves this annotation when the control-flow
edge `(src, dest)` is taken.

predicate.name: assignedBy
predicate.body:
this.marks(src.(AssignExpr).getRValue()) and
    src = v.getAnAssignment() and
    src.getASuccessor() = dest
predicate.comments:
Holds if the variable `v` is assigned this annotation when `src` is an assignment
expression that assigns to `v` and the control-flow edge `(src, dest)` is taken.

class.comments:
INTERNAL: Do not use.
Two analyses relating to nullness: `"pointer-null"` and `"pointer-valid"`.
These analyses mark expressions that are possibly null or possibly non-null,
respectively.

predicate.name: generatedOn
predicate.body:
exists(Variable v |
      v.getAnAccess() = e and
      (v instanceof GlobalVariable or v instanceof Field) and
      this.isDefault()
    )
    or
    e instanceof Call and this = "pointer-valid"
    or
    nullValue(e) and this = "pointer-null"

predicate.name: killedBy
predicate.body:
src.(AnalysedExpr).getNullSuccessor(v) = dest and this = "pointer-valid"
    or
    src.(AnalysedExpr).getNonNullSuccessor(v) = dest and this = "pointer-null"
    or
    dest = src.getASuccessor() and callByReference(src, v) and not this.isDefault()
    or
    dest = src.getASuccessor() and deref(v, src) and this = "pointer-null"

predicate.name: generatedBy
predicate.body:
dest = src.getASuccessor() and
    callByReference(src, v) and
    this.isDefault()

class.comments: Utility class: A definition or use of a stack variable.

method.name: getVariable
method.comments:
Gets the target variable of this definition or use.

The `isDef` parameter is needed in order to ensure disjointness
of definitions and uses; in a variable initialization such as
`int x = y`, `y` is both a definition of `x`, as well as a use of
`y`, and `isDef` is used to distinguish the two situations.

predicate.name: reaches
predicate.body:
/*
     * Implementation detail: this predicate and its private auxiliary
     * predicates are instances of the more general predicates in
     * StackVariableReachability.qll, and should be kept in sync.
     *
     * Unfortunately, caching of abstract predicates does not work well, so the
     * predicates are duplicated for now.
     */

    exists(BasicBlock bb, int i | this.reaches_helper(isDef, v, bb, i) |
      exists(int j |
        j > i and
        (bbDefAt(bb, j, v, defOrUse) or bbUseAt(bb, j, v, defOrUse)) and
        not exists(int k | this.firstBarrierAfterThis(isDef, k, v) and k < j)
      )
      or
      not this.firstBarrierAfterThis(isDef, _, v) and
      bbSuccessorEntryReachesDefOrUse(bb, v, defOrUse, _)
    )
predicate.comments:
Holds if the value of `v` in this control-flow node reaches
`defOrUse` along some control-flow path without crossing a
definition of `v`.

class.comments: A definition of a stack variable.
A definition of a parameter.

method.name: getVariable
method.body:
exists(Function f | parameterIsOverwritten(f, result) | this = f.getEntryPoint()) and
    isDef = true

class.comments: A use of a stack variable.
An `SsaDefinition` with an additional predicate `isLt`.

predicate.name: isLt
predicate.comments: Holds if this `SsaDefinition` is guarded such that `this(var) < gt + k` is `testIsTrue` in `block`.

class.comments:
DEPRECATED: Use `GuardValue` instead.

An abstract value. This is either a boolean value, or a `switch` case.

DEPRECATED: Use `GuardValue` instead.

A Boolean value.

method.name: getValue
method.comments: Gets the underlying Boolean value.

class.comments:
DEPRECATED: Use `GuardValue` instead.

A value that represents a match against a specific `switch` case.

method.name: getCase
method.comments: Gets the case.

class.comments:
A Boolean condition in the AST that guards one or more basic blocks. This includes
operands of logical operators but not switch statements.

predicate.name: valueControls
predicate.comments:
Holds if this condition controls `controlled`, meaning that `controlled` is only
entered if the value of this condition is `v`.

For details on what "controls" mean, see the QLDoc for `controls`.

predicate.name: controls
predicate.comments:
Holds if this condition controls `controlled`, meaning that `controlled` is only
entered if the value of this condition is `testIsTrue`.

Illustration:

```
[                    (testIsTrue)                        ]
[             this ----------------succ ---- controlled  ]
[               |                    |                   ]
[ (testIsFalse) |                     ------ ...         ]
[             other                                      ]
```

The predicate holds if all paths to `controlled` go via the `testIsTrue`
edge of the control-flow graph. In other words, the `testIsTrue` edge
must dominate `controlled`. This means that `controlled` must be
dominated by both `this` and `succ` (the target of the `testIsTrue`
edge). It also means that any other edge into `succ` must be a back-edge
from a node which is dominated by `succ`.

The short-circuit boolean operations have slightly surprising behavior
here: because the operation itself only dominates one branch (due to
being short-circuited) then it will only control blocks dominated by the
true (for `&&`) or false (for `||`) branch.

predicate.name: valueControlsEdge
predicate.comments:
Holds if the control-flow edge `(pred, succ)` may be taken only if
the value of this condition is `v`.

predicate.name: controlsEdge
predicate.body: this.valueControlsEdge(pred, succ, any(GuardValue bv | bv.asBooleanValue() = testIsTrue))
predicate.comments:
Holds if  the control-flow edge `(pred, succ)` may be taken only if
this the value of this condition is `testIsTrue`.

predicate.name: comparesLt
predicate.comments:
Holds if (determined by this guard) `left < right + k` evaluates to `isLessThan` if this
expression evaluates to `testIsTrue`. Note that there's a 4-argument
("unary") and a 5-argument ("binary") version of this predicate (see `comparesEq`).

predicate.name: ensuresLt
predicate.comments:
Holds if (determined by this guard) `left < right + k` must be `isLessThan` in `block`.
If `isLessThan = false` then this implies `left >= right + k`. Note that there's a 4-argument
("unary") and a 5-argument ("binary") version of this predicate (see `comparesEq`).

predicate.name: comparesLt
predicate.comments:
Holds if (determined by this guard) `e < k` evaluates to `isLessThan` if
this expression evaluates to `value`. Note that there's a 4-argument
("unary") and a 5-argument ("binary") version of this predicate (see `comparesEq`).

predicate.name: ensuresLt
predicate.comments:
Holds if (determined by this guard) `e < k` must be `isLessThan` in `block`.
If `isLessThan = false` then this implies `e >= k`. Note that there's a 4-argument
("unary") and a 5-argument ("binary") version of this predicate (see `comparesEq`).

predicate.name: comparesEq
predicate.comments:
Holds if (determined by this guard) `left == right + k` evaluates to `areEqual` if this
expression evaluates to `testIsTrue`. Note that there's a 4-argument ("unary") and a
5-argument ("binary") version of `comparesEq` and they are not equivalent:
- the unary version is suitable for guards where there is no expression representing the
right-hand side, such as `if (x)`, and also works for equality with an integer constant
(such as `if (x == k)`).
- the binary version is the more general case for comparison of any expressions (not
necessarily integer).

predicate.name: ensuresEq
predicate.comments:
Holds if (determined by this guard) `left == right + k` must be `areEqual` in `block`.
If `areEqual = false` then this implies `left != right + k`. Note that there's a 4-argument
("unary") and a 5-argument ("binary") version of this predicate (see `comparesEq`).

predicate.name: comparesEq
predicate.comments:
Holds if (determined by this guard) `e == k` evaluates to `areEqual` if this expression
evaluates to `value`. Note that there's a 4-argument ("unary") and a 5-argument ("binary")
version of `comparesEq` and they are not equivalent:
- the unary version is suitable for guards where there is no expression representing the
right-hand side, such as `if (x)`, and also works for equality with an integer constant
(such as `if (x == k)`).
- the binary version is the more general case for comparison of any expressions (not
necessarily integer).

predicate.name: ensuresEq
predicate.comments:
Holds if (determined by this guard) `e == k` must be `areEqual` in `block`.
If `areEqual = false` then this implies `e != k`. Note that there's a 4-argument
("unary") and a 5-argument ("binary") version of this predicate (see `comparesEq`).

predicate.name: ensuresEqEdge
predicate.body:
exists(boolean testIsTrue |
      this.comparesEq(left, right, k, areEqual, testIsTrue) and
      this.controlsEdge(pred, succ, testIsTrue)
    )
predicate.comments:
Holds if (determined by this guard) `left == right + k` must be `areEqual` on the edge from
`pred` to `succ`. If `areEqual = false` then this implies `left != right + k`.

predicate.name: ensuresEqEdge
predicate.body:
exists(GuardValue v |
      this.comparesEq(e, k, areEqual, v) and
      this.valueControlsEdge(pred, succ, v)
    )
predicate.comments:
Holds if (determined by this guard) `e == k` must be `areEqual` on the edge from
`pred` to `succ`. If `areEqual = false` then this implies `e != k`.

predicate.name: ensuresLtEdge
predicate.body:
exists(boolean testIsTrue |
      this.comparesLt(left, right, k, isLessThan, testIsTrue) and
      this.controlsEdge(pred, succ, testIsTrue)
    )
predicate.comments:
Holds if (determined by this guard) `left < right + k` must be `isLessThan` on the edge from
`pred` to `succ`. If `isLessThan = false` then this implies `left >= right + k`.

predicate.name: ensuresLtEdge
predicate.body:
exists(GuardValue v |
      this.comparesLt(e, k, isLessThan, v) and
      this.valueControlsEdge(pred, succ, v)
    )
predicate.comments:
Holds if (determined by this guard) `e < k` must be `isLessThan` on the edge from
`pred` to `succ`. If `isLessThan = false` then this implies `e >= k`.

class.comments: A binary logical operator in the AST that guards one or more basic blocks.

predicate.name: valueControls
predicate.body:
exists(Cpp::BinaryLogicalOperation binop, GuardCondition lhs, GuardCondition rhs |
      this = binop and
      lhs = binop.getLeftOperand() and
      rhs = binop.getRightOperand() and
      lhs.valueControls(controlled, v) and
      rhs.valueControls(controlled, v)
    )

predicate.name: valueControlsEdge
predicate.body:
exists(Cpp::BinaryLogicalOperation binop, GuardCondition lhs, GuardCondition rhs |
      this = binop and
      lhs = binop.getLeftOperand() and
      rhs = binop.getRightOperand() and
      lhs.valueControlsEdge(pred, succ, v) and
      rhs.valueControlsEdge(pred, succ, v)
    )

predicate.name: comparesLt
predicate.body:
exists(boolean partIsTrue, GuardCondition part |
      this.(Cpp::BinaryLogicalOperation).impliesValue(part, partIsTrue, testIsTrue)
    |
      part.comparesLt(left, right, k, isLessThan, partIsTrue)
    )

predicate.name: comparesLt
predicate.body:
exists(GuardValue partValue, GuardCondition part |
      this.(Cpp::BinaryLogicalOperation)
          .impliesValue(part, partValue.asBooleanValue(), value.asBooleanValue())
    |
      part.comparesLt(e, k, isLessThan, partValue)
    )

predicate.name: ensuresLt
ensuresLt
comparesEq
predicate.body:
exists(boolean partIsTrue, GuardCondition part |
      this.(Cpp::BinaryLogicalOperation).impliesValue(part, partIsTrue, testIsTrue)
    |
      part.comparesEq(left, right, k, areEqual, partIsTrue)
    )

predicate.name: ensuresEq
comparesEq
predicate.body:
exists(GuardValue partValue, GuardCondition part |
      this.(Cpp::BinaryLogicalOperation)
          .impliesValue(part, partValue.asBooleanValue(), value.asBooleanValue())
    |
      part.comparesEq(e, k, areEqual, partValue)
    )

predicate.name: ensuresEq
GuardConditionFromNotExpr
predicate.body:
exists(Cpp::NotExpr notExpr | this = notExpr.getOperand() |
      ir.getUnconvertedResultExpression() = notExpr
      or
      ir.(ConditionalBranchInstruction).getCondition().getUnconvertedResultExpression() = notExpr
    )

predicate.name: valueControls
comparesLt
predicate.body:
exists(Instruction li, Instruction ri |
      li.getUnconvertedResultExpression() = left and
      ri.getUnconvertedResultExpression() = right and
      ir.comparesLt(li.getAUse(), ri.getAUse(), k, isLessThan, testIsTrue.booleanNot())
    )

predicate.name: comparesLt
predicate.body:
exists(Instruction i |
      i.getUnconvertedResultExpression() = e and
      ir.comparesLt(i.getAUse(), k, isLessThan, value.getDualValue())
    )

predicate.name: ensuresLt
predicate.body:
exists(Instruction li, Instruction ri, boolean testIsTrue |
      li.getUnconvertedResultExpression() = left and
      ri.getUnconvertedResultExpression() = right and
      ir.comparesLt(li.getAUse(), ri.getAUse(), k, isLessThan, testIsTrue.booleanNot()) and
      this.controls(block, testIsTrue)
    )

predicate.name: ensuresLt
predicate.body:
exists(Instruction i, GuardValue value |
      i.getUnconvertedResultExpression() = e and
      ir.comparesLt(i.getAUse(), k, isLessThan, value.getDualValue()) and
      this.valueControls(block, value)
    )

predicate.name: comparesEq
predicate.body:
exists(Instruction li, Instruction ri |
      li.getUnconvertedResultExpression() = left and
      ri.getUnconvertedResultExpression() = right and
      ir.comparesEq(li.getAUse(), ri.getAUse(), k, areEqual, testIsTrue.booleanNot())
    )

predicate.name: ensuresEq
predicate.body:
exists(Instruction li, Instruction ri, boolean testIsTrue |
      li.getUnconvertedResultExpression() = left and
      ri.getUnconvertedResultExpression() = right and
      ir.comparesEq(li.getAUse(), ri.getAUse(), k, areEqual, testIsTrue.booleanNot()) and
      this.controls(block, testIsTrue)
    )

predicate.name: comparesEq
predicate.body:
exists(Instruction i |
      i.getUnconvertedResultExpression() = e and
      ir.comparesEq(i.getAUse(), k, areEqual, value.getDualValue())
    )

predicate.name: ensuresEq
predicate.body:
exists(Instruction i, GuardValue value |
      i.getUnconvertedResultExpression() = e and
      ir.comparesEq(i.getAUse(), k, areEqual, value.getDualValue()) and
      this.valueControls(block, value)
    )

class.comments:
A Boolean condition in the AST that guards one or more basic blocks and has a corresponding IR
instruction.

predicate.name: GuardConditionFromIR
predicate.body:
ir.(InitializeParameterInstruction).getParameter() = this
    or
    ir.(ConditionalBranchInstruction).getCondition().getUnconvertedResultExpression() = this
    or
    ir.getUnconvertedResultExpression() = this

predicate.name: valueControls
comparesLt
predicate.body:
exists(Instruction li, Instruction ri |
      li.getUnconvertedResultExpression() = left and
      ri.getUnconvertedResultExpression() = right and
      ir.comparesLt(li.getAUse(), ri.getAUse(), k, isLessThan, testIsTrue)
    )

predicate.name: comparesLt
predicate.body:
exists(Instruction i |
      i.getUnconvertedResultExpression() = e and
      ir.comparesLt(i.getAUse(), k, isLessThan, value)
    )

predicate.name: ensuresLt
predicate.body:
exists(Instruction li, Instruction ri, boolean testIsTrue |
      li.getUnconvertedResultExpression() = left and
      ri.getUnconvertedResultExpression() = right and
      ir.comparesLt(li.getAUse(), ri.getAUse(), k, isLessThan, testIsTrue) and
      this.controls(block, testIsTrue)
    )

predicate.name: ensuresLt
predicate.body:
exists(Instruction i, GuardValue value |
      i.getUnconvertedResultExpression() = e and
      ir.comparesLt(i.getAUse(), k, isLessThan, value) and
      this.valueControls(block, value)
    )

predicate.name: comparesEq
predicate.body:
exists(Instruction li, Instruction ri |
      li.getUnconvertedResultExpression() = left and
      ri.getUnconvertedResultExpression() = right and
      ir.comparesEq(li.getAUse(), ri.getAUse(), k, areEqual, testIsTrue)
    )

predicate.name: ensuresEq
predicate.body:
exists(Instruction li, Instruction ri, boolean testIsTrue |
      li.getUnconvertedResultExpression() = left and
      ri.getUnconvertedResultExpression() = right and
      ir.comparesEq(li.getAUse(), ri.getAUse(), k, areEqual, testIsTrue) and
      this.controls(block, testIsTrue)
    )

predicate.name: comparesEq
predicate.body:
exists(Instruction i |
      i.getUnconvertedResultExpression() = e and
      ir.comparesEq(i.getAUse(), k, areEqual, value)
    )

predicate.name: ensuresEq
predicate.body:
exists(Instruction i, GuardValue value |
      i.getUnconvertedResultExpression() = e and
      ir.comparesEq(i.getAUse(), k, areEqual, value) and
      this.valueControls(block, value)
    )

class.comments:
A guard. This may be any expression whose value determines subsequent
control flow. It may also be a switch case, which as a guard is considered
to evaluate to either true or false depending on whether the case matches.

predicate.name: comparesLt
predicate.body:
exists(GuardValue value |
      compares_lt(valueNumber(this), left, right, k, isLessThan, value) and
      value.asBooleanValue() = testIsTrue
    )
predicate.comments: Holds if (determined by this guard) `left < right + k` evaluates to `isLessThan` if this expression evaluates to `testIsTrue`.

predicate.name: comparesLt
predicate.comments:
Holds if (determined by this guard) `op < k` evaluates to `isLessThan` if
this expression evaluates to `value`.

predicate.name: ensuresLt
predicate.body:
exists(GuardValue value |
      compares_lt(valueNumber(this), left, right, k, isLessThan, value) and
      this.valueControls(block, value)
    )
predicate.comments:
Holds if (determined by this guard) `left < right + k` must be `isLessThan` in `block`.
If `isLessThan = false` then this implies `left >= right + k`.

predicate.name: ensuresLt
predicate.body:
exists(GuardValue value |
      unary_compares_lt(valueNumber(this), op, k, isLessThan, value) and
      this.valueControls(block, value)
    )
predicate.comments:
Holds if (determined by this guard) `op < k` must be `isLessThan` in `block`.
If `isLessThan = false` then this implies `op >= k`.

predicate.name: ensuresLtEdge
predicate.body:
exists(GuardValue value |
      compares_lt(valueNumber(this), left, right, k, isLessThan, value) and
      this.valueControlsBranchEdge(pred, succ, value)
    )
predicate.comments:
Holds if (determined by this guard) `left < right + k` must be `isLessThan` on the edge from
`pred` to `succ`. If `isLessThan = false` then this implies `left >= right + k`.

predicate.name: ensuresLtEdge
predicate.body:
exists(GuardValue value |
      unary_compares_lt(valueNumber(this), left, k, isLessThan, value) and
      this.valueControlsBranchEdge(pred, succ, value)
    )
predicate.comments:
Holds if (determined by this guard) `op < k` must be `isLessThan` on the edge from
`pred` to `succ`. If `isLessThan = false` then this implies `op >= k`.

predicate.name: comparesEq
predicate.body:
exists(GuardValue value |
      compares_eq(valueNumber(this), left, right, k, areEqual, value) and
      value.asBooleanValue() = testIsTrue
    )
predicate.comments: Holds if (determined by this guard) `left == right + k` evaluates to `areEqual` if this expression evaluates to `testIsTrue`.

predicate.name: comparesEq
predicate.comments: Holds if (determined by this guard) `op == k` evaluates to `areEqual` if this expression evaluates to `value`.

predicate.name: ensuresEq
predicate.body:
exists(GuardValue value |
      compares_eq(valueNumber(this), left, right, k, areEqual, value) and
      this.valueControls(block, value)
    )
predicate.comments:
Holds if (determined by this guard) `left == right + k` must be `areEqual` in `block`.
If `areEqual = false` then this implies `left != right + k`.

predicate.name: ensuresEq
predicate.body:
exists(GuardValue value |
      unary_compares_eq(valueNumber(this), op, k, areEqual, value) and
      this.valueControls(block, value)
    )
predicate.comments:
Holds if (determined by this guard) `op == k` must be `areEqual` in `block`.
If `areEqual = false` then this implies `op != k`.

predicate.name: ensuresEqEdge
predicate.body:
exists(GuardValue value |
      compares_eq(valueNumber(this), left, right, k, areEqual, value) and
      this.valueControlsBranchEdge(pred, succ, value)
    )
predicate.comments:
Holds if (determined by this guard) `left == right + k` must be `areEqual` on the edge from
`pred` to `succ`. If `areEqual = false` then this implies `left != right + k`.

predicate.name: ensuresEqEdge
predicate.body:
exists(GuardValue value |
      unary_compares_eq(valueNumber(this), op, k, areEqual, value) and
      this.valueControlsBranchEdge(pred, succ, value)
    )
predicate.comments:
Holds if (determined by this guard) `op == k` must be `areEqual` on the edge from
`pred` to `succ`. If `areEqual = false` then this implies `op != k`.

predicate.name: controlsEdge
predicate.comments: DEPRECATED: Use `controlsBranchEdge` instead.

class.comments: A C/C++ literal whose value is considered null.
A C/C++ literal whose value is zero.

An expression that has been extended with member predicates that provide
information about the role of this expression in nullness checks.

method.name: getNullSuccessor
method.body:
this.isNullCheck(v) and result = this.getATrueSuccessor()
    or
    this.isValidCheck(v) and result = this.getAFalseSuccessor()
method.comments:
Gets a successor of `this` in the control flow graph, where that successor
is among the nodes to which control may flow when `this` tests `v` to be
_null_.

method.name: getNonNullSuccessor
method.body:
this.isNullCheck(v) and result = this.getAFalseSuccessor()
    or
    this.isValidCheck(v) and result = this.getATrueSuccessor()
method.comments:
Gets a successor of `this` in the control flow graph, where that successor
is among the nodes to which control may flow when `this` tests `v` to be
_not null_.

predicate.name: isNullCheck
predicate.comments: Holds if `v` is null when this expression evaluates to a true value.

predicate.name: isValidCheck
predicate.comments: Holds if `v` is non-null when this expression evaluates to a true value.

predicate.name: isUse
predicate.comments: Holds if this is a `VariableAccess` of `v` nested inside a condition.

predicate.name: isDef
predicate.body:
this.inCondition() and
    (
      this.(Assignment).getLValue() = v.getAnAccess() or
      this.(ConditionDeclExpr).getVariableAccess() = v.getAnAccess()
    )
predicate.comments: Holds if this is an `Assignment` to `v` nested inside a condition.

predicate.name: inCondition
predicate.comments:
Holds if `this` occurs at or below the controlling expression of an `if`,
`while`, `?:`, or similar.

class.comments:
The SSA logic comes in two versions: the standard SSA and range-analysis RangeSSA.
This class provides the standard SSA logic.

NOTE: If possible, prefer the SSA classes exposed by the new dataflow
library:
```
import semmle.code.cpp.dataflow.new.DataFlow
// use `DataFlow::Ssa::Definition`
```

A definition of one or more SSA variables, including phi node definitions.
An _SSA variable_, as defined in the literature, is effectively the pair of
an `SsaDefinition d` and a `StackVariable v`, written `(d, v)` in this
documentation. Note that definitions and uses can be coincident due to the
presence of parameter definitions and phi nodes.

Not all `StackVariable`s of a function have SSA definitions. If the variable
has its address taken, either explicitly or implicitly, then it is excluded
from analysis. `SsaDefinition`s are not generated in locations that are
statically seen to be unreachable.

method.name: getAVariable
method.comments:
Gets a variable corresponding to an SSA StackVariable defined by
this definition.

method.name: toString
method.comments:
Gets a string representation of the SSA variable represented by the pair
`(this, v)`.

method.name: getAUse
method.comments: Gets a use of the SSA variable represented by the pair `(this, v)`.

method.name: getDefinition
method.comments:
Gets the control-flow node for this definition. This will usually be the
control-flow node that assigns to this variable as a side effect, but
there are some exceptions. If `this` is defined by initialization, the
result is the value of `Initializer.getExpr()` for that initialization.
If `this` is a function parameter (see `definedByParameter`), the result
will be the function entry point. If `this` variable is defined by being
passed as a reference in a function call, including overloaded
operators, the result will be the `VariableAccess` expression for this
parameter. If `this` is a phi node (see `isPhiNode`), the result will be
the node where control flow is joined from multiple paths.

method.name: getBasicBlock
method.comments: Gets the `BasicBlock` containing this definition.

method.name: getLocation
method.comments: Gets the location of this definition.

method.name: getAPhiInput
method.comments:
Holds if the SSA variable `(result, v)` is an input to the phi definition
`(this, v)`.

method.name: getDefiningValue
method.body:
exists(ControlFlowNode def | def = this.getDefinition() |
      def = v.getInitializer().getExpr() and def = result
      or
      exists(AssignExpr assign |
        def = assign and
        assign.getLValue() = v.getAnAccess() and
        result = assign.getRValue()
      )
      or
      exists(AssignOperation assign |
        def = assign and
        assign.getLValue() = v.getAnAccess() and
        result = assign
      )
      or
      exists(CrementOperation crement |
        def = crement and
        crement.getOperand() = v.getAnAccess() and
        result = crement
      )
    )
method.comments:
Gets the expression assigned to the SSA variable `(this, v)`, if any,
when it is not a phi definition. The following is an exhaustive list of
expressions that may be the result of this predicate.

- The contained expression of an `Initializer`.
- The right-hand side of an `AssignExpr`.
- An `AssignOperation`.
- A `CrementOperation`.

In all cases except `PostfixCrementOperation`, the variable `v` will be
equal to the result of this predicate after evaluation of
`this.getDefinition()`.

If the SSA variable is defined in other ways than those four (such as
function parameters or `f(&x)`) there is no result. These cases are
instead covered via `definedByParameter` and `getDefinition`,
respectively.

method.name: getAnUltimateSsaDefinition
method.body:
result = this.getAPhiInput(v).getAnUltimateSsaDefinition(v)
    or
    v = this.getAVariable() and
    not this.isPhiNode(v) and
    result = this
method.comments:
Gets a definition that ultimately defines this variable and is not
itself a phi node.

method.name: getAnUltimateDefiningValue
method.comments:
Gets a possible defining expression for `v` at this SSA definition,
recursing backwards through phi definitions. Not all definitions have a
defining expression---see the documentation for `getDefiningValue`.

predicate.name: isPhiNode
predicate.comments: Holds if this definition is a phi node for variable `v`.

predicate.name: definedByParameter
predicate.comments: Holds if the SSA variable `(this, p)` is defined by parameter `p`.

predicate.name: reachesEndOfBB
predicate.comments: Holds if `(this, v)` reaches the end of basic block `b`.

class.comments: Common SSA logic for standard SSA and range-analysis SSA.

method.name: toString
method.comments:
Gets a string representation of the SSA variable represented by the pair
`(node, v)`.

method.name: getAUse
method.body:
this.ssaDefinitionReaches(v, def, result) and
    ssa_use(v, result, _, _)
method.comments:
Holds if SSA variable `(v, def)` reaches `result`, where `result` is an
access of `v`.

predicate.name: custom_phi_node
predicate.comments:
Override to insert a custom phi node for variable `v` at the start of
basic block `b`.

predicate.name: phi_node
predicate.comments:
Holds if there is a phi node for variable `v` at the start of basic block
`b`.

predicate.name: ssa_defn
predicate.body:
this.phi_node(v, b) and b.getStart() = node and index = -1
    or
    variableUpdate(v, node, b, index)
predicate.comments:
Holds if `v` is defined, for the purpose of SSA, at `node`, which is at
position `index` in block `b`. This includes definitions from phi nodes.

predicate.name: ssaDefinitionReachesEndOfBB
predicate.body:
live_at_exit_of_bb(v, b) and this.ssaDefReachesRank(v, def, b, this.lastRank(v, b))
    or
    exists(BasicBlock idom |
      this.ssaDefinitionReachesEndOfBB(v, def, idom) and
      this.noDefinitionsSinceIDominator(v, idom, b)
    )
predicate.comments: Holds if SSA variable `(v, def)` reaches the end of block `b`.

predicate.name: isSource
predicate.comments: Holds if `node` is a source for the reachability analysis using variable `v`.

predicate.name: isSink
predicate.comments: Holds if `sink` is a (potential) sink for the reachability analysis using variable `v`.

predicate.name: isBarrier
predicate.comments: Holds if `node` is a barrier for the reachability analysis using variable `v`.

predicate.name: reaches
predicate.body:
/*
     * Implementation detail: the predicates in this class are a generalization of
     * those in DefinitionsAndUses.qll, and should be kept in sync.
     *
     * Unfortunately, caching of abstract predicates does not work well, so the
     * predicates in DefinitionsAndUses.qll cannot use this library.
     */

    exists(BasicBlock bb, int i |
      this.isSource(source, v) and
      bb.getNode(i) = source and
      not bb.isUnreachable()
    |
      exists(int j |
        j > i and
        sink = bb.getNode(j) and
        this.isSink(sink, v) and
        not exists(int k, ControlFlowNode node |
          node = bb.getNode(k) and this.isBarrier(pragma[only_bind_into](node), v)
        |
          k in [i + 1 .. j - 1]
        )
      )
      or
      not exists(int k | this.isBarrier(bb.getNode(k), v) | k > i) and
      this.bbSuccessorEntryReaches(bb, v, sink, _)
    )
predicate.comments:
Holds if the source node `source` can reach the sink `sink` without crossing
a barrier. This is (almost) equivalent to the following QL predicate but
uses basic blocks internally for better performance:

```
predicate reaches(ControlFlowNode source, SemanticStackVariable v, ControlFlowNode sink) {
reachesImpl(source, v, sink)
and
isSink(sink, v)
}

predicate reachesImpl(ControlFlowNode source, SemanticStackVariable v, ControlFlowNode sink) {
sink = source.getASuccessor() and isSource(source, v)
or
exists(ControlFlowNode mid | reachesImpl(source, v, mid) |
not isBarrier(mid, v)
and
sink = mid.getASuccessor()
)
}
```

In addition to using a better performing implementation, this analysis
accounts for loops where the condition is provably true upon entry.

class.comments:
Reachability analysis for control-flow nodes involving stack variables.
Unlike `StackVariableReachability`, this analysis takes variable
reassignments into account.

This class is used like `StackVariableReachability`, except that
subclasses should override `isSourceActual` and `isSinkActual` instead of
`isSource` and `isSink`, and that there is a `reachesTo` predicate in
addition to `reaches`.

predicate.name: isSourceActual
predicate.comments: Override this predicate rather than `isSource` (`isSource` is used internally).

predicate.name: isSinkActual
predicate.comments: Override this predicate rather than `isSink` (`isSink` is used internally).

predicate.name: reaches
predicate.comments:
Holds if the source node `source` can reach the sink `sink` without crossing
a barrier, taking reassignments into account. This is (almost) equivalent
to the following QL predicate, but uses basic blocks internally for better
performance:

```
predicate reaches(ControlFlowNode source, SemanticStackVariable v, ControlFlowNode sink) {
reachesImpl(source, v, sink)
and
isSinkActual(sink, v)
}

predicate reachesImpl(ControlFlowNode source, SemanticStackVariable v, ControlFlowNode sink) {
isSourceActual(source, v)
and
(
sink = source.getASuccessor()
or
exists(ControlFlowNode mid, SemanticStackVariable v0 | reachesImpl(source, v0, mid) |
// ordinary successor
not isBarrier(mid, v) and
sink = mid.getASuccessor() and
v = v0
or
// reassigned from v0 to v
exprDefinition(v, mid, v0.getAnAccess()) and
sink = mid.getASuccessor()
)
)
}
```

In addition to using a better performing implementation, this analysis
accounts for loops where the condition is provably true upon entry.

predicate.name: reachesTo
predicate.body:
exists(ControlFlowNode def |
      this.actualSourceReaches(source, v, def, v0) and
      StackVariableReachability.super.reaches(def, v0, sink) and
      this.isSinkActual(sink, v0)
    )
predicate.comments: As `reaches`, but also specifies the last variable it was reassigned to (`v0`).

predicate.name: isSource
predicate.body:
this.isSourceActual(node, v)
    or
    // Reassignment generates a new (non-actual) source
    this.reassignment(_, _, node, v)

predicate.name: isSink
predicate.body:
this.isSinkActual(node, v)
    or
    // Reassignment generates a new (non-actual) sink
    exprDefinition(_, node, v.getAnAccess())

class.comments:
Same as `StackVariableReachability`, but `isBarrier` works on control-flow
edges rather than nodes and is therefore parameterized by the original
source node as well. Otherwise, this class is used like
`StackVariableReachability`.

predicate.name: isSource
predicate.comments: `node` is a source for the reachability analysis using variable `v`.

predicate.name: isSink
predicate.comments: `sink` is a (potential) sink for the reachability analysis using variable `v`.

predicate.name: isBarrier
predicate.comments: `node` is a barrier for the reachability analysis using variable `v` and starting from `source`.

predicate.name: reaches
predicate.body:
exists(BasicBlock bb, int i |
      this.isSource(source, v) and
      bb.getNode(i) = source and
      not bb.isUnreachable()
    |
      exists(int j |
        j > i and
        sink = bb.getNode(j) and
        this.isSink(sink, v) and
        not exists(int k | this.isBarrier(source, bb.getNode(k), bb.getNode(k + 1), v) |
          k in [i .. j - 1]
        )
      )
      or
      not exists(int k | this.isBarrier(source, bb.getNode(k), bb.getNode(k + 1), v) | k >= i) and
      this.bbSuccessorEntryReaches(source, bb, v, sink, _)
    )
predicate.comments: See `StackVariableReachability.reaches`.

class.comments:
An abstract class that directs where in the control-flow graph a new
`SubBasicBlock` must start. If a `ControlFlowNode` is an instance of this
class, that node is guaranteed to be the first node in a `SubBasicBlock`.
If multiple libraries use the `SubBasicBlock` library, basic blocks may be
split in more places than either library expects, but nothing should break
as a direct result of that.

A block that can be smaller than or equal to a `BasicBlock`. Use this class
when `ControlFlowNode` is too fine-grained and `BasicBlock` too
coarse-grained. Their successor graph is like that of basic blocks, except
that the blocks are split up with an extra edge right before any instance of
the abstract class `SubBasicBlockCutNode`. Users of this library must
therefore extend `SubBasicBlockCutNode` to direct where basic blocks will be
split up.

method.name: getBasicBlock
method.comments: Gets the basic block in which this `SubBasicBlock` is contained.

method.name: getRankInBasicBlock
method.body:
exists(int thisIndexInBB |
      thisIndexInBB = this.getIndexInBasicBlock(bb) and
      thisIndexInBB = rank[result](int i | i = any(SubBasicBlock n).getIndexInBasicBlock(bb))
    )
method.comments:
Gets the (1-based) rank of this `SubBasicBlock` among the other `SubBasicBlock`s in
its containing basic block `bb`, where `bb` is equal to `getBasicBlock()`.

method.name: getASuccessor
method.body:
this.lastInBB() and
    result = this.getBasicBlock().getASuccessor()
    or
    exists(BasicBlock bb | result.getRankInBasicBlock(bb) = this.getRankInBasicBlock(bb) + 1)
method.comments: Gets a successor in the control-flow graph of `SubBasicBlock`s.

method.name: getNode
method.comments:
Gets the `index`th control-flow node in this `SubBasicBlock`. Indexes
start from 0, and the node at index 0 always exists and compares equal
to `this`.

method.name: getANode
method.comments: Gets a control-flow node in this `SubBasicBlock`.

method.name: getAPredecessor
method.comments: Gets a predecessor in the control-flow graph of `SubBasicBlock`s.

method.name: getATrueSuccessor
