method.comments:
Gets a node such that the control-flow edge `(this, result)` may be taken
when the final node of this `SubBasicBlock` is a conditional expression
and evaluates to true.

method.name: getAFalseSuccessor
method.comments:
Gets a node such that the control-flow edge `(this, result)` may be taken
when the final node of this `SubBasicBlock` is a conditional expression
and evaluates to false.

method.name: getNumberOfNodes
method.comments:
Gets the number of control-flow nodes in this `SubBasicBlock`. There is
always at least one.

method.name: getEnd
method.comments: Gets the last control-flow node in this `SubBasicBlock`.

method.name: getStart
method.comments: Gets the first control-flow node in this `SubBasicBlock`.

method.name: getEnclosingFunction
method.comments: Gets the function that contains this `SubBasicBlock`.

predicate.name: SubBasicBlock
predicate.body:
this instanceof BasicBlock
    or
    this instanceof SubBasicBlockCutNode

predicate.name: firstInBB
predicate.comments:
Holds if this `SubBasicBlock` comes first in its basic block. This is the
only condition under which a `SubBasicBlock` may have multiple
predecessors.

predicate.name: lastInBB
predicate.comments:
Holds if this `SubBasicBlock` comes last in its basic block. This is the
only condition under which a `SubBasicBlock` may have multiple successors.

predicate.name: contains
predicate.comments: Holds if `this` contains `node`.

class.comments:
A control-flow node. This class exists to provide a shorter name than
`ControlFlowNodeBase` within this file and to avoid a seemingly cyclic
dependency on the `ControlFlowNode` class, whose implementation relies on
this file.

method.name: getParentNode
method.comments:
Gets the nearest control-flow node that's a parent of this one, never
crossing function boundaries.

class.comments:
A constant that indicates the type of sub-node in a pair of `(Node, Pos)`.
See the comment block at the top of this file.

predicate.name: isBefore
predicate.comments: Holds if this is the position just _before_ the associated `Node`.

predicate.name: isAt
predicate.comments: Holds if `(n, this)` is the sub-node that represents `n` itself.

predicate.name: isAfter
predicate.comments: Holds if this is the position just _after_ the associated `Node`.

predicate.name: isBeforeDestructors
predicate.comments:
Holds if `(n, this)` is the virtual sub-node that comes just _before_ any
implicit destructor calls following `n`. The node `n` will be some node
that may be followed by local variables going out of scope.

predicate.name: isAfterDestructors
predicate.comments:
Holds if `(n, this)` is the virtual sub-node that comes just _after_ any
implicit destructor calls following `n`. The node `n` will be some node
that may be followed by local variables going out of scope.

class.comments:
An initializer that should be a `PostOrderNode` instead of a `PreOrderNode`.
This class only exists for extractor CFG compatibility.

predicate.name: PostOrderInitializer
predicate.body:
exists(RangeBasedForStmt for |
      this.getDeclaration() = for.getVariable()
      or
      this.getDeclaration() = for.getRangeVariable()
      or
      this.getDeclaration() = for.getBeginEndDeclaration().getADeclaration()
    )

class.comments:
Holds if control flow for this node starts with its children according to
the order in `getControlOrderChildSparse`, ends with the node itself, and
does not jump. This is the case for most types of expression.

predicate.name: PostOrderNode
predicate.body:
this instanceof Expr and
    not this instanceof ShortCircuitOperator and
    not this instanceof ThrowExpr and
    not this instanceof Conversion and // not in CFG
    not excludeNode(this) // for performance
    or
    // VlaDeclStmt is a post-order node for extractor CFG compatibility only.
    this instanceof VlaDeclStmt
    or
    this instanceof PostOrderInitializer

class.comments:
Holds if control flow for this node starts with the node itself, is followed
by its children according to the order in `getControlOrderChildSparse`, and
does not jump.

predicate.name: PreOrderNode
predicate.body:
this.(Initializer).getDeclaration() instanceof LocalVariable and
    not this instanceof PostOrderInitializer
    or
    this instanceof DeclStmt
    or
    this instanceof LabelStmt
    or
    this instanceof ExprStmt
    or
    this instanceof EmptyStmt
    or
    this instanceof AsmStmt
    or
    this instanceof VlaDimensionStmt
    or
    this instanceof MicrosoftTryFinallyStmt

class.comments:
A constant that represents two positions: one position for when it's used as
the _source_ of a sub-edge, and another position for when it's used as the
_target_. Values include all the values of `Pos`, which resolve to
themselves as both source and target, as well as two _around_ values and a
_barrier_ value.

method.name: getSourcePos
method.body:
this = [0 .. 4] and
    result = this
    or
    this.isAround() and
    result.isAfter()
    or
    this.isAroundDestructors() and
    result.isAfterDestructors()

method.name: getTargetPos
method.body:
this = [0 .. 4] and
    result = this
    or
    this.isAround() and
    result.isBefore()
    or
    this.isAroundDestructors() and
    result.isBeforeDestructors()

predicate.name: isAround
predicate.comments:
Holds if this spec, when used on a node `n` between `(n1, p1)` and
`(n2, p2)`, should add the following sub-edges.

(n1, p1) ----> before(n)
after(n) ----> (n2, p2)

predicate.name: isAroundDestructors
predicate.comments:
Holds if this spec, when used on a node `n` between `(n1, p1)` and
`(n2, p2)`, should add the following sub-edges.

(n1, p1)            ----> beforeDestructors(n)
afterDestructors(n) ----> (n2, p2)

predicate.name: isBarrier
predicate.comments:
Holds if this node is a _barrier_. A barrier resolves to no positions and
can be inserted between nodes that should have no sub-edges between them.

class.comments:
An expression whose outgoing true/false sub-edges may come from different
sub-nodes.
An expression whose control flow is the same as `&&`.
An expression whose control flow is the same as `||`.

predicate.name: LogicalOrLikeExpr
predicate.body:
this =
      any(LogicalOrExpr e |
        left = e.getLeftOperand() and
        right = e.getRightOperand()
      )
    or
    // GNU extension: the binary `? :` operator
    this =
      any(ConditionalExpr e |
        e.isTwoOperand() and
        left = e.getCondition() and
        right = e.getElse()
      )

class.comments: An expression whose control flow is the same as `b ? x : y`.

predicate.name: ConditionalLikeExpr
predicate.body:
this =
      any(ConditionalExpr e |
        not e.isTwoOperand() and
        condition = e.getCondition() and
        thenExpr = e.getThen() and
        elseExpr = e.getElse()
      )
    or
    this =
      any(BuiltInChooseExpr e |
        condition = e.getChild(0) and
        thenExpr = e.getChild(1) and
        elseExpr = e.getChild(2)
      )

class.comments:
A `Handler` that might fail to match its exception and instead propagate it
further up the AST. This can happen in the last `Handler` of a `TryStmt` if
it's not a catch-all handler.

predicate.name: PropagatingHandler
predicate.body:
exists(this.getParameter()) and
    exists(int i, TryStmt try |
      this = try.getChild(i) and
      i = max(int j | exists(try.getChild(j)))
    )

class.comments: A control-flow node that might pass an exception up in the AST.

method.name: getExceptionTarget
method.body:
exists(Stmt parent | this.reachesParent(parent) |
      result.(Function).getEntryPoint() = parent
      or
      exists(TryStmt try |
        parent = try.getStmt() and
        result = try.getChild(1)
      )
      or
      exists(MicrosoftTryExceptStmt try |
        parent = try.getStmt() and
        result = try.getCondition()
      )
      or
      exists(MicrosoftTryFinallyStmt try |
        parent = try.getStmt() and
        result = try.getFinally()
      )
    )
method.comments:
Gets the target node where this exception source will jump in case it
throws or propagates an exception. The jump will target the "before"
position of this node, not the "at" position. This is because possible
jump targets include the condition of a `MicrosoftTryExceptStmt`, which is
an `Expr`.

predicate.name: ExceptionSource

class.comments:
INTERNAL: Do not use.
An expression that has been found to have an integer value at compile
time.
A helper class for evaluation of expressions.

method.name: getValue
method.comments: Gets the value of (interesting) expression `e`, if any.

predicate.name: interesting
predicate.comments: `e` is an expression for which we want to calculate a value.

predicate.name: ignoreNonAnalyzableVariableDefinition
predicate.comments:
When evaluating a syntactic subexpression of `e`, we may
ignore the non-analyzable variable definition `def` for
variable `v`.

Subclasses may implement this predicate when needed.

predicate.name: ignoreVariableAssignment
predicate.comments:
When evaluating a syntactic subexpression of `e`, we may
ignore the expression `value` assigned to variable `v`.

Subclasses may implement this predicate when needed.

predicate.name: allowVariableWithoutInitializer
predicate.comments:
When evaluating a syntactic subexpression of `e`, we may
consider variable `v` even though it lacks an initializer.
Normally, this is not sound, as uninitialized variables
can contain arbitrary data (in fact, a query
UninitialisedLocal.ql exists for finding that).

Subclasses may implement this predicate when needed.

predicate.name: interestingInternal
predicate.body:
this.interesting(e) and req = e and sub = true
    or
    exists(Expr mid | this.interestingInternal(e, mid, sub) |
      req = mid.(NotExpr).getOperand() or
      req = mid.(BinaryLogicalOperation).getAnOperand() or
      req = mid.(RelationalOperation).getAnOperand() or
      req = mid.(EQExpr).getAnOperand() or
      req = mid.(NEExpr).getAnOperand() or
      req = mid.(AddExpr).getAnOperand() or
      req = mid.(SubExpr).getAnOperand() or
      req = mid.(MulExpr).getAnOperand() or
      req = mid.(RemExpr).getAnOperand() or
      req = mid.(DivExpr).getAnOperand() or
      req = mid.(AssignExpr).getRValue()
    )
    or
    exists(Variable v, boolean sub1 |
      this.interestingVariableAccess(e, _, v, sub1) and
      req = v.getAnAssignedValue() and
      (sub1 = true implies not this.ignoreVariableAssignment(e, v, req)) and
      sub = false
    )
    or
    exists(Function f |
      this.interestingFunction(e, f) and
      returnStmt(f, req) and
      sub = false
    )
predicate.comments:
`req` is an expression for which a value is required to be evaluated in
order to calculate a value for interesting expression `e`. `sub`
indicates whether `req` is a syntactic subexpression of `e`.

This predicate enumerates the nodes top-down; `getValueInternal()`
calculates the values bottom-up.

class.comments: A helper class for evaluation of conditions.
A helper class for evaluation of switch expressions.
A helper class for evaluation of loop entry conditions.

predicate.name: isLoopEntry
predicate.comments: Holds if `cfn` is the entry point of the loop for which `e` is the condition.

predicate.name: isLoopBody
predicate.comments: Holds if `s` is the loop body guarded by the condition `e`.

predicate.name: ignoreNonAnalyzableVariableDefinition
predicate.body:
this.maybeInterestingVariable(e, v) and
    nonAnalyzableVariableDefinition(v, def) and
    this.isLoopBodyDescendant(e, def) and
    not this.reachesLoopEntryFromLoopBody(e, v, def)

predicate.name: ignoreVariableAssignment
predicate.body:
this.maybeInterestingVariable(e, v) and
    value = v.getAnAssignedValue() and
    this.isLoopBodyDescendant(e, value) and
    not this.reachesLoopEntryFromLoopBody(e, v, value)

class.comments: A helper class for evaluation of while-loop entry conditions.

predicate.name: isLoopBody
predicate.body: exists(WhileStmt while | e = while.getCondition() | s = while.getStmt())

class.comments: A helper class for evaluation of for-loop entry conditions.

predicate.name: isLoopBody
predicate.body:
exists(ForStmt for | e = for.getCondition() |
      s = for.getUpdate() or
      s = for.getStmt()
    )

predicate.name: ignoreNonAnalyzableVariableDefinition
ignoreVariableAssignment
allowVariableWithoutInitializer

class.comments:
A primitive basic block in the C/C++ control-flow graph constructed using
the primitive `successors_extended` relation only.

A unit class for adding additional source model rows.

Extend this class to add additional source definitions.

predicate.name: row
predicate.comments: Holds if `row` specifies a source definition.

class.comments:
A unit class for adding additional sink model rows.

Extend this class to add additional sink definitions.

predicate.name: row
predicate.comments: Holds if `row` specifies a sink definition.

class.comments:
A unit class for adding additional summary model rows.

Extend this class to add additional flow summary definitions.

predicate.name: row
predicate.comments: Holds if `row` specifies a summary definition.

predicate.name: propagatesFlow
predicate.body:
exists(string kind |
      this.relevantSummaryElementManual(input, output, kind, model)
      or
      not this.relevantSummaryElementManual(_, _, _, _) and
      this.relevantSummaryElementGenerated(input, output, kind, model)
    |
      if kind = "value" then preservesValue = true else preservesValue = false
    )

class.comments: A parameter position represented by an integer.
An argument position represented by an integer.
An argument to a call.

method.name: getCall
method.comments: Gets the call that has this argument.

method.name: getPosition
method.comments: Gets the position of this argument.

class.comments:
A data flow node that occurs as the argument of a call and is passed as-is
to the callable. Arguments that are wrapped in an implicit varargs array
creation are not included, but the implicitly created array is.
Instance arguments are also included.

method.name: getCall
method.comments: Gets the call in which this node is an argument.

predicate.name: ArgumentNode
predicate.body:
this.asExpr() instanceof Argument or
    this = getInstanceArgument(_)

predicate.name: argumentOf
predicate.body:
exists(Argument arg | this.asExpr() = arg | call = arg.getCall() and pos = arg.getPosition())
    or
    pos = -1 and this = getInstanceArgument(call)
predicate.comments:
Holds if this argument occurs at the given position in the given call.
The instance argument is considered to have index `-1`.

class.comments:
A return kind. A return kind describes how a value can be returned
from a callable. For C++, this is simply a function return.

method.name: toString
method.body:
this instanceof TNormalReturnKind and
    result = "return"
    or
    this instanceof TRefReturnKind and
    result = "ref"
method.comments: Gets a textual representation of this return kind.

class.comments: A data flow node that represents a returned value in the called function.

method.name: getKind
method.comments: Gets the kind of this returned value.

class.comments: A `ReturnNode` that occurs as the result of a `ReturnStmt`.

method.name: getKind
method.comments: Gets the kind of this returned value.

class.comments:
A `ReturnNode` that occurs as a result of a definition of a reference
parameter reaching the end of a function body.

method.name: getKind
method.comments: Gets the kind of this returned value.

class.comments: A data flow node that represents the output of a call at the call site.

method.name: getCall
method.comments: Gets the underlying call.

method.name: getCall
method.comments: Gets the underlying call.

method.name: getCall
method.comments: Gets the underlying call.

class.comments: A node that performs a type cast.
A function call relevant for data flow.

method.name: getArgument
method.comments:
Gets the nth argument for this call.

The range of `n` is from `0` to `getNumberOfArguments() - 1`.

method.name: getNode
method.comments: Gets the data flow node corresponding to this call.

method.name: getEnclosingCallable
method.comments: Gets the enclosing callable of this call.

class.comments: An approximated `Content`.

A node in a data flow graph.

A node can be either an expression, a parameter, or an uninitialized local
variable. Such nodes are created with `DataFlow::exprNode`,
`DataFlow::parameterNode`, and `DataFlow::uninitializedNode` respectively.

method.name: getFunction
method.comments: Gets the function to which this node belongs.

method.name: getEnclosingCallable
method.comments: INTERNAL: Do not use. Alternative name for `getFunction`.

method.name: getType
method.comments: Gets the type of this node.

method.name: asExpr
method.comments:
Gets the expression corresponding to this node, if any. This predicate
only has a result on nodes that represent the value of evaluating the
expression. For data flowing _out of_ an expression, like when an
argument is passed by reference, use `asDefiningArgument` instead of
`asExpr`.

method.name: asParameter
method.comments: Gets the parameter corresponding to this node, if any.

method.name: asDefiningArgument
method.comments:
Gets the argument that defines this `DefinitionByReferenceNode`, if any.
This predicate should be used instead of `asExpr` when referring to the
value of a reference argument _after_ the call has returned. For example,
in `f(&x)`, this predicate will have `&x` as its result for the `Node`
that represents the new value of `x`.

method.name: asPartialDefinition
method.comments:
Gets the expression that is partially defined by this node, if any.

Partial definitions are created for field stores (`x.y = taint();` is a partial
definition of `x`), and for calls that may change the value of an object (so
`x.set(taint())` is a partial definition of `x`, and `transfer(&x, taint())` is
a partial definition of `&x`).

method.name: asUninitialized
method.comments:
Gets the uninitialized local variable corresponding to this node, if
any.

method.name: toString
method.comments: Gets a textual representation of this element.

method.name: getLocation
method.comments: Gets the location of this element.

method.name: getTypeBound
method.comments: Gets an upper bound on the type of this node.

class.comments: An expression, viewed as a node in a data flow graph.

method.name: getExpr
method.comments: Gets the expression corresponding to this node.

predicate.name: isParameterOf
predicate.comments:
Holds if this node is the parameter of `c` at the specified (zero-based)
position. The implicit `this` parameter is considered to have index `-1`.

class.comments:
The value of a parameter at function entry, viewed as a node in a data
flow graph.

method.name: getParameter
method.comments: Gets the parameter corresponding to this node.

class.comments:
INTERNAL: do not use.

A node that represents the value of a variable after a function call that
may have changed the variable because it's passed by reference or because an
iterator for it was passed by value or by reference.

method.name: getArgument
method.comments: Gets the argument corresponding to this node.

method.name: getParameter
method.body:
exists(FunctionCall call, int i |
      argument = call.getArgument(i) and
      result = call.getTarget().getParameter(i)
    )
method.comments: Gets the parameter through which this value is assigned.

predicate.name: DefinitionByReferenceOrIteratorNode
predicate.body:
this.getPartialDefinition().definesExpressions(inner, argument) and
    (
      this.getPartialDefinition() instanceof DefinitionByReference
      or
      this.getPartialDefinition() instanceof DefinitionByIterator
    )

class.comments:
A node that represents the value of a variable after a function call that
may have changed the variable because it's passed by reference.

A typical example would be a call `f(&x)`. Firstly, there will be flow into
`x` from previous definitions of `x`. Secondly, there will be a
`DefinitionByReferenceNode` to represent the value of `x` after the call has
returned. This node will have its `getArgument()` equal to `&x`.

The value of an uninitialized local variable, viewed as a node in a data
flow graph.

method.name: getLocalVariable
method.comments: Gets the uninitialized local variable corresponding to this node.

class.comments: INTERNAL: do not use. The final value of a non-const ref parameter.

A node associated with an object after an operation that might have
changed its state.

This can be either the argument to a callable after the callable returns
(which might have mutated the argument), or the qualifier of a field after
an update to the field.

Nodes corresponding to AST elements, for example `ExprNode`, usually refer
to the value before the update with the exception of `ClassInstanceExpr`,
which represents the value after the constructor has run.

method.name: getPreUpdateNode
method.comments: Gets the node before the state update.

class.comments:
INTERNAL: do not use.

A synthetic data flow node used for flow into a collection when an iterator
write occurs in a callee.
A post-update node on the `e->f` in `f(&e->f)` (and other forms).

A node representing the temporary value of an object that was just
constructed by a constructor call or an aggregate initializer. This is only
for objects, not for pointers to objects.

These expressions are their own post-update nodes but instead have synthetic
pre-update nodes.

INTERNAL: do not use.

A synthetic data-flow node that plays the role of a temporary object that
has not yet been initialized.

A synthetic data-flow node that plays the role of the post-update `this`
pointer in a `ConstructorFieldInit`. For example, the `x(1)` in
`C() : x(1) { }` is roughly equivalent to `this.x = 1`, and this node is
equivalent to the `this` _after_ the field has been assigned.

INTERNAL: do not use.

A synthetic data-flow node that plays the role of the pre-update `this`
pointer in a `ConstructorFieldInit`. For example, the `x(1)` in
`C() : x(1) { }` is roughly equivalent to `this.x = 1`, and this node is
equivalent to the `this` _before_ the field has been assigned.

A description of the way data may be stored inside an object. Examples
include instance fields, the contents of a collection object, or the contents
of an array.

method.name: toString
method.comments: Gets a textual representation of this element.

predicate.name: hasLocationInfo
predicate.body: path = "" and sl = 0 and sc = 0 and el = 0 and ec = 0

class.comments: A reference through an instance field.
A reference through an array.
A reference through the contents of some collection-like container.

An entity that represents a set of `Content`s.

The set may be interpreted differently depending on whether it is
stored into (`getAStoreContent`) or read from (`getAReadContent`).

method.name: getAStoreContent
method.comments: Gets a content that may be stored into when storing into this set.

method.name: getAReadContent
method.comments: Gets a content that may be read from when reading from this set.

method.name: toString
method.comments: Gets a textual representation of this content set.

predicate.name: hasLocationInfo
predicate.comments:
Holds if this element is at the specified location.
The location spans column `startcolumn` of line `startline` to
column `endcolumn` of line `endline` in file `filepath`.
For more information, see
[Locations](https://codeql.github.com/docs/writing-codeql-queries/providing-locations-in-codeql-queries/).

class.comments:
A conceptual variable that is assigned only once, like an SSA variable. This
class is used for tracking data flow through variables, where the desired
semantics is sometimes different from what the SSA library provides. Unlike
SSA, there are no _phi_ nodes; instead, each `VariableAccess` may be
associated with more than one `FlowVar`.

Each instance of this class corresponds to a modification or an initial
value of a variable. A `FlowVar` has exactly one result for either
`definedByExpr` or `definedByInitialValue`. The documentation on those two
member predicates explains how a `FlowVar` relates to syntactic constructs of
the language.

method.name: getAnAccess
method.comments:
Gets a `VariableAccess` that _may_ take its value from `this`. Consider
the following snippet.

```
int x = 0;
if (b) { f(x); x = 1; }
g(x)
```

The access to `x` in `f(x)` may take its value from the `FlowVar`
corresponding to `int x = 0`, while the access to `x` in `g(x)` may take
its value from the `FlowVar` corresponding to `int x = 0` or the `FlowVar`
corresponding to `x = 1`. The `x` in `x = 1` is not considered to be an
access.

method.name: toString
method.comments: Gets a textual representation of this element.

method.name: getLocation
method.comments: Gets the location of this element.

predicate.name: definedByExpr
predicate.comments:
Holds if this `FlowVar` corresponds to a modification occurring when `node` is
evaluated, receiving a value best described by `e`. The following is an
exhaustive list of cases where this may happen.

- `node` is an `Initializer` and `e` is its contained expression.
- `node` is an `AssignExpr`, and `e` is its right-hand side.
- `node` is an `AssignOperation`, and `e` is `node`.
- `node` is a `CrementOperation`, and `e` is `node`. The case where
`node instanceof PostCrementOperation` is an exception to the rule that
`this` contains the value of `e` after the evaluation of `node`.

predicate.name: definedPartiallyAt
predicate.comments:
Holds if this `FlowVar` is a `PartialDefinition` whose outer defined
expression is `e`. For example, in `f(&x)`, the outer defined expression
is `&x`.

predicate.name: reachesRefParameter
predicate.comments:
Holds if this `FlowVar` is a definition of a reference parameter `p` that
persists until the function returns.

predicate.name: definedByInitialValue
predicate.comments:
Holds if this `FlowVar` corresponds to the initial value of `v`. The following
is an exhaustive list of cases where this may happen.

- `v` is a parameter, and `this` contains the value of the parameter at
the entry point of its function body.
- `v` is an uninitialized local variable, and `v` contains its (arbitrary)
value before it is reassigned. If it can be statically determined that a
local variable is always overwritten before it is used, there is no
`FlowVar` instance for the uninitialized value of that variable.

class.comments:
An abstract class that directs where in the control-flow graph a new
`SubBasicBlock` must start. If a `ControlFlowNode` is an instance of this
class, that node is guaranteed to be the first node in a `SubBasicBlock`.
If multiple libraries use the `SubBasicBlock` library, basic blocks may be
split in more places than either library expects, but nothing should break
as a direct result of that.

A block that can be smaller than or equal to a `BasicBlock`. Use this class
when `ControlFlowNode` is too fine-grained and `BasicBlock` too
coarse-grained. Their successor graph is like that of basic blocks, except
that the blocks are split up with an extra edge right before any instance of
the abstract class `SubBasicBlockCutNode`. Users of this library must
therefore extend `SubBasicBlockCutNode` to direct where basic blocks will be
split up.

method.name: getBasicBlock
method.comments: Gets the basic block in which this `SubBasicBlock` is contained.

method.name: getRankInBasicBlock
method.body:
exists(int thisIndexInBB |
      thisIndexInBB = this.getIndexInBasicBlock(bb) and
      thisIndexInBB = rank[result](int i | i = any(SubBasicBlock n).getIndexInBasicBlock(bb))
    )
method.comments:
Gets the (1-based) rank of this `SubBasicBlock` among the other `SubBasicBlock`s in
its containing basic block `bb`, where `bb` is equal to `getBasicBlock()`.

method.name: getASuccessor
method.body:
this.lastInBB() and
    result = this.getBasicBlock().getASuccessor()
    or
    exists(BasicBlock bb | result.getRankInBasicBlock(bb) = this.getRankInBasicBlock(bb) + 1)
method.comments: Gets a successor in the control-flow graph of `SubBasicBlock`s.

method.name: getNode
method.comments:
Gets the `index`th control-flow node in this `SubBasicBlock`. Indexes
start from 0, and the node at index 0 always exists and compares equal
to `this`.

method.name: getANode
method.comments: Gets a control-flow node in this `SubBasicBlock`.

method.name: getAPredecessor
method.comments: Gets a predecessor in the control-flow graph of `SubBasicBlock`s.

method.name: getATrueSuccessor
method.comments:
Gets a node such that the control-flow edge `(this, result)` may be taken
when the final node of this `SubBasicBlock` is a conditional expression
and evaluates to true.

method.name: getAFalseSuccessor
method.comments:
Gets a node such that the control-flow edge `(this, result)` may be taken
when the final node of this `SubBasicBlock` is a conditional expression
and evaluates to false.

method.name: getNumberOfNodes
method.comments:
Gets the number of control-flow nodes in this `SubBasicBlock`. There is
always at least one.

method.name: getEnd
method.comments: Gets the last control-flow node in this `SubBasicBlock`.

method.name: getStart
method.comments: Gets the first control-flow node in this `SubBasicBlock`.

method.name: getEnclosingFunction
method.comments: Gets the function that contains this `SubBasicBlock`.

predicate.name: SubBasicBlock
predicate.body:
this instanceof BasicBlock
    or
    this instanceof SubBasicBlockCutNode

predicate.name: firstInBB
predicate.comments:
Holds if this `SubBasicBlock` comes first in its basic block. This is the
only condition under which a `SubBasicBlock` may have multiple
predecessors.

predicate.name: lastInBB
predicate.comments:
Holds if this `SubBasicBlock` comes last in its basic block. This is the
only condition under which a `SubBasicBlock` may have multiple successors.

predicate.name: contains
predicate.comments: Holds if `this` contains `node`.

class.comments: A C/C++ access expression. This refers to a function (excluding function references in function call expressions), variable, or enum constant.

method.name: getTarget
method.comments: Gets the accessed function, variable, or enum constant.

method.name: getTarget
method.comments: Gets the accessed `enum` constant.

method.name: toString
method.comments: Gets a textual representation of this `enum` constant access.

method.name: getTarget
method.comments: Gets the accessed variable.

method.name: getQualifier
method.comments:
Gets the expression generating the variable being accessed.

As a few examples:
For `ptr->x`, this gives `ptr`.
For `(*ptr).x`, this gives `(*ptr)`.
For `smart_ptr->x`, this gives the call to `operator->`.

This applies mostly to FieldAccesses, but also to static member variables accessed
"through" a pointer. Note that it does NOT apply to static member variables accessed
through a type name, as in that case the type name is a qualifier on the variable
rather than a qualifier on the access.

method.name: toString
method.comments: Gets a textual representation of this variable access.

predicate.name: isUsedAsLValue
predicate.body:
exists(Assignment a | a.getLValue() = this) or
    exists(CrementOperation c | c.getOperand() = this) or
    exists(AddressOfExpr addof | addof.getOperand() = this) or
    this.getConversion() instanceof ReferenceToExpr or
    this.getConversion() instanceof ArrayToPointerConversion
predicate.comments:
Holds if this variable access is providing an LValue in a meaningful way.
For example, this includes accesses on the left-hand side of an assignment.
It does not include accesses on the right-hand side of an assignment, even if they could appear on the left-hand side of some assignment.

predicate.name: isModified
predicate.body:
exists(Assignment a | a.getLValue() = this)
    or
    exists(CrementOperation c | c.getOperand() = this)
    or
    exists(FunctionCall c | c.getQualifier() = this and c.getTarget().hasName("operator="))
predicate.comments:
Holds if this variable access is in a position where it is (directly) modified,
for instance by an assignment or increment/decrement operator.

predicate.name: isRValue
predicate.body:
not exists(AssignExpr ae | ae.getLValue() = this) and
    not exists(AddressOfExpr addof | addof.getOperand() = this) and
    not this.getConversion() instanceof ReferenceToExpr and
    not this.getConversion() instanceof ArrayToPointerConversion
predicate.comments: Holds if this variable access is an rvalue.

predicate.name: isAddressOfAccess
predicate.comments:
Holds if this access is used to get the address of the underlying variable
in such a way that the address might escape. This can be either explicit,
for example `&x`, or implicit, for example `T& y = x`.

predicate.name: isAddressOfAccessNonConst
predicate.comments:
Holds if this access is used to get the address of the underlying variable
in such a way that the address might escape as a pointer or reference to
non-const data. This can be either explicit, for example `&x`, or
implicit, for example `T& y = x`.

method.name: getTarget
method.comments: Gets the accessed field.

predicate.name: PointerFieldAccess
predicate.body:
exists(PointerType t |
      t = this.getQualifier().getFullyConverted().getUnspecifiedType() and
      t.getBaseType() instanceof Class
    )

class.comments:
A field access of the form `obj.field`. The type of `obj` is either a
class/struct/union or a reference to one. `DotFieldAccess` has two
sub-classes, `ValueFieldAccess` and `ReferenceFieldAccess`, to
distinguish whether or not the type of `obj` is a reference type.

A C++ _pointer to non-static data member_ literal. For example, `&C::x` is
an expression that refers to field `x` of class `C`. If the type of that
field is `int`, then `&C::x` ought to have type `int C::*`. It is currently
modeled in QL as having type `int`.

See [dcl.mptr] in the C++17 standard or see
https://en.cppreference.com/w/cpp/language/pointer#Pointers_to_data_members.

method.name: getTarget
method.comments: Gets the accessed function.

method.name: getQualifier
method.comments: Gets the expression generating the function being accessed.

method.name: toString
method.comments: Gets a textual representation of this function access.

method.name: getArrayBase
method.comments:
Gets the array or pointer expression being subscripted.

This is `arr` in both `arr[0]` and `0[arr]`.

method.name: getArrayOffset
method.comments:
Gets the expression giving the index into the array.

This is `0` in both `arr[0]` and `0[arr]`.

predicate.name: isModified
predicate.body:
exists(Assignment a | a.getLValue() = this)
    or
    exists(CrementOperation c | c.getOperand() = this)
    or
    exists(FunctionCall c | c.getQualifier() = this and c.getTarget().hasName("operator="))
predicate.comments:
Holds if this array access is in a position where it is (directly) modified,
for instance by an assignment or an increment/decrement operation.

class.comments: A C/C++ unary arithmetic operation.

A C/C++ unary minus expression.
```
b = - a;
```

A C/C++ unary plus expression.
```
b = + a;
```

A C/C++ GNU conjugation expression.  It operates on `_Complex` or
`__complex__ `numbers, and is similar to the C99 `conj`, `conjf` and `conjl`
functions.
```
_Complex double a =  ( 1.0, 2.0 );
_Complex double b = ~ a;  // ( 1.0, - 2.0 )
```

A C/C++ `++` or `--` expression (either prefix or postfix).

This is the base QL class for increment and decrement operations.

Note that this does not include calls to user-defined `operator++`
or `operator--`.

predicate.name: mayBeGloballyImpure
predicate.body:
not exists(VariableAccess va, StackVariable v |
      va = this.getOperand() and
      v = va.getTarget() and
      not va.getConversion+() instanceof ReferenceDereferenceExpr
    )

class.comments:
A C/C++ `++` expression (either prefix or postfix).

Note that this does not include calls to user-defined `operator++`.

A C/C++ `--` expression (either prefix or postfix).

Note that this does not include calls to user-defined `operator--`.

A C/C++ `++` or `--` prefix expression.

Note that this does not include calls to user-defined operators.

A C/C++ `++` or `--` postfix expression.

Note that this does not include calls to user-defined operators.

A C/C++ prefix increment expression, as in `++x`.

Note that this does not include calls to user-defined `operator++`.
```
b = ++a;
```

A C/C++ prefix decrement expression, as in `--x`.

Note that this does not include calls to user-defined `operator--`.
```
b = --a;
```

A C/C++ postfix increment expression, as in `x++`.

Note that this does not include calls to user-defined `operator++`.
```
b = a++;
```

A C/C++ postfix decrement expression, as in `x--`.

Note that this does not include calls to user-defined `operator--`.
```
b = a--;
```

A C/C++ GNU real part expression.  It operates on `_Complex` or
`__complex__` numbers.
```
_Complex double f = { 2.0, 3.0 };
double d = __real(f);  // 2.0
```

A C/C++ GNU imaginary part expression.  It operates on `_Complex` or
`__complex__` numbers.
```
_Complex double f = { 2.0, 3.0 };
double d = __imag(f);  // 3.0
```

A C/C++ binary arithmetic operation.

This is an abstract base QL class for all binary arithmetic operations.

A C/C++ add expression.
```
c = a + b;
```

A C/C++ subtract expression.
```
c = a - b;
```

A C/C++ multiply expression.
```
c = a * b;
```

A C/C++ divide expression.
```
c = a / b;
```

A C/C++ remainder expression.
```
c = a % b;
```

A C/C++ GNU min expression.
```
c = a <? b;
```

A C/C++ GNU max expression.
```
c = a >? b;
```
A C/C++ pointer arithmetic operation.

A non-overloaded binary assignment operation, including `=`, `+=`, `&=`,
etc. A C++ overloaded assignment operation looks syntactically identical but is instead
a `FunctionCall`. This class does _not_ include variable initializers. To get a variable
initializer, use `Initializer` instead.

This is a QL base class for all (non-overloaded) assignments.

method.name: getLValue
method.comments: Gets the _lvalue_ of this assignment.

method.name: getRValue
method.comments: Gets the rvalue of this assignment.

predicate.name: mayBeGloballyImpure
predicate.body:
this.getRValue().mayBeGloballyImpure()
    or
    not exists(VariableAccess va, StackVariable v |
      va = this.getLValue() and
      v = va.getTarget() and
      not va.getConversion+() instanceof ReferenceDereferenceExpr
    )

class.comments:
A non-overloaded assignment operation with the operator `=`.
```
a = b;
```
Note that `int a = b;` is _not_ an `AssignExpr`. It is a `Variable`,
and `b` can be obtained using `Variable.getInitializer()`.

method.name: toString
method.comments: Gets a textual representation of this assignment.

class.comments:
A compiler generated assignment operation that may occur in a compiler generated
copy/move constructor or assignment operator, and which functions like `memcpy`
where the size argument is based on the type of the rvalue of the assignment.

method.name: toString
method.comments: Gets a textual representation of this assignment.

class.comments:
A non-overloaded binary assignment operation other than `=`.

This class does _not_ include variable initializers. To get a variable
initializer, use `Initializer` instead.

A non-overloaded arithmetic assignment operation on a non-pointer _lvalue_:
`+=`, `-=`, `*=`, `/=` and `%=`.

A non-overloaded `+=` assignment expression on a non-pointer _lvalue_.
```
a += b;
```

A non-overloaded `-=` assignment expression on a non-pointer _lvalue_.
```
a -= b;
```

A non-overloaded `*=` assignment expression.
```
a *= b;
```

A non-overloaded `/=` assignment expression.
```
a /= b;
```

A non-overloaded `%=` assignment expression.
```
a %= b;
```

A non-overloaded bitwise assignment operation:
`&=`, `|=`, `^=`, `<<=`, and `>>=`.

A non-overloaded AND (`&=`) assignment expression.
```
a &= b;
```

A non-overloaded OR (`|=`) assignment expression.
```
a |= b;
```

A non-overloaded XOR (`^=`) assignment expression.
```
a ^= b;
```

A non-overloaded `<<=` assignment expression.
```
a <<= b;
```

A non-overloaded `>>=` assignment expression.
```
a >>= b;
```

A non-overloaded `+=` pointer assignment expression.
```
ptr += index;
```

A non-overloaded `-=` pointer assignment expression.
```
ptr -= index;
```

method.name: getVariableAccess
method.comments:
Gets the compiler-generated variable access that conceptually occurs after
the initialization of the declared variable, if any.

method.name: getExpr
method.comments:
Gets the expression that is evaluated after the initialization of the declared
variable.

method.name: getInitializingExpr
method.comments:
Gets the expression that initializes the declared variable. This predicate
always has a result.

method.name: getVariable
method.comments: Gets the variable that is declared.

class.comments: A C/C++ unary bitwise operation.

A C/C++ complement expression.
```
unsigned c = ~a;
```
A C/C++ binary bitwise operation.

A C/C++ left shift expression.
```
unsigned c = a << b;
```

A C/C++ right shift expression.
```
unsigned c = a >> b;
```

A C/C++ bitwise AND expression.
```
unsigned c = a & b;
```

A C/C++ bitwise OR expression.
```
unsigned c = a | b;
```

A C/C++ bitwise XOR expression.
```
unsigned c = a ^ b;
```

A C/C++ built-in operation. This is the root QL class encompassing
built-in functionality.

A C/C++ built-in operation that is used to support functions with variable numbers of arguments.
This includes `va_start`, `va_end`, `va_copy`, and `va_arg`.

method.name: getVAList
method.comments: Gets the `va_list` argument.

method.name: getLastNamedParameter
method.comments: Gets the argument that specifies the last named parameter before the ellipsis.

method.name: getVAList
method.comments: Gets the `va_list` argument.

class.comments:
A C/C++ `__builtin_va_arg` built-in operation (used by some implementations
of `va_arg`).
```
ap = __builtin_va_arg(ap, long);
```

method.name: getVAList
method.comments: Gets the `va_list` argument.

method.name: getDestinationVAList
method.comments: Gets the destination `va_list` argument.

method.name: getSourceVAList
method.comments: Gets the the source `va_list` argument.

class.comments:
A Microsoft C/C++ `__noop` expression, which does nothing.
```
__noop;
```

A C++ `__has_assign` built-in operation (used by some implementations of
the `<type_traits>` header).

Returns `true` if the type has a copy assignment operator.
```
bool v = __has_assign(MyType);
```

A C++ `__has_copy` built-in operation (used by some implementations of the
`<type_traits>` header).

Returns `true` if the type has a copy constructor.
```
std::integral_constant<bool, __has_copy(_Tp)> hc;
```

A C++ `__has_nothrow_assign` built-in operation (used by some
implementations of the `<type_traits>` header).

Returns `true` if a copy assignment operator has an empty exception
specification.
```
std::integral_constant<bool, __has_nothrow_assign(_Tp)> hnta;
```

A C++ `__has_nothrow_constructor` built-in operation (used by some
implementations of the `<type_traits>` header).

Returns `true` if the default constructor has an empty exception
specification.
```
bool v = __has_nothrow_constructor(MyType);
```

A C++ `__has_nothrow_copy` built-in operation (used by some implementations
of the `<type_traits>` header).

Returns `true` if the copy constructor has an empty exception specification.
```
std::integral_constant<bool, __has_nothrow_copy(MyType) >;
```

A C++ `__has_trivial_assign` built-in operation (used by some implementations
of the `<type_traits>` header).

Returns `true` if the type has a trivial assignment
operator (`operator =`).
```
bool v = __has_trivial_assign(MyType);
```

A C++ `__has_trivial_constructor` built-in operation (used by some
implementations of the `<type_traits>` header).

Returns `true` if the type has a trivial constructor.
```
bool v = __has_trivial_constructor(MyType);
```

A C++ `__has_trivial_copy` built-in operation (used by some implementations
of the `<type_traits>` header).

Returns true if the type has a trivial copy constructor.
```
std::integral_constant<bool, __has_trivial_copy(MyType)> htc;
```

A C++ `__has_trivial_destructor` built-in operation (used by some
implementations of the `<type_traits>` header).

Returns `true` if the type has a trivial destructor.
```
bool v = __has_trivial_destructor(MyType);
```

A C++ `__has_user_destructor` built-in operation (used by some
implementations of the `<type_traits>` header).

Returns true if the type has a user-declared destructor.
```
bool v = __has_user_destructor(MyType);
```

A C++ `__has_virtual_destructor` built-in operation (used by some
implementations of the `<type_traits>` header).

Returns `true` if the type has a virtual destructor.
```
template<typename _Tp>
struct has_virtual_destructor
: public integral_constant<bool, __has_virtual_destructor(_Tp)>
{ };
```

A C++ `__is_abstract` built-in operation (used by some implementations of the
`<type_traits>` header).

Returns `true` if the class has at least one pure virtual function.
```
bool v = __is_abstract(MyType);
```

A C++ `__is_base_of` built-in operation (used by some implementations of the
`<type_traits>` header).

Returns `true` if the first type is a base class of the second type, of if both types are the same.
```
bool v = __is_base_of(MyType, OtherType);
```

A C++ `__is_class` built-in operation (used by some implementations of the
`<type_traits>` header).

Returns `true` if the type is a `class` or a `struct`.
```
bool v = __is_class(MyType);
```

A C++ `__is_convertible_to` built-in operation (used by some implementations
of the `<type_traits>` header).

Returns `true` if the first type can be converted to the second type.
```
bool v = __is_convertible_to(MyType, OtherType);
```

A C++ `__is_convertible` built-in operation (used by some implementations
of the `<type_traits>` header).

Returns `true` if the first type can be converted to the second type.
```
bool v = __is_convertible(MyType, OtherType);
```

A C++ `__is_nothrow_convertible` built-in operation (used by some implementations
of the `<type_traits>` header).

Returns `true` if the first type can be converted to the second type and the
conversion operator has an empty exception specification.
```
bool v = __is_nothrow_convertible(MyType, OtherType);
```

A C++ `__is_empty` built-in operation (used by some implementations of the
`<type_traits>` header).

Returns `true` if the type has no instance data members.
```
bool v = __is_empty(MyType);
```

A C++ `__is_enum` built-in operation (used by some implementations of the
`<type_traits>` header).

Returns true if the type is an `enum`.
```
bool v = __is_enum(MyType);
```

A C++ `__is_pod` built-in operation (used by some implementations of the
`<type_traits>` header).

Returns `true` if the type is a `class`, `struct` or `union`,  WITHOUT
(1) constructors, (2) private or protected non-static members, (3) base
classes, or (4) virtual functions.
```
bool v = __is_pod(MyType);
```

A C++ `__is_polymorphic` built-in operation (used by some implementations
of the `<type_traits>` header).

Returns `true` if the type has at least one virtual function.
```
bool v = __is_polymorphic(MyType);
```

A C++ `__is_union` built-in operation (used by some implementations of the
`<type_traits>` header).

Returns `true` if the type is a `union`.
```
bool v = __is_union(MyType);
```

A C++ `__builtin_types_compatible_p` built-in operation (used by some
implementations of the `<type_traits>` header).

Returns `true` if the two types are the same (modulo qualifiers).
```
template<typename _Tp1, typename _Tp2>
struct types_compatible
: public integral_constant<bool, __builtin_types_compatible_p(_Tp1, _Tp2)>
{ };
```

A clang `__builtin_shufflevector` expression.

It outputs a permutation of elements from one or two input vectors. See
https://releases.llvm.org/3.7.0/tools/clang/docs/LanguageExtensions.html#langext-builtin-shufflevector
for more information.
```
// Concatenate every other element of 4-element vectors V1 and V2.
V3 = __builtin_shufflevector(V1, V2, 0, 2, 4, 6);
```

A clang `__builtin_addressof` function (can be used to implement C++'s
`std::addressof`).

This function disregards any overloads created for `operator &`.
```
int a = 1;
int *b = __builtin_addressof(a);
```

method.name: getAddressable
method.body:
result = this.getOperand().(Access).getTarget()
    or
    // this handles the case where we are taking the address of a reference variable
    result = this.getOperand().(ReferenceDereferenceExpr).getChild(0).(Access).getTarget()
method.comments: Gets the function or variable whose address is taken.

class.comments:
The `__is_trivially_constructible` built-in operation (used by some
implementations of the `<type_traits>` header).

Returns `true` if the type has a trivial default
constructor, copy constructor or move constructor.
```
template<typename T, typename... Args>
struct is_trivially_constructible
: public integral_constant<bool, __is_trivially_constructible(T, Args...)>
{ };
```

The `__is_destructible` built-in operation (used by some implementations
of the `<type_traits>` header).

Returns `true` if the type's destructor is not `delete`d and is accessible
in derived `class`es, and whose base `class` and all non-static data members
are also destructible.
```
bool v = __is_destructible(MyType);
```

The `__is_nothrow_destructible` built-in operation (used by some
implementations of the `<type_traits>` header).

Returns `true` if the type is destructible and whose destructor, and those
of member data and any super`class`es all have an empty exception
specification.
```
bool v = __is_nothrow_destructible(MyType);
```

The `__is_trivially_destructible` built-in operation (used by some
implementations of the `<type_traits>` header).

Returns `true` if the type is destructible and whose destructor, and those
of member data and any superclasses are all trivial.
```
bool v = __is_trivially_destructible(MyType);
```

The `__is_trivially_assignable` built-in operation (used by some
implementations of the `<type_traits>` header).

Returns `true` if the assignment operator `C::operator =(const D& d)` is
trivial (i.e., it will not call any operation that is non-trivial).
```
bool v = __is_trivially_assignable(MyType1, MyType2);
```

The `__is_nothrow_assignable` built-in operation (used by some
implementations of the `<type_traits>` header).

Returns true if there exists an assignment operator with an empty exception specification.
```
bool v = __is_nothrow_assignable(MyType1, MyType2);
```

The `__is_assignable` built-in operation (used by some implementations
of the `<type_traits>` header).

Returns true if there exists an assignment operator.
```
bool v = __is_assignable(MyType1, MyType2);
```

The `__is_assignable_no_precondition_check` built-in operation (used by some
implementations of the `<type_traits>` header).

Returns true if there exists an assignment operator.
```
bool v = __is_assignable_no_precondition_check(MyType1, MyType2);
```

The `__is_standard_layout` built-in operation (used by some implementations
of the `<type_traits>` header).

Returns `true` if the type is a primitive type, or a `class`, `struct` or
`union` without (1) virtual functions or base classes, (2) reference member
variable, or (3) multiple occurrences of base `class` objects, among other
restrictions. See https://en.cppreference.com/w/cpp/named_req/StandardLayoutType
for more information.
```
bool v = __is_standard_layout(MyType);
```

The `__is_trivially_copyable` built-in operation (used by some
implementations of the `<type_traits>` header).

Returns `true` if instances of this type can be copied by trivial
means. The copying is done in a manner similar to the `memcpy`
function.

The `__is_trivially_copy_assignable` built-in operation (used by some
implementations of the `<type_traits>` header).

Returns `true` if instances of this type can be copied using a trivial
copy operator.

The `__is_literal_type` built-in operation (used by some implementations of
the `<type_traits>` header).

Returns `true` if the type is a scalar type, a reference type or an array of
literal types, among others. See
https://en.cppreference.com/w/cpp/named_req/LiteralType
for more information.

```
template <typename _Tp>
std::integral_constant<bool, __is_literal_type(_Tp)> ilt;
```

The `__has_trivial_move_constructor` built-in operation (used by some
implementations of the `<type_traits>` header).

Returns true if the move (`&&`) constructor can be generated by the
compiler, with semantics of the `memcpy` operation.
```
template <typename _Tp>
std::integral_constant<bool, __has_trivial_move_constructor(_Tp)> htmc;
```

The `__has_trivial_move_assign` built-in operation (used by some
implementations of the `<type_traits>` header).

Returns if the move-assign operator `C::operator =(C &&c)` is trivial.
```
template<typename T>
struct has_trivial_move_assign
: public integral_constant<bool, __has_trivial_move_assign(T)>
{ };
```

The `__has_nothrow_move_assign` built-in operation (used by some
implementations of the `<type_traits>` header).

Returns `true` if the type has a `C::operator=(C&& c) nothrow`, that is,
an assignment operator with an empty exception specification.
```
bool v = __has_nothrow_move_assign(MyType);
```

The `__is_constructible` built-in operation (used by some implementations
of the `<type_traits>` header).

Returns `true` if the type can be constructed using specified arguments
(or none).
```
template<typename T, typename... Args>
struct is_constructible
: public integral_constant<bool, __is_constructible(T, Args...)>
{ };
```

The `__is_nothrow_constructible` built-in operation (used by some
implementations of the `<type_traits>` header).

Returns `true` if the type is constructable and all its constructors have an
empty exception specification (i.e., are declared with `nothrow`);
```
bool v = __is_nothrow_constructible(MyType);
```

The `__has_finalizer` built-in operation. This is a Microsoft extension.

Returns `true` if the type defines a _finalizer_ `C::!C(void)`, to be called
from either the regular destructor or the garbage collector.
```
bool v = __has_finalizer(MyType);
```

The `__is_delegate` built-in operation. This is a Microsoft extension.

Returns `true` if the function has been declared as a `delegate`, used in
message forwarding. See
https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/cpp/extensions/delegate-cpp-component-extensions
for more information.

The `__is_interface_class` built-in operation. This is a Microsoft extension.

Returns `true` if the type has been declared as an `interface`. See
https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/cpp/extensions/interface-class-cpp-component-extensions
for more information.

The `__is_ref_array` built-in operation. This is a Microsoft extension.

Returns `true` if the object passed in is a _platform array_. See
https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/cpp/extensions/arrays-cpp-component-extensions
for more information.
```
array<int>^ x = gcnew array<int>(10);
bool b = __is_ref_array(array<int>);
```

The `__is_ref_class` built-in operation. This is a Microsoft extension.

Returns `true` if the type is a _reference class_. See
https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/cpp/extensions/classes-and-structs-cpp-component-extensions
for more information.
```
ref class R {};
bool b = __is_ref_class(R);
```

The `__is_simple_value_class` built-in operation. This is a Microsoft extension.

Returns `true` if passed a value type that contains no references to the
garbage-collected heap.
```
ref class R {};             // __is_simple_value_class(R) == false
value struct V {};          // __is_simple_value_class(V) == true
value struct V2 {           // __is_simple_value_class(V2) == false
R ^ r;   // not a simple value type
};
```

The `__is_value_class` built-in operation. This is a Microsoft extension.

Returns `true` if passed a value type. See
https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/cpp/extensions/classes-and-structs-cpp-component-extensions
For more information.
```
value struct V {};
bool v = __is_value_class(V);
```

The `__is_final` built-in operation (used by some implementations of the
`<type_traits>` header).

Returns `true` if the `class` has been marked with the `final` specifier.
```
template<typename T>
struct is_final
: public integral_constant<bool, __is_final(T)>
{ };
```

The `__builtin_choose_expr` expression. This is a gcc/clang extension.

The expression functions similarly to the ternary `?:` operator, except
that it is evaluated at compile-time.
```
int sz = __builtin_choose_expr(__builtin_types_compatible_p(int, long), 4, 8);
```
The GNU `__builtin_complex` operation.

method.name: getRealOperand
method.comments: Gets the operand corresponding to the real part of the complex number.

method.name: getImaginaryOperand
method.comments: Gets the operand corresponding to the imaginary part of the complex number.

class.comments:
A C++ `__is_aggregate` built-in operation (used by some implementations of the
`<type_traits>` header).

Returns `true` if the type has is an aggregate type.
```
std::integral_constant<bool, __is_aggregate(_Tp)> ia;
```

A C++ `__has_unique_object_representations` built-in operation (used by some
implementations of the `<type_traits>` header).

Returns `true` if the type is trivially copyable and if the object representation
is unique for two objects with the same value.
```
bool v = __has_unique_object_representations(MyType);
```

A C++ `__is_same` built-in operation (used by some implementations of the
`<type_traits>` header).

Returns `true` if two types are the same.
```
template<typename _Tp, typename _Up>
struct is_same
: public integral_constant<bool, __is_same(_Tp, _Up)>
{ };
```

A C++ `__is_same_as` built-in operation (used by some implementations of the
`<type_traits>` header).

Returns `true` if two types are the same.
```
template<typename _Tp, typename _Up>
struct is_same
: public integral_constant<bool, __is_same_as(_Tp, _Up)>
{ };
```

A C++ `__is_function` built-in operation (used by some implementations of the
`<type_traits>` header).

Returns `true` if a type is a function type.
```
template<typename _Tp>
struct is_function
: public integral_constant<bool, __is_function(_Tp)>
{ };
```

A C++ `__is_layout_compatible` built-in operation (used by some implementations
of the `<type_traits>` header).

Returns `true` if two types are layout-compatible.
```
template<typename _Tp, typename _Up>
struct is_layout_compatible
: public integral_constant<bool, __is_layout_compatible(_Tp, _Up)>
{ };
```

A C++ `__is_pointer_interconvertible_base_of` built-in operation (used
by some implementations of the `<type_traits>` header).

Returns `true` if the second type is pointer-interconvertible with the first type.
```
template<typename _Tp, typename _Up>
struct is_pointer_interconvertible_base_of_v
: public integral_constant<bool, __is_pointer_interconvertible_base_of(_Tp, _Up)>
{ };
```

A C++ `__is_pointer_interconvertible_with_class` built-in operation (used
by some implementations of the `<type_traits>` header).

Returns `true` if a member pointer is pointer-interconvertible with a
class type.
```
template<typename _Tp, typename _Up>
constexpr bool is_pointer_interconvertible_with_class(_Up _Tp::*mp) noexcept
= __is_pointer_interconvertible_with_class(_Tp, mp);
```

A C++ `__builtin_is_pointer_interconvertible_with_class` built-in operation (used
by some implementations of the `<type_traits>` header).

Returns `true` if a member pointer is pointer-interconvertible with a class type.
```
template<typename _Tp, typename _Up>
constexpr bool is_pointer_interconvertible_with_class(_Up _Tp::*mp) noexcept
= __builtin_is_pointer_interconvertible_with_class(mp);
```

A C++ `__is_corresponding_member` built-in operation (used
by some implementations of the `<type_traits>` header).

Returns `true` if two member pointers refer to corresponding
members in the initial sequences of two class types.
```
template<typename _Tp1, typename _Tp2, typename _Up1, typename _Up2>
constexpr bool is_corresponding_member(_Up1 _Tp1::*mp1, _Up2 _Tp2::*mp2 ) noexcept
= __is_corresponding_member(_Tp1, _Tp2, mp1, mp2);
```

A C++ `__builtin_is_corresponding_member` built-in operation (used
by some implementations of the `<type_traits>` header).

Returns `true` if two member pointers refer to corresponding
members in the initial sequences of two class types.
```
template<typename _Tp1, typename _Tp2, typename _Up1, typename _Up2>
constexpr bool is_corresponding_member(_Up1 _Tp1::*mp1, _Up2 _Tp2::*mp2 ) noexcept
= __builtin_is_corresponding_member(mp1, mp2);
```

A C++ `__is_array` built-in operation (used by some implementations of the
`<type_traits>` header).

Returns `true` if a type is an array type.
```
template<typename _Tp>
struct is_array
: public integral_constant<bool, __is_array(_Tp)>
{ };
```

A C++ `__is_bounded_array` built-in operation (used by some implementations
of the `<type_traits>` header).

Returns `true` if a type is a bounded array type.
```
template<typename _Tp>
struct is_bounded_array
: public integral_constant<bool, __is_bounded_array(_Tp)>
{ };
```

A C++ `__is_unbounded_array` built-in operation (used by some implementations
of the `<type_traits>` header).

Returns `true` if a type is an unbounded array type.
```
template<typename _Tp>
struct is_bounded_array
: public integral_constant<bool, __is_unbounded_array(_Tp)>
{ };
```

A C++ `__array_rank` built-in operation (used by some implementations of the
`<type_traits>` header).

If known, returns the number of dimensions of an arrary type.
```
template<typename _Tp>
struct rank
: public integral_constant<size_t, __array_rank(_Tp)>
{ };
```

A C++ `__array_extent` built-in operation (used by some implementations of the
`<type_traits>` header).

If known, returns the number of elements of an arrary type in the specified
dimension.
```
template<typename _Tp,  unsigned int _Dim>
struct extent
: public integral_constant<size_t, __array_extent(_Tp, _Dim)>
{ };
```

A C++ `__is_arithmetic` built-in operation (used by some implementations of the
`<type_traits>` header).

Returns `true` if a type is an arithmetic type.
```
template<typename _Tp>
struct is_arithmetic
: public integral_constant<bool, __is_arithmetic(_Tp)>
{ };
```

A C++ `__is_complete_type` built-in operation.

Returns `true` if a type is a complete type. Note that this built-in operation
can return different values for the same type at different points in a program.
```
class S;
bool b = __complete_type(S);
```

A C++ `__is_compound` built-in operation (used by some implementations of the
`<type_traits>` header).

Returns `true` if a type is a compound type.
```
template<typename _Tp>
struct is_compound
: public integral_constant<bool, __is_compound(_Tp)>
{ };
```

A C++ `__is_const` built-in operation (used by some implementations of the
`<type_traits>` header).

Returns `true` if a type is a const-qualified type.
```
template<typename _Tp>
struct is_const
: public integral_constant<bool, __is_const(_Tp)>
{ };
```

A C++ `__is_floating_point` built-in operation (used by some implementations
of the `<type_traits>` header).

Returns `true` if a type is a floating point type.
```
template<typename _Tp>
struct is_floating_point
: public integral_constant<bool, __is_floating_point(_Tp)>
{ };
```

A C++ `__is_fundamental` built-in operation (used by some implementations of the
`<type_traits>` header).

Returns `true` if a type is a fundamental C++ type.
```
template<typename _Tp>
struct is_fundamental
: public integral_constant<bool, __is_fundamental(_Tp)>
{ };
```

A C++ `__is_integral` built-in operation (used by some implementations of the
`<type_traits>` header).

Returns `true` if a type is an integral type.
```
template<typename _Tp>
struct is_integral
: public integral_constant<bool, __is_integral(_Tp)>
{ };
```

A C++ `__is_lvalue_reference` built-in operation (used by some implementations
of the `<type_traits>` header).

Returns `true` if a type is an lvalue reference type.
```
template<typename _Tp>
struct is_lvalue_reference
: public integral_constant<bool, __is_lvalue_reference(_Tp)>
{ };
```

A C++ `__is_member_function_pointer` built-in operation (used by some implementations
of the `<type_traits>` header).

Returns `true` if a type is an non-static member function pointer type.
```
template<typename _Tp>
struct is_member_function_pointer
: public integral_constant<bool, __is_member_function_pointer(_Tp)>
{ };
```

A C++ `__is_member_object_pointer` built-in operation (used by some implementations
of the `<type_traits>` header).

Returns `true` if a type is an non-static member object pointer type.
```
template<typename _Tp>
struct is_member_object_pointer
: public integral_constant<bool, __is_member_object_pointer(_Tp)>
{ };
```

A C++ `__is_member_pointer` built-in operation (used by some implementations
of the `<type_traits>` header).

Returns `true` if a type is an non-static member object of function pointer type.
```
template<typename _Tp>
struct is_member_pointer
: public integral_constant<bool, __is_member_pointer(_Tp)>
{ };
```

A C++ `__is_object` built-in operation (used by some implementations
of the `<type_traits>` header).

Returns `true` if a type is an object type.
```
template<typename _Tp>
struct is_object
: public integral_constant<bool, __is_object(_Tp)>
{ };
```

A C++ `__is_pointer` built-in operation (used by some implementations
of the `<type_traits>` header).

Returns `true` if a type is a pointer to an object or function type.
```
template<typename _Tp>
struct is_pointer
: public integral_constant<bool, __is_pointer(_Tp)>
{ };
```

A C++ `__is_reference` built-in operation (used by some implementations
of the `<type_traits>` header).

Returns `true` if a type is an lvalue or rvalue reference type.
```
template<typename _Tp>
struct is_reference
: public integral_constant<bool, __is_reference(_Tp)>
{ };
```

A C++ `__is_rvalue_reference` built-in operation (used by some implementations
of the `<type_traits>` header).

Returns `true` if a type is an rvalue reference type.
```
template<typename _Tp>
struct is_rvalue_reference
: public integral_constant<bool, __is_rvalue_reference(_Tp)>
{ };
```

A C++ `__is_scalar` built-in operation (used by some implementations of the
`<type_traits>` header).

Returns `true` if a type is a scalar type.
```
template<typename _Tp>
struct is_scalar
: public integral_constant<bool, __is_scalar(_Tp)>
{ };
```

A C++ `__is_signed` built-in operation (used by some implementations of the
`<type_traits>` header).

Returns `true` if a type is a signed arithmetic type.
```
template<typename _Tp>
struct is_signed
: public integral_constant<bool, __is_signed(_Tp)>
{ };
```

A C++ `__is_unsigned` built-in operation (used by some implementations of the
`<type_traits>` header).

Returns `true` if a type is an unsigned arithmetic type.
```
template<typename _Tp>
struct is_unsigned
: public integral_constant<bool, __is_unsigned(_Tp)>
{ };
```

A C++ `__is_void` built-in operation (used by some implementations of the
`<type_traits>` header).

Returns `true` if a type is a void type.
```
template<typename _Tp>
struct is_void
: public integral_constant<bool, __is_void(_Tp)>
{ };
```

A C++ `__is_volatile` built-in operation (used by some implementations of the
`<type_traits>` header).

Returns `true` if a type is a volatile-qualified type.
```
template<typename _Tp>
struct is_volatile
: public integral_constant<bool, __is_volatile(_Tp)>
{ };
```

A C/C++ `__builtin_bit_cast` built-in operation (used by some implementations
of `std::bit_cast`).

Performs a bit cast from a value to a type.
```
__builtin_bit_cast(Type, value);
```

A C++ `__is_trivial` built-in operation (used by some implementations of the
`<type_traits>` header).

Returns `true` if a type is a trivial type.
```
template<typename _Tp>
struct is_trivial
: public integral_constant<bool, __is_trivial(_Tp)>
{};
```

A C++ `__reference_constructs_from_temporary` built-in operation
(used by some implementations of the `<type_traits>` header).

Returns `true` if a reference type `_Tp` is bound to an expression of
type `_Up` in direct-initialization, and a temporary object is bound.
```
template<typename _Tp, typename _Up>
struct reference_constructs_from_temporary
: public integral_constant<bool, __reference_constructs_from_temporary(_Tp, _Up)>
{};
```

A C++ `__reference_converts_from_temporary` built-in operation
(used by some implementations of the `<type_traits>` header).

Returns `true` if a reference type `_Tp` is bound to an expression of
type `_Up` in copy-initialization, and a temporary object is bound.
```
template<typename _Tp, typename _Up>
struct reference_converts_from_temporary
: public integral_constant<bool, __reference_converts_from_temporary(_Tp, _Up)>
{};
```

A C++ `__reference_binds_to_temporary` built-in operation (used by some
implementations of the `<tuple>` header).

Returns `true` if a reference of type `Type1` is bound to an expression of
type `Type1`, and a temporary object is bound.
```
__reference_binds_to_temporary(Type1, Type2)

A C++ `__builtin_has_attribute` built-in operation.

Returns `true` if a type or expression has been declared with the
specified attribute.
```
__attribute__ ((aligned(8))) int v;
bool has_attribute = __builtin_has_attribute(v, aligned);
```

A C++ `__is_referenceable` built-in operation.

Returns `true` if a type can be referenced.
```
bool is_referenceable = __is_referenceable(int);
```

The `__is_valid_winrt_type` built-in operation. This is a Microsoft extension.

Returns `true` if the type is a valid WinRT type.

The `__is_win_class` built-in operation. This is a Microsoft extension.

Returns `true` if the class is a ref class.

The `__is_win_class` built-in operation. This is a Microsoft extension.

Returns `true` if the class is an interface class.

A C++ `__is_trivially_equality_comparable` built-in operation.

Returns `true` if comparing two objects of type `_Tp` is equivalent to
comparing their object representations.

```
template<typename _Tp>
struct is_trivially_equality_comparable
: public integral_constant<bool, __is_trivially_equality_comparable(_Tp)>
{};
```

A C++ `__is_scoped_enum` built-in operation (used by some implementations
of the `<type_traits>` header).

Returns `true` if a type is a scoped enum.
```
template<typename _Tp>
constexpr bool is_scoped_enum = __is_scoped_enum(_Tp);
```

A C++ `__is_trivially_relocatable` built-in operation.

Returns `true` if moving an object of type `_Tp` is equivalent to
copying the underlying bytes.

```
template<typename _Tp>
struct is_trivially_relocatable
: public integral_constant<bool, __is_trivially_relocatable(_Tp)>
{};
```
A C/C++ call.

method.name: getNumberOfArguments
method.comments:
Gets the number of arguments (actual parameters) of this call. The count
does _not_ include the qualifier of the call, if any.

method.name: getQualifier
method.comments:
Gets the expression to the left of the function name or function pointer variable name.

As a few examples:
For the call to `f` in `ptr->f()`, this gives `ptr`.
For the call to `f` in `(*ptr).f()`, this gives `(*ptr)`.

method.name: getAnArgument
method.body: exists(int i | result = this.getChild(i) and i >= 0)
method.comments:
Gets an argument for this call. To get the qualifier of this call, if
any, use `getQualifier()`.

method.name: getArgument
method.comments:
Gets the nth argument for this call.

The range of `n` is from `0` to `getNumberOfArguments() - 1`. To get the
qualifier of this call, if any, use `getQualifier()`.

method.name: getAnArgumentSubExpr
method.body:
result = this.getArgument(index)
    or
    exists(Expr mid |
      mid = this.getAnArgumentSubExpr(index) and
      not mid instanceof Call and
      not mid instanceof SizeofOperator and
      result = mid.getAChild()
    )
method.comments:
Gets a subexpression of the argument at position `index`. If the
argument itself contains calls, such calls will be considered
leaves in the expression tree. The qualifier of the call, if any, is not
considered to be an argument.

Example: the call `f(2, 3 + 4, g(4 + 5))` has sub expression(s)
`2` at index 0; `3`, `4`, and `3 + 4` at index 1; and `g(4 + 5)`
at index 2, respectively.

method.name: getTarget
method.comments:
Gets the target of the call, as best as makes sense for this kind of call.
The precise meaning depends on the kind of call it is:
- For a call to a function, it's the function being called.
- For a C++ method call, it's the statically resolved method.
- For an Objective C message expression, it's the statically resolved
method, and it might not exist.
- For a variable call, it never exists.

predicate.name: hasQualifier
predicate.comments:
Holds if this call has a qualifier.

For example, `ptr->f()` has a qualifier, whereas plain `f()` does not.

predicate.name: passesByReference
predicate.body:
variableAddressEscapesTree(va, this.getArgument(i).getFullyConverted())
    or
    variableAddressEscapesTree(va, this.getQualifier().getFullyConverted()) and
    i = -1
predicate.comments:
Holds if this call passes the variable accessed by `va` by
reference as the `i`th argument. The qualifier of a call to a member
function is `i = -1`.

A variable is passed by reference if the `i`th parameter of the function
receives an address that points within the object denoted by `va`. For a
variable named `x`, passing by reference includes both explicit pointers
(`&x`) and implicit conversion to a C++ reference (`x`), but it also
includes deeper expressions such as `&x[0] + length` or `&*&*&x`.

When `Field`s are involved, an argument `i` may pass more than one
variable by reference simultaneously. For example, the call `f(&x.m1.m2)`
counts as passing both `x`, `m1` and `m2` to argument 0 of `f`.

This predicate holds for variables passed by reference even if they are
passed as references to `const` and thus cannot be changed through that
reference. See `passesByNonConstReference` for a predicate that only holds
for variables passed by reference to non-const.

predicate.name: passesByReferenceNonConst
predicate.body:
variableAddressEscapesTreeNonConst(va, this.getArgument(i).getFullyConverted())
    or
    variableAddressEscapesTreeNonConst(va, this.getQualifier().getFullyConverted()) and
    i = -1
predicate.comments:
Holds if this call passes the variable accessed by `va` by
reference to non-const data as the `i`th argument. The qualifier of a
call to a member function is `i = -1`.

A variable is passed by reference if the `i`th parameter of the function
receives an address that points within the object denoted by `va`. For a
variable named `x`, passing by reference includes both explicit pointers
(`&x`) and implicit conversion to a C++ reference (`x`), but it also
includes deeper expressions such as `&x[0] + length` or `&*&*&x`.

When `Field`s are involved, an argument `i` may pass more than one
variable by reference simultaneously. For example, the call `f(&x.m1.m2)`
counts as passing both `x`, `m1` and `m2` to argument 0 of `f`.

This predicate only holds for variables passed by reference to non-const
data and thus can be changed through that reference. See
`passesByReference` for a predicate that also holds for variables passed
by reference to const.

predicate.name: mayBeFromImplicitlyDeclaredFunction
predicate.comments: Holds if this expression could be the return value of an implicitly declared function.

class.comments:
A C/C++ function call where the name of the target function is known at compile-time.

This includes various kinds of call:
1. Calls such as `f(x)` where `f` is the name of a function.
2. Calls such as `ptr->f()` where `f` is the name of a (possibly virtual) member function.
3. Constructor calls for stack-allocated objects.
4. Implicit and explicit calls to user-defined operators.
5. Base class initializers in constructors.

method.name: getAnExplicitTemplateArgument
method.comments: Gets an explicit template argument for this call.

method.name: getAnExplicitTemplateArgumentKind
method.comments: Gets an explicit template argument value for this call.

method.name: getATemplateArgument
method.comments: Gets a template argument for this call.

method.name: getATemplateArgumentKind
method.comments: Gets a template argument value for this call.

method.name: getExplicitTemplateArgument
method.comments: Gets the nth explicit template argument for this call.

method.name: getExplicitTemplateArgumentKind
method.comments: Gets the nth explicit template argument value for this call.

method.name: getNumberOfExplicitTemplateArguments
method.comments: Gets the number of explicit template arguments for this call.

method.name: getNumberOfTemplateArguments
method.comments: Gets the number of template arguments for this call.

method.name: getTemplateArgument
method.comments: Gets the nth template argument for this call (indexed from 0).

method.name: getTemplateArgumentKind
method.comments: Gets the nth template argument value for this call (indexed from 0).

method.name: getTargetType
method.comments:
Gets the `RoutineType` of the call target as visible at the call site.  For
constructor calls, this predicate instead gets the `Class` of the constructor
being called.

method.name: getExpectedReturnType
method.comments:
Gets the expected return type of the function called by this call.

In most cases, the expected return type will be the return type of the function being called.
It is only different when the function being called is ambiguously declared, at which point
the expected return type is the return type of the (unambiguous) function declaration that was
visible at the call site.

method.name: getExpectedParameterType
method.comments:
Gets the expected type of the nth parameter of the function called by this call.

In most cases, the expected parameter types match the parameter types of the function being called.
They are only different when the function being called is ambiguously declared, at which point
the expected parameter types are the parameter types of the (unambiguous) function declaration that
was visible at the call site.

method.name: getTarget
method.comments:
Gets the function called by this call.

In the case of virtual function calls, the result is the most-specific function in the override tree
such that the target at runtime will be one of `result.getAnOverridingFunction*()`. The most-specific
function is determined by the compiler based on the compile time type of the object the function is a
member of.

method.name: getType
method.comments: Gets the type of this expression, that is, the return type of the function being called.

method.name: toString
method.comments: Gets a textual representation of this function call.

predicate.name: hasImplicitTemplateArguments
predicate.comments: Holds if any template arguments for this call are implicit / deduced.

predicate.name: hasTemplateArgumentList
predicate.comments: Holds if a template argument list was provided for this call.

predicate.name: isVirtual
predicate.comments:
Holds if this is a call to a virtual function.

Note that this holds even in cases where a sufficiently clever compiler could perform static dispatch.

predicate.name: isOnlyFoundByADL
predicate.comments:
Holds if the target of this function call was found by argument-dependent lookup and wouldn't have been
found by any other means.

predicate.name: mayBeImpure
predicate.body:
this.getChild(_).mayBeImpure() or
    this.getTarget().mayHaveSideEffects() or
    this.isVirtual() or
    this.getTarget().getAnAttribute().getName() = "weak"

predicate.name: mayBeGloballyImpure
predicate.body:
this.getChild(_).mayBeGloballyImpure() or
    this.getTarget().mayHaveSideEffects() or
    this.isVirtual() or
