    this.getTarget().getAnAttribute().getName() = "weak"

class.comments: A _user-defined_ unary `operator*` function.

method.name: getExpr
method.comments: Gets the expression this operator * applies to.

predicate.name: mayBeImpure
predicate.body:
FunctionCall.super.mayBeImpure() and
    (
      this.getExpr().mayBeImpure()
      or
      not exists(Class declaring |
        this.getTarget().getDeclaringType().isConstructedFrom*(declaring)
      |
        declaring.getNamespace() instanceof StdNamespace
      )
    )

predicate.name: mayBeGloballyImpure
predicate.body:
FunctionCall.super.mayBeGloballyImpure() and
    (
      this.getExpr().mayBeGloballyImpure()
      or
      not exists(Class declaring |
        this.getTarget().getDeclaringType().isConstructedFrom*(declaring)
      |
        declaring.getNamespace() instanceof StdNamespace
      )
    )

method.name: getArrayBase
method.body: if exists(this.getQualifier()) then result = this.getQualifier() else result = this.getChild(0)
method.comments: Gets the expression being subscripted.

method.name: getArrayOffset
method.body: if exists(this.getQualifier()) then result = this.getChild(0) else result = this.getChild(1)
method.comments: Gets the expression giving the index.

method.name: getExpr
method.comments: Gets the expression which yields the function pointer to call.

method.name: getAnArgument
method.body: exists(int i | result = this.getChild(i) and i >= 1)

method.name: getArgument
method.body: result = this.getChild(index + 1) and index in [0 .. this.getNumChild() - 2]

method.name: getVariable
method.comments: Gets the variable which yields the function pointer to call.

method.name: getTarget
method.comments: Gets the constructor being called.

method.name: getTarget
method.comments: Gets the destructor being called.

method.name: getQualifier
method.comments: Gets the expression for the object whose destructor would be called.

class.comments:
An initialization of a base class or member variable performed as part
of a constructor's explicit initializer list or implicit actions.

This is a QL root class for representing various types of constructor
initializations.

A call to a constructor of a base class as part of a constructor's
initializer list or compiler-generated actions.

method.name: getTarget
method.comments: Gets the field being initialized.

method.name: getExpr
method.comments:
Gets the expression to which the field is initialized.

This is typically either a Literal or a FunctionCall to a
constructor, but more complex expressions can also occur.

class.comments:
A call to a destructor of a base class or field as part of a destructor's
compiler-generated actions.

A call to a destructor of a base class as part of a destructor's
compiler-generated actions.

method.name: getTarget
method.comments: Gets the field being destructed.

method.name: getExpr
method.comments: Gets the compiler-generated call to the variable's destructor.

class.comments:
A C/C++ cast expression or similar unary expression that doesn't affect the logical value of its operand.

Instances of this class are not present in the main AST which is navigated by parent/child links. Instead,
instances of this class are attached to nodes in the main AST via special conversion links.

method.name: getExpr
method.comments: Gets the expression being converted.

predicate.name: isImplicit
predicate.comments: Holds if this conversion is an implicit conversion.

class.comments:
A C/C++ cast expression.

To get the type which the expression is being cast to, use `Cast::getType()`.

There are two groups of subtypes of `Cast`. The first group differentiates
between the different cast syntax forms, e.g. `CStyleCast`, `StaticCast`,
etc. The second group differentiates between the semantic operation being
performed by the cast, e.g. `IntegralConversion`, `PointerBaseClassConversion`,
etc.
The two groups are largely orthogonal to one another. For example, a
cast that is syntactically as `CStyleCast` may also be an `IntegralConversion`,
a `PointerBaseClassConversion`, or some other semantic conversion. Similarly,
a `PointerDerivedClassConversion` may also be a `CStyleCast` or a `StaticCast`.

This is a root QL class representing the different casts.  For
specific examples, consult the documentation for any of QL classes mentioned above.

method.name: getSemanticConversionString
method.comments:
Gets a string describing the semantic conversion operation being performed by
this cast.

class.comments:
A C++ `static_cast` expression.

Please see https://en.cppreference.com/w/cpp/language/static_cast for
more information.
```
struct T: S {};
struct S *s = get_S();
struct T *t = static_cast<struct T *>(s); // downcast
```

A C++ `const_cast` expression.

Please see https://en.cppreference.com/w/cpp/language/const_cast for
more information.
```
const struct S *s = get_S();
struct S *t = const_cast<struct S *>(s);
```

A C++ `reinterpret_cast` expression.

Please see https://en.cppreference.com/w/cpp/language/reinterpret_cast for
more information.
```
struct S *s = get_S();
std::uintptr_t p = reinterpret_cast<std::uintptr_t>(s);
```

A conversion from one arithmetic or `enum` type to another.

The conversion is either implicit or underlies a particular cast.
Please see `CStyleCast`, `StaticCast`, `ConstCast`
or `ReinterpretCast` for more information.

A conversion from one integral or enum type to another.

The conversion is either implicit or underlies a particular cast.
Please see `CStyleCast`, `StaticCast`,  `ConstCast`
or `ReinterpretCast` for more information.

A conversion from one floating point type.

The conversion is either implicit or underlies a particular cast.
Please see `CStyleCast`, `StaticCast`, `ConstCast`
or `ReinterpretCast` for more information.

predicate.name: FloatingPointConversion
predicate.body:
this.getUnspecifiedType() instanceof FloatingPointType and
    this.getExpr().getUnspecifiedType() instanceof FloatingPointType

class.comments:
A conversion from a floating point type to an integral or enum type.

The conversion is either implicit or underlies a particular cast.
Please see `CStyleCast`, `StaticCast`, `ConstCast`
or `ReinterpretCast` for more information.

A conversion from an integral or enum type to a floating point type.

The conversion is either implicit or underlies a particular cast.
Please see `CStyleCast`, `StaticCast`, `ConstCast`
or `ReinterpretCast` for more information.

A conversion from one pointer type to another.

The conversion is either implicit or underlies a particular cast.
Please see `CStyleCast`, `StaticCast`,  `ConstCast`
or `ReinterpretCast` for more information.

The conversion does
not modify the value of the pointer. For pointer conversions involving
casts between base and derived classes, please see see `BaseClassConversion` or
`DerivedClassConversion`.

A conversion from one pointer-to-member type to another.

The conversion is either implicit or underlies a particular cast.
Please see `CStyleCast`, `StaticCast`, `ConstCast`
or `ReinterpretCast` for more information.

The conversion does not modify the value of the pointer-to-member.
For pointer-to-member conversions involving casts between base and
derived classes, please see `PointerToMemberBaseClassConversion`
or `PointerToMemberDerivedClassConversion`.

predicate.name: PointerToMemberConversion

class.comments:
A conversion from a pointer type to an integral or enum type.

The conversion is either implicit or underlies a particular cast.
Please see `CStyleCast`, `StaticCast`, `ConstCast`
or `ReinterpretCast` for more information.

A conversion from an integral or enum type to a pointer type.

The conversion is either implicit or underlies a particular cast.
Please see `CStyleCast`, `StaticCast`, `ConstCast`
or `ReinterpretCast` for more information.

A conversion to `bool`. Returns `false` if the source value is zero,
`false`, or `nullptr`. Returns `true` otherwise.

The conversion is either implicit or underlies a particular cast.
Please see `CStyleCast`, `StaticCast`, `ConstCast`
or `ReinterpretCast` for more information.

A conversion to `void`.

The conversion is either implicit or underlies a particular cast.
Please see `CStyleCast`, `StaticCast`, `ConstCast`
or `ReinterpretCast` for more information.

A conversion between two pointers or _glvalue_s related by inheritance.

The base class will always be either a direct base class of the derived class,
or a virtual base class of the derived class. A conversion to an indirect
non-virtual base class will be represented as a sequence of conversions to
direct base classes.

The conversion is either implicit or underlies a particular cast.
Please see `CStyleCast`, `StaticCast`, `ConstCast`
or `ReinterpretCast` for more information.

method.name: getDerivation
method.comments:
Gets the `ClassDerivation` for the inheritance relationship between
the base and derived classes. This predicate does not hold if the
conversion is to an indirect virtual base class.

method.name: getBaseClass
method.comments:
Gets the base class of the conversion. This will be either a direct
base class of the derived class, or a virtual base class of the
derived class.

method.name: getDerivedClass
method.comments: Gets the derived class of the conversion.

class.comments:
A conversion from a pointer or _glvalue_ of a derived class to a pointer or
_glvalue_ of a direct or virtual base class.

The conversion is either implicit or underlies a particular cast.
Please see `CStyleCast`, `StaticCast`, `ConstCast`
or `ReinterpretCast` for more information.

predicate.name: isVirtual
predicate.comments: Holds if this conversion is to a virtual base class.

class.comments:
A conversion from a pointer or _glvalue_ to a base class to a pointer or _glvalue_
to a direct derived class.

The conversion is either implicit or underlies a particular cast.
Please see `CStyleCast`, `StaticCast`, `ConstCast`
or `ReinterpretCast` for more information.

A conversion from a pointer-to-member of a derived class to a pointer-to-member
of an immediate base class.

The conversion is either implicit or underlies a particular cast.
Please see `CStyleCast`, `StaticCast`, `ConstCast`
or `ReinterpretCast` for more information.

A conversion from a pointer-to-member of a base class to a pointer-to-member
of an immediate derived class.

The conversion is either implicit or underlies a particular cast.
Please see `CStyleCast`, `StaticCast`, `ConstCast`
or `ReinterpretCast` for more information.

A conversion of a _glvalue_ from one type to another. The conversion does not
modify the address of the _glvalue_. For _glvalue_ conversions involving base and
derived classes, see `BaseClassConversion` and `DerivedClassConversion`.

The conversion is either implicit or underlies a particular cast.
Please see `CStyleCast`, `StaticCast`, `ConstCast`
or `ReinterpretCast` for more information.

A C++ `dynamic_cast` expression.

Please see https://en.cppreference.com/w/cpp/language/dynamic_cast for
more information.
```
struct T: S {};
struct S *s = get_S();
struct T *t = dynamic_cast<struct T *>(s); // downcast
```

method.name: toString
getTypeOperand
method.comments: Gets the contained type.

method.name: getResultType
method.comments: Gets the type that is returned by this typeid expression.

method.name: getExpr
method.comments:
Gets the contained expression, if any (if this typeid contains
a type rather than an expression, there is no result).

class.comments:
A C++11 `sizeof...` expression which determines the size of a template
parameter pack.

This expression only appears in templates themselves - in any actual
instantiations, "sizeof...(x)" will be replaced by its integer value.
```
template < typename... T >
int count ( T &&... t ) { return sizeof... ( t ); }
```

A C++11 `sizeof...` expression which determines the size of a template
parameter pack and whose operand is an expression.

This expression only appears in templates themselves - in any actual
instantiations, "sizeof...(x)" will be replaced by its integer value.
```
template < typename... T >
int count ( T &&... t ) { return sizeof... ( t ); }
```

method.name: getExprOperand
method.comments: Gets the contained expression.

class.comments:
A C++11 `sizeof...` expression which determines the size of a template
parameter pack and whose operand is an type name or a template template
parameter.

This expression only appears in templates themselves - in any actual
instantiations, "sizeof...(x)" will be replaced by its integer value.
```
template < typename... T >
int count ( T &&... t ) { return sizeof... ( T ); }
```

method.name: getTypeOperand
method.comments: Gets the contained type.

class.comments: A C/C++ sizeof expression.

A C/C++ sizeof expression whose operand is an expression.
```
if (sizeof(a) == sizeof(b)) { c = (b)a; }
```

method.name: getExprOperand
method.comments: Gets the contained expression.

class.comments:
A C/C++ sizeof expression whose operand is a type name.
```
int szlong = sizeof(int) == sizeof(long)? 4 : 8;
```

method.name: getTypeOperand
method.comments: Gets the contained type.

class.comments: A C++11 `alignof` expression.

A C++11 `alignof` expression whose operand is an expression.
```
int addrMask = ~(alignof(expr) - 1);
```

method.name: getExprOperand
method.comments: Gets the contained expression.

class.comments:
A C++11 `alignof` expression whose operand is a type name.
```
bool proper_alignment = (alignof(T) == alignof(T[0]);
```

method.name: getTypeOperand
method.comments: Gets the contained type.

class.comments:
A C++ `__datasizeof` expression (used by some implementations
of the `<type_traits>` header).

The `__datasizeof` expression behaves identically to `sizeof` except
that the result ignores tail padding.
A C++ `__datasizeof` expression whose operand is an expression.

method.name: getExprOperand
method.comments: Gets the contained expression.

class.comments: A C++ `__datasizeof` expression whose operand is a type name.

method.name: getTypeOperand
method.comments: Gets the contained type.

class.comments:
A C/C++ array to pointer conversion.

The conversion is either implicit or underlies a particular cast.
Please see `CStyleCast`, `StaticCast`,  `ConstCast`
or `ReinterpretCast` for more information.

method.name: toString
method.comments: Gets a textual representation of this conversion.

method.name: toString
method.comments: Gets a textual representation of this conversion.

class.comments:
A C/C++ comparison operation, that is, either an equality operation or a relational operation.

This is a QL base class for all comparisons.
A C/C++ equality operation, that is, either "==" or "!=".

A C/C++ equal expression.
```
bool c = (a == b);
```

A C/C++ not equal expression.
```
bool c = (a != b);
```
A C/C++ relational operation, that is, one of `<=`, `<`, `>`, or `>=`.

method.name: getGreaterOperand
method.comments:
Gets the operand on the "greater" (or "greater-or-equal") side
of this relational expression, that is, the side that is larger
if the overall expression evaluates to `true`; for example on
`x <= 20` this is the `20`, and on `y > 0` it is `y`.

method.name: getLesserOperand
method.comments:
Gets the operand on the "lesser" (or "lesser-or-equal") side
of this relational expression, that is, the side that is smaller
if the overall expression evaluates to `true`; for example on
`x <= 20` this is `x`, and on `y > 0` it is the `0`.

class.comments:
A C/C++ greater than expression.
```
bool c = (a > b);
```

A C/C++ less than expression.
```
bool c = (a < b);
```

A C/C++ greater than or equal expression.
```
bool c = (a >= b);
```

A C/C++ less than or equal expression.
```
bool c = (a <= b);
```

A C/C++ expression.

This is the root QL class for all expressions.

method.name: getChild
method.comments: Gets the nth child of this expression.

method.name: getNumChild
method.comments: Gets the number of direct children of this expression.

method.name: getEnclosingFunction
method.comments: Gets the enclosing function of this expression, if any.

method.name: getEnclosingBlock
method.comments: Gets the nearest enclosing set of curly braces around this expression in the source, if any.

method.name: getEnclosingStmt
method.body:
result = this.getParent().(Expr).getEnclosingStmt()
    or
    result = this.getParent()
    or
    exists(Expr other | result = other.getEnclosingStmt() and other.getConversion() = this)
    or
    exists(DeclStmt d, LocalVariable v |
      d.getADeclaration() = v and v.getInitializer().getExpr() = this and result = d
    )
    or
    exists(ConditionDeclExpr cde, LocalVariable v |
      cde.getVariable() = v and
      v.getInitializer().getExpr() = this and
      result = cde.getEnclosingStmt()
    )

method.name: getEnclosingVariable
method.comments: Gets the enclosing variable of this expression, if any.

method.name: getEnclosingDeclaration
method.comments: Gets the enclosing variable or function of this expression.

method.name: getAChild
method.comments: Gets a child of this expression.

method.name: getParent
method.comments: Gets the parent of this expression, if any.

method.name: getImplicitDestructorCall
method.body:
exists(Expr e |
      e = this.(TemporaryObjectExpr).getExpr() and
      synthetic_destructor_call(e, max(int i | synthetic_destructor_call(e, i, _)) - n, result)
    )
    or
    not this = any(TemporaryObjectExpr temp).getExpr() and
    synthetic_destructor_call(this, max(int i | synthetic_destructor_call(this, i, _)) - n, result)
method.comments:
Gets the `n`th compiler-generated destructor call that is performed after this expression, in
order of destruction.

method.name: getAnImplicitDestructorCall
method.comments: Gets a compiler-generated destructor call that is performed after this expression.

method.name: getLocation
method.body:
result = this.getExprLocationOverride()
    or
    not exists(this.getExprLocationOverride()) and
    result = this.getDbLocation()
method.comments: Gets the location of this expression.

method.name: getType
method.comments:
Gets the type of this expression.

As the type of an expression can sometimes be a TypedefType, calling getUnderlyingType()
is often more useful than calling this predicate.

method.name: getUnderlyingType
method.comments:
Gets the type of this expression after typedefs have been resolved.

In most cases, this predicate will be the same as getType().  It will
only differ when the result of getType() is a TypedefType, in which
case this predicate will (possibly recursively) resolve the typedef.

method.name: getUnspecifiedType
method.comments:
Gets the type of this expression after specifiers have been deeply
stripped and typedefs have been resolved.

method.name: toString
method.comments: Gets a textual representation of this expression.

method.name: getValue
method.body: exists(@value v | values(v, result) and valuebind(v, underlyingElement(this)))
method.comments: Gets the value of this expression, if it is a constant.

method.name: getValueText
method.comments: Gets the source text for the value of this expression, if it is a constant.

method.name: getValueCategoryString
method.body:
this.isLValueCategory() and
    result = "lvalue"
    or
    this.isXValueCategory() and
    result = "xvalue"
    or
    (
      this.isPRValueCategory() and
      if this.hasLValueToRValueConversion() then result = "prvalue(load)" else result = "prvalue"
    )
method.comments:
Gets a string representation of the value category of the expression.
This is intended only for debugging. The possible values are:

- "lvalue"
- "xvalue"
- "prvalue"
- "prvalue(load)"

The "prvalue(load)" string is used when the expression is a _prvalue_, but
`hasLValueToRvalueConversion()` holds.

method.name: getParentWithConversions
method.comments:
Gets the parent of this expression, if any, in an alternative syntax tree
that has `Conversion`s as part of the tree.

method.name: getPrecedence
method.comments:
Gets the precedence of the main operator of this expression;
higher precedence binds tighter.

method.name: getConversion
method.comments: Gets the conversion associated with this expression, if any.

method.name: getConversionString
method.body:
result = this.getConversion().toString() and this.hasConversion()
    or
    result = "" and not this.hasConversion()
method.comments:
Gets a string describing the conversion associated with this expression,
or "" if there is none.

method.name: getFullyConverted
method.body:
hasNoConversions(this) and
    result = this
    or
    result = this.getConversion().getFullyConverted()
method.comments: Gets the fully converted form of this expression, including all type casts and other conversions.

method.name: getExplicitlyConverted
method.comments:
Gets this expression with all of its explicit casts, but none of its
implicit casts. More precisely this takes conversions up to the last
explicit cast (there may be implicit conversions along the way), but does
not include conversions after the last explicit cast.

C++ example: `C c = (B)d` might have three casts: (1) an implicit cast
from A to some D, (2) an explicit cast from D to B, and (3) an implicit
cast from B to C. Only (1) and (2) would be included.

method.name: getImplicitlyConverted
method.comments:
Gets this expression with all of its initial implicit casts, but none of
its explicit casts. More precisely, this takes all implicit conversions
up to (but not including) the first explicit cast (if any).

method.name: getUnconverted
method.body:
not this instanceof Conversion and
    result = this
    or
    result = this.(Conversion).getExpr().getUnconverted()
method.comments:
Gets the unique non-`Conversion` expression `e` for which
`this = e.getConversion*()`.

For example, if called on the expression `(int)(char)x`, this predicate
gets the expression `x`.

method.name: getActualType
method.comments:
Gets the type of this expression, after any implicit conversions and explicit casts, and after resolving typedefs.

As an example, consider the AST fragment `(i64)(void*)0` in the context of `typedef long long i64;`. The fragment
contains three expressions: two CStyleCasts and one literal Zero. For all three expressions, the result of this
predicate will be `long long`.

method.name: getControlFlowScope
method.comments: Gets the function containing this control-flow node.

predicate.name: hasChild
predicate.comments: Holds if e is the nth child of this expression.

predicate.name: isCompilerGenerated
predicate.comments: Holds if this is an auxiliary expression generated by the compiler.

predicate.name: isConstant
predicate.body:
valuebind(_, underlyingElement(this))
    or
    addressConstantExpression(this)
    or
    constantTemplateLiteral(this)

predicate.name: isPure
predicate.comments:
Holds if this expression is side-effect free (conservative
approximation). This predicate cannot be overridden;
override mayBeImpure() instead.

Note that this predicate does not strictly correspond with
the usual definition of a 'pure' function because reading
from global state is permitted, just not writing / output.

predicate.name: mayBeImpure
predicate.comments:
Holds if it is possible that the expression may be impure. If we are not
sure, then it holds.

predicate.name: mayBeGloballyImpure
predicate.comments:
Holds if it is possible that the expression may be impure. If we are not
sure, then it holds. Unlike `mayBeImpure()`, this predicate does not
consider modifications to temporary local variables to be impure. If you
call a function in which nothing may be globally impure then the function
as a whole will have no side-effects, even if it mutates its own fresh
stack variables.

predicate.name: isLValueCategory
predicate.comments:
Holds if this expression is an _lvalue_. An _lvalue_ is an expression that
represents a location, rather than a value.
See [basic.lval] for more about lvalues.

predicate.name: isXValueCategory
predicate.comments:
Holds if this expression is an _xvalue_. An _xvalue_ is a location whose
lifetime is about to end (e.g. an _rvalue_ reference returned from a function
call).
See [basic.lval] for more about xvalues.

predicate.name: isPRValueCategory
predicate.comments:
Holds if this expression is a _prvalue_. A _prvalue_ is an expression that
represents a value, rather than a location.
See [basic.lval] for more about prvalues.

predicate.name: isGLValueCategory
predicate.comments:
Holds if this expression is a _glvalue_. A _glvalue_ is either an _lvalue_ or an
_xvalue_.

predicate.name: isRValueCategory
predicate.comments:
Holds if this expression is an _rvalue_. An _rvalue_ is either a _prvalue_ or an
_xvalue_.

predicate.name: isUnevaluated
predicate.body:
exists(Element e | e = this.getParentWithConversions+() |
      e instanceof SizeofOperator
      or
      exists(Expr e2 |
        e.(TypeidOperator).getExpr() = e2 and
        (
          not e2.getFullyConverted().getUnspecifiedType().(Class).isPolymorphic() or
          not e2.isGLValueCategory()
        )
      )
      or
      e instanceof NoExceptExpr
      or
      e instanceof AlignofOperator
      or
      e instanceof DatasizeofOperator
    )
    or
    exists(Decltype d | d.getExpr() = this.getParentWithConversions*())
    or
    exists(TypeofExprType t | t.getExpr() = this.getParentWithConversions*())
    or
    exists(ConstexprIfStmt constIf |
      constIf.getControllingExpr() = this.getParentWithConversions*()
    )
predicate.comments:
Holds if this expression will not be evaluated because of its context,
such as an expression inside a sizeof.

predicate.name: hasLValueToRValueConversion
predicate.comments:
Holds if this expression has undergone an _lvalue_-to-_rvalue_ conversion to
extract its value.
for example:
```
y = x;
```
The `VariableAccess` for `x` is a _prvalue_, and `hasLValueToRValueConversion()`
holds because the value of `x` was loaded from the location of `x`.
The `VariableAccess` for `y` is an _lvalue_, and `hasLValueToRValueConversion()`
does not hold because the value of `y` was not extracted.

See [conv.lval] for more about the _lvalue_-to-_rvalue_ conversion

predicate.name: isLValue
predicate.comments:
Holds if this expression is an _lvalue_, in the sense of having an address.

Being an _lvalue_ is best approximated as having an address.
This is a strict superset of modifiable _lvalue_s, which are best approximated by things which could be on the left-hand side of an assignment.
This is also a strict superset of expressions which provide an _lvalue_, which is best approximated by things whose address is important.

See [basic.lval] in the C++ language specification.
In C++03, every expression is either an _lvalue_ or an _rvalue_.
In C++11, every expression is exactly one of an _lvalue_, an _xvalue_, or a _prvalue_ (with _rvalue_s being the union of _xvalue_s and _prvalue_s).
Using the C++11 terminology, this predicate selects expressions whose value category is _lvalue_.

predicate.name: hasConversion
predicate.comments:
Holds if this expression has a conversion.

Type casts and parameterized expressions are not part of the main
expression tree structure but attached on the nodes they convert,
for example:
```
2 + (int)(bool)1
```
has the main tree:
```
2 + 1
```
and 1 has a bool conversion, while the bool conversion itself has
an int conversion.

predicate.name: hasImplicitConversion
predicate.comments:
Holds if this expression has an implicit conversion.

For example in `char *str = 0`, the `0` has an implicit conversion to type `char *`.

predicate.name: hasExplicitConversion
predicate.comments:
Holds if this expression has an explicit conversion.

For example in `(MyClass *)ptr`, the `ptr` has an explicit
conversion to type `MyClass *`.

predicate.name: isParenthesised
predicate.comments: Holds if this expression is parenthesised.

class.comments:
A C/C++ operation.

This is the QL root class for all operations.

method.name: getOperator
method.comments: Gets the operator of this operation.

method.name: getAnOperand
method.comments: Gets an operand of this operation.

class.comments: A C/C++ unary operation.

method.name: getOperand
method.comments: Gets the operand of this unary operation.

class.comments: A C/C++ binary operation.

method.name: getLeftOperand
method.comments: Gets the left operand of this binary operation.

method.name: getRightOperand
method.comments: Gets the right operand of this binary operation.

predicate.name: hasOperands
predicate.body:
exists(int i | i in [0, 1] |
      this.hasChild(e1, i) and
      this.hasChild(e2, 1 - i)
    )
predicate.comments:
Holds if `e1` and `e2` (in either order) are the two operands of this
binary operation.

class.comments:
A C/C++ parenthesis expression.

It is typically used to raise the syntactic precedence of the subexpression that
it contains.  For example:
```
int d = a & ( b | c );
```

A node representing a C11 `_Generic` selection expression.

For example:
```
_Generic(e, int: "int", default: "unknown")
```

method.name: getControllingExpr
method.comments:
Gets the controlling expression of the generic selection.

For example, for
```
_Generic(e, int: "a", default: "b")
```
the result is `e`.

method.name: getAssociationType
method.body:
n in [0 .. associationCount - 1] and
    result = this.getChild(n * 2 + 1).(TypeName).getType()
method.comments:
Gets the type of the `n`th element in the association list of the generic selection.

For example, for
```
_Generic(e, int: "a", default: "b")
```
the type of the 0th element is `int`. In the case of the default element the
type will an instance of `VoidType`.

method.name: getAnAssociationType
method.comments: Gets the type of an element in the association list of the generic selection.

method.name: getAssociationExpr
method.body:
n in [0 .. associationCount - 1] and
    result = this.getChild(n * 2 + 2)
method.comments:
Gets the expression of the `n`th element in the association list of
the generic selection.

For example, for
```
_Generic(e, int: "a", default: "b")
```
the expression for 0th element is `"a"`, and the expression for the
1st element is `"b"`. For the selected expression, this predicate
will yield a `ReuseExpr`, such that
```
this.getAssociationExpr(n).(ReuseExpr).getReusedExpr() = this.getExpr()
```

method.name: getAnAssociationExpr
method.comments: Gets the expression of an element in the association list of the generic selection.

predicate.name: isDefaultAssociation
predicate.comments:
Holds if the `n`th element of the association list of the generic selection is the
default element.

For example, for
```
_Generic(e, int: "a", default: "b")
```
this holds for 1.

predicate.name: isSelectedAssociation
predicate.comments:
Holds if the `n`th element of the association list of the generic selection is the
one whose expression was selected.

For example, with `e` of type `int` and
```
_Generic(e, int: "a", default: "b")
```
this holds for 0.

class.comments:
A C/C++ expression that could not be resolved, or that can no longer be
represented due to a database upgrade or downgrade.

If the expression could not be resolved, it has type `ErroneousType`. In the
case of a database upgrade or downgrade, the original type from before the
upgrade or downgrade is kept if that type can be represented.

A Microsoft C/C++ __assume expression.

Unlike `assert`, `__assume` is evaluated at compile time and
is treated as a hint to the optimizer
```
__assume(ptr < end_buf);
```

method.name: getOperand
method.comments: Gets the operand of the `__assume` expressions.

class.comments:
A C/C++ comma expression.

For example:
```
int c = compute1(), compute2(), resulting_value;
```

method.name: getLeftOperand
method.comments: Gets the left operand, which is the one whose value is discarded.

method.name: getRightOperand
method.comments:
Gets the right operand, which is the one whose value is equal to the value
of the comma expression itself.

class.comments:
A C/C++ address-of expression.
```
int *ptr = &var;
```

method.name: getAddressable
method.body:
result = this.getOperand().(Access).getTarget()
    or
    // this handles the case where we are taking the address of a reference variable
    result = this.getOperand().(ReferenceDereferenceExpr).getChild(0).(Access).getTarget()
method.comments: Gets the function or variable whose address is taken.

class.comments:
An implicit conversion from type `T` to type `T &`.

This typically occurs when an expression of type `T` is used to initialize a variable or parameter of
type `T &`, and is to reference types what `AddressOfExpr` is to pointer types, though this class is
considered to be a conversion rather than an operation, and as such doesn't occur in the main AST.
```
int &var_ref = var;
```

An instance of the built-in unary `operator *` applied to a type.

For user-defined overloads of `operator *`, see `OverloadedPointerDereferenceExpr`.
```
int var = *varptr;
```

An implicit conversion from type `T &` to type `T`.

This typically occurs when an variable of type `T &` is used in a context which expects type `T`, and
is to reference types what `PointerDereferenceExpr` is to pointer types - though this class is
considered to be a conversion rather than an operation, and as such doesn't occur in the main AST.
```
float &f_ref = get_ref();
float f = f_ref;
```
A C++ `new` or `new[]` expression.

method.name: getAllocator
method.comments: Gets the `operator new` or `operator new[]` that allocates storage.

method.name: getAlignmentArgument
method.comments: Gets the alignment argument passed to the allocation function, if any.

method.name: getAllocatorCall
method.comments:
Gets the call to a non-default `operator new` that allocates storage, if any.

As a rule of thumb, there will be an allocator call precisely when the type
being allocated has a custom `operator new`, or when an argument list appears
after the `new` keyword and before the name of the type being allocated.

In particular note that uses of placement-new and nothrow-new will have an
allocator call.

method.name: getDeallocator
method.comments:
Gets the `operator delete` that deallocates storage if the initialization
throws an exception, if any.

method.name: getAllocatedType
method.comments:
Gets the type that is being allocated.

For example, for `new int` the result is `int`.
For `new int[5]` the result is `int[5]`.

method.name: getPlacementPointer
method.comments:
Gets the pointer `p` if this expression is of the form `new(p) T...`.
Invocations of this form are non-allocating `new` expressions that may
call the constructor of `T` but will not allocate memory.

method.name: getInitializer
method.comments:
For `operator new`, this gets the call or expression that initializes the allocated object, if any.

As examples, for `new int(4)`, this will be `4`, and for `new std::vector(4)`, this will
be a call to the constructor `std::vector::vector(size_t)` with `4` as an argument.

For `operator new[]`, this gets the call or expression that initializes the first element of the
array, if any.

This will either be a call to the default constructor for the array's element type (as
in `new std::string[10]`), or a literal zero for arrays of scalars which are zero-initialized
due to extra parentheses (as in `new int[10]()`).

At runtime, the constructor will be called once for each element in the array, but the
constructor call only exists once in the AST.

predicate.name: hasAlignedAllocation
predicate.comments:
Holds if the allocation function is the version that expects an alignment
argument of type `std::align_val_t`.

predicate.name: hasSizedDeallocation
predicate.body:
exists(int form |
      expr_deallocator(underlyingElement(this), _, form) and
      form.bitAnd(1) != 0 // Bit zero is the "size" bit
    )
predicate.comments: Holds if the deallocation function expects a size argument.

predicate.name: hasAlignedDeallocation
predicate.body:
exists(int form |
      expr_deallocator(underlyingElement(this), _, form) and
      form.bitAnd(2) != 0 // Bit one is the "alignment" bit
    )
predicate.comments: Holds if the deallocation function expects an alignment argument.

predicate.name: isDestroyingDeleteDeallocation
predicate.body:
exists(int form |
      expr_deallocator(underlyingElement(this), _, form) and
      form.bitAnd(4) != 0 // Bit two is the "destroying delete" bit
    )
predicate.comments: Holds if the deallocation function is a destroying delete.

class.comments:
A C++ `new` (non-array) expression.
```
Foo *ptr = new Foo(3);
```

method.name: getAllocatedType
method.comments:
Gets the type that is being allocated.

For example, for `new int` the result is `int`.

method.name: getAllocatedType
method.comments:
Gets the type that is being allocated.

For example, for `new int[5]` the result is `int[5]`.

method.name: getAllocatedElementType
method.comments: Gets the element type of the array being allocated.

method.name: getExtent
method.comments:
Gets the extent of the non-constant array dimension, if any.

As examples, for `new char[n]` and `new char[n][10]`, this gives `n`, but for `new char[10]` this
gives nothing, as the 10 is considered part of the type.

method.name: getArraySize
method.comments:
Gets the number of elements in the array, if available.

For example, `new int[]{1,2,3}` has an array size of 3.

class.comments: A C++ `delete` or `delete[]` expression.

method.name: getDestructorCall
method.comments:
Gets the call to a destructor that occurs prior to the object's memory being deallocated, if any.

In the case of `delete[]` at runtime, the destructor will be called once for each element in the array, but the
destructor call only exists once in the AST.

method.name: getDestructor
method.comments: Gets the destructor to be called to destroy the object or array, if any.

method.name: getDeallocator
method.comments:
Gets the `operator delete` or `operator delete[]` that deallocates storage.
Does not hold if the type being destroyed has a virtual destructor. In that case, the
`operator delete` that will be called is determined at runtime based on the
dynamic type of the object.

method.name: getDeallocatorCall
method.comments:
Gets the call to a non-default `operator delete`/`delete[]` that deallocates storage, if any.

This will only be present when the type being deleted has a custom `operator delete` and
does not have a virtual destructor.

method.name: getExpr
method.comments: Gets the object or array being deleted.

method.name: getExprWithReuse

predicate.name: hasSizedDeallocation
predicate.body:
exists(int form |
      expr_deallocator(underlyingElement(this), _, form) and
      form.bitAnd(1) != 0 // Bit zero is the "size" bit
    )
predicate.comments: Holds if the deallocation function expects a size argument.

predicate.name: hasAlignedDeallocation
predicate.body:
exists(int form |
      expr_deallocator(underlyingElement(this), _, form) and
      form.bitAnd(2) != 0 // Bit one is the "alignment" bit
    )
predicate.comments: Holds if the deallocation function expects an alignment argument.

predicate.name: isDestroyingDeleteDeallocation
predicate.body:
exists(int form |
      expr_deallocator(underlyingElement(this), _, form) and
      form.bitAnd(4) != 0 // Bit two is the "destroying delete" bit
    )
predicate.comments: Holds if the deallocation function is a destroying delete.

class.comments:
A C++ `delete` (non-array) expression.
```
delete ptr;
```

method.name: getDeletedObjectType
method.comments: Gets the compile-time type of the object being deleted.

class.comments:
A C++ `delete[]` (array) expression.
```
delete[] arr;
```

method.name: getDeletedElementType
method.comments: Gets the element type of the array being deleted.

class.comments:
A compound statement enclosed in parentheses used as an expression (a GNU extension to C/C++).
In the example below, `b` is the return value from the compound statement.
```
int a = ({ int b = c + d; b; });
```

method.name: getStmt
method.comments: Gets the statement enclosed by this `StmtExpr`.

method.name: getResultExpr
method.comments:
Gets the result expression of the enclosed statement. For example,
`a+b` is the result expression in this example:

```
x = ({ dosomething(); a+b; });
```

class.comments: A C++ `this` expression.

A code block expression, for example:
```
^ int (int x, int y) {return x + y;}
```
Blocks are a language extension supported by Clang, and by Apple's
branch of GCC.

method.name: getFunction
method.comments: Gets the (anonymous) function associated with this code block expression.

class.comments:
A C++ `throw` expression.
```
throw Exc(2);
```

method.name: getExpr
method.comments:
Gets the expression that will be thrown, if any. There is no result if
`this` is a `ReThrowExpr`.

class.comments:
A C++ `throw` expression with no argument (which causes the current exception to be re-thrown).
```
throw;
```

A C++11 `noexcept` expression, returning `true` if its subexpression is guaranteed
not to `throw` exceptions.  For example:
```
if (noexcept(func_1() + func_2())) { }
```

method.name: getExpr
method.comments: Gets the expression inside this noexcept expression.

class.comments:
A C++17 fold expression. This will only appear in an uninstantiated template; any instantiations
of the template will instead contain the sequence of expressions given by expanding the fold.
```
template < typename... T >
auto sum ( T... t ) { return ( t + ... + 0 ); }
```

method.name: toString
getOperatorString
method.comments: Gets the binary operator used in this fold expression, as a string.

method.name: getPackExpr
method.body:
this.isUnaryFold() and
    result = this.getChild(0)
    or
    this.isBinaryFold() and
    if this.isRightFold() then result = this.getChild(0) else result = this.getChild(1)
method.comments: Gets the child expression containing the unexpanded parameter pack.

method.name: getInitExpr
method.body:
this.isBinaryFold() and
    if this.isRightFold() then result = this.getChild(1) else result = this.getChild(0)
method.comments: If this is a binary fold, gets the expression representing the initial value.

predicate.name: isLeftFold
predicate.comments: Holds if this is a left-fold expression.

predicate.name: isRightFold
predicate.comments: Holds if this is a right-fold expression.

predicate.name: isUnaryFold
predicate.comments: Holds if this is a unary fold expression.

predicate.name: isBinaryFold
predicate.comments: Holds if this is a binary fold expression.

class.comments:
A C++ three-way comparison operation, also known as the _spaceship
operation_.  This is specific to C++20 and later.
```
auto c = (a <=> b);
```

A C/C++ `co_await` expression.
```
co_await foo();
```

method.name: getAwaitReady
method.comments:
Gets the Boolean expression that is used to decide if the enclosing
coroutine should be suspended.

method.name: getAwaitResume
method.comments:
Gets the expression that represents the resume point if the enclosing
coroutine was suspended.

method.name: getAwaitSuspend
method.comments:
Gets the expression that is evaluated when the enclosing coroutine is
suspended.

class.comments:
A C/C++ `co_yield` expression.
```
co_yield 1;
```

method.name: getAwaitReady
method.comments:
Gets the Boolean expression that is used to decide if the enclosing
coroutine should be suspended.

method.name: getAwaitResume
method.comments:
Gets the expression that represents the resume point if the enclosing
coroutine was suspended.

method.name: getAwaitSuspend
method.comments:
Gets the expression that is evaluated when the enclosing coroutine is
suspended.

class.comments:
An expression representing the re-use of another expression.

In some specific cases an expression may be referred to outside its
original context. A re-use expression wraps any such reference. A
re-use expression can for example occur as the qualifier of an implicit
destructor called on a temporary object, where the original use of the
expression is in the definition of the temporary.

method.name: getReusedExpr
method.comments: Gets the expression that is being re-used.

method.name: getACapture
method.comments: Gets an implicitly or explicitly captured value of this lambda expression.

method.name: getCapture
method.comments: Gets the nth implicitly or explicitly captured value of this lambda expression.

method.name: getDefaultCaptureMode
method.comments:
Gets the default variable capture mode for the lambda expression.

Will be one of:
- "" if no default was specified, meaning that all captures must be explicit.
- "&" if capture-by-reference is the default for implicit captures.
- "=" if capture-by-value is the default for implicit captures.

method.name: getLambdaFunction
method.comments:
Gets the function which will be invoked when the resulting object is called.

Various components of the lambda expression can be obtained from components of this
function, such as:
- The number and type of parameters.
- Whether the mutable keyword was used (iff this function is not const).
- The return type.
- The statements comprising the lambda body.

method.name: getInitializer
method.comments:
Gets the initializer that initializes the captured variables in the closure, if any.
A lambda that does not capture any variables will not have an initializer.

predicate.name: returnTypeIsExplicit
predicate.comments: Holds if the return type (of the call operator of the resulting object) was explicitly specified.

predicate.name: hasParameterList
predicate.comments: Holds if the lambda has an explicitly specified parameter list, even when empty.

method.name: getLambdaExpression
method.comments: Gets the lambda expression of which this is the type.

method.name: getLambdaFunction
method.comments: Gets the compiler-generated operator() of this closure type.

method.name: getLocation
method.comments:
Gets the location of the declaration of this capture.

For explicit captures, this is a location within the "[...]" part of the lambda expression.

For implicit captures, this is the first location within the "{...}" part of the lambda
expression which accesses the captured variable.

method.name: getField
method.comments: Gets the field of the lambda expression's closure type which is used to store this capture.

method.name: getInitializer
method.comments:
Gets the expression which yields the final captured value.

In many cases, this will be an instance of VariableAccess.
If a this-pointer is being captured, this will be an instance of ThisExpr.
For by-value captures of non-primitive types, this will be a call to a copy constructor.

predicate.name: isImplicit
predicate.comments: Holds if this capture was made implicitly.

predicate.name: isCapturedByReference
predicate.comments:
Holds if the variable was captured by reference.

An identifier is captured by reference if:
- It is explicitly captured by reference.
- It is implicitly captured, and the lambda's default capture mode is by-reference.
- The identifier is "this". [Said behavior is dictated by the C++11 standard, but it
is actually "*this" being captured rather than "this".]

class.comments:
A C/C++ literal.

The is the QL root class for all literals.

method.name: toString
method.body:
result = this.getValue()
    or
    not exists(this.getValue()) and
    result = "Unknown literal"
method.comments: Gets a textual representation of this literal.

class.comments:
A label literal, that is, a use of the '&&' operator to take the address of a
label for use in a computed goto statement.  This is a non-standard C/C++ extension.

For example:
```
void *label_ptr = &&myLabel; // &&myLabel is a LabelLiteral
goto *label_ptr; // this is a ComputedGotoStmt
myLabel: // this is a LabelStmt
```

method.name: getLabel
method.comments: Gets the corresponding label statement.

class.comments: A character literal or a string literal.

method.name: getAHexEscapeSequence
method.comments: Gets a hex escape sequence that appears in the character or string literal (see [lex.ccon] in the C++ Standard).

method.name: getAnOctalEscapeSequence
method.comments: Gets an octal escape sequence that appears in the character or string literal (see [lex.ccon] in the C++ Standard).

method.name: getANonStandardEscapeSequence
method.comments:
Gets a non-standard escape sequence that appears in the character or string literal. This is one that has the
form of an escape sequence but is not one of the valid types of escape sequence in the C++ Standard.

method.name: getASimpleEscapeSequence
method.comments: Gets a simple escape sequence that appears in the char or string literal (see [lex.ccon] in the C++ Standard).

method.name: getAStandardEscapeSequence
method.body:
result = this.getASimpleEscapeSequence(occurrence, offset) or
    result = this.getAnOctalEscapeSequence(occurrence, offset) or
    result = this.getAHexEscapeSequence(occurrence, offset)
method.comments: Gets a standard escape sequence that appears in the char or string literal (see [lex.ccon] in the C++ Standard).

method.name: getOriginalLength
method.comments: Gets the length of the string literal (including null) before escape sequences added by the extractor.

predicate.name: TextLiteral
predicate.body:
this.getType() instanceof ArrayType
    or
    // Char literal
    this.getValueText().regexpMatch("(?s)\\s*L?'.*")

method.name: getCharacter
method.comments: Gets the character of this literal. For example `L'a'` has character `"a"`.

class.comments:
An octal literal. For example:
```
char esc = 033;
```
Octal literals must always start with the digit `0`.

A hexadecimal literal.
```
unsigned int32_t minus2 = 0xfffffffe;
```
A C/C++ aggregate literal.

method.name: toString
method.comments: Gets a textual representation of this aggregate literal.

class.comments:
A C/C++ aggregate literal that initializes a `class`, `struct`, or `union`.
For example:
```
S s = { arg1, arg2, { arg3, arg4 }, arg5 };
```

method.name: getAFieldExpr
method.comments:
Gets an expression within the aggregate literal that is used to initialize
field `field`, if present.

This predicate may have multiple results since a field can be initialized
multiple times in the same initializer.

method.name: getFieldExpr
method.body:
field = classType.getAField() and
    aggregate_field_init(underlyingElement(this), unresolveElement(result), unresolveElement(field),
      position, _)
method.comments:
Gets the expression within the aggregate literal that is used to initialize
field `field`, if present. The expression is the `position`'th entry in the
aggregate literal.

For example, if `aggr` represents the initialization literal `{.x = 123, .y = 456 .x = 789}` in
```cpp
struct Foo { int x; int y; };
struct Foo foo = {.x = 123, .y = 456 .x = 789};
```
then:
- `aggr.getFieldExpr(x, 0)` gives `123`.
- `aggr.getFieldExpr(y, 1)` gives `456`.
- `aggr.getFieldExpr(x, 2)` gives `789`.

predicate.name: hasDesignator
predicate.body:
field = classType.getAField() and
    aggregate_field_init(underlyingElement(this), _, unresolveElement(field), position, true)
predicate.comments:
Holds if the `position`-th initialization of `field` in this aggregate initializer
uses a designated (e.g., `.x = ...`) rather than a positional initializer.

For example, in:
```c
struct S { int x, y; };
struct S s = { .x = 1, 2 };
```
- `.x = 1` is a designated initializer, therefore `hasDesignator(x, 0)` holds.
- `2` is a positional initializer for `s.y`, therefore `hasDesignator(y, 1)` does not hold.

predicate.name: isInitialized
predicate.comments:
Holds if the field `field` is initialized by this initializer list, either
explicitly with an expression, or implicitly value initialized.

predicate.name: isValueInitialized
predicate.comments:
Holds if the field `field` is value initialized because it is not
explicitly initialized by this initializer list.

Value initialization (see [dcl.init]/8) recursively initializes all fields
of an object to `false`, `0`, `nullptr`, or by calling the default
constructor, as appropriate to the type.

class.comments: A C/C++ aggregate literal that initializes an array or a GNU vector type.

method.name: getArraySize
method.comments:
Gets the number of elements initialized by this initializer list, either explicitly with an
expression, or by implicit value initialization.

method.name: getElementType
method.comments: Gets the type of the elements in the initializer list.

method.name: getAnElementExpr
method.comments:
Gets an expression within the aggregate literal that is used to initialize
element `elementIndex`, if present.

This predicate may have multiple results since an element can be initialized
multiple times in the same initializer.

method.name: getElementExpr
method.comments:
Gets the expression within the aggregate literal that is used to initialize
element `elementIndex`, if present. The expression is the `position`'th entry
in the aggregate literal.

For example, if `a` represents the initialization literal `{[0] = 123, [1] = 456, [0] = 789 }` in
```cpp
int x[2] = {[0] = 123, [1] = 456, [0] = 789 };
```
then:
- `a.getElementExpr(0, 0)` gives `123`.
- `a.getElementExpr(1, 1)` gives `456`.
- `a.getElementExpr(0, 2)` gives `789`.

predicate.name: ArrayOrVectorAggregateLiteral
predicate.body:
exists(DerivedType type |
      type = this.getUnspecifiedType() and
      (
        type instanceof ArrayType or
        type instanceof GNUVectorType
      )
    )

predicate.name: hasDesignator
predicate.comments:
Holds if the `position`-th initialization of the array element at `elementIndex`
in this aggregate initializer uses a designated (e.g., `[0] = ...`) rather than
a positional initializer.

For example, in:
```c
int x[] = { [0] = 1, 2 };
```
- `[0] = 1` is a designated initializer, therefore `hasDesignator(0, 0)` holds.
- `2` is a positional initializer for `x[1]`, therefore `hasDesignator(1, 1)` does not hold.

predicate.name: isInitialized
predicate.comments:
Holds if the element `elementIndex` is initialized by this initializer
list, either explicitly with an expression, or implicitly value
initialized.

predicate.name: isValueInitialized
predicate.comments:
Holds if the element `elementIndex` is value initialized because it is not
explicitly initialized by this initializer list.

Value initialization (see [dcl.init]/8) recursively initializes all fields
of an object to `false`, `0`, `nullptr`, or by calling the default
constructor, as appropriate to the type.

class.comments:
A C/C++ aggregate literal that initializes an array
```
S s[4] = { s_1, s_2, s_3, s_n };
```
A C/C++ unary logical operation.

A C/C++ logical not expression.
```
c = !a;
```
A C/C++ binary logical operation.

predicate.name: impliesValue
predicate.comments:
Holds if the truth of this binary logical expression having value `wholeIsTrue`
implies that the truth of the child expression `part` has truth value `partIsTrue`.

For example if the binary operation:
```
x && y
```
is true, `x` and `y` must also be true, so `impliesValue(x, true, true)` and
`impliesValue(y, true, true)` hold.

class.comments:
A C/C++ logical AND expression.
```
if (a && b) { }
```

predicate.name: impliesValue
predicate.body:
wholeIsTrue = true and partIsTrue = true and part = this.getAnOperand()
    or
    wholeIsTrue = true and
    this.getAnOperand().(BinaryLogicalOperation).impliesValue(part, partIsTrue, true)

class.comments:
A C/C++ logical OR expression.
```
if (a || b) { }
```

predicate.name: impliesValue
predicate.body:
wholeIsTrue = false and partIsTrue = false and part = this.getAnOperand()
    or
    wholeIsTrue = false and
    this.getAnOperand().(BinaryLogicalOperation).impliesValue(part, partIsTrue, false)

class.comments:
A C/C++ conditional ternary expression.
```
a = (b > c ? d : e);
```

method.name: getCondition
method.comments: Gets the condition of this conditional expression.

method.name: getThen
method.comments: Gets the 'then' expression of this conditional expression.

method.name: getElse
method.comments: Gets the 'else' expression of this conditional expression.

predicate.name: isTwoOperand
predicate.comments: Holds if this expression used the two operand form `guard ? : false`.

class.comments: A header file with an include guard.

A header file that uses a non-portable mechanism to prevent multiple
inclusion.

method.name: blame
method.comments: Gets the element to blame for this bad include guard pattern.

class.comments:
A header file with the correct include guard: `#ifndef` (or equivalent),
`#define`, and `#endif`.

method.name: getIncludeGuardName
method.comments: Gets the name of the preprocessor define used to prevent multiple inclusion of this file.

method.name: getDefine
method.comments: Gets the preprocessor macro used to prevent multiple inclusion of this file.

method.name: getIfndef
method.comments: Gets the `#ifndef` or `#if` directive used to prevent multiple inclusion of this file.

method.name: getEndif
method.comments: Gets the `#endif` directive closing this file.

class.comments: A file with no code in it.
A header file with the `#pragma once` include guard.

method.name: blame
method.body: exists(PreprocessorPragma p | p.getFile() = this and p = result and p.getHead() = "once")

predicate.name: PragmaOnceIncludeGuard
predicate.body: exists(PreprocessorPragma p | p.getFile() = this and p.getHead() = "once")

class.comments:
A chunk of code from one preprocessor branch (`#if`, `#ifdef`,
`#ifndef`, `#elif`, `#elifdef`, `#elifndef`, or `#else`) to the
directive that closes it (`#elif`, `#elifdef`, `#elifndef`, `#else`,
or `#endif`).  The `getParent()` method allows these blocks to be
navigated as a tree, with the root being the entire file.

method.name: toString
method.comments: Gets a textual representation of this element.

method.name: getFile
method.comments: Gets the file this `PreprocessorBlock` is located in.

method.name: getStartLine
method.comments: Gets the start line number of this `PreprocessorBlock`.

method.name: getEndLine
method.comments: Gets the end line number of this `PreprocessorBlock`.

method.name: getParent
method.comments:
Gets the `PreprocessorBlock` that's directly surrounding this one.
Has no result if this is a file.

method.name: getAChild
method.comments: Gets a `PreprocessorBlock` that's directly inside this one.

method.name: getAnInclude
method.body:
result = this.getAnEnclosedInclude() and
    not result = this.getAChild().getAnEnclosedInclude()
method.comments:
Gets an include directive that is directly in this
`PreprocessorBlock`.

method.name: getAMacro
method.body:
result = this.getAnEnclosedMacro() and
    not result = this.getAChild().getAnEnclosedMacro()
method.comments:
Gets a macro definition that is directly in this
`PreprocessorBlock`.

predicate.name: PreprocessorBlock
predicate.body:
mkElement(this) instanceof File or
    mkElement(this) instanceof PreprocessorBranch or
    mkElement(this) instanceof PreprocessorElse

predicate.name: hasLocationInfo
predicate.body:
filepath = this.getFile().toString() and
    startline = this.getStartLine() and
    startcolumn = 0 and
    endline = this.getEndLine() and
    endcolumn = 0
predicate.comments:
Holds if this element is at the specified location.
The location spans column `startcolumn` of line `startline` to
column `endcolumn` of line `endline` in file `filepath`.
For more information, see
[Locations](https://codeql.github.com/docs/writing-codeql-queries/providing-locations-in-codeql-queries/).

method.name: getQualifiedName
getAQualifierForMembers
method.comments:
Gets a namespace qualifier, like `"namespace1::namespace2"`, through which
the members of this namespace can be named. When `inline namespace` is
used, this predicate may have multiple results.

This predicate does not take namespace aliases into account. Unlike inline
namespaces, specialization of templates cannot happen through an alias.
Aliases are also local to the compilation unit, while inline namespaces
affect the whole program.

method.name: getADeclaration
getName
method.comments: Gets the name of this declaration.

method.name: getTypeQualifierWithoutArgs
method.body:
exists(UserType declaringType |
      declaringType = this.(EnumConstant).getDeclaringEnum()
      or
      declaringType = this.getDeclaringType()
    |
      result = getTypeQualifierForMembersWithoutArgs(declaringType)
    )

method.name: getTypeQualifierWithArgs
method.body:
exists(UserType declaringType |
      declaringType = this.(EnumConstant).getDeclaringEnum()
      or
      declaringType = this.getDeclaringType()
    |
      result = getTypeQualifierForMembersWithArgs(declaringType)
    )

method.name: getNamespace
method.body:
// Top level declaration in a namespace ...
    result.getADeclaration() = this
    or
    // ... or nested in another structure.
    exists(Declaration m | m = this and result = m.getDeclaringType().getNamespace())
    or
    exists(EnumConstant c | c = this and result = c.getDeclaringEnum().getNamespace())

method.name: getQualifiedName
method.body:
exists(string ns, string name |
      ns = this.getNamespace().getQualifiedName() and
      name = this.getName() and
      this.canHaveQualifiedName()
    |
      exists(string t | t = this.getTypeQualifierWithArgs() |
        if ns != "" then result = ns + "::" + t + "::" + name else result = t + "::" + name
      )
      or
      not hasTypeQualifier(this) and
      if ns != "" then result = ns + "::" + name else result = name
    )

method.name: getDeclaringType
method.comments:
Gets the class where this member is declared, if it is a member.
For templates, both the template itself and all instantiations of
the template are considered to have the same declaring class.

predicate.name: canHaveQualifiedName
predicate.body:
this.hasDeclaringType()
    or
    this instanceof EnumConstant
    or
    this instanceof Function
    or
    this instanceof UserType
    or
    this instanceof GlobalOrNamespaceVariable

predicate.name: isTopLevel
predicate.body:
not (
      this.isMember() or
      this instanceof FriendDecl or
      this instanceof EnumConstant or
      this instanceof Parameter or
      this instanceof ProxyClass or
      this instanceof LocalVariable or
      this instanceof TypeTemplateParameter or
      this.(UserType).isLocal()
    )

predicate.name: isMember
predicate.comments: Holds if this declaration is a member of a class/struct/union.

predicate.name: hasDeclaringType
predicate.comments: Holds if this declaration is a member of a class/struct/union.

class.comments: A particular declaration or definition of a C/C++ variable.

method.name: getVariable
method.comments: Gets the variable which is being declared or defined.

method.name: getAnInstantiation
method.body:
function_instantiation(result, this) and
    not exists(@fun_decl fd | fun_decls(fd, this, _, _, _) and fun_specialized(fd))

method.name: getAMember
method.body:
exists(Declaration d | member(this, _, d) |
      result = d or
      result = d.(TemplateClass).getAnInstantiation() or
      result = d.(TemplateFunction).getAnInstantiation() or
      result = d.(TemplateVariable).getAnInstantiation()
    )

class.comments:
A `Content` that should be implicitly regarded as tainted whenever an object with such `Content`
is itself tainted.

For example, if we had a type `struct Container { int field; }`, then by default a tainted
`Container` and a `Container` with a tainted `int` stored in its `field` are distinct.

If `any(DataFlow::FieldContent fc | fc.getField().hasQualifiedName("Container", "field"))` was
included in this type however, then a tainted `Container` would imply that its `field` is also
tainted (but not vice versa).

A configuration of a data flow analysis that performs must-flow analysis. This is different
from `DataFlow.qll` which performs may-flow analysis (i.e., it finds paths where the source _may_
flow to the sink).

Like in `DataFlow.qll`, each use of the `MustFlow.qll` library must define its own unique extension
of this abstract class. To create a configuration, extend this class with a subclass whose
characteristic predicate is a unique singleton string and override `isSource`, `isSink` (and
`isAdditionalFlowStep` if additional steps are required).

predicate.name: isSource
predicate.comments: Holds if `source` is a relevant data flow source.

predicate.name: isSink
predicate.comments: Holds if `sink` is a relevant data flow sink.

predicate.name: isBarrier
predicate.comments: Holds if data flow through `instr` is prohibited.

predicate.name: isAdditionalFlowStep
predicate.comments:
Holds if the additional flow step from `node1` to `node2` must be taken
into account in the analysis.

predicate.name: allowInterproceduralFlow
predicate.comments: Holds if this configuration allows flow from arguments to parameters.

predicate.name: hasFlowPath
predicate.comments:
Holds if data must flow from `source` to `sink` for this configuration.

The corresponding paths are generated from the end-points and the graph
included in the module `PathGraph`.

exists(int indirectionIndex |

A return kind. A return kind describes how a value can be returned

A value returned from a callable through a parameter.
A data flow node that occurs as the result of a `ReturnStmt`.

method.name: getKind
method.body:
exists(Operand op, int indirectionIndex |
      hasOperandAndIndex(this, pragma[only_bind_into](op), pragma[only_bind_into](indirectionIndex))
    |
      exists(ReturnValueInstruction return |
        op = return.getReturnAddressOperand() and
        result = TNormalReturnKind(indirectionIndex - 1)
      )
    )
    or
    exists(int argumentIndex, int indirectionIndex |
      finalParameterNodeHasArgumentAndIndex(this, argumentIndex, indirectionIndex) and
      result = TIndirectReturnKind(argumentIndex, indirectionIndex)
    )

A variable that behaves like a global variable.

class.comments: A node that performs a type cast.

A callable, which may be:
- a function (that may contain code)
- a summarized function (that may contain only `FlowSummaryNode`s)
- a variable (this is used as context for global initialization, and also
for the mid-point in interprocedural data flow between a write and read
of a global variable in different functions).
When flow crosses from one _enclosing callable_ to another, the
interprocedural data-flow library discards call contexts and inserts a node
in the big-step relation used for human-readable path explanations.

method.name: getLocation
method.comments: Gets the location of this callable.

method.name: toString
method.comments: Gets a textual representation of this callable.

method.name: asSourceCallable

Gets the `Declaration` corresponding to this callable if it exists in the database.
For summarized callables (which may not exist in the database), use `asSummarizedCallable`.

method.name: asSummarizedCallable
method.comments:
Gets the underlying summarized callable, if
this callable is generated from a models-as-data
model.

method.name: getUnderlyingCallable
method.body:
result = this.asSummarizedCallable() or // SummarizedCallable = Function (in CPP)
    result = this.asSourceCallable()
method.comments:
Gets the underlying `Declaration` of this `DataFlowCallable`. This
predicate returns a result for both source and summarized callables.

class.comments:
A source callable, conceptually, a function in the source code for the
purpose of computing data flow. In practice this excludes functions that
are summarized using models-as-data (as we don't want to create
unmodeled flows or duplicate paths), and includes variables (for reasons
explained in `DataFlowCallable`).

A summarized callable, that is, a function synthesized from one or more
models-as-data models as a place to contain the corresponding
`FlowSummaryNode`s.

A function call relevant for data flow. This includes calls from source
code and calls inside library callables with a flow summary.

method.name: asCallInstruction
method.comments: Gets the underlying data flow call instruction, if any.

method.name: getCallTargetOperand
method.comments: Gets the operand the specifies the target function of the call.

method.name: getStaticCallSourceTarget
method.comments: Gets the `Function` that the call targets, if this is statically known.

method.name: getStaticCallTarget
method.comments:
Gets the target of this call. We use the following strategy for deciding
between the source callable and a summarized callable:
- If there is a manual summary then we always use the manual summary.
- If there is a source callable and we only have generated summaries
we use the source callable.
- If there is no source callable then we use the summary regardless of
whether is it manual or generated.

method.name: getArgumentOperand
method.comments: Gets the `index`'th argument operand. The qualifier is considered to have index `-1`.

method.name: getArgument
method.comments: Gets the argument at the specified index, or `this` if `index` is `-1`.

method.name: getNumberOfArguments
method.comments: Gets the number of arguments of the call, including the `this` pointer, if any.

method.name: getEnclosingCallable
method.comments: Gets the enclosing callable, if any.

method.name: toString
method.comments: Gets a textual representation of this call.

method.name: getLocation
method.comments: Gets the location of this call.

class.comments: A function call relevant for data flow, that exists in source code.

A synthesized call inside a callable with a flow summary.

For example, consider the function:
```
int myFunction(int (*funPtr)());
```
with an accompanying models-as-data flow summary involving `funPtr` (for
example from `Argument[0].ReturnValue` to `ReturnValue`). A `SummaryCall`
will be synthesized representing a call to `funPtr` inside `myFunction`,
so that flow can be connected as described in the model.

method.name: getReceiver
method.comments:
Gets the data flow node that holds the address of the function this call
targets.

method.name: toString
method.body: result = "[summary] call to " + receiver + " in " + c

method.name: toString
method.body:
this.isFunctionPointer() and
    result = "Function pointer kind"
    or
    this.isFunctor() and
    result = "Functor kind"

class.comments: An approximated `Content`.

A second-level control-flow scope in a `switch` or a chained `if` statement.

This is a `switch` case or a branch of a chained `if` statement, given that
the `switch` or `if` statement is top level, that is, it is not nested inside
other CFG constructs.

method.name: toString
method.body:
exists(Cpp::Stmt s | this = TTopLevelIfBranch(s) | result = s.toString())
    or
    exists(Cpp::SwitchCase s | this = TTopLevelSwitchCase(s) | result = s.toString())

exists(Cpp::Stmt s | this = TTopLevelIfBranch(s) | result = s.getLocation())
    or
    exists(Cpp::SwitchCase s | this = TTopLevelSwitchCase(s) | result = s.getLocation())
method.comments: Gets the primary location of this element.

method.name: getANode
method.comments: Gets a data-flow node nested within this scope.

class.comments: An operand that is defined by a `FieldAddressInstruction`.

method.name: getField

method.name: getObjectAddress
method.comments: Gets the instruction whose result provides the address of the object containing the field.
