method.comments: Gets the operand that provides the address of the array object.

method.name: getArrayObjectAddress
method.comments: Gets the instruction whose result provides the address of the array object.

class.comments:
An instruction that produces a well-defined but unknown result and has
unknown side effects, including side effects that are not conservatively
modeled in the SSA graph.

This type of instruction appears when there is an `ErrorExpr` in the AST,
meaning that the extractor could not understand the expression and therefore
produced a partial AST. Queries that give alerts when some action is _not_
taken may want to ignore any function that contains an `ErrorInstruction`.

An instruction that returns an uninitialized value.

This instruction is used to provide an initial definition for a stack variable that does not have
an initializer, or whose initializer only partially initializes the variable.

method.name: getLocalVariable
method.comments: Gets the variable that is uninitialized.

method.name: getDestinationAddressOperand
method.comments:
Gets the operand that provides the address of the location to which the
uninitialized value will be stored.

method.name: getDestinationAddress
method.comments:
Gets the instruction whose result provides the address of the location to
which the value will be stored, if an exact definition is available.

class.comments:
An instruction that has no effect.

This instruction is typically inserted to ensure that a particular AST is associated with at
least one instruction, even when the AST has no semantic effect.

An instruction that returns control to the caller of the function.

This instruction represents the normal (non-exception) return from a function, either from an
explicit `return` statement or from control flow reaching the end of the function's body.

Each function has exactly one `ReturnInstruction`. Each `return` statement in a function is
represented as an initialization of the temporary variable that holds the return value, with
control then flowing to the common `ReturnInstruction` for that function. Exception: A function
that never returns will not have a `ReturnInstruction`.

The `ReturnInstruction` for a function will have a control-flow successor edge to a block
containing the `ExitFunction` instruction for that function.

There are two different return instructions: `ReturnValueInstruction`, for returning a value from
a non-`void`-returning function, and `ReturnVoidInstruction`, for returning from a
`void`-returning function.
An instruction that returns control to the caller of the function, without returning a value.
An instruction that returns control to the caller of the function, including a return value.

method.name: getReturnValueOperand
method.comments: Gets the operand that provides the value being returned by the function.

method.name: getReturnAddressOperand
method.comments: Gets the operand that provides the address of the value being returned by the function.

method.name: getReturnValue
method.comments:
Gets the instruction whose result provides the value being returned by the function, if an
exact definition is available.

method.name: getReturnAddress
method.comments: Gets the instruction whose result provides the address of the value being returned by the function.

class.comments:
An instruction that represents the use of the value pointed to by a parameter of the function
after the function returns control to its caller.

This instruction does not itself return control to the caller. It merely represents the potential
for a caller to use the memory pointed to by the parameter sometime after the call returns. This
is the counterpart to the `InitializeIndirection` instruction, which represents the possibility
that the caller initialized the memory pointed to by the parameter before the call.

method.name: getSideEffectOperand
method.comments: Gets the operand that provides the value of the pointed-to memory.

method.name: getSideEffect
method.comments:
Gets the instruction whose result provides the value of the pointed-to memory, if an exact
definition is available.

method.name: getSourceAddressOperand
method.comments: Gets the operand that provides the address of the pointed-to memory.

method.name: getSourceAddress
method.comments: Gets the instruction whose result provides the address of the pointed-to memory.

method.name: getParameter
method.comments:
Gets the parameter for which this instruction reads the final pointed-to value within the
function.

predicate.name: isThisIndirection
predicate.comments: Holds if this instruction is the return indirection for `this`.

predicate.name: hasIndex
predicate.body:
index >= 0 and index = this.getParameter().getIndex()
    or
    index = -1 and this.isThisIndirection()
predicate.comments:
Holds if this instruction is the return indirection for the parameter with index `index`, or
if this instruction is the return indirection for `this` and `index` is `-1`.

class.comments:
An instruction that returns a copy of its operand.

There are several different copy instructions, depending on the source and destination of the
copy operation:
- `CopyValueInstruction` - Copies a register operand to a register result.
- `LoadInstruction` - Copies a memory operand to a register result.
- `StoreInstruction` - Copies a register operand to a memory result.

method.name: getSourceValueOperand
method.comments: Gets the operand that provides the input value of the copy.

method.name: getSourceValue
method.comments:
Gets the instruction whose result provides the input value of the copy, if an exact definition
is available.

class.comments: An instruction that returns a register result containing a copy of its register operand.
An instruction that returns a register result containing a copy of its memory operand.

method.name: getSourceAddressOperand
method.comments: Gets the operand that provides the address of the value being loaded.

method.name: getSourceAddress
method.comments: Gets the instruction whose result provides the address of the value being loaded.

class.comments: An instruction that returns a memory result containing a copy of its register operand.

method.name: getDestinationAddressOperand
method.comments: Gets the operand that provides the address of the location to which the value will be stored.

method.name: getDestinationAddress
method.comments:
Gets the instruction whose result provides the address of the location to which the value will
be stored, if an exact definition is available.

class.comments:
An instruction that branches to one of two successor instructions based on the value of a Boolean
operand.

method.name: getConditionOperand
method.comments: Gets the operand that provides the Boolean condition controlling the branch.

method.name: getCondition
method.comments: Gets the instruction whose result provides the Boolean condition controlling the branch.

method.name: getTrueSuccessor
method.comments: Gets the instruction to which control will flow if the condition is true.

method.name: getFalseSuccessor
method.comments: Gets the instruction to which control will flow if the condition is false.

class.comments:
An instruction representing the exit point of a function.

Each `IRFunction` has exactly one `ExitFunction` instruction, unless the function neither returns
nor throws an exception. Control flows to the `ExitFunction` instruction from both normal returns
(`ReturnVoid`, `ReturnValue`) and propagated exceptions (`Unwind`). This instruction has no
successors.
An instruction whose result is a constant value.
An instruction whose result is a constant value of integer or Boolean type.

predicate.name: IntegerConstantInstruction

class.comments: An instruction whose result is a constant value of floating-point type.
An instruction whose result is a constant value of a pointer type.

predicate.name: PointerConstantInstruction

class.comments: An instruction whose result is the address of a string literal.

method.name: getValue
method.comments: Gets the string literal whose address is returned by this instruction.

class.comments: An instruction whose result is computed from two operands.

method.name: getLeftOperand
method.comments: Gets the left operand of this binary instruction.

method.name: getRightOperand
method.comments: Gets the right operand of this binary instruction.

method.name: getLeft
method.comments:
Gets the instruction whose result provides the value of the left operand of this binary
instruction.

method.name: getRight
method.comments:
Gets the instruction whose result provides the value of the right operand of this binary
instruction.

method.name: getAnInput
method.comments:
Gets the instruction whose result provides the value of the left or right
operand of this binary instruction.

predicate.name: hasOperands
predicate.body:
op1 = this.getLeftOperand() and op2 = this.getRightOperand()
    or
    op1 = this.getRightOperand() and op2 = this.getLeftOperand()
predicate.comments: Holds if this instruction's operands are `op1` and `op2`, in either order.

class.comments: An instruction that computes the result of an arithmetic operation.
An instruction that performs an arithmetic operation on two numeric operands.

An instruction whose result is computed by performing an arithmetic operation on a single
numeric operand.

An instruction that computes the sum of two numeric operands.

Both operands must have the same numeric type, which will also be the result type. The result of
integer overflow is the infinite-precision result modulo 2^n. Floating-point addition is
performed according to IEEE-754.

An instruction that computes the difference of two numeric operands.

Both operands must have the same numeric type, which will also be the result type. The result of
integer overflow is the infinite-precision result modulo 2^n. Floating-point subtraction is performed
according to IEEE-754.

An instruction that computes the product of two numeric operands.

Both operands must have the same numeric type, which will also be the result type. The result of
integer overflow is the infinite-precision result modulo 2^n. Floating-point multiplication is
performed according to IEEE-754.

An instruction that computes the quotient of two numeric operands.

Both operands must have the same numeric type, which will also be the result type. The result of
division by zero or integer overflow is undefined. Floating-point division is performed according
to IEEE-754.

An instruction that computes the remainder of two integer operands.

Both operands must have the same integer type, which will also be the result type. The result of
division by zero or integer overflow is undefined.

An instruction that negates a single numeric operand.

The operand must have a numeric type, which will also be the result type. The result of integer
negation uses two's complement, and is computed modulo 2^n. The result of floating-point negation
is performed according to IEEE-754.
An instruction that computes the result of a bitwise operation.
An instruction that performs a bitwise operation on two integer operands.
An instruction that performs a bitwise operation on a single integer operand.

An instruction that computes the bitwise "and" of two integer operands.

Both operands must have the same integer type, which will also be the result type.

An instruction that computes the bitwise "or" of two integer operands.

Both operands must have the same integer type, which will also be the result type.

An instruction that computes the bitwise "xor" of two integer operands.

Both operands must have the same integer type, which will also be the result type.

An instruction that shifts its left operand to the left by the number of bits specified by its
right operand.

Both operands must have an integer type. The result has the same type as the left operand. The
rightmost bits are zero-filled.

An instruction that shifts its left operand to the right by the number of bits specified by its
right operand.

Both operands must have an integer type. The result has the same type as the left operand. If the
left operand has an unsigned integer type, the leftmost bits are zero-filled. If the left operand
has a signed integer type, the leftmost bits are filled by duplicating the most significant bit
of the left operand.

An instruction that shifts its left operand to the right by the number of bits specified by its
right operand.

Both operands must have an integer type. The result has the same type as the left operand.
The leftmost bits are zero-filled.

An instruction that performs a binary arithmetic operation involving at least one pointer
operand.

method.name: getElementSize
method.comments:
Gets the size of the elements pointed to by the pointer operands, in bytes.

When adding an integer offset to a pointer (`PointerAddInstruction`) or subtracting an integer
offset from a pointer (`PointerSubInstruction`), the integer offset is multiplied by the
element size to compute the actual number of bytes added to or subtracted from the pointer
address. When computing the integer difference between two pointers (`PointerDiffInstruction`),
the result is computed by computing the difference between the two pointer byte addresses, then
dividing that byte count by the element size.

predicate.name: PointerArithmeticInstruction
predicate.body:
this.getOpcode() instanceof PointerArithmeticOpcode and
    elementSize = Raw::getInstructionElementSize(this)

class.comments: An instruction that adds or subtracts an integer offset from a pointer.

An instruction that adds an integer offset to a pointer.

The result is the byte address computed by adding the value of the right (integer) operand,
multiplied by the element size, to the value of the left (pointer) operand. The result of pointer
overflow is undefined.

An instruction that subtracts an integer offset from a pointer.

The result is the byte address computed by subtracting the value of the right (integer) operand,
multiplied by the element size, from the value of the left (pointer) operand. The result of
pointer underflow is undefined.

An instruction that computes the difference between two pointers.

Both operands must have the same pointer type. The result must have an integer type whose size is
the same as that of the pointer operands. The result is computed by subtracting the byte address
in the right operand from the byte address in the left operand, and dividing by the element size.
If the difference in byte addresses is not divisible by the element size, the result is
undefined.
An instruction whose result is computed from a single operand.

method.name: getUnaryOperand
method.comments: Gets the sole operand of this instruction.

method.name: getUnary
method.comments: Gets the instruction whose result provides the sole operand of this instruction.

class.comments: An instruction that converts the value of its operand to a value of a different type.

An instruction that converts the address of a polymorphic object to the address of a different
subobject of the same polymorphic object, returning a null address if the dynamic type of the
object is not compatible with the result type.

If the operand holds a null address, the result is a null address.

This instruction is used to represent a C++ `dynamic_cast<>` to a pointer type, or a C# `is` or
`as` expression.

An instruction that converts the address of a polymorphic object to the address of a different
subobject of the same polymorphic object, throwing an exception if the dynamic type of the object
is not compatible with the result type.

If the operand holds a null address, the result is a null address.

This instruction is used to represent a C++ `dynamic_cast<>` to a reference type, or a C# cast
expression.

An instruction that returns the address of the complete object that contains the subobject
pointed to by its operand.

If the operand holds a null address, the result is a null address.

This instruction is used to represent `dynamic_cast<void*>` in C++, which returns the pointer to
the most-derived object.

An instruction that converts the address of an object to the address of a different subobject of
the same object, without any type checking at runtime.

method.name: getDerivation
method.comments:
Gets the `ClassDerivation` for the inheritance relationship between
the base and derived classes. This predicate does not hold if the
conversion is to an indirect virtual base class.

method.name: getBaseClass
method.comments:
Gets the base class of the conversion. This will be either a direct
base class of the derived class, or a virtual base class of the
derived class.

method.name: getDerivedClass
method.comments: Gets the derived class of the conversion.

class.comments: An instruction that converts from the address of a derived class to the address of a base class.

An instruction that converts from the address of a derived class to the address of a direct
non-virtual base class.

If the operand holds a null address, the result is a null address.

An instruction that converts from the address of a derived class to the address of a virtual base
class.

If the operand holds a null address, the result is a null address.

An instruction that converts from the address of a base class to the address of a direct
non-virtual derived class.

If the operand holds a null address, the result is a null address.

An instruction that computes the bitwise complement of its operand.

The operand must have an integer type, which will also be the result type.

An instruction that computes the logical complement of its operand.

The operand must have a Boolean type, which will also be the result type.
An instruction that compares two numeric operands.

An instruction that returns a `true` result if its operands are equal.

Both operands must have the same numeric or address type. The result must have a Boolean type.
The result is `true` if `left == right`, and `false` if `left != right` or the two operands are
unordered. Floating-point comparison is performed according to IEEE-754.

An instruction that returns a `true` result if its operands are not equal.

Both operands must have the same numeric or address type. The result must have a Boolean type.
The result is `true` if `left != right` or if the two operands are unordered, and `false` if
`left == right`. Floating-point comparison is performed according to IEEE-754.
An instruction that does a relative comparison of two values, such as `<` or `>=`.

method.name: getGreater
method.comments:
Gets the operand on the "greater" (or "greater-or-equal") side
of this relational instruction, that is, the side that is larger
if the overall instruction evaluates to `true`; for example on
`x <= 20` this is the `20`, and on `y > 0` it is `y`.

method.name: getLesser
method.comments:
Gets the operand on the "lesser" (or "lesser-or-equal") side
of this relational instruction, that is, the side that is smaller
if the overall instruction evaluates to `true`; for example on
`x <= 20` this is `x`, and on `y > 0` it is the `0`.

predicate.name: isStrict
predicate.comments: Holds if this relational instruction is strict (is not an "or-equal" instruction).

class.comments:
An instruction that returns a `true` result if its left operand is less than its right operand.

Both operands must have the same numeric or address type. The result must have a Boolean type.
The result is `true` if the `left < right`, and `false` if `left >= right` or if the two operands
are unordered. Floating-point comparison is performed according to IEEE-754.

An instruction that returns a `true` result if its left operand is greater than its right operand.

Both operands must have the same numeric or address type. The result must have a Boolean type.
The result is `true` if the `left > right`, and `false` if `left <= right` or if the two operands
are unordered. Floating-point comparison is performed according to IEEE-754.

An instruction that returns a `true` result if its left operand is less than or equal to its
right operand.

Both operands must have the same numeric or address type. The result must have a Boolean type.
The result is `true` if the `left <= right`, and `false` if `left > right` or if the two operands
are unordered. Floating-point comparison is performed according to IEEE-754.

An instruction that returns a `true` result if its left operand is greater than or equal to its
right operand.

Both operands must have the same numeric or address type. The result must have a Boolean type.
The result is `true` if the `left >= right`, and `false` if `left < right` or if the two operands
are unordered. Floating-point comparison is performed according to IEEE-754.
An instruction that represents a three-way comparison operator.

An instruction that branches to one of multiple successor instructions based on the value of an
integer operand.

This instruction will have zero or more successors whose edge kind is `CaseEdge`, each
representing the branch that will be taken if the controlling expression is within the range
specified for that case edge. The range of a case edge must be disjoint from the range of each
other case edge.

The instruction may optionally have a successor edge whose edge kind is `DefaultEdge`,
representing the branch that will be taken if the controlling expression is not within the range
of any case edge.

method.name: getExpressionOperand
method.comments: Gets the operand that provides the integer value controlling the switch.

method.name: getExpression
method.comments: Gets the instruction whose result provides the integer value controlling the switch.

method.name: getACaseSuccessor
method.comments: Gets the successor instructions along the case edges of the switch.

method.name: getDefaultSuccessor
method.comments: Gets the successor instruction along the default edge of the switch, if any.

class.comments: An instruction that calls a function.

method.name: getImmediateString
method.body:
result = this.getStaticCallTarget().toString()
    or
    not exists(this.getStaticCallTarget()) and result = "?"

method.name: getCallTargetOperand
method.comments: Gets the operand the specifies the target function of the call.

method.name: getCallTarget
method.comments:
Gets the `Instruction` that computes the target function of the call. This is usually a
`FunctionAddress` instruction, but can also be an arbitrary instruction that produces a
function pointer.

method.name: getAnArgumentOperand
method.comments: Gets all of the argument operands of the call, including the `this` pointer, if any.

method.name: getStaticCallTarget
method.comments: Gets the `Function` that the call targets, if this is statically known.

method.name: getAnArgument
method.comments: Gets all of the arguments of the call, including the `this` pointer, if any.

method.name: getThisArgumentOperand
method.comments: Gets the `this` pointer argument operand of the call, if any.

method.name: getThisArgument
method.comments: Gets the `this` pointer argument of the call, if any.

method.name: getPositionalArgumentOperand
method.comments: Gets the argument operand at the specified index.

method.name: getPositionalArgument
method.comments: Gets the argument at the specified index.

method.name: getArgumentOperand
method.body:
index >= 0 and result = this.getPositionalArgumentOperand(index)
    or
    index = -1 and result = this.getThisArgumentOperand()
method.comments: Gets the argument operand at the specified index, or `this` if `index` is `-1`.

method.name: getArgument
method.comments: Gets the argument at the specified index, or `this` if `index` is `-1`.

method.name: getNumberOfArguments
method.comments: Gets the number of arguments of the call, including the `this` pointer, if any.

method.name: getAParameterSideEffect
method.comments:
Holds if the result is a side effect for the argument at the specified index, or `this` if
`index` is `-1`.

This helper predicate makes it easy to join on both of these columns at once, avoiding
pathological join orders in case the argument index should get joined first.

class.comments: An instruction representing a side effect of a function call.

method.name: getPrimaryInstruction
method.comments: Gets the instruction whose execution causes this side effect.

class.comments:
An instruction representing the side effect of a function call on any memory that might be
accessed by that call.

An instruction representing the side effect of a function call on any memory
that might be read by that call.

This instruction is emitted instead of `CallSideEffectInstruction` when it is certain that the
call target cannot write to escaped memory.

An instruction representing a read side effect of a function call on a
specific parameter.

method.name: getSideEffectOperand
method.comments: Gets the operand for the value that will be read from this instruction, if known.

method.name: getSideEffect
method.comments: Gets the value that will be read from this instruction, if known.

method.name: getArgumentOperand
method.comments: Gets the operand for the address from which this instruction may read.

method.name: getArgumentDef
method.comments: Gets the address from which this instruction may read.

class.comments: An instruction representing the read of an indirect parameter within a function call.
An instruction representing the read of an indirect buffer parameter within a function call.
An instruction representing the read of an indirect buffer parameter within a function call.

method.name: getBufferSizeOperand
method.comments: Gets the operand that holds the number of bytes read from the buffer.

method.name: getBufferSize
method.comments: Gets the instruction whose result provides the number of bytes read from the buffer.

class.comments:
An instruction representing a write side effect of a function call on a
specific parameter.

method.name: getDestinationAddressOperand
method.comments: Get the operand that holds the address of the memory to be written.

method.name: getDestinationAddress
method.comments: Gets the instruction whose result provides the address of the memory to be written.

class.comments: An instruction representing the write of an indirect parameter within a function call.

An instruction representing the write of an indirect buffer parameter within a function call. The
entire buffer is overwritten.

An instruction representing the write of an indirect buffer parameter within a function call. The
entire buffer is overwritten.

method.name: getBufferSizeOperand
method.comments: Gets the operand that holds the number of bytes written to the buffer.

method.name: getBufferSize
method.comments: Gets the instruction whose result provides the number of bytes written to the buffer.

class.comments:
An instruction representing the potential write of an indirect parameter within a function call.

Unlike `IndirectWriteSideEffectInstruction`, the location might not be completely overwritten.
written.

An instruction representing the write of an indirect buffer parameter within a function call.

Unlike `BufferWriteSideEffectInstruction`, the buffer might not be completely overwritten.

An instruction representing the write of an indirect buffer parameter within a function call.

Unlike `BufferWriteSideEffectInstruction`, the buffer might not be completely overwritten.

method.name: getBufferSizeOperand
method.comments: Gets the operand that holds the number of bytes written to the buffer.

method.name: getBufferSize
method.comments: Gets the instruction whose result provides the number of bytes written to the buffer.

class.comments:
An instruction representing the initial value of newly allocated memory, such as the result of a
call to `malloc`.

method.name: getAllocationAddressOperand
method.comments: Gets the operand that represents the address of the allocation this instruction is initializing.

method.name: getAllocationAddress
method.comments: Gets the address for the allocation this instruction is initializing.

class.comments: An instruction representing a GNU or MSVC inline assembly statement.
An instruction that throws an exception.
An instruction that throws a new exception.

method.name: getExceptionAddressOperand
method.comments: Gets the address operand of the exception thrown by this instruction.

method.name: getExceptionAddress
method.comments: Gets the address of the exception thrown by this instruction.

method.name: getExceptionOperand
method.comments: Gets the operand for the exception thrown by this instruction.

method.name: getException
method.comments: Gets the exception thrown by this instruction.

class.comments: An instruction that re-throws the current exception.
An instruction that exits the current function by propagating an exception.
An instruction that starts a `catch` handler.
An instruction that catches an exception of a specific type.

method.name: getExceptionType
method.comments: Gets the type of exception to be caught.

predicate.name: CatchByTypeInstruction
predicate.body:
this.getOpcode() instanceof Opcode::CatchByType and
    exceptionType = Raw::getInstructionExceptionType(this)

class.comments: An instruction that catches any exception.
An instruction that initializes all escaped memory.
An instruction that consumes all escaped memory on exit from the function.

An instruction representing the choice of one of multiple input values based on control flow.

A `PhiInstruction` is inserted at the beginning of a block whenever two different definitions of
the same variable reach that block. The `PhiInstruction` will have one operand corresponding to
each control flow predecessor of the block, with that operand representing the version of the
variable that flows from that predecessor. The result value of the `PhiInstruction` will be
a copy of whichever operand corresponds to the actual predecessor that entered the block at
runtime.

method.name: getAnInputOperand
method.comments: Gets all of the instruction's `PhiInputOperand`s, representing the values that flow from each predecessor block.

method.name: getAnInput
method.comments:
Gets an instruction that defines the input to one of the operands of this
instruction. It's possible for more than one operand to have the same
defining instruction, so this predicate will have the same number of
results as `getAnInputOperand()` or fewer.

method.name: getInputOperand
method.comments: Gets the input operand representing the value that flows from the specified predecessor block.

class.comments:
An instruction representing the effect that a write to a memory may have on potential aliases of
that memory.

A `ChiInstruction` is inserted immediately after an instruction that writes to memory. The
`ChiInstruction` has two operands. The first operand, given by `getTotalOperand()`, represents
the previous state of all of the memory that might be aliased by the memory write. The second
operand, given by `getPartialOperand()`, represents the memory that was actually modified by the
memory write. The result of the `ChiInstruction` represents the same memory as
`getTotalOperand()`, updated to include the changes due to the value that was actually stored by
the memory write.

As an example, suppose that variable `p` and `q` are pointers that may or may not point to the
same memory:
```
p = 5;
x = *q;
```

The IR would look like:
```
r1_1 = VariableAddress[p]
r1_2 = Load r1_1, m0_0  // Load the value of `p`
r1_3 = Constant[5]
m1_4 = Store r1_2, r1_3  // Store to `*p`
m1_5 = ^Chi m0_1, m1_4  // Side effect of the previous Store on aliased memory
r1_6 = VariableAddress[x]
r1_7 = VariableAddress[q]
r1_8 = Load r1_7, m0_2  // Load the value of `q`
r1_9 = Load r1_8, m1_5  // Load the value of `*q`
m1_10 = Store r1_6, r1_9  // Store to x
```

Note the `Chi` instruction after the store to `*p`. The indicates that the previous contents of
aliased memory (`m0_1`) are merged with the new value written by the store (`m1_4`), producing a
new version of aliased memory (`m1_5`). On the subsequent load from `*q`, the source operand of
`*q` is `m1_5`, indicating that the store to `*p` may (or may not) have updated the memory
pointed to by `q`.

For more information about how `Chi` instructions are used to model memory side effects, see
https://link.springer.com/content/pdf/10.1007%2F3-540-61053-7_66.pdf.

method.name: getTotalOperand
method.comments:
Gets the operand that represents the previous state of all memory that might be aliased by the
memory write.

method.name: getTotal
method.comments:
Gets the operand that represents the previous state of all memory that might be aliased by the
memory write.

method.name: getPartialOperand
method.comments: Gets the operand that represents the new value written by the memory write.

method.name: getPartial
method.comments: Gets the operand that represents the new value written by the memory write.

predicate.name: isPartialUpdate
predicate.comments:
Holds if the `ChiPartialOperand` totally, but not exactly, overlaps with the `ChiTotalOperand`.
This means that the `ChiPartialOperand` will not override the entire memory associated with the
`ChiTotalOperand`.

method.name: getAnIRVariable
method.comments:
Gets an `IRVariable` whose memory is initialized by this instruction, if any.
Note: Allocations that are not represented as `IRVariable`s (such as
dynamic allocations) are not returned by this predicate even if this
instruction initializes such memory.

class.comments:
An instruction representing unreachable code.

This instruction is inserted in place of the original target instruction of a `ConditionalBranch`
or `Switch` instruction where that particular edge is infeasible.

An instruction representing a built-in operation.

This is used to represent a variety of intrinsic operations provided by the compiler
implementation, such as vector arithmetic.

method.name: getBuiltInOperation
method.comments:
Gets the language-specific `BuiltInOperation` object that specifies the operation that is
performed by this instruction.

predicate.name: BuiltInOperationInstruction
predicate.body:
this.getOpcode() instanceof BuiltInOperationOpcode and
    operation = Raw::getInstructionBuiltInOperation(this)

class.comments:
An instruction representing a built-in operation that does not have a specific opcode. The
actual operation is specified by the `getBuiltInOperation()` predicate.

An instruction that returns a `va_list` to access the arguments passed to the `...` parameter.

The operand specifies the address of the `IREllipsisVariable` used to represent the `...`
parameter. The result is a `va_list` that initially refers to the first argument that was passed
to the `...` parameter.

An instruction that cleans up a `va_list` after it is no longer in use.

The operand specifies the address of the `va_list` to clean up. This instruction does not return
a result.

An instruction that returns the address of the argument currently pointed to by a `va_list`.

The operand is the `va_list` that points to the argument. The result is the address of the
argument.

An instruction that modifies a `va_list` to point to the next argument that was passed to the
`...` parameter.

The operand is the current `va_list`. The result is an updated `va_list` that points to the next
argument of the `...` parameter.

An instruction that allocates a new object on the managed heap.

This instruction is used to represent the allocation of a new object in C# using the `new`
expression. This instruction does not invoke a constructor for the object. Instead, there will be
a subsequent `Call` instruction to invoke the appropriate constructor directory, passing the
result of the `NewObj` as the `this` argument.

The result is the address of the newly allocated object.
An instruction that returns the type info for its operand.

An instruction that returns the type info for its operand, where the
operand occurs as an expression in the AST.

An instruction that returns the type info for its operand, where the
operand occurs as a type in the AST.

An operand of an `Instruction` in this stage of the IR. Implemented as a union of the branches
of `TOperand` that are used in this stage.

A known location. Testing `loc instanceof KnownLocation` will account for non existing locations, as
opposed to testing `not loc isntanceof UnknownLocation`

An operand of an `Instruction`. The operand represents a use of the result of one instruction
(the defining instruction) in another instruction (the use instruction)

method.name: toString
method.comments: Gets a textual representation of this element.

method.name: getLocation
method.comments:
Gets the location of the source code for this operand.
By default this is where the operand is used, but some subclasses may override this
using `getAnyDef()` if it makes more sense.

method.name: getEnclosingIRFunction
method.comments: Gets the function that contains this operand.

method.name: getUse
method.comments: Gets the `Instruction` that consumes this operand.

method.name: getAnyDef
method.comments:
Gets the `Instruction` whose result is the value of the operand. Unlike
`getDef`, this also has a result when `isDefinitionInexact` holds, which
means that the resulting instruction may only _partially_ or _potentially_
be the value of this operand.

method.name: getDef
method.body:
result = this.getAnyDef() and
    this.getDefinitionOverlap() instanceof MustExactlyOverlap
method.comments:
Gets the `Instruction` whose result is the value of the operand. Unlike
`getAnyDef`, this also has no result when `isDefinitionInexact` holds,
which means that the resulting instruction must always be exactly the be
the value of this operand.

method.name: getDefinitionOverlap
method.comments: Gets the overlap relationship between the operand's definition and its use.

method.name: getDumpLabel
method.comments: Gets a prefix to use when dumping the operand in an operand list.

method.name: getDumpId
method.comments: Gets a string that uniquely identifies this operand on its use instruction.

method.name: getDumpString
method.comments:
Gets a string describing this operand, suitable for display in IR dumps. This consists of the
result ID of the instruction consumed by the operand, plus a label identifying the operand
kind.

For example: `this:r3_5`

method.name: getDumpSortOrder
method.comments: Get the order in which the operand should be sorted in the operand list.

method.name: getLanguageType
method.comments:
Gets the type of the value consumed by this operand. This is usually the same as the
result type of the definition instruction consumed by this operand. For register operands,
this is always the case. For some memory operands, the operand type may be different from
the definition type, such as in the case of a partial read or a read from a pointer that
has been cast to a different type.

method.name: getIRType
method.comments:
Gets the language-neutral type of the value consumed by this operand. This is usually the same
as the result type of the definition instruction consumed by this operand. For register
operands, this is always the case. For some memory operands, the operand type may be different
from the definition type, such as in the case of a partial read or a read from a pointer that
has been cast to a different type.

method.name: getType
method.comments:
Gets the type of the value consumed by this operand. This is usually the same as the
result type of the definition instruction consumed by this operand. For register operands,
this is always the case. For some memory operands, the operand type may be different from
the definition type, such as in the case of a partial read or a read from a pointer that
has been cast to a different type.

method.name: getSize
method.comments:
Gets the size of the value consumed by this operand, in bytes. If the operand does not have
a known constant size, this predicate does not hold.

predicate.name: Operand
predicate.body:
exists(Instruction use, Instruction def | this = registerOperand(use, _, def))
    or
    exists(Instruction use | this = nonSsaMemoryOperand(use, _))
    or
    exists(Instruction use, Instruction def, IRBlock predecessorBlock |
      this = phiOperand(use, def, predecessorBlock, _) or
      this = reusedPhiOperand(use, def, predecessorBlock, _)
    )
    or
    this = chiOperand(_, _)

predicate.name: isDefinitionInexact
predicate.comments: Holds if the result of the definition instruction does not exactly overlap this use.

predicate.name: isGLValue
predicate.comments:
Holds if the value consumed by this operand is a glvalue. If this
holds, the value of the operand represents the address of a location,
and the type of the location is given by `getType()`. If this does
not hold, the value of the operand represents a value whose type is
given by `getType()`.

class.comments: An operand that consumes a memory result (e.g. the `LoadOperand` on a `Load` instruction).

method.name: getMemoryAccess
method.comments: Gets the kind of memory access performed by the operand.

method.name: getAddressOperand
method.comments:
Returns the operand that holds the memory address from which the current operand loads its
value, if any. For example, in `r3 = Load r1, m2`, the result of `getAddressOperand()` for `m2`
is `r1`.

predicate.name: MemoryOperand
predicate.body:
this instanceof TNonSsaMemoryOperand or
    this instanceof TPhiOperand or
    this instanceof TChiOperand

predicate.name: hasMayReadMemoryAccess
predicate.comments:
Holds if the memory access performed by this operand will not always read from every bit in the
memory location. This is most commonly used for memory accesses that may or may not actually
occur depending on runtime state (for example, the write side effect of an output parameter
that is not written to on all paths), or for accesses where the memory location is a
conservative estimate of the memory that might actually be accessed at runtime (for example,
the global side effects of a function call).

class.comments: An operand that is not an operand of a `PhiInstruction`.

method.name: getOperandTag
method.comments: Gets the `OperandTag` that specifies how this operand is used by its `Instruction`.

predicate.name: NonPhiOperand
predicate.body:
this = registerOperand(useInstr, tag, _) or
    this = nonSsaMemoryOperand(useInstr, tag) or
    this = chiOperand(useInstr, tag)

class.comments: An operand that consumes a register (non-memory) result.

method.name: getDefinitionOverlap
method.body:
// All register results overlap exactly with their uses.
    result instanceof MustExactlyOverlap

class.comments: A memory operand other than the operand of a `Phi` instruction.

predicate.name: NonPhiMemoryOperand
predicate.body:
this = nonSsaMemoryOperand(useInstr, tag)
    or
    this = chiOperand(useInstr, tag)

predicate.name: getUsedInterval
predicate.comments:
Holds if the operand totally overlaps with its definition and consumes the
bit range `[startBitOffset, endBitOffset)` relative to the start address of the definition.

class.comments: A memory operand whose type may be different from the type of the result of its definition.

The address operand of an instruction that loads or stores a value from
memory (e.g. `Load`, `Store`).

The buffer size operand of an instruction that represents a read or write of
a buffer.

The source value operand of an instruction that loads a value from memory (e.g. `Load`,
`ReturnValue`, `ThrowValue`).
The source value operand of a `Store` instruction.
The sole operand of a unary instruction (e.g. `Convert`, `Negate`, `Copy`).
The left operand of a binary instruction (e.g. `Add`, `CompareEQ`).
The right operand of a binary instruction (e.g. `Add`, `CompareEQ`).
The condition operand of a `ConditionalBranch` or `Switch` instruction.
The operand representing the target function of an `Call` instruction.

An operand representing an argument to a function call. This includes both
positional arguments (represented by `PositionalArgumentOperand`) and the
implicit `this` argument, if any (represented by `ThisArgumentOperand`).

method.name: getCall
method.comments: Gets the `CallInstruction` for which this is an argument.

class.comments:
An operand representing the implicit `this` argument to a member function
call.

method.name: getLocation

class.comments: An operand representing an argument to a function call.

method.name: getIndex
method.comments: Gets the zero-based index of the argument.

class.comments: An operand representing memory read as a side effect of evaluating another instruction.
An operand of a `PhiInstruction`.

method.name: getPredecessorBlock
method.comments: Gets the predecessor block from which this value comes.

predicate.name: PhiInputOperand
predicate.body:
this = phiOperand(useInstr, defInstr, predecessorBlock, overlap)
    or
    this = reusedPhiOperand(useInstr, defInstr, predecessorBlock, overlap)

class.comments: The total operand of a Chi node, representing the previous value of the memory.
The partial operand of a Chi node, representing the value being written to part of the memory.
The query can extend this class to control which declarations are printed.

method.name: toString
method.comments: Gets a textual representation of this configuration.

predicate.name: shouldPrintDeclaration
predicate.comments:
Holds if the IR for `func` should be printed. By default, holds for all
functions, global and namespace variables, and static local variables.

class.comments: Override of `IRConfiguration` to only evaluate debug strings for the functions that are to be dumped.
A node to be emitted in the IR graph.

method.name: getLocation
method.comments: Gets the location to be emitted for the node.

method.name: getLabel
method.comments: Gets the label to be emitted for the node.

method.name: getOrder
method.comments: Gets the order in which the node appears in its parent node.

method.name: getParent
method.comments: Gets the parent of this node.

method.name: getGraphKind
method.comments: Gets the kind of graph represented by this node ("graph" or "tree").

method.name: getProperty
method.body:
key = "semmle.label" and result = this.getLabel()
    or
    key = "semmle.order" and result = this.getOrder().toString()
    or
    key = "semmle.graphKind" and result = this.getGraphKind()
    or
    key = "semmle.forceText" and this.forceText() and result = "true"
method.comments: Gets the value of the node property with the specified key.

predicate.name: forceText
predicate.comments:
Holds if this node should always be rendered as text, even in a graphical
viewer.

class.comments: An IR graph node representing a `IRFunction` object.

method.name: getOrder
method.body:
this =
      rank[result + 1](PrintableIRFunction orderedFunc, Language::Location location |
        location = orderedFunc.getIRFunction().getLocation()
      |
        orderedFunc
        order by
          location.getFile().getAbsolutePath(), location.getStartLine(), location.getStartColumn(),
          orderedFunc.getLabel()
      )

class.comments: An IR graph node representing an `IRBlock` object.

method.name: getProperty
method.body:
result = PrintableIRNode.super.getProperty(key) or
    result = getAdditionalBlockProperty(block, key)

class.comments: An IR graph node representing an `Instruction`.

method.name: getLabel
method.body:
exists(IRBlock block |
      instr = block.getAnInstruction() and
      exists(
        string resultString, string operationString, string operandsString, int resultWidth,
        int operationWidth
      |
        resultString = instr.getResultString() and
        operationString = instr.getOperationString() and
        operandsString = this.getOperandsString() and
        columnWidths(block, resultWidth, operationWidth) and
        result =
          resultString + getPaddingString(resultWidth - resultString.length()) + " = " +
            operationString + getPaddingString(operationWidth - operationString.length()) + " : " +
            operandsString
      )
    )

method.name: getProperty
method.body:
result = PrintableIRNode.super.getProperty(key) or
    result = getAdditionalInstructionProperty(instr, key)

class.comments: Provides additional information about value numbering in IR dumps.

method.name: getInstructionProperty
method.body:
exists(ValueNumber vn |
      vn = valueNumber(instr) and
      key = "valnum" and
      if strictcount(vn.getAnInstruction()) > 1
      then result = vn.getDebugString()
      else result = "unique"
    )

class.comments: The value number assigned to a particular set of instructions that produce equivalent results.

method.name: getLocation
method.body:
if
      exists(Instruction i |
        i = this.getAnInstruction() and not i.getLocation() instanceof Language::UnknownLocation
      )
    then
      result =
        min(Language::Location l |
          l = this.getAnInstruction().getLocation() and not l instanceof Language::UnknownLocation
        |
          l
          order by
            l.getFile().getAbsolutePath(), l.getStartLine(), l.getStartColumn(), l.getEndLine(),
            l.getEndColumn()
        )
    else result instanceof Language::UnknownLocation

method.name: getAnInstruction
method.comments:
Gets the instructions that have been assigned this value number. This will always produce at
least one result.

method.name: getExampleInstruction
method.body:
result =
      min(Instruction instr |
        instr = this.getAnInstruction()
      |
        instr order by instr.getBlock().getDisplayIndex(), instr.getDisplayIndexInBlock()
      )
method.comments:
Gets one of the instructions that was assigned this value number. The chosen instruction is
deterministic but arbitrary. Intended for use only in debugging.

method.name: getAUse
method.comments: Gets an `Operand` whose definition is exact and has this value number.

method.name: getKind
method.body:
this instanceof TVariableAddressValueNumber and result = "VariableAddress"
    or
    this instanceof TInitializeParameterValueNumber and result = "InitializeParameter"
    or
    this instanceof TConstantValueNumber and result = "Constant"
    or
    this instanceof TStringConstantValueNumber and result = "StringConstant"
    or
    this instanceof TFieldAddressValueNumber and result = "FieldAddress"
    or
    this instanceof TBinaryValueNumber and result = "Binary"
    or
    this instanceof TPointerArithmeticValueNumber and result = "PointerArithmetic"
    or
    this instanceof TUnaryValueNumber and result = "Unary"
    or
    this instanceof TInheritanceConversionValueNumber and result = "InheritanceConversion"
    or
    this instanceof TLoadTotalOverlapValueNumber and result = "LoadTotalOverlap"
    or
    this instanceof TUniqueValueNumber and result = "Unique"

class.comments:
A `ConvertInstruction` which converts data of type `T` to data of type `U`
where `T` and `U` only differ in specifiers. For example, if `T` is `int`
and `U` is `const T` this is a conversion from a non-const integer to a
const integer.

Generally, the value number of a converted value is different from the value
number of an unconverted value, but conversions which only modify specifiers
leave the resulting value bitwise identical to the old value.

A `CopyInstruction` whose source operand's value is congruent to the definition of that source
operand.
For example:
```
Point p = { 1, 2 };
Point q = p;
int a = p.x;
```
The use of `p` on line 2 is linked to the definition of `p` on line 1, and is congruent to that
definition because it accesses the exact same memory.
The use of `p.x` on line 3 is linked to the definition of `p` on line 1 as well, but is not
congruent to that definition because `p.x` accesses only a subset of the memory defined by `p`.

A `Call` or `NewOrNewArrayExpr` or `DeleteOrDeleteArrayExpr`.

All kinds of expression invoke a function as part of their evaluation. This class provides a
way to treat both kinds of function similarly, and to get the invoked `Function`.

method.name: getTarget
method.body:
result = this.(Call).getTarget()
    or
    result = this.(NewOrNewArrayExpr).getAllocator()
    or
    result = this.(DeleteOrDeleteArrayExpr).getDeallocator()
method.comments: Gets the `Function` invoked by this expression, if known.

predicate.name: CallOrAllocationExpr
predicate.body:
this instanceof Call
    or
    this instanceof NewOrNewArrayExpr
    or
    this instanceof DeleteOrDeleteArrayExpr

class.comments:
The IR translation of a call to a function. The call may be from an actual
call in the source code, or could be a call that is part of the translation
of a higher-level constructor (e.g. the allocator call in a `NewExpr`).

method.name: getChildInternal
getFirstInstruction
getChildSuccessorInternal
method.body:
child = this.getQualifier() and
    result = this.getFirstCallTargetInstruction(kind)
    or
    child = this.getCallTarget() and
    result = this.getFirstArgumentOrCallInstruction(kind)
    or
    exists(int argIndex |
      child = this.getArgument(argIndex) and
      if exists(this.getArgument(argIndex + 1))
      then result = this.getArgument(argIndex + 1).getFirstInstruction(kind)
      else (
        result = this.getInstruction(CallTag()) and kind instanceof GotoEdge
      )
    )
    or
    child = this.getSideEffects() and
    if this.isNoReturn()
    then
      kind instanceof GotoEdge and
      result =
        any(UnreachedInstruction instr |
          this.getEnclosingFunction().getFunction() = instr.getEnclosingFunction()
        )
    else (
      not this.mustThrowException(_) and
      result = this.getParent().getChildSuccessor(this, kind)
      or
      this.mayThrowException(kind) and
      result = this.getParent().getExceptionSuccessorInstruction(any(GotoEdge edge))
    )

method.name: getInstructionRegisterOperand
method.body:
tag = CallTag() and
    (
      operandTag instanceof CallTargetOperandTag and
      result = this.getCallTargetResult()
      or
      operandTag instanceof ThisArgumentOperandTag and
      result = this.getQualifierResult()
      or
      exists(PositionalArgumentOperandTag argTag |
        argTag = operandTag and
        result = this.getArgument(argTag.getArgIndex()).getResult()
      )
    )

method.name: getCallResultType
method.comments: Gets the result type of the call.

method.name: getCallTarget
method.comments: Gets the `TranslatedExpr` for the indirect target of the call, if any.

method.name: getFirstCallTargetInstruction
method.comments:
Gets the first instruction of the sequence to evaluate the call target.
By default, this is just the first instruction of `getCallTarget()`, but
it can be overridden by a subclass for cases where there is a call target
that is not computed from an expression (e.g. a direct call).

method.name: getCallTargetResult
method.comments:
Gets the instruction whose result value is the target of the call. By
default, this is just the result of `getCallTarget()`, but it can be
overridden by a subclass for cases where there is a call target that is not
computed from an expression (e.g. a direct call).

method.name: getQualifier
method.comments:
Gets the `TranslatedExpr` for the qualifier of the call (i.e. the value
that is passed as the `this` argument.

method.name: getQualifierResult
method.comments:
Gets the instruction whose result value is the `this` argument of the call.
By default, this is just the result of `getQualifier()`, but it can be
overridden by a subclass for cases where there is a `this` argument that is
not computed from a child expression (e.g. a constructor call).

method.name: getArgument
method.comments:
Gets the argument with the specified `index`. Does not include the `this`
argument.

method.name: getFirstArgumentOrCallInstruction
method.comments:
If there are any arguments, gets the first instruction of the first
argument. Otherwise, returns the call instruction.

predicate.name: hasInstruction
predicate.body:
tag = CallTag() and
    opcode instanceof Opcode::Call and
    resultType = getTypeForPRValue(this.getCallResultType())

predicate.name: mayThrowException
predicate.comments: Holds if the evaluation of this call may throw an exception of the kind represented by the `ExceptionEdge`.

predicate.name: mustThrowException
predicate.comments: Holds if the evaluation of this call always throws an exception of the kind represented by the `ExceptionEdge`.

predicate.name: hasQualifier
predicate.comments: Holds if the call has a `this` argument.

predicate.name: hasArguments
predicate.comments: Holds if the call has any arguments, not counting the `this` argument.

class.comments:
The IR translation of the side effects of the parent `TranslatedElement`.

This object does not itself generate the side effect instructions. Instead, its children provide
the actual side effects, with this object acting as a placeholder so the parent only needs to
insert this one element at the point where all the side effects are supposed to occur.

method.name: getExpr
method.comments: Gets the expression whose side effects are being modeled.

method.name: getChild
method.body:
result =
      rank[i + 1](TranslatedSideEffect tse, int group, int indexInGroup |
        tse.getPrimaryExpr() = this.getExpr() and
        tse.sortOrder(group, indexInGroup)
      |
        tse order by group, indexInGroup
      )

method.name: getChildSuccessorInternal
getFirstInstruction
method.body:
result = this.getChild(0).getFirstInstruction(kind)
    or
    // Some functions, like `std::move()`, have no side effects whatsoever.
    not exists(this.getChild(0)) and
    result = this.getParent().getChildSuccessor(this, kind)

method.name: getALastInstructionInternal
getPrimaryInstruction
method.comments: Gets the primary instruction to be associated with each side effect instruction.

class.comments:
IR translation of a direct call to a specific function. Used for both
explicit calls (`TranslatedFunctionCall`) and implicit calls
(`TranslatedAllocatorCall`).

method.name: getFirstCallTargetInstruction
method.body:
result = this.getInstruction(CallTargetTag()) and
    kind instanceof GotoEdge

method.name: getInstructionSuccessorInternal
method.body:
result = TranslatedCall.super.getInstructionSuccessorInternal(tag, kind)
    or
    tag = CallTargetTag() and
    result = this.getFirstArgumentOrCallInstruction(kind)

predicate.name: hasInstruction
predicate.body:
TranslatedCall.super.hasInstruction(opcode, tag, resultType)
    or
    tag = CallTargetTag() and
    opcode instanceof Opcode::FunctionAddress and
    resultType = getFunctionGLValueType()

class.comments: The IR translation of a call to a function.
Represents the IR translation of a call through a function pointer.

predicate.name: mayThrowException

class.comments: Represents the IR translation of a direct function call.

predicate.name: hasQualifier
predicate.body:
exists(this.getQualifier()) and
    not exists(MemberFunction func | expr.getTarget() = func and func.isStatic())

predicate.name: mayThrowException
predicate.body:
this.mustThrowException(e)
    or
    exists(MicrosoftTryStmt tryStmt | tryStmt.getStmt() = expr.getEnclosingStmt().getParent*()) and
    e instanceof SehExceptionEdge
    or
    not expr.getTarget() instanceof NonCppThrowingFunction and
    exists(TryStmt tryStmt | tryStmt.getStmt() = expr.getEnclosingStmt().getParent*()) and
    e instanceof CppExceptionEdge

predicate.name: mustThrowException
predicate.body:
expr.getTarget() instanceof AlwaysSehThrowingFunction and
    e instanceof SehExceptionEdge

class.comments: Represents the IR translation of a call to a constructor.

method.name: getQualifierResult
method.body:
exists(StructorCallContext context |
      context = this.getParent() and
      result = context.getReceiver()
    )
    or
    exists(Stmt parent |
      expr = parent.getAnImplicitDestructorCall() and
      result = getTranslatedExpr(expr.getQualifier().getFullyConverted()).getResult()
    )
    or
    exists(Expr parent |
      expr = parent.getAnImplicitDestructorCall() and
      result = getTranslatedExpr(expr.getQualifier().getFullyConverted()).getResult()
    )

predicate.name: TranslatedStructorCall
predicate.body:
expr instanceof ConstructorCall or
    expr instanceof DestructorCall

class.comments:
The IR translation of the side effects of a function call, including the implicit allocator
call in a `new` or `new[]` expression.

method.name: getPrimaryInstruction
method.body:
expr instanceof Call and result = getTranslatedCallInstruction(expr)
    or
    expr instanceof NewOrNewArrayExpr and
    result = getTranslatedAllocatorCall(expr).getInstruction(CallTag())
    or
    expr instanceof DeleteOrDeleteArrayExpr and
    result = getTranslatedDeleteOrDeleteArray(expr).getInstruction(CallTag())

class.comments: The IR translation of a single side effect of a call.

method.name: getFirstInstruction
method.body:
result = this.getInstruction(OnlyInstructionTag()) and
    kind instanceof GotoEdge

method.name: getInstructionSuccessorInternal
method.body:
result = this.getParent().getChildSuccessor(this, kind) and
    tag = OnlyInstructionTag()

method.name: getPrimaryExpr
method.comments:
Gets the expression that caused this side effect.

All side effects with the same `getPrimaryExpr()` will appear in the same contiguous sequence
in the IR.

predicate.name: sortOrder
predicate.comments:
Gets the order in which this side effect should be sorted with respect to other side effects
for the same expression.

Side effects are sorted first by `group`, and then by `indexInGroup`.

predicate.name: sideEffectInstruction
predicate.comments: Gets the opcode and result type for the side effect instruction.

class.comments: The IR translation of a single argument side effect for a call.

method.name: toString
method.body:
this.isWrite() and
    result = "(write side effect for " + this.getArgString() + ")"
    or
    not this.isWrite() and
    result = "(read side effect for " + this.getArgString() + ")"

method.name: getInstructionMemoryOperandType
method.body:
not this.isWrite() and
    if sideEffectOpcode instanceof BufferAccessOpcode
    then
      result = getUnknownType() and
      tag instanceof OnlyInstructionTag and
      operandTag instanceof SideEffectOperandTag
    else
      exists(Type operandType |
        tag instanceof OnlyInstructionTag and
        operandType = this.getIndirectionType() and
        operandTag instanceof SideEffectOperandTag
      |
        // If the type we select is an incomplete type (e.g. a forward-declared `struct`), there will
        // not be a `CppType` that represents that type. In that case, fall back to `UnknownCppType`.
        result = getTypeForPRValueOrUnknown(operandType)
      )

method.name: getInstructionRegisterOperand
method.body:
tag instanceof OnlyInstructionTag and
    operandTag instanceof AddressOperandTag and
    result = this.getArgInstruction()
    or
    tag instanceof OnlyInstructionTag and
    operandTag instanceof BufferSizeOperandTag and
    result =
      getTranslatedExpr(call.getArgument(call.getTarget()
              .(SideEffectFunction)
              .getParameterSizeIndex(index)).getFullyConverted()).getResult()

method.name: getArgString
method.comments: Gets a text representation of the argument.

method.name: getArgInstruction
method.comments: Gets the `Instruction` whose result is the value of the argument.

method.name: getIndirectionType
method.comments: Gets the type pointed to by the argument.

predicate.name: sortOrder
predicate.body:
indexInGroup = index and
    if this.isWrite() then group = argumentWriteGroup() else group = argumentReadGroup()

predicate.name: sideEffectInstruction
predicate.body:
opcode = sideEffectOpcode and
    (
      this.isWrite() and
      (
        opcode instanceof BufferAccessOpcode and
        type = getUnknownType()
        or
        not opcode instanceof BufferAccessOpcode and
        exists(Type indirectionType | indirectionType = this.getIndirectionType() |
          if indirectionType instanceof VoidType
          then type = getUnknownType()
          else type = getTypeForPRValueOrUnknown(indirectionType)
        )
      )
      or
      not this.isWrite() and
      type = getVoidType()
    )

predicate.name: isWrite
predicate.comments: Holds if this side effect is a write side effect, rather than a read side effect.

class.comments:
The IR translation of an argument side effect where the argument has an `Expr` object in the AST.

This generally applies to all positional arguments, as well as qualifier (`this`) arguments for
calls other than constructor calls.

method.name: getIndirectionType
method.body:
result = arg.getUnspecifiedType().(DerivedType).getBaseType()
    or
    // Sometimes the qualifier type gets the type of the class itself, rather than a pointer to the
    // class.
    index = -1 and
    not arg.getUnspecifiedType() instanceof DerivedType and
    result = arg.getUnspecifiedType()

class.comments:
The IR translation of an argument side effect for `*this` on a call, where there is no `Expr`
object that represents the `this` argument.

The applies only to constructor calls, as the AST has exploit qualifier `Expr`s for all other
calls to non-static member functions.

method.name: getArgInstruction
method.body:
exists(TranslatedStructorCall structorCall |
      structorCall.getExpr() = call and
      result = structorCall.getQualifierResult()
    )

class.comments: The IR translation of the non-argument-specific side effect of a call.

method.name: getInstructionMemoryOperandType
method.body:
tag instanceof OnlyInstructionTag and
    operandTag instanceof SideEffectOperandTag and
    result = getUnknownType()

predicate.name: sideEffectInstruction
predicate.body:
opcode = sideEffectOpcode and
    (
      opcode instanceof Opcode::CallSideEffect and
      type = getUnknownType()
      or
      opcode instanceof Opcode::CallReadSideEffect and
      type = getVoidType()
    )

class.comments:
The IR translation of the allocation side effect of a call to a memory allocation function.

This side effect provides a definition for the newly-allocated memory.

method.name: getInstructionRegisterOperand
method.body:
tag = OnlyInstructionTag() and
    operandTag = addressOperand() and
    result = this.getPrimaryInstructionForSideEffect(OnlyInstructionTag())

predicate.name: sideEffectInstruction
predicate.body:
opcode instanceof Opcode::InitializeDynamicAllocation and
    type = getUnknownType()

method.name: getChildTrueSuccessor
method.comments:
Gets the instruction to be executed when `child` evaluates to `true`. The
successor edge kind is specified by `kind`.

method.name: getChildFalseSuccessor
method.comments:
Gets the instruction to be executed when `child` evaluates to `false`. The
successor edge kind is specified by `kind`.

method.name: getFunction
method.body:
result = getEnclosingFunction(expr) or
    result = getEnclosingVariable(expr).(GlobalOrNamespaceVariable) or
    result = getEnclosingVariable(expr).(StaticInitializedStaticLocalVariable)

method.name: getChild
method.body:
id = 0 and result = this.getLeftOperand()
    or
    id = 1 and result = this.getRightOperand()

method.name: getChildTrueSuccessor
method.body:
child = this.getLeftOperand() and
    result = this.getRightOperand().getFirstInstruction(kind)
    or
    child = this.getRightOperand() and
    result = this.getConditionContext().getChildTrueSuccessor(this, kind)

method.name: getChildFalseSuccessor
method.body:
(child = this.getLeftOperand() or child = this.getRightOperand()) and
    result = this.getConditionContext().getChildFalseSuccessor(this, kind)

method.name: getChildTrueSuccessor
method.body:
(child = this.getLeftOperand() or child = this.getRightOperand()) and
    result = this.getConditionContext().getChildTrueSuccessor(this, kind)

method.name: getChildFalseSuccessor
method.body:
child = this.getLeftOperand() and
    result = this.getRightOperand().getFirstInstruction(kind)
    or
    child = this.getRightOperand() and
    result = this.getConditionContext().getChildFalseSuccessor(this, kind)

method.name: getChildSuccessorInternal
getInstructionSuccessorInternal
method.body:
this.shouldGenerateCompareNE() and
    (
      tag = ValueConditionConstantTag() and
      kind instanceof GotoEdge and
      result = this.getInstruction(ValueConditionCompareTag())
      or
      tag = ValueConditionCompareTag() and
      kind instanceof GotoEdge and
      result = this.getInstruction(ValueConditionConditionalBranchTag())
    )
    or
    tag = ValueConditionConditionalBranchTag() and
    (
      kind instanceof TrueEdge and
      result = this.getConditionContext().getChildTrueSuccessor(this, any(GotoEdge edge))
      or
      kind instanceof FalseEdge and
      result = this.getConditionContext().getChildFalseSuccessor(this, any(GotoEdge edge))
    )

method.name: getInstructionRegisterOperand
method.body:
this.shouldGenerateCompareNE() and
    tag = ValueConditionCompareTag() and
    (
      operandTag instanceof LeftOperandTag and
      result = this.getValueExpr().getResult()
      or
      operandTag instanceof RightOperandTag and
      result = this.getInstruction(ValueConditionConstantTag())
    )
    or
    tag = ValueConditionConditionalBranchTag() and
    operandTag instanceof ConditionOperandTag and
    if this.shouldGenerateCompareNE()
    then result = this.getInstruction(ValueConditionCompareTag())
    else result = this.getValueExpr().getResult()

predicate.name: hasInstruction
predicate.body:
this.shouldGenerateCompareNE() and
    (
      tag = ValueConditionCompareTag() and
      opcode instanceof Opcode::CompareNE and
      resultType = getBoolType()
      or
      tag = ValueConditionConstantTag() and
      opcode instanceof Opcode::Constant and
      resultType = getTypeForPRValue(this.getValueExprType())
    )
    or
    tag = ValueConditionConditionalBranchTag() and
    opcode instanceof Opcode::ConditionalBranch and
    resultType = getVoidType()

class.comments:
Represents the IR translation of a declaration within the body of a function.
Most often, this is the declaration of an automatic local variable, although
it can also be the declaration of a static local variable. Declarations of extern variables and
functions do not have a `TranslatedDeclarationEntry`.

method.name: getFunction
method.body:
exists(DeclStmt stmt | stmt = entry.getStmt() |
      result = entry.getDeclaration().(StaticInitializedStaticLocalVariable)
      or
      result = entry.getDeclaration().(GlobalOrNamespaceVariable)
      or
      not entry.getDeclaration() instanceof StaticInitializedStaticLocalVariable and
      not entry.getDeclaration() instanceof GlobalOrNamespaceVariable and
      result = stmt.getEnclosingFunction()
    )

class.comments:
Represents the IR translation of the declaration of a local variable,
including its initialization, if any.

method.name: getVariable
method.comments: Gets the local variable being declared.

class.comments: The IR translation of a local variable declaration within a declaration statement.

The IR translation of the declaration of a static local variable.
This element generates the logic that determines whether or not the variable has already been
initialized, and if not, invokes the initializer and sets the dynamic initialization flag for the
variable. The actual initialization code is handled in
`TranslatedStaticLocalVariableInitialization`, which is a child of this element.

The generated code to do the initialization only once is:
```
Block 1
r1225_1(glval<bool>) = VariableAddress[c#init] :
r1225_2(bool)        = Load                    : &:r1225_1, ~mu1222_4
v1225_3(void)        = ConditionalBranch       : r1225_2
False -> Block 2
True -> Block 3

Block 2
r1225_4(glval<int>) = VariableAddress[c] :
<actual initialization of `c`>
r1225_8(bool)       = Constant[1]        :
mu1225_9(bool)      = Store              : &:r1225_1, r1225_8
Goto -> Block 3

Block 3
```

Note that the flag variable, `c#init`, is assumed to be zero-initialized at program startup, just
like any other variable with static storage duration.

method.name: getInstructionSuccessorInternal
method.body:
tag = DynamicInitializationFlagAddressTag() and
    kind instanceof GotoEdge and
    result = this.getInstruction(DynamicInitializationFlagLoadTag())
    or
    tag = DynamicInitializationFlagLoadTag() and
    kind instanceof GotoEdge and
    result = this.getInstruction(DynamicInitializationConditionalBranchTag())
    or
    tag = DynamicInitializationConditionalBranchTag() and
    (
      kind instanceof TrueEdge and
      result = this.getParent().getChildSuccessor(this, any(GotoEdge edge))
      or
      kind instanceof FalseEdge and
      result = this.getInitialization().getFirstInstruction(any(GotoEdge edge))
    )
    or
    tag = DynamicInitializationFlagConstantTag() and
    kind instanceof GotoEdge and
    result = this.getInstruction(DynamicInitializationFlagStoreTag())
    or
    tag = DynamicInitializationFlagStoreTag() and
    result = this.getParent().getChildSuccessor(this, kind)

method.name: getChildSuccessorInternal
method.body:
child = this.getInitialization() and
    result = this.getInstruction(DynamicInitializationFlagConstantTag()) and
    kind instanceof GotoEdge

method.name: getInstructionRegisterOperand
method.body:
tag = DynamicInitializationFlagLoadTag() and
    (
      operandTag instanceof AddressOperandTag and
      result = this.getInstruction(DynamicInitializationFlagAddressTag())
    )
    or
    tag = DynamicInitializationConditionalBranchTag() and
    operandTag instanceof ConditionOperandTag and
    result = this.getInstruction(DynamicInitializationFlagLoadTag())
    or
    tag = DynamicInitializationFlagStoreTag() and
    (
      operandTag instanceof AddressOperandTag and
      result = this.getInstruction(DynamicInitializationFlagAddressTag())
      or
      operandTag instanceof StoreValueOperandTag and
      result = this.getInstruction(DynamicInitializationFlagConstantTag())
    )

predicate.name: hasInstruction
predicate.body:
tag = DynamicInitializationFlagAddressTag() and
    opcode instanceof Opcode::VariableAddress and
    type = getBoolGLValueType()
    or
    tag = DynamicInitializationFlagLoadTag() and
    opcode instanceof Opcode::Load and
    type = getBoolType()
    or
    tag = DynamicInitializationConditionalBranchTag() and
    opcode instanceof Opcode::ConditionalBranch and
    type = getVoidType()
    or
    tag = DynamicInitializationFlagConstantTag() and
    opcode instanceof Opcode::Constant and
    type = getBoolType()
    or
    tag = DynamicInitializationFlagStoreTag() and
    opcode instanceof Opcode::Store and
    type = getBoolType()

class.comments:
The initialization of a static local variable. This element will only exist for a static variable
with a dynamic initializer.

Represents an AST node for which IR needs to be generated.

In most cases, there is a single `TranslatedElement` for each AST node.
However, when a single AST node performs two separable operations (e.g.
a `VariableAccess` that is also a load), there may be multiple
`TranslatedElement` nodes for a single AST node.

method.name: getAst
method.comments: Gets the AST node being translated.

method.name: getLocation
method.comments: Gets the location of this element.

method.name: getFirstInstruction
method.comments:
Get the first instruction to be executed in the evaluation of this
element when the edge kind is `kind`.

method.name: getAChild
method.comments: Get the immediate child elements of this element.

method.name: getChild
method.comments:
Gets the immediate child element of this element. The `id` is unique
among all children of this element, but the values are not necessarily
consecutive.

method.name: getId
method.comments:
Gets the an identifier string for the element. This id is unique within
the scope of the element's function.

method.name: getFirstDestructorCallIndex
method.comments: Gets the child index of the first destructor call that should be executed after this `TranslatedElement`

method.name: getFunction
method.comments: Gets the `Function` that contains this element.

method.name: getInstructionSuccessorInternal
method.comments:
Gets the successor instruction of the instruction that was generated by
this element for tag `tag`. The successor edge kind is specified by `kind`.
This predicate does not usually include destructors, which are inserted as
part of `getInstructionSuccessor` unless `handlesDestructorsExplicitly`
holds.

method.name: getInstructionSuccessor
method.body:
if
      this.hasAnImplicitDestructorCall() and
      this.getInstruction(tag) = this.getALastInstructionInternal() and
      not this.handlesDestructorsExplicitly()
    then
      result = this.getChild(this.getFirstDestructorCallIndex()).getFirstInstruction(kind) and
      kind instanceof GotoEdge
    else result = this.getInstructionSuccessorInternal(tag, kind)
method.comments:
Gets the successor instruction of the instruction that was generated by
this element for tag `tag`. The successor edge kind is specified by `kind`.

method.name: getALastInstruction
method.comments:
Gets an instruction within this `TranslatedElement` (including its transitive children) which
will be followed by an instruction outside the `TranslatedElement`.

method.name: getALastInstructionInternal
method.comments:
Gets an instruction within this `TranslatedElement` (including its transitive children) which
will be followed by an instruction outside the `TranslatedElement`.
This predicate does not usually include destructors, which are inserted as
part of `getALastInstruction` unless `handlesDestructorsExplicitly` holds.

method.name: getChildSuccessorInternal
method.comments:
Gets the successor instruction to which control should flow after the
child element specified by `child` has finished execution. The successor
edge kind is specified by `kind`.
This predicate does not usually include destructors, which are inserted as
part of `getChildSuccessor` unless `handlesDestructorsExplicitly` holds.

method.name: getChildSuccessor
method.comments:
Gets the successor instruction to which control should flow after the
child element specified by `child` has finished execution. The successor
edge kind is specified by `kind`.

method.name: getExceptionSuccessorInstruction
method.comments:
Gets the instruction to which control should flow if an exception is thrown
within this element. This will generally return first `catch` block of the
nearest enclosing `try`, or the `Unwind` instruction for the function if
there is no enclosing `try`. The successor edge kind is specified by `kind`.

method.name: getPrimaryInstructionForSideEffect
method.comments:
Gets the primary instruction for the side effect instruction that was
generated by this element for tag `tag`.

method.name: getInstructionFunction
method.comments:
If the instruction specified by `tag` is a `FunctionInstruction`, gets the
`Function` for that instruction.

method.name: getInstructionVariable
method.comments:
If the instruction specified by `tag` is a `VariableInstruction`, gets the
`IRVariable` for that instruction.

method.name: getInstructionField
method.comments:
If the instruction specified by `tag` is a `FieldInstruction`, gets the
`Field` for that instruction.

method.name: getInstructionConstantValue
method.comments:
If the instruction specified by `tag` is a `ConstantValueInstruction`, gets
the constant value for that instruction.

method.name: getInstructionIndex
method.comments:
If the instruction specified by `tag` is an `IndexedInstruction`, gets the
index for that instruction.

method.name: getInstructionElementSize
method.comments:
If the instruction specified by `tag` is a `PointerArithmeticInstruction`,
