predicate.name: parameterNeverEscapes
parameterEscapesOnlyViaReturn
predicate.comments:
Holds if the address passed to the parameter at the specified index escapes via the return
value of the function, but does not otherwise escape. See the comment for
`parameterNeverEscapes` for an example.

predicate.name: parameterIsAlwaysReturned
predicate.comments: Holds if the function always returns the value of the parameter at the specified index.

predicate.name: hasAddressFlow
predicate.comments: Holds if the address passed in via `input` is always propagated to `output`.

class.comments: An allocation expression such as call to `malloc` or a `new` expression.

method.name: getSizeExpr
method.comments:
Gets an expression for the allocation size, if any. The actual allocation
size is the value of this expression multiplied by the result of
`getSizeMult()`, in bytes.

method.name: getSizeMult
method.comments:
Gets a constant multiplier for the allocation size given by `getSizeExpr`,
in bytes.

method.name: getSizeBytes
method.comments:
Gets the size of this allocation in bytes, if it is a fixed size and that
size can be determined.

method.name: getReallocPtr
method.comments:
Gets the expression for the input pointer argument to be reallocated, if
this is a `realloc` function.

method.name: getAllocatedElementType
method.comments: Gets the type of the elements that are allocated, if it can be determined.

predicate.name: requiresDealloc
predicate.comments:
Whether or not this allocation requires a corresponding deallocation of
some sort (most do, but `alloca` for example does not).  If it is unclear,
we default to no (for example a placement `new` allocation may or may not
require a corresponding `delete`).

class.comments:
An allocation function such as `malloc`.

Note: `AllocationExpr` includes calls to allocation functions, so prefer
to use that class unless you specifically need to reason about functions.

method.name: getSizeArg
method.comments:
Gets the index of the argument for the allocation size, if any. The actual
allocation size is the value of this argument multiplied by the result of
`getSizeMult()`, in bytes.

method.name: getSizeMult
method.comments:
Gets the index of an argument that multiplies the allocation size given by
`getSizeArg`, if any.

method.name: getReallocPtrArg
method.comments:
Gets the index of the input pointer argument to be reallocated, if this
is a `realloc` function.

predicate.name: requiresDealloc
predicate.comments:
Whether or not this allocation requires a corresponding deallocation of
some sort (most do, but `alloca` for example does not).  If it is unclear,
we default to no (for example a placement `new` allocation may or may not
require a corresponding `delete`).

class.comments:
An `operator new` or `operator new[]` function that may be associated with
`new` or `new[]` expressions.  Note that `new` and `new[]` are not function
calls, but these functions may also be called directly.

method.name: getPlacementArgument
method.body:
this.getNumberOfParameters() = 2 and
    this.getParameter(1).getType() instanceof VoidPointerType and
    result = 1
method.comments:
Gets the position of the placement pointer if this is a placement
`operator new` function.

predicate.name: OperatorNewAllocationFunction
predicate.body:
this.hasGlobalName([
        "operator new", // operator new(bytes, ...)
        "operator new[]" // operator new[](bytes, ...)
      ])

class.comments:
An expression that _might_ allocate memory.

Unlike `AllocationExpr`, this class uses heuristics (such as a call target's
name and parameters) to include additional expressions.

method.name: getSizeExpr
method.comments:
Gets an expression for the allocation size, if any. The actual allocation
size is the value of this expression multiplied by the result of
`getSizeMult()`, in bytes.

method.name: getSizeMult
method.comments:
Gets a constant multiplier for the allocation size given by `getSizeExpr`,
in bytes. This predicate should be used with caution as it can be
inaccurate for allocations identified using heuristics.

method.name: getSizeBytes
method.comments:
Gets the size of this allocation in bytes, if it is a fixed size and that
size can be determined. This predicate should be used with caution as it
can be inaccurate for allocations identified using heuristics.

method.name: getReallocPtr
method.comments:
Gets the expression for the input pointer argument to be reallocated, if
this is a `realloc` function.

method.name: getAllocatedElementType
method.comments: Gets the type of the elements that are allocated, if it can be determined.

predicate.name: requiresDealloc
predicate.comments:
Whether or not this allocation requires a corresponding deallocation of
some sort (most do, but `alloca` for example does not).  If it is unclear,
we default to no (for example a placement `new` allocation may or may not
require a corresponding `delete`).

class.comments:
An function that _might_ allocate memory.

Unlike `AllocationFunction`, this class uses heuristics (such as the function's
name and its parameters) to include additional functions.

method.name: getSizeArg
method.comments:
Gets the index of the argument for the allocation size, if any. The actual
allocation size is the value of this argument multiplied by the result of
`getSizeMult()`, in bytes.

method.name: getSizeMult
method.comments:
Gets the index of an argument that multiplies the allocation size given by
`getSizeArg`, if any.

method.name: getReallocPtrArg
method.comments:
Gets the index of the input pointer argument to be reallocated, if this
is a `realloc` function.

predicate.name: requiresDealloc
predicate.comments:
Whether or not this allocation requires a corresponding deallocation of
some sort (most do, but `alloca` for example does not).  If it is unclear,
we default to no (for example a placement `new` allocation may or may not
require a corresponding `delete`).

class.comments: A library function with input and/or output buffer parameters

predicate.name: hasArrayWithNullTerminator
predicate.comments:
Holds if parameter `bufParam` is a null-terminated buffer and the
null-terminator will not be written past.

predicate.name: hasArrayWithFixedSize
predicate.comments:
Holds if parameter `bufParam` should always point to a buffer with exactly
`elemCount` elements.

predicate.name: hasArrayWithVariableSize
predicate.comments:
Holds if parameter `bufParam` should always point to a buffer with the
number of elements indicated by `countParam`.

predicate.name: hasArrayWithUnknownSize
predicate.comments:
Holds if parameter `bufParam` points to a buffer with no fixed size and no
size parameter, which is not null-terminated or which is null-terminated
but for which the null value may be written past.  For example, the first
parameters of `sprintf` and `strcat`.

predicate.name: hasArrayInput
predicate.comments:
Holds if parameter `bufParam` is used as an input buffer.

Note that this is not mutually exclusive with isOutBuffer.

predicate.name: hasArrayOutput
predicate.comments:
Holds if parameter `bufParam` is used as an output buffer.

Note that this is not mutually exclusive with isInBuffer.

class.comments:
A function, such as `exec` or `popen` that starts a new process by
interpreting a string as a shell command.

predicate.name: hasCommandArgument
predicate.comments: Holds if `input` is interpreted as a shell command.

class.comments:
A library function for which a value is or may be copied from a parameter
or qualifier to an output buffer, return value, or qualifier.

Note that this does not include partial copying of values or partial writes
to destinations; that is covered by `TaintModel.qll`.

predicate.name: hasDataFlow
predicate.comments:
Holds if data can be copied from the argument, qualifier, or buffer
represented by `input` to the return value or buffer represented by
`output`

class.comments: An deallocation expression such as call to `free` or a `delete` expression.

method.name: getFreedExpr
method.comments: Gets the expression that is freed by this function.

class.comments:
A deallocation function such as `free`.

Note: `DeallocationExpr` includes calls to deallocation functions, so prefer
to use that class unless you specifically need to reason about functions.

method.name: getFreedArg
method.comments: Gets the index of the argument that is freed by this function.

class.comments:
An `operator delete` or `operator delete[]` function that may be associated
with `delete` or `delete[]` expressions.  Note that `delete` and `delete[]`
are not function calls, but these functions may also be called directly.

predicate.name: OperatorDeleteDeallocationFunction
predicate.body:
this.hasGlobalName([
        "operator delete", // operator delete(pointer, ...)
        "operator delete[]" // operator delete[](pointer, ...)
      ])

class.comments: A library function that returns data that may be read from a network connection.

predicate.name: hasRemoteFlowSource
predicate.comments: Holds if remote data described by `description` flows from `output` of a call to this function.

predicate.name: hasSocketInput
predicate.comments:
Holds if remote data from this source comes from a socket or stream
described by `input`. There is no result if none is specified by a
parameter.

class.comments: A library function that returns data that is directly controlled by a user.

predicate.name: hasLocalFlowSource
predicate.comments: Holds if data described by `description` flows from `output` of a call to this function.

class.comments: A library function that sends data over a network connection.

predicate.name: hasRemoteFlowSink
predicate.comments:
Holds if data described by `description` flows into `input` to a call to this function, and is then
send over a network connection.

predicate.name: hasSocketInput
predicate.comments:
Holds if data put into this sink is transmitted through a socket or stream
described by `input`. There is no result if none is specified by a
parameter.

class.comments: A standard library function that uses a `printf`-like formatting string.

method.name: getFormatParameterIndex
method.comments: Gets the position at which the format parameter occurs.

method.name: getFormatCharType
method.comments: Gets the character type used in the format string for this function.

method.name: getDefaultCharType
method.body:
this.isMicrosoft() and
    result = this.getFormatCharType()
    or
    not this.isMicrosoft() and
    result instanceof PlainCharType
method.comments:
Gets the default character type expected for `%s` by this function.  Typically
`char` or `wchar_t`.

method.name: getNonDefaultCharType
method.body:
this.getDefaultCharType().getSize() = 1 and
    result = this.getWideCharType()
    or
    not this.getDefaultCharType().getSize() = 1 and
    result instanceof PlainCharType
method.comments:
Gets the non-default character type expected for `%S` by this function.  Typically
`wchar_t` or `char`.  On some snapshots there may be multiple results where we can't tell
which is correct for a particular function.

method.name: getWideCharType
method.body:
result = this.getFormatCharType() and
    result.getSize() > 1
    or
    not this.getFormatCharType().getSize() > 1 and
    result = getAFormatterWideTypeOrDefault() // may have more than one result
method.comments:
Gets the wide character type for this function.  This is usually `wchar_t`.  On some
snapshots there may be multiple results where we can't tell which is correct for a
particular function.

method.name: getOutputParameterIndex
method.comments:
Gets the position at which the output parameter, if any, occurs. If
`isStream` is `true`, the output parameter is a stream (that is, this
function behaves like `fprintf`). If `isStream` is `false`, the output
parameter is a buffer (that is, this function behaves like `sprintf`).

method.name: getFirstFormatArgumentIndex
method.comments:
Gets the position of the first format argument, corresponding with
the first format specifier in the format string. We ignore all
implicit function definitions.

method.name: getSizeParameterIndex
method.comments: Gets the position of the buffer size argument, if any.

predicate.name: FormattingFunction
predicate.body:
firstFormatArgumentIndex > 0 and
    if this.hasDefinition()
    then firstFormatArgumentIndex = this.getDefinition().getNumberOfParameters()
    else
      if this instanceof BuiltInFunction
      then firstFormatArgumentIndex = this.getNumberOfParameters()
      else
        forex(FunctionDeclarationEntry fde | fde = this.getAnExplicitDeclarationEntry() |
          firstFormatArgumentIndex = fde.getNumberOfParameters()
        )

predicate.name: isMicrosoft
predicate.comments:
Holds if this `FormattingFunction` is in a context that supports
Microsoft rules and extensions.

predicate.name: isOutputGlobal
predicate.comments:
Holds if this function outputs to a global stream such as standard output,
standard error or a system log. For example `printf`.

predicate.name: hasTaintFlow
predicate.body:
exists(int arg |
      arg = this.getFormatParameterIndex() or
      arg >= this.getFirstFormatArgumentIndex()
    |
      (input.isParameterDeref(arg) or input.isParameter(arg)) and
      output.isParameterDeref(this.getOutputParameterIndex(_))
    )

predicate.name: isPartialWrite

class.comments:
The standard functions `snprintf` and `swprintf`, and their
Microsoft and glib variants.

predicate.name: returnsFullFormatLength
predicate.comments:
Holds if this function returns the length of the formatted string
that would have been output, regardless of the amount of space
in the buffer.

class.comments:
An input to a function. This can be:
- The value of one of the function's parameters
- The value pointed to by one of function's pointer or reference parameters
- The value of the function's `this` pointer
- The value pointed to by the function's `this` pointer

method.name: getIndirectionInput
method.comments:
INTERNAL: Do not use.

Gets the `FunctionInput` that represents the indirection of this input,
if any.

predicate.name: isParameter
predicate.comments:
Holds if this is the input value of the parameter with index `index`.

Example:
```
void func(int n, char* p, float& r);
```
- `isParameter(0)` holds for the `FunctionInput` that represents the value of `n` (with type
`int`) on entry to the function.
- `isParameter(1)` holds for the `FunctionInput` that represents the value of `p` (with type
`char*`) on entry to the function.
- `isParameter(2)` holds for the `FunctionInput` that represents the "value" of the reference
`r` (with type `float&`) on entry to the function, _not_ the value of the referred-to
`float`.

predicate.name: isParameterDeref
predicate.comments:
Holds if this is the input value pointed to (through `ind` number of indirections) by a
pointer parameter to a function, or the input value referred to by a reference parameter
to a function, where the parameter has index `index`.

Example:
```
void func(int n, char* p, float& r);
```
- `isParameterDeref(1, 1)` holds for the `FunctionInput` that represents the value of `*p` (with
type `char`) on entry to the function.
- `isParameterDeref(2, 1)` holds for the `FunctionInput` that represents the value of `r` (with type
`float`) on entry to the function.
- There is no `FunctionInput` for which `isParameterDeref(0, _)` holds, because `n` is neither a
pointer nor a reference.

predicate.name: isParameterDeref
predicate.comments:
Holds if this is the input value pointed to by a pointer parameter to a function, or the input
value referred to by a reference parameter to a function, where the parameter has index
`index`.

Example:
```
void func(int n, char* p, float& r);
```
- `isParameterDeref(1)` holds for the `FunctionInput` that represents the value of `*p` (with
type `char`) on entry to the function.
- `isParameterDeref(2)` holds for the `FunctionInput` that represents the value of `r` (with type
`float`) on entry to the function.
- There is no `FunctionInput` for which `isParameterDeref(0)` holds, because `n` is neither a
pointer nor a reference.

predicate.name: isQualifierObject
isQualifierObject
isQualifierAddress
isParameterOrQualifierAddress
predicate.body:
i >= 0 and this.isParameter(i)
    or
    i = -1 and this.isQualifierAddress()
predicate.comments:
Holds if `i >= 0` and `isParameter(i)` holds for this value, or
if `i = -1` and `isQualifierAddress()` holds for this value.

predicate.name: isReturnValueDeref
predicate.comments:
Holds if this is the input value pointed to by the return value of a
function, if the function returns a pointer, or the input value referred
to by the return value of a function, if the function returns a reference.

Example:
```
char* getPointer();
float& getReference();
int getInt();
```
- `isReturnValueDeref()` holds for the `FunctionInput` that represents the
value of `*getPointer()` (with type `char`).
- `isReturnValueDeref()` holds for the `FunctionInput` that represents the
value of `getReference()` (with type `float`).
- There is no `FunctionInput` of `getInt()` for which
`isReturnValueDeref()` holds because the return type of `getInt()` is
neither a pointer nor a reference.

Note that data flows in through function return values are relatively
rare, but they do occur when a function returns a reference to itself,
part of itself, or one of its other inputs.

predicate.name: isReturnValueDeref
predicate.comments:
Holds if this is the input value pointed to by the return value of a
function, if the function returns a pointer, or the input value referred
to by the return value of a function, if the function returns a reference.

Example:
```
char* getPointer();
float& getReference();
int getInt();
```
- `isReturnValueDeref(1)` holds for the `FunctionInput` that represents the
value of `*getPointer()` (with type `char`).
- `isReturnValueDeref(1)` holds for the `FunctionInput` that represents the
value of `getReference()` (with type `float`).
- There is no `FunctionInput` of `getInt()` for which
`isReturnValueDeref(_)` holds because the return type of `getInt()` is
neither a pointer nor a reference.

Note that data flows in through function return values are relatively
rare, but they do occur when a function returns a reference to itself,
part of itself, or one of its other inputs.

predicate.name: isParameterDerefOrQualifierObject
predicate.body:
i >= 0 and this.isParameterDeref(i, ind)
    or
    i = -1 and this.isQualifierObject(ind)
predicate.comments:
Holds if `i >= 0` and `isParameterDeref(i, ind)` holds for this value, or
if `i = -1` and `isQualifierObject(ind)` holds for this value.

predicate.name: isParameterDerefOrQualifierObject
predicate.comments:
Holds if `i >= 0` and `isParameterDeref(i)` holds for this value, or
if `i = -1` and `isQualifierObject()` holds for this value.

class.comments:
The input value of a parameter.

Example:
```
void func(int n, char* p, float& r);
```
- There is an `InParameter` representing the value of `n` (with type `int`) on entry to the
function.
- There is an `InParameter` representing the value of `p` (with type `char*`) on entry to the
function.
- There is an `InParameter` representing the "value" of the reference `r` (with type `float&`) on
entry to the function, _not_ the value of the referred-to `float`.

method.name: getIndex
method.comments: Gets the zero-based index of the parameter.

class.comments:
The input value pointed to by a pointer parameter to a function, or the input value referred to
by a reference parameter to a function.

Example:
```
void func(int n, char* p, float& r);
```
- There is an `InParameterDeref` with `getIndex() = 1` that represents the value of `*p` (with
type `char`) on entry to the function.
- There is an `InParameterDeref` with `getIndex() = 2` that represents the value of `r` (with
type `float`) on entry to the function.
- There is no `InParameterDeref` representing the value of `n`, because `n` is neither a pointer
nor a reference.

method.name: getIndex
method.comments: Gets the zero-based index of the parameter.

class.comments:
The input value pointed to by the return value of a function, if the
function returns a pointer, or the input value referred to by the return
value of a function, if the function returns a reference.

Example:
```
char* getPointer();
float& getReference();
int getInt();
```
- `InReturnValueDeref` represents the value of `*getPointer()` (with type
`char`).
- `InReturnValueDeref` represents the value of `getReference()` (with type
`float`).
- `InReturnValueDeref` does not represent the return value of `getInt()`
because the return type of `getInt()` is neither a pointer nor a reference.

Note that data flows in through function return values are relatively
rare, but they do occur when a function returns a reference to itself,
part of itself, or one of its other inputs.

An output from a function. This can be:
- The value pointed to by one of function's pointer or reference parameters
- The value pointed to by the function's `this` pointer
- The function's return value
- The value pointed to by the function's return value, if the return value is a pointer or
reference

method.name: getIndirectionOutput
method.comments:
INTERNAL: Do not use.

Gets the `FunctionOutput` that represents the indirection of this output,
if any.

predicate.name: isParameterDeref
predicate.comments:
Holds if this is the output value pointed to by a pointer parameter to a function, or the
output value referred to by a reference parameter to a function, where the parameter has
index `i`.

Example:
```
void func(int n, char* p, float& r);
```
- `isParameterDeref(1)` holds for the `FunctionOutput` that represents the value of `*p` (with
type `char`) on return from the function.
- `isParameterDeref(2)` holds for the `FunctionOutput` that represents the value of `r` (with
type `float`) on return from the function.
- There is no `FunctionOutput` for which `isParameterDeref(0)` holds, because `n` is neither a
pointer nor a reference.

predicate.name: isParameterDeref
predicate.comments:
Holds if this is the output value pointed to by a pointer parameter (through `ind` number
of indirections) to a function, or the output value referred to by a reference parameter to
a function, where the parameter has index `i`.

Example:
```
void func(int n, char* p, float& r);
```
- `isParameterDeref(1, 1)` holds for the `FunctionOutput` that represents the value of `*p` (with
type `char`) on return from the function.
- `isParameterDeref(2, 1)` holds for the `FunctionOutput` that represents the value of `r` (with
type `float`) on return from the function.
- There is no `FunctionOutput` for which `isParameterDeref(0, _)` holds, because `n` is neither a
pointer nor a reference.

predicate.name: isQualifierObject
isQualifierObject
isReturnValue
predicate.comments:
Holds if this is the value returned by a function.

Example:
```
int getInt();
char* getPointer();
float& getReference();
```
- `isReturnValue()` holds for the `FunctionOutput` that represents the value returned by
`getInt()` (with type `int`).
- `isReturnValue()` holds for the `FunctionOutput` that represents the value returned by
`getPointer()` (with type `char*`).
- `isReturnValue()` holds for the `FunctionOutput` that represents the "value" of the reference
returned by `getReference()` (with type `float&`), _not_ the value of the referred-to
`float`.

predicate.name: isReturnValueDeref
predicate.comments:
Holds if this is the output value pointed to by the return value of a function, if the function
returns a pointer, or the output value referred to by the return value of a function, if the
function returns a reference.

Example:
```
char* getPointer();
float& getReference();
int getInt();
```
- `isReturnValueDeref()` holds for the `FunctionOutput` that represents the value of
`*getPointer()` (with type `char`).
- `isReturnValueDeref()` holds for the `FunctionOutput` that represents the value of
`getReference()` (with type `float`).
- There is no `FunctionOutput` of `getInt()` for which `isReturnValueDeref()` holds because the
return type of `getInt()` is neither a pointer nor a reference.

predicate.name: isReturnValueDeref
predicate.comments:
Holds if this is the output value pointed to by the return value of a function, if the function
returns a pointer, or the output value referred to by the return value of a function, if the
function returns a reference.

Example:
```
char* getPointer();
float& getReference();
int getInt();
```
- `isReturnValueDeref(1)` holds for the `FunctionOutput` that represents the value of
`*getPointer()` (with type `char`).
- `isReturnValueDeref(1)` holds for the `FunctionOutput` that represents the value of
`getReference()` (with type `float`).
- There is no `FunctionOutput` of `getInt()` for which `isReturnValueDeref(_)` holds because the
return type of `getInt()` is neither a pointer nor a reference.

predicate.name: isParameterDerefOrQualifierObject
predicate.body:
i >= 0 and this.isParameterDeref(i, ind)
    or
    i = -1 and this.isQualifierObject(ind)
predicate.comments:
Holds if `i >= 0` and `isParameterDeref(i, ind)` holds for this is the value, or
if `i = -1` and `isQualifierObject(ind)` holds for this value.

predicate.name: isParameterDerefOrQualifierObject
predicate.comments:
Holds if `i >= 0` and `isParameterDeref(i)` holds for this is the value, or
if `i = -1` and `isQualifierObject()` holds for this value.

class.comments:
The output value pointed to by a pointer parameter to a function, or the output value referred to
by a reference parameter to a function.

Example:
```
void func(int n, char* p, float& r);
```
- There is an `OutParameterDeref` with `getIndex()=1` that represents the value of `*p` (with
type `char`) on return from the function.
- There is an `OutParameterDeref` with `getIndex()=2` that represents the value of `r` (with
type `float`) on return from the function.
- There is no `OutParameterDeref` representing the value of `n`, because `n` is neither a
pointer nor a reference.

The value returned by a function.

Example:
```
int getInt();
char* getPointer();
float& getReference();
```
- `OutReturnValue` represents the value returned by
`getInt()` (with type `int`).
- `OutReturnValue` represents the value returned by
`getPointer()` (with type `char*`).
- `OutReturnValue` represents the "value" of the reference returned by `getReference()` (with
type `float&`), _not_ the value of the referred-to `float`.

The output value pointed to by the return value of a function, if the function returns a pointer,
or the output value referred to by the return value of a function, if the function returns a
reference.

Example:
```
char* getPointer();
float& getReference();
int getInt();
```
- `OutReturnValueDeref` represents the value of `*getPointer()` (with type `char`).
- `OutReturnValueDeref` represents the value of `getReference()` (with type `float`).
- `OutReturnValueDeref` does not represent the return value of `getInt()` because the return type
of `getInt()` is neither a pointer nor a reference.

A function which takes an iterator argument and returns a reference that
can be used to write to the iterator's underlying collection.
A function which takes a container and returns an iterator over that container.

predicate.name: getsIterator
predicate.comments:
Holds if the return value or buffer represented by `output` is an iterator over the container
passed in the argument, qualifier, or buffer represented by `input`.

class.comments:
A type which can be used as an iterator.

Note: Do _not_ `extend` when inheriting from this class in queries. Always use `instanceof`:
```
class MyIterator instanceof Iterator { ... }
```

method.name: getValueType
method.comments:
Gets the value type of this iterator, if any.

For example, the value type of a `std::vector<int>::iterator` is `int`.

class.comments:
A function that is guaranteed to never throw a C++ exception

The function may still raise a structured exception handling (SEH) exception.

A function that is guaranteed to never throw.

DEPRECATED: use `NonCppThrowingFunction` instead.

A function that may update part of a `FunctionOutput`.

For example, the destination argument of `strcat` only updates part of the
argument.

predicate.name: isPartialWrite
predicate.comments:
Holds if the write to output does not overwrite the entire value that was
there before, or does not do so reliably. For example the destination
argument of `strcat` is modified but not overwritten.

class.comments: A class that wraps a pointer type. For example, `std::unique_ptr` and `std::shared_ptr`.

method.name: getAnUnwrapperFunction
method.comments:
Gets a member function of this class that returns the wrapped pointer, if any.

This includes both functions that return the wrapped pointer by value, and functions
that return a reference to the pointed-to object.

method.name: getBaseType
method.comments: Gets the type pointed to by this pointer wrapper type.

predicate.name: pointsToConst
predicate.comments: Holds if the type of the data that is pointed to by this pointer wrapper is `const`.

class.comments: Models the side effects of a library function.

method.name: getParameterSizeIndex
method.comments:
Gets the index of the parameter that indicates the size of the buffer pointed to by the
parameter at index `i`.

predicate.name: hasOnlySpecificReadSideEffects
predicate.comments:
Holds if the function never reads from memory that was defined before entry to the function.
This memory could be from global variables, or from other memory that was reachable from a
pointer that was passed into the function. Input side-effects, and reads from memory that
cannot be visible to the caller (for example a buffer inside an I/O library) are not modeled
here.

predicate.name: hasOnlySpecificWriteSideEffects
predicate.comments:
Holds if the function never writes to memory that remains allocated after the function
returns. This memory could be from global variables, or from other memory that was reachable
from a pointer that was passed into the function. Output side-effects, and writes to memory
that cannot be visible to the caller (for example a buffer inside an I/O library) are not
modeled here.

predicate.name: hasSpecificWriteSideEffect
predicate.comments:
Holds if the value pointed to by the parameter at index `i` is written to. `buffer` is true
if the write may be at an offset. `mustWrite` is true if the write is unconditional.

predicate.name: hasSpecificReadSideEffect
predicate.comments:
Holds if the value pointed to by the parameter at index `i` is read from. `buffer` is true
if the read may be at an offset.

class.comments: An abstract class that represents a function that executes an SQL query.

predicate.name: hasSqlArgument
predicate.comments: Holds if `input` to this function represents SQL code to be executed.

class.comments: An abstract class that represents a function that is a barrier to an SQL query string.

predicate.name: barrierSqlArgument
predicate.comments:
Holds if the `output` is a barrier to the SQL input `input` such that is it safe to pass to
an `SqlExecutionFunction`.

class.comments:
A library function for which a taint-tracking library should propagate taint
from a parameter or qualifier to an output buffer, return value, or qualifier.

An expression is tainted if it could be influenced by an attacker to have
an unusual value.

Note that this does not include direct copying of values; that is covered by
DataFlowModel.qll. If a value is sometimes copied in full, and sometimes
altered (for example copying a string with `strncpy`), this is also considered
data flow.

predicate.name: hasTaintFlow
predicate.comments:
Holds if data passed into the argument, qualifier, or buffer represented by
`input` influences the return value or buffer represented by `output`

class.comments:
A function that is known to raise an exception.

DEPRECATED: use `AlwaysSehThrowingFunction` instead.

predicate.name: mayThrowException
predicate.comments:
Holds if this function may throw an exception during evaluation.
If `unconditional` is `true` the function always throws an exception.

class.comments: A function that unconditionally raises a structured exception handling (SEH) exception.

A string that represents an architecture.
An "architecture" defines the sizes of variable-sized types and
the properties of alignment for fields of various
types. Two are provided out-of-the-box: ILP32 and LP64,
corresponding to gcc's behavior on x86 and amd64.

method.name: pointerSize
method.comments: Gets the size of a pointer, in bits.

method.name: longSize
method.comments: Gets the size of a `long int`, in bits.

method.name: longDoubleSize
method.comments: Gets the size of a `long double`, in bits.

method.name: longLongSize
method.comments: Gets the size of a `long long`, in bits.

method.name: wideCharSize
method.comments: Gets the size of a `wchar_t`, in bits.

method.name: doubleAlign
method.comments: Gets the alignment boundary for doubles, in bits.

method.name: longDoubleAlign
method.comments: Gets the alignment boundary for long doubles, in bits.

method.name: longLongAlign
method.comments: Gets the alignment boundary for long longs, in bits.

method.name: baseClassSize
method.comments:
Gets the bit size of class `cd.getBaseClass()` when used as a base class of
class `cd.getDerivedClass()`.

method.name: integralBitSize
method.body:
t instanceof BoolType and result = 8
    or
    t instanceof CharType and result = 8
    or
    t instanceof WideCharType and result = this.wideCharSize()
    or
    t instanceof Char8Type and result = 8
    or
    t instanceof Char16Type and result = 16
    or
    t instanceof Char32Type and result = 32
    or
    t instanceof ShortType and result = 16
    or
    t instanceof IntType and result = 32
    or
    t instanceof LongType and result = this.longSize()
    or
    t instanceof LongLongType and result = this.longLongSize()
    or
    result = this.enumBitSize(t)
    or
    result = this.integralBitSize(t.(SpecifiedType).getBaseType())
    or
    result = this.integralBitSize(t.(TypedefType).getBaseType())
method.comments: Gets the bit size of type `t`. Only holds if `t` is an integral or enum type.

method.name: enumBitSize
method.body:
result = this.integralBitSize(e.getExplicitUnderlyingType())
    or
    not exists(e.getExplicitUnderlyingType()) and result = 32
method.comments: Gets the bit size of enum type `e`.

method.name: enumAlignment
method.body:
result = this.alignment(e.getExplicitUnderlyingType())
    or
    not exists(e.getExplicitUnderlyingType()) and result = 32
method.comments: Gets the alignment of enum type `e`.

method.name: bitSize
method.body:
result = this.integralBitSize(t)
    or
    t instanceof FloatType and result = 32
    or
    t instanceof DoubleType and result = 64
    or
    t instanceof LongDoubleType and result = this.longDoubleSize()
    or
    t instanceof PointerType and result = this.pointerSize()
    or
    t instanceof ReferenceType and result = this.pointerSize()
    or
    t instanceof FunctionPointerType and result = this.pointerSize()
    or
    result = this.bitSize(t.(SpecifiedType).getBaseType())
    or
    result = this.bitSize(t.(TypedefType).getBaseType())
    or
    exists(ArrayType array | array = t |
      result = array.getArraySize() * this.paddedSize(array.getBaseType())
    )
    or
    result = t.(PaddedType).typeBitSize(this)
method.comments:
Gets the bit size of type `t`; that is, the number of bits a value
with type `t` takes up on this architecture, without any trailing
padding on structs and unions.

method.name: alignment
method.body:
t instanceof BoolType and result = 8
    or
    t instanceof CharType and result = 8
    or
    t instanceof WideCharType and result = this.wideCharSize()
    or
    t instanceof Char8Type and result = 8
    or
    t instanceof Char16Type and result = 16
    or
    t instanceof Char32Type and result = 32
    or
    t instanceof ShortType and result = 16
    or
    t instanceof IntType and result = 32
    or
    t instanceof FloatType and result = 32
    or
    t instanceof DoubleType and result = this.doubleAlign()
    or
    t instanceof LongType and result = this.longSize()
    or
    t instanceof LongDoubleType and result = this.longDoubleAlign()
    or
    t instanceof LongLongType and result = this.longLongAlign()
    or
    t instanceof PointerType and result = this.pointerSize()
    or
    t instanceof FunctionPointerType and result = this.pointerSize()
    or
    t instanceof ReferenceType and result = this.pointerSize()
    or
    result = this.enumAlignment(t)
    or
    result = this.alignment(t.(SpecifiedType).getBaseType())
    or
    result = this.alignment(t.(TypedefType).getBaseType())
    or
    result = this.alignment(t.(ArrayType).getBaseType())
    or
    result = t.(PaddedType).typeAlignment(this)
method.comments:
Gets the desired alignment boundary of type `t` as a struct field
on this architecture, in bits.

method.name: paddedSize
method.comments:
Gets the padded size of type `t` on this architecture; that is,
the number of bits that 'sizeof' should return, taking into account
any trailing padding on top of the bit size.

method.name: wastedSpace
method.body: if t instanceof PaddedType then result = t.(PaddedType).wastedSpace(this) else result = 0
method.comments:
Gets the wasted space of type `t`; that is, the number of bits
spent on padding. This is zero for primitive types, and depends on
struct fields and their alignment otherwise. Trailing padding is
counted.

predicate.name: allowHeterogeneousBitfields
predicate.comments:
Holds if this architecture allow bitfields with declared types of different sizes
to be packed together.

class.comments:
Base class for architectures that follow the Itanium ABI. This includes
pretty much everything except Windows, so we'll refer to this as
"UnixArchitecture" to avoid any confusion due to the use of the name
"Itanium".

method.name: baseClassSize

class.comments:
The ILP32 architecture has ints, longs and pointers
of 32 bits.
The LP64 architecture has longs and pointers of 64 bits.
Base class for Windows architectures.

method.name: baseClassSize

class.comments:
The ILP32_MS architecture is essentially the same as the
ILP32 architecture, except that long doubles are 64 bits.

The LLP64_MS architecture has pointers of 64 bits, but both
long and int are still 32 bits.

A class that is subject to padding by the compiler, and hence can
introduce waste. Does not include types with virtual member functions,
virtual base classes, or multiple base classes. These are excluded due
to the complexity of the implementation.

method.name: paddedSize
method.comments:
Gets the padded size of this type on architecture `arch`, in bits.
This is its `bitSize`, rounded up to the next multiple of its
`alignment`.

method.name: trailingPadding
method.comments:
Gets the number of bits wasted by padding at the end of this
struct.

method.name: wastedSpace
method.comments:
Gets the number of bits wasted in this struct definition; that is.
the waste between fields plus any waste from trailing padding.
Only the space wasted directly in this type is counted, not space
wasted in nested structs. Note that for unions, the wasted space
is simply the amount of trailing padding, as other fields are not
laid out one after another, and hence there is no padding between
them.

method.name: dataSize
method.body: result = sum(PaddedType c | c = this.getABaseClass*() | c.fieldDataSize(arch))
method.comments:
Gets the data size of this type on architecture `arch`; that is,
the number of bits taken up by data, rather than any kind of
padding. Padding of fields that have struct type is
considered "data" for the purposes of this definition, since
removing it requires reorganizing other parts of the code.

method.name: optimalSize
method.comments:
Gets the optimal size of this type on architecture `arch`, that is,
the sum of the sizes of all fields, ignoring padding
between them, but adding any trailing padding required to align
the type properly. This is a lower bound on the actual size that
can be achieved just by reordering fields, and without
reorganizing member structs' field layouts.

method.name: typeBitSize
method.comments:
Gets the bit size of this type on architecture `arch`, that is, the
size its fields and required padding take up, without including
any trailing padding that is necessary.

method.name: typeAlignment
method.comments:
Gets the alignment, in bits, of the entire struct/union type for
architecture `arch`.

method.name: biggestFieldSizeUpTo
method.body:
if index = 0
    then result = 0
    else
      exists(Field f, int fSize | index = this.fieldIndex(f) and fSize = this.fieldSize(f, arch) |
        result = fSize.maximum(this.biggestFieldSizeUpTo(index - 1, arch))
      )
method.comments:
Gets the largest size, in bits, of the size of a field with
(1-based) index less than or equal to `index` on architecture
`arch`.

method.name: biggestAlignmentUpTo
method.body:
if index = 0
    then result = 1 // Minimum possible alignment
    else
      exists(Field f, int fAlign |
        index = this.fieldIndex(f) and fAlign = arch.alignment(f.getType())
      |
        result = fAlign.maximum(this.biggestAlignmentUpTo(index - 1, arch))
      )
method.comments:
Gets the largest alignment boundary, in bits, required by a field
with (1-based) index less than or equal to `index` on architecture
`arch`.

method.name: fieldIndex
method.body:
this.memberIndex(f) =
      rank[result](Field field, int index | this.memberIndex(field) = index | index)
method.comments: Gets the 1-based index for each field.

method.name: lastFieldIndex
method.comments: Gets the 1-based index for the last field.

method.name: fieldSize
method.body:
exists(this.fieldIndex(f)) and
    if f instanceof BitField
    then result = f.(BitField).getNumBits()
    else result = arch.paddedSize(f.getType())
method.comments:
Gets the size, in bits, of field `f` on architecture
`arch`.

method.name: lastField
method.comments: Gets the last field of this type.

method.name: baseClassEnd
method.comments:
Gets the offset, in bits, of the end of the class' last base class
subobject, or zero if the class has no base classes.

method.name: fieldEnd
method.comments:
Gets the 0-based offset, in bits, of the first free bit after
field `f` (which is the `index`th field of
this type), taking padding into account, on architecture `arch`.

predicate.name: PaddedType
isPrecise
predicate.comments:
Holds if, for each architecture, a single padded size is
calculated for this type.
This is normally the case, but sometimes the same type can be
defined in different compilations with different sizes, normally
due to use of the preprocessor in its definition.

method.name: resolveToSet
method.body:
exists(int cset, VirtualFunction static |
      this.interesting() and
      parentSetFor(cset, underlyingElement(this)) and
      static = this.staticTarget() and
      childrenByElement(cset, static, result)
    )

method.name: staticTarget
method.body:
exists(Function f, DeleteExpr d |
      f.calls(result, d) and
      d.getExpr() = this
    )
    or
    exists(Function f, FunctionCall c |
      f.calls(result, c) and
      c.getQualifier() = this
    )

predicate.name: interesting
predicate.body:
exists(ExprCall ec | ec.getExpr() = this)
    or
    exists(Call c | c.getQualifier() = this)
    or
    exists(DeleteExpr d | d.getExpr() = this)

method.name: pointsTo
method.body:
this.interesting() and
    exists(int set, @element thisEntity, @element resultEntity |
      thisEntity = underlyingElement(this) and
      pointstosets(set, thisEntity) and
      setlocations(set, resultEntity) and
      resultEntity = localUnresolveElement(result)
    )

predicate.name: interesting
predicate.comments:
This predicate is empty by default. It should be overridden and defined to
include just those expressions for which points-to information is desired.

class.comments: A variable access which cannot be NaN.

The strictness of a relation. Either 'strict' (that is, a `<` or `>`
relation) or 'non-strict' (a `<=` or `>=` relation).

method.name: toString
method.body:
this = Strict() and result = "strict"
    or
    this = Nonstrict() and result = "non-strict"
method.comments: Gets the string representation of this relation strictness.

class.comments:
The SSA logic comes in two versions: the standard SSA and range-analysis RangeSSA.
This class provides the range-analysis SSA logic.

predicate.name: custom_phi_node
predicate.comments: Add a phi node on the out-edge of a guard.

class.comments:
A definition of one or more SSA variables, including phi node
definitions.  An SSA variable is effectively the pair of a definition
and the (non-SSA) variable that it defines.  Note definitions and uses
can be coincident, due to the presence of parameter definitions and phi
nodes.

method.name: getAVariable
method.comments:
Gets a variable corresponding to a SSA StackVariable defined by
this definition.

method.name: toString
method.comments:
A string representation of the SSA variable represented by the pair
`(this, v)`.

method.name: getAUse
method.comments: Gets a use of the SSA variable represented by the pair `(this, v)`.

method.name: getDefinition
method.comments: Gets the control flow node for this definition.

method.name: getBasicBlock
method.comments: Gets the basic block containing this definition.

method.name: getLocation
method.comments: Gets the primary location of this definition.

method.name: getAPhiInput
method.comments: Gets a definition of `v` that is a phi input for this basic block.

method.name: getDefiningValue
method.body:
exists(ControlFlowNode def | def = this.getDefinition() |
      def = v.getInitializer().getExpr() and def = result
      or
      exists(AssignExpr assign |
        def = assign and
        assign.getLValue() = v.getAnAccess() and
        result = assign.getRValue()
      )
      or
      exists(AssignOperation assign |
        def = assign and
        assign.getLValue() = v.getAnAccess() and
        result = assign
      )
    )
method.comments: Gets the expression assigned to this SsaDefinition.

predicate.name: isPhiNode
predicate.comments: Whether this definition is a phi node for variable `v`.

predicate.name: isGuardPhi
predicate.body:
guard_defn(va, guard, this, branch) and
    va.getTarget() = v
predicate.comments:
If this definition is a phi node corresponding to a guard,
then return the variable guarded, the variable access and the guard.

predicate.name: definedByParameter
predicate.comments: Whether this definition is from a parameter

predicate.name: reachesEndOfBB
predicate.comments: Holds if this definition of the variable `v` reached the end of the basic block `b`.

class.comments:
A bitwise `&` expression in which both operands are unsigned, or are effectively
unsigned due to being a non-negative constant.
A `MulExpr` where exactly one operand is constant.

method.name: getConstant
method.comments: Gets the value of the constant operand.

method.name: getOperand
method.comments: Gets the non-constant operand.

predicate.name: MulByConstantExpr
predicate.body:
exists(Expr constantExpr |
      this.hasOperands(constantExpr, operand) and
      constant = getValue(constantExpr.getFullyConverted()).toFloat() and
      not exists(getValue(operand.getFullyConverted()).toFloat())
    )

predicate.name: UnsignedMulExpr

class.comments: Get standard `getc` function or related variants.
A call to `getc`

A call to `getc` that we can analyze because we know
the value of the `EOF` macro.

predicate.name: UnsignedAssignMulExpr

class.comments:
A reason for an inferred bound. This can either be `CondReason` if the bound
is due to a specific condition, or `NoReason` if the bound is inferred
without going through a bounding condition.

method.name: toString
method.comments: Gets a string representation of this reason

class.comments:
A reason for an inferred bound that indicates that the bound is inferred
without going through a bounding condition.
A reason for an inferred bound pointing to a condition.

method.name: getCond
method.comments: Gets the guard condition that caused the inferred bound

class.comments: A bound that may be inferred for an expression plus/minus an integer delta.

method.name: toString
method.comments: Gets a string representation of this bound.

method.name: getAnExpr
method.comments: Gets an expression that equals this bound.

method.name: getAnExpr
method.comments: Gets an expression that equals this bound plus `delta`.

method.name: getLocation
method.comments: Gets a representative locaiton for this bound

class.comments:
The bound that corresponds to the integer 0. This is used to represent all
integer bounds as bounds are always accompanied by an added integer delta.
A bound corresponding to the value of an `Instruction`.

method.name: getValueNumber
method.comments: Gets the value number that equals this bound.

class.comments:
A valid base for an expression bound.

Can be either a variable (`SemSsaBound`) or zero (`SemZeroBound`).
A bound that is a constant zero.
A bound that is an SSA definition.

method.name: getAVariable
method.comments: Gets a variable whose value is used as the bound.

class.comments: A basic block in the control-flow graph.

method.name: getAnExpr
method.comments: Gets an expression that is evaluated in this basic block.

predicate.name: dominates
predicate.comments: Holds if this block (transitively) dominates `otherblock`.

class.comments:
An language-neutral expression.

The expression computes a value of type `getSemType()`. The actual computation is determined by
the expression's opcode (`getOpcode()`).
An expression with an opcode other than `Unknown`.
An expression that returns a literal value.

predicate.name: SemLiteralExpr
predicate.body:
Specific::integerLiteral(this, type, _) and opcode instanceof Opcode::Constant
    or
    Specific::largeIntegerLiteral(this, type, _) and opcode instanceof Opcode::Constant
    or
    Specific::booleanLiteral(this, type, _) and opcode instanceof Opcode::Constant
    or
    Specific::floatingPointLiteral(this, type, _) and opcode instanceof Opcode::Constant
    or
    Specific::nullLiteral(this, type) and opcode instanceof Opcode::Constant
    or
    Specific::stringLiteral(this, type, _) and opcode instanceof Opcode::StringConstant

class.comments: An expression that returns a numeric literal value.

method.name: getApproximateFloatValue
method.comments:
Gets an approximation of the value of the literal, as a `float`.

If the value can be precisely represented as a `float`, the result will be exact. If the actual
value cannot be precisely represented (for example, it is an integer with more than 53
significant bits), then the result is an approximation.

class.comments: An expression that returns an integer literal value.

method.name: getIntValue
method.comments:
Gets the value of the literal, if it can be represented as an `int`.

If the value is outside the range of an `int`, use `getApproximateFloatValue()` to get a value
that is equal to the actual integer value, within rounding error.

class.comments: An expression that returns a floating-point literal value.

method.name: getFloatValue
method.comments: Gets the value of the literal.

class.comments: An expression that consumes two operands.

method.name: getLeftOperand
method.comments: Gets the left operand.

method.name: getRightOperand
method.comments: Gets the right operand.

method.name: getAnOperand
method.comments: Gets the two operands.

predicate.name: hasOperands
predicate.body:
a = this.getLeftOperand() and b = this.getRightOperand()
    or
    a = this.getRightOperand() and b = this.getLeftOperand()
predicate.comments: Holds if `a` and `b` are the two operands, in either order.

class.comments: An expression that performs and ordered comparison of two operands.

method.name: getLesserOperand
method.comments:
Get the operand that will be less than the other operand if the result of the comparison is
`true`.

For `x < y` or `x <= y`, this will return `x`.
For `x > y` or `x >= y`, this will return `y`.`

method.name: getGreaterOperand
method.comments:
Get the operand that will be greater than the other operand if the result of the comparison is
`true`.

For `x < y` or `x <= y`, this will return `y`.
For `x > y` or `x >= y`, this will return `x`.`

predicate.name: SemRelationalExpr
predicate.body:
opcode instanceof Opcode::CompareLT
    or
    opcode instanceof Opcode::CompareLE
    or
    opcode instanceof Opcode::CompareGT
    or
    opcode instanceof Opcode::CompareGE

predicate.name: isStrict
predicate.body: opcode instanceof Opcode::CompareLT or opcode instanceof Opcode::CompareGT
predicate.comments: Holds if this comparison returns `false` if the two operands are equal.

predicate.name: SemAddExpr
predicate.body: opcode instanceof Opcode::Add or opcode instanceof Opcode::PointerAdd

predicate.name: SemSubExpr
predicate.body: opcode instanceof Opcode::Sub or opcode instanceof Opcode::PointerSub

class.comments: A conversion instruction which is guaranteed to not overflow.

predicate.name: SafeConversion
predicate.body:
exists(SemType tFrom, SemType tTo |
      tFrom = getSemanticType(super.getUnary().getResultIRType()) and
      tTo = getSemanticType(super.getResultIRType()) and
      conversionCannotOverflow(tFrom, tTo)
    )

predicate.name: SemCopyValueExpr
predicate.body: opcode instanceof Opcode::CopyValue or this instanceof SafeConversion

method.name: getBranchExpr
method.body:
branch = true and result = trueResult
    or
    branch = false and result = falseResult

predicate.name: SemConditionalExpr
predicate.body:
opcode instanceof Opcode::Conditional and
    Specific::conditionalExpr(this, type, any(SemExpr condition), trueResult, falseResult)

method.name: toString
method.comments:
Gets a textual representation of this element.

The format is "file://filePath:startLine:startColumn:endLine:endColumn".

method.name: getStartLine
method.comments: Gets the 1-based line number (inclusive) where this location starts.

method.name: getStartColumn
method.comments: Gets the 1-based column number (inclusive) where this location starts.

method.name: getEndLine
method.comments: Gets the 1-based line number (inclusive) where this location ends.

method.name: getEndColumn
method.comments: Gets the 1-based column number (inclusive) where this location ends.

predicate.name: hasLocationInfo
predicate.comments:
Holds if this element is at the specified location.
The location spans column `startcolumn` of line `startline` to
column `endcolumn` of line `endline` in file `filepath`.
For more information, see
[Locations](https://codeql.github.com/docs/writing-codeql-queries/providing-locations-in-codeql-queries/).

class.comments:
The language-neutral type of a semantic expression,
The interface to `SemType` and its subclasses is the same across all languages for which the IR
is supported, so analyses that expect to be used for multiple languages should generally use
`SemType` rather than a language-specific type.

Many types from the language-specific type system will map to a single canonical `SemType`. Two
types that map to the same `SemType` are considered equivalent by semantic analysis. As an
example, in C++, all pointer types map to the same instance of `SemAddressType`.

method.name: toString
method.comments: Gets a textual representation of this type.

method.name: getIdentityString
method.comments:
Gets a string that uniquely identifies this `SemType`. This string is often the same as the
result of `SemType.toString()`, but for some types it may be more verbose to ensure uniqueness.

method.name: getByteSize
method.comments:
Gets the size of the type, in bytes, if known.

This will hold for all `SemType` objects except `SemUnknownType` and `SemErrorType`.

class.comments:
An unknown type. Generally used to represent results and operands that access an unknown set of
memory locations, such as the side effects of a function call.

A void type, which has no values. Used to represent the result type of an expression that does
not produce a result.

An error type. Used when an error in the source code prevents the extractor from determining the
proper type.

predicate.name: SemSizedType
predicate.body:
this = TSemBooleanType(byteSize) or
    this = TSemIntegerType(byteSize, _) or
    this = TSemFloatingPointType(byteSize) or
    this = TSemAddressType(byteSize) or
    this = TSemFunctionAddressType(byteSize) or
    this = TSemOpaqueType(byteSize, _)

class.comments: A Boolean type, which can hold the values `true` (non-zero) or `false` (zero).

A numeric type. This includes `SemSignedIntegerType`, `SemUnsignedIntegerType`, and
`SemFloatingPointType`.
An integer type. This includes `SemSignedIntegerType` and `SemUnsignedIntegerType`.

predicate.name: isSigned
predicate.comments: Holds if this integer type is signed.

predicate.name: isUnsigned
predicate.comments: Holds if this integer type is unsigned.

class.comments:
A signed two's-complement integer. Also used to represent enums whose underlying type is a signed
integer, as well as character types whose representation is signed.

An unsigned two's-complement integer. Also used to represent enums whose underlying type is an
unsigned integer, as well as character types whose representation is unsigned.
A floating-point type.

An address type, representing the memory address of data. Used to represent pointers, references,
and lvalues, include those that are garbage collected.

The address of a function is represented by the separate `SemFunctionAddressType`.

An address type, representing the memory address of code. Used to represent function pointers,
function references, and the target of a direct function call.

A type with known size that does not fit any of the other kinds of type. Used to represent
classes, structs, unions, fixed-size arrays, pointers-to-member, and more.

method.name: getTag
method.comments:
Gets the "tag" that differentiates this type from other incompatible opaque types that have the
same size.

predicate.name: SemNumericOrBooleanType
predicate.body:
this instanceof SemNumericType
    or
    this instanceof SemBooleanType

class.comments: A bound that may be inferred for an expression plus/minus an integer delta.

method.name: getInstruction
method.comments: Gets an expression that equals this bound plus `delta`.

method.name: getInstruction
method.comments: Gets an expression that equals this bound.

class.comments:
The bound that corresponds to the integer 0. This is used to represent all
integer bounds as bounds are always accompanied by an added integer delta.
A bound corresponding to the value of an `Instruction`.

method.name: getInstruction
method.comments: Gets an `Instruction` that equals this bound.

method.name: getValueNumber
method.comments: Gets the value number that equals this bound.

class.comments: An expression that always has the same integer value.

method.name: getIntValue
method.comments: Gets the integer value of this expression.

class.comments: Class representing expression signs (+, -, 0).

method.name: toString
method.body:
result = "-" and this = TNeg()
    or
    result = "0" and this = TZero()
    or
    result = "+" and this = TPos()
method.comments: Gets the string representation of this sign.

method.name: inc
method.body:
this = TNeg() and result = TNeg()
    or
    this = TNeg() and result = TZero()
    or
    this = TZero() and result = TPos()
    or
    this = TPos() and result = TPos()
method.comments: Gets a possible sign after incrementing an expression that has this sign.

method.name: dec
method.comments: Gets a possible sign after decrementing an expression that has this sign.

method.name: neg
method.body:
this = TNeg() and result = TPos()
    or
    this = TZero() and result = TZero()
    or
    this = TPos() and result = TNeg()
method.comments: Gets a possible sign after negating an expression that has this sign.

method.name: bitnot
method.body:
this = TNeg() and result = TPos()
    or
    this = TNeg() and result = TZero()
    or
    this = TZero() and result = TNeg()
    or
    this = TPos() and result = TNeg()
method.comments:
Gets a possible sign after bitwise complementing an expression that has this
sign.

method.name: add
method.body:
this = TZero() and result = s
    or
    s = TZero() and result = this
    or
    this = s and this = result
    or
    this = TPos() and s = TNeg()
    or
    this = TNeg() and s = TPos()
method.comments:
Gets a possible sign after adding an expression with sign `s` to an expression
that has this sign.

method.name: sub
method.comments:
Gets a possible sign after subtracting an expression with sign `s` from an expression
that has this sign.

method.name: mul
method.body:
result = TZero() and this = TZero()
    or
    result = TZero() and s = TZero()
    or
    result = TNeg() and this = TPos() and s = TNeg()
    or
    result = TNeg() and this = TNeg() and s = TPos()
    or
    result = TPos() and this = TPos() and s = TPos()
    or
    result = TPos() and this = TNeg() and s = TNeg()
method.comments:
Gets a possible sign after multiplying an expression with sign `s` to an expression
that has this sign.

method.name: div
method.body:
result = TZero() and s = TNeg() // ex: 3 / -5 = 0
    or
    result = TZero() and s = TPos() // ex: 3 / 5 = 0
    or
    result = TNeg() and this = TPos() and s = TNeg()
    or
    result = TNeg() and this = TNeg() and s = TPos()
    or
    result = TPos() and this = TPos() and s = TPos()
    or
    result = TPos() and this = TNeg() and s = TNeg()
method.comments:
Gets a possible sign after integer dividing an expression that has this sign
by an expression with sign `s`.

method.name: rem
method.body:
result = TZero() and s = TNeg()
    or
    result = TZero() and s = TPos()
    or
    result = this and s = TNeg()
    or
    result = this and s = TPos()
method.comments:
Gets a possible sign after modulo dividing an expression that has this sign
by an expression with sign `s`.

method.name: bitand
method.body:
result = TZero() and this = TZero()
    or
    result = TZero() and s = TZero()
    or
    result = TZero() and this = TPos()
    or
    result = TZero() and s = TPos()
    or
    result = TNeg() and this = TNeg() and s = TNeg()
    or
    result = TPos() and this = TNeg() and s = TPos()
    or
    result = TPos() and this = TPos() and s = TNeg()
    or
    result = TPos() and this = TPos() and s = TPos()
method.comments:
Gets a possible sign after bitwise `and` of an expression that has this sign
and an expression with sign `s`.

method.name: bitor
method.body:
result = TZero() and this = TZero() and s = TZero()
    or
    result = TNeg() and this = TNeg()
    or
    result = TNeg() and s = TNeg()
    or
    result = TPos() and this = TPos() and s = TZero()
    or
    result = TPos() and this = TZero() and s = TPos()
    or
    result = TPos() and this = TPos() and s = TPos()
method.comments:
Gets a possible sign after bitwise `or` of an expression that has this sign
and an expression with sign `s`.

method.name: bitxor
method.body:
result = TZero() and this = s
    or
    result = this and s = TZero()
    or
    result = s and this = TZero()
    or
    result = TPos() and this = TPos() and s = TPos()
    or
    result = TNeg() and this = TNeg() and s = TPos()
    or
    result = TNeg() and this = TPos() and s = TNeg()
    or
    result = TPos() and this = TNeg() and s = TNeg()
method.comments:
Gets a possible sign after bitwise `xor` of an expression that has this sign
and an expression with sign `s`.

method.name: lshift
method.body:
result = TZero() and this = TZero()
    or
    result = this and s = TZero()
    or
    this != TZero() and s != TZero()
method.comments:
Gets a possible sign after left shift of an expression that has this sign
by an expression with sign `s`.

method.name: rshift
method.body:
result = TZero() and this = TZero()
    or
    result = this and s = TZero()
    or
    result = TNeg() and this = TNeg()
    or
    result != TNeg() and this = TPos() and s != TZero()
method.comments:
Gets a possible sign after right shift of an expression that has this sign
by an expression with sign `s`.

method.name: urshift
method.body:
result = TZero() and this = TZero()
    or
    result = this and s = TZero()
    or
    result != TZero() and this = TNeg() and s != TZero()
    or
    result != TNeg() and this = TPos() and s != TZero()
method.comments:
Gets a possible sign after unsigned right shift of an expression that has
this sign by an expression with sign `s`.

method.name: applyUnaryOp
method.body:
op instanceof Opcode::CopyValue and result = this
    or
    op instanceof Opcode::Store and result = this
    or
    op instanceof Opcode::AddOne and result = this.inc()
    or
    op instanceof Opcode::SubOne and result = this.dec()
    or
    op instanceof Opcode::Negate and result = this.neg()
    or
    op instanceof Opcode::BitComplement and result = this.bitnot()
method.comments: Perform `op` on this sign.

method.name: applyBinaryOp
method.body:
op instanceof Opcode::Add and result = this.add(s)
    or
    op instanceof Opcode::Sub and result = this.sub(s)
    or
    op instanceof Opcode::Mul and result = this.mul(s)
    or
    op instanceof Opcode::Div and result = this.div(s)
    or
    op instanceof Opcode::Rem and result = this.rem(s)
    or
    op instanceof Opcode::BitAnd and result = this.bitand(s)
    or
    op instanceof Opcode::BitOr and result = this.bitor(s)
    or
    op instanceof Opcode::BitXor and result = this.bitxor(s)
    or
    op instanceof Opcode::ShiftLeft and result = this.lshift(s)
    or
    op instanceof Opcode::ShiftRight and result = this.rshift(s)
    or
    op instanceof Opcode::ShiftRightUnsigned and result = this.urshift(s)
method.comments: Perform `op` on this sign and sign `s`.

class.comments:
An operation that reads data from or writes data to a buffer.

See the BufferWrite class for an explanation of how BufferAccess and
BufferWrite differ.

method.name: getBuffer
method.comments:
Gets the expression that denotes the buffer, along with a textual label
for it and an access type.

accessType:
- 1 = buffer range [0, getSize) is accessed entirely.
- 2 = buffer range [0, getSize) may be accessed partially or entirely.
- 3 = buffer is accessed at offset getSize - 1.
- 4 = buffer is accessed with null terminator read protections
(does not read past null terminator, regardless of access size)

method.name: getSizeExpr

method.name: getSizeMult
method.comments:
Gets a constant multiplier for the buffer access size given by
`getSizeExpr`, in bytes.

method.name: getSize

class.comments:
An operation that writes a variable amount of data to a buffer
(strcpy, strncat, sprintf etc).

Note that there are two related class frameworks:
- BufferWrite provides detailed coverage of null-terminated
buffer write operations.
- BufferAccess provides general coverage of buffer read and write
operations whose size is either not data-dependent, or has an upper
bound which is not data-dependent.
This design has some overlaps between the two classes, for example
the write of a 'strncpy'.

method.name: getBufferType
method.comments:
Gets the (unspecified) type of the buffer this operation works
with (for example `char *`).

method.name: getASource
method.comments:
Gets a data source of this operation (e.g. the source string,
format string; not necessarily copied as-is).

method.name: getDest
method.comments: Gets the destination buffer of this operation.

method.name: getExplicitLimit
method.comments:
Gets the explicit limit of bytes copied by this operation, if it exists
and it's value can be determined.

method.name: getMaxData
method.comments:
Gets an upper bound to the amount of data that's being written (if one
can be found).

method.name: getMaxData
method.body: reason instanceof UnspecifiedEstimateReason and result = this.getMaxData()
method.comments:
Gets an upper bound to the amount of data that's being written (if one
can be found), specifying the reason for the estimation.

method.name: getMaxDataLimited
method.comments:
Gets an upper bound to the amount of data that's being written (if one
can be found), except that float to string conversions are assumed to be
much smaller (8 bytes) than their true maximum length.  This can be
helpful in determining the cause of a buffer overflow issue.

method.name: getMaxDataLimited
method.comments:
Gets an upper bound to the amount of data that's being written (if one
can be found), specifying the reason for the estimation, except that
float to string conversions are assumed to be much smaller (8 bytes)
than their true maximum length.  This can be helpful in determining the
cause of a buffer overflow issue.

method.name: getCharSize
method.comments:
Gets the size of a single character of the type this
operation works with, in bytes.

method.name: getBWDesc
method.comments: Gets a description of this buffer write.

predicate.name: hasExplicitLimit
predicate.comments:
Holds if the operation has an explicit parameter that limits the amount
of data written (e.g. `strncpy` does, whereas `strcpy` does not); this
is not the same as exists(getExplicitLimit()) because the limit may exist
though it's value is unknown.

An abstract class for representing functions that may have wrapper functions.
Wrapper functions propagate an argument (without any evident changes) to this function
through one or more steps in a call chain.

The design motivation is to report a violation at the location of the argument
in a call to the wrapper function rather than the function being wrapped, since
that is usually the more appropriate place to fix the violation.

Subclasses should override the characteristic predicate and 'interestingArg'.

predicate.name: interestingArg
predicate.comments: Which argument indices are relevant for wrapper function detection.

predicate.name: wrapperFunction
predicate.body:
cause =
      min(string callChain, int depth |
        this.wrapperFunctionLimitedDepth(func, paramIndex, callChain, depth) and
        depth = min(int d | this.wrapperFunctionLimitedDepth(func, paramIndex, _, d) | d)
      |
        callChain
      )
    or
    not this.wrapperFunctionLimitedDepth(func, paramIndex, _, _) and
    cause = this.wrapperFunctionAnyDepthUnique(func, paramIndex)
predicate.comments:
Whether 'func' is a (possibly nested) wrapper function that feeds a parameter at the given index
through to an interesting parameter of 'this' function.

If there exists a call chain with depth at most 4, the 'cause' reports the smallest call chain.
Otherwise, the 'cause' merely reports the name of 'this' interesting function and its relevant
parameter at the end of the call chain.

If there is more than one possible 'cause', a unique one is picked (by lexicographic order).

predicate.name: outermostWrapperFunctionCall
predicate.body:
exists(Function targetFunc, FunctionCall call, int argIndex |
      targetFunc = call.getTarget() and
      this.wrapperFunction(targetFunc, argIndex, callChain) and
      (
        exists(Function sourceFunc | sourceFunc = call.getEnclosingFunction() |
          not wrapperFunctionStep(sourceFunc, _, targetFunc, argIndex)
        )
        or
        not exists(call.getEnclosingFunction())
      ) and
      arg = call.getArgument(argIndex)
    )
predicate.comments: Whether 'arg' is an argument in a call to an outermost wrapper function of 'this' function.
