predicate.body:
exists(FunctionInput input, SqlExecutionFunction sql |
      sql.hasName(function) and
      input.isParameterDeref(arg) and
      sql.hasSqlArgument(input)
    )
predicate.comments: An argument to a function that is passed to a SQL server.

predicate.name: isProcessOperationArgument
predicate.comments:
The argument of the given function is used for running a process or loading
a library.

predicate.name: raisesPrivilege
predicate.body:
exists(ReturnStmt ret | ret.getExpr() = expr |
      ret.getEnclosingFunction().getName() = "checkPinCode" and
      ret.getExpr().getValue() = "1"
    )
    or
    exists(AssignExpr assign, Variable adminPrivileges |
      assign = expr and
      adminPrivileges.hasName("adminPrivileges") and
      assign.getLValue().(Access).getTarget() = adminPrivileges and
      not assign.getRValue().(Literal).getValue() = "0"
    )
predicate.comments:
This predicate should hold if the expression raises privilege for the
current session. The default definition only holds true for some
example code in the test suite. This predicate must be extended for
a particular code base to be useful.

class.comments:
This class overrides `SecurityOptions` and can be used to add project
specific customization.

predicate.name: sqlArgument

class.comments: A variable that might contain a password or other credential.

predicate.name: SensitiveVariable
predicate.body:
suspicious(this.getName()) and
    not this.getUnspecifiedType() instanceof IntegralType

class.comments: A function that might return a password or other credential.

predicate.name: SensitiveFunction
predicate.body:
suspicious(this.getName()) and
    not this.getUnspecifiedType() instanceof IntegralType

class.comments: An expression whose value might be a password or other credential.

predicate.name: SensitiveExpr
predicate.body:
this.(VariableAccess).getTarget() instanceof SensitiveVariable or
    this.(FunctionCall).getTarget() instanceof SensitiveFunction

method.name: getADeclaration
method.comments:
Gets a child declaration of this block.

For example, for the block
```
{ int a; int b = 1; a = b; }
```
it would have 2 results, for the declarations of `a` and `b`.

method.name: getAStmt
method.comments:
Gets a body statement of this block.

For example, for the block
```
{ int a; int b = 1; a = b; }
```
it would have 3 results, for the declarations of `a` and `b` and
for the expression statement `a = b`.

method.name: getStmt
method.comments:
Gets the `n`th body statement of this block, indexed from 0.

For example, for the block
```
{ int a; int b = 1; a = b; }
```
`getStmt(2)`'s result is the expression statement `a = b`.

method.name: getLastStmt
method.comments:
Gets the last body statement of this block.

For example, for the block
```
{ int a; int b = 1; a = b; }
```
the result is the expression statement `a = b`.

method.name: getLastStmtIn
method.comments:
Gets the last body statement of this block. If this last statement
is itself a block, returns the last statement of that block, and so on.

For example, for the block
```
{ int a; int b = 1; { a = b; } }
```
the result is the expression statement `a = b`.

method.name: getNumStmt
method.comments:
Gets the number of body statements in this block.

For example, for the block
```
{ int a; int b = 1; a = b; }
```
the result is 3.

method.name: getIndexOfStmt
method.comments:
Gets the index of the given statement within this block, indexed from 0.

For example, for the block
```
{ int a; int b = 1; a = b; }
```
if `s` is the expression statement `a = b` then `getIndexOfStmt(s)`
has result 2.

predicate.name: isEmpty

class.comments: A C/C++ statement.

method.name: getChild
method.body:
stmtparents(unresolveElement(result), n, underlyingElement(this)) or
    exprparents(unresolveElement(result), n, underlyingElement(this))
method.comments: Gets the `n`th child of this statement.

method.name: getEnclosingFunction
method.comments: Gets the enclosing function of this statement, if any.

method.name: getEnclosingBlock
method.body:
if
      this.getParentStmt() instanceof BlockStmt and
      not this.getParentStmt().(BlockStmt).getLocation() instanceof UnknownLocation
    then result = this.getParentStmt()
    else result = this.getParentStmt().getEnclosingBlock()
method.comments: Gets the nearest enclosing block of this statement in the source, if any.

method.name: getAChild
method.comments: Gets a child of this statement.

method.name: getParent
method.comments: Gets the parent of this statement, if any.

method.name: getParentStmt
method.comments: Gets the parent statement of this statement, if any.

method.name: getChildStmt
method.comments: Gets a child statement of this statement.

method.name: getFollowingStmt
method.body:
exists(BlockStmt b, int i |
      this = b.getStmt(i) and
      result = b.getStmt(i + 1)
    )
method.comments:
Gets the statement following this statement in the same block, if any.

Note that this is not widely useful, because this doesn't have a result for
the last statement of a block.  Consider using the `ControlFlowNode` class
to trace the flow of control instead.

method.name: getImplicitDestructorCall
method.body: synthetic_destructor_call(this, max(int i | synthetic_destructor_call(this, i, _)) - n, result)

method.name: getAnImplicitDestructorCall
method.comments: Gets a compiler-generated destructor call that is performed after this statement.

method.name: getAnAttribute
method.comments:
Gets an attribute of this statement, for example
`[[clang::fallthrough]]`.

method.name: getGeneratingMacro
method.comments:
Gets a macro invocation that generates this entire statement.

For example, given
```
#define SOMEFUN a()
#define FOO do { SOMEFUN; b(); } while (0)
void f(void) {
FOO;
}
```
this predicate would have results of `SOMEFUN` and `FOO` for the
function call `a()`, and just `FOO` for the function call `b()`,
the block within the 'do' statement, and the entire 'do' statement.

Note that, unlike `isInMacroExpansion()` it is not necessary for
the macro to generate the terminating semi-colon.

predicate.name: hasChild
predicate.comments: Holds if `e` is the `n`th child of this statement.

predicate.name: isPure
predicate.comments:
Holds if this statement is side-effect free (a conservative
approximation; that is, it may be side-effect free even if this
predicate doesn't hold).

This predicate cannot be overridden; override `mayBeImpure()`
instead.

Note that this predicate only considers whether the statement has
any side-effects, such as writing to a file. Even if it holds, the
statement may be impure in the sense that its behavior is affected
by external factors, such as the contents of global variables.

predicate.name: mayBeImpure
predicate.comments:
Holds if it is possible that this statement is impure. If we are not
sure, then it holds.

predicate.name: mayBeGloballyImpure
predicate.comments:
Holds if it is possible that this statement is globally impure.

Similar to `mayBeImpure()`, except that `mayBeGloballyImpure()`
does not consider modifications to temporary local variables to be
impure. That is, if you call a function in which
`mayBeGloballyImpure()` doesn't hold for any statement, then the
function as a whole will have no side-effects, even if it mutates
its own fresh stack variables.

predicate.name: isCompilerGenerated
predicate.comments: Holds if this statement was generated by the compiler.

class.comments:
An element that is the parent of a statement in the C/C++ AST.

This is normally a statement, but may be a `StmtExpr`.

A C/C++ 'expression' statement.

For example,
```
x = 1;
```
is an assignment expression inside an 'expression' statement.

method.name: getExpr
method.comments:
Gets the expression of this 'expression' statement.

For example, for
```
x = 1;
```
the result would be an `AssignExpr`.

method.name: getGeneratingMacro
method.body:
// We only need the expression to be in the macro, not the semicolon.
    result.getAnExpandedElement() = this.getExpr()

class.comments:
A C/C++ control structure, that is, either a conditional statement or
a loop.

method.name: getControllingExpr
method.comments:
Gets the controlling expression of this control structure.

This is the condition of 'if' statements and loops, and the
switched expression for 'switch' statements.

method.name: getADeclaration
method.comments: Gets a child declaration of this scope.

class.comments:
A C/C++ conditional statement, that is, either an 'if' statement or a
'switch' statement.

A C/C++ 'if' statement. For example, the `if` statement in the following
code:
```
if (x == 1) {
...
}
```

method.name: getInitialization
method.comments:
Gets the initialization statement of this 'if' statement, if any.

For example, for
```
if (int x = y; b) { f(); }
```
the result is `int x = y;`.

Does not hold if the initialization statement is missing or an empty statement, as in
```
if (b) { f(); }
```
or
```
if (; b) { f(); }
```

method.name: getCondition
method.comments:
Gets the condition expression of this 'if' statement.

For example, for
```
if (b) { x = 1; }
```
the result is `b`.

method.name: getThen
method.comments:
Gets the 'then' statement of this 'if' statement.

For example, for
```
if (b) { x = 1; }
```
the result is the `BlockStmt` `{ x = 1; }`.

method.name: getElse
method.comments:
Gets the 'else' statement of this 'if' statement, if any.

For example, for
```
if (b) { x = 1; } else { x = 2; }
```
the result is the `BlockStmt` `{ x = 2; }`, and for
```
if (b) { x = 1; }
```
there is no result.

method.name: getGeneratingMacro
method.body:
result.getAnExpandedElement() = this.getCondition() and
    this.getThen().getGeneratingMacro() = result and
    (this.hasElse() implies this.getElse().getGeneratingMacro() = result)

predicate.name: hasElse
predicate.comments:
Holds if this 'if' statement has an 'else' statement.

For example, this holds for
```
if (b) { x = 1; } else { x = 2; }
```
but not for
```
if (b) { x = 1; }
```

class.comments:
A C/C++ 'constexpr if' statement. For example, the `if constexpr` statement
in the following code:
```
if constexpr (x) {
...
}
```

method.name: getInitialization
method.comments:
Gets the initialization statement of this 'constexpr if' statement, if any.

For example, for
```
if constexpr (int x = y; b) { f(); }
```
the result is `int x = y;`.

Does not hold if the initialization statement is missing or an empty statement, as in
```
if constexpr (b) { f(); }
```
or
```
if constexpr (; b) { f(); }
```

method.name: getCondition
method.comments:
Gets the condition expression of this 'constexpr if' statement.

For example, for
```
if constexpr (b) { x = 1; }
```
the result is `b`.

method.name: getThen
method.comments:
Gets the 'then' statement of this 'constexpr if' statement.

For example, for
```
if constexpr (b) { x = 1; }
```
the result is the `BlockStmt` `{ x = 1; }`.

method.name: getElse
method.comments:
Gets the 'else' statement of this 'constexpr if' statement, if any.

For example, for
```
if constexpr (b) { x = 1; } else { x = 2; }
```
the result is the `BlockStmt` `{ x = 2; }`, and for
```
if constexpr (b) { x = 1; }
```
there is no result.

method.name: getGeneratingMacro
method.body:
result.getAnExpandedElement() = this.getCondition() and
    this.getThen().getGeneratingMacro() = result and
    (this.hasElse() implies this.getElse().getGeneratingMacro() = result)

predicate.name: hasElse
predicate.comments:
Holds if this 'constexpr if' statement has an 'else' statement.

For example, this holds for
```
if constexpr (b) { x = 1; } else { x = 2; }
```
but not for
```
if constexpr (b) { x = 1; }
```

class.comments:
A C/C++ '(not) consteval if'. For example, the `if consteval` statement
in the following code:
```cpp
if consteval {
...
}
```

method.name: toString
method.body: if this.isNot() then result = "if ! consteval ..." else result = "if consteval ..."

method.name: getThen
method.comments:
Gets the 'then' statement of this '(not) consteval if' statement.

For example, for
```cpp
if consteval { return true; }
```
the result is the `BlockStmt` `{ return true; }`.

method.name: getElse
method.comments:
Gets the 'else' statement of this '(not) constexpr if' statement, if any.

For example, for
```cpp
if consteval { return true; } else { return false; }
```
the result is the `BlockStmt` `{ return false; }`, and for
```cpp
if consteval { return true; }
```
there is no result.

method.name: getGeneratingMacro
method.body:
this.getThen().getGeneratingMacro() = result and
    (this.hasElse() implies this.getElse().getGeneratingMacro() = result)

method.name: getCompileTimeEvaluatedBranch
method.body: if this.isNot() then result = this.getElse() else result = this.getThen()
method.comments:
Gets the statement of this '(not) consteval if' statement evaluated during compile time, if any.

For example, for
```cpp
if ! consteval { return true; } else { return false; }
```
the result is the `BlockStmt` `{ return false; }`, and for
```cpp
if ! consteval { return true; }
```
there is no result.

method.name: getRuntimeEvaluatedBranch
method.body: if this.isNot() then result = this.getThen() else result = this.getElse()
method.comments:
Gets the statement of this '(not) consteval if' statement evaluated during runtime, if any.

For example, for
```cpp
if consteval { return true; } else { return false; }
```
the result is the `BlockStmt` `{ return false; }`, and for
```cpp
if consteval { return true; }
```
there is no result.

predicate.name: isNot
predicate.comments:
Holds if this is a 'not consteval if' statement.

For example, this holds for
```cpp
if ! consteval { return true; }
```
but not for
```cpp
if consteval { return true; }
```

predicate.name: hasElse
predicate.comments:
Holds if this '(not) constexpr if' statement has an 'else' statement.

For example, this holds for
```cpp
if consteval { return true; } else { return false; }
```
but not for
```cpp
if consteval { return true; }
```

predicate.name: hasCompileTimeEvaluatedBranch
predicate.comments:
Holds if this '(not) constexpr if' statement has a compile time evaluated statement.

For example, this holds for
```cpp
if ! consteval { return true; } else { return false; }
```
but not for
```cpp
if ! consteval { return true; }
```

predicate.name: hasRuntimeEvaluatedBranch
predicate.comments:
Holds if this '(not) constexpr if' statement has a runtime evaluated statement.

For example, this holds for
```cpp
if consteval { return true; } else { return false; }
```
but not for
```cpp
if consteval { return true; }
```

class.comments:
A C/C++ loop, that is, either a 'while' loop, a 'for' loop, or a
'do' loop.

method.name: getCondition
method.comments: Gets the condition expression of this loop.

method.name: getStmt
method.comments: Gets the body statement of this loop.

predicate.name: conditionAlwaysTrue
predicate.comments:
Holds if the loop condition is provably `true`.

For example, this holds for
```
while(1) { ...; if(b) break; ...; }
```

predicate.name: conditionAlwaysFalse
predicate.comments:
Holds if the loop condition is provably `false`.

For example, this holds for
```
while(0) { ...; }
```

predicate.name: conditionAlwaysTrueUponEntry
predicate.comments:
Holds if the loop condition is provably `true` upon entry,
that is, at least one iteration of the loop is guaranteed.

For example, with
```
bool done = false;
while (!done) { ... done = true; ... }
```
the condition `!done` always evaluates to `true` upon entry since
`done = false`, but the condition may evaluate to `false` after
some iterations.

class.comments: A C/C++ jump statement.

method.name: getTarget
method.comments: Gets the target of this jump statement.

class.comments:
A C/C++ 'goto' statement which jumps to a label.

For example, the `goto` statement in the following code:
```
goto someLabel;
...
somelabel:
```

method.name: getName
method.comments:
Gets the name of the label this 'goto' statement refers to.

For example, for
```
goto someLabel;
```
the result is `"someLabel"`.

predicate.name: hasName
predicate.body: exists(string s | jumpinfo(underlyingElement(this), s, _) and s != "")
predicate.comments: Holds if this 'goto' statement refers to a label.

predicate.name: breaksFromNestedLoops
predicate.body:
exists(Loop l1, Loop l2 |
      this.getParentStmt+() = l1 and
      l1.getParentStmt+() = l2 and
      l2.getParentStmt+() = this.getASuccessor().(Stmt).getParentStmt()
    )

class.comments:
A 'goto' statement whose target is computed by a non-constant
expression (a non-standard extension to C/C++).

For example, the `goto` statement in the following code:
```
goto *ptr;
```

method.name: getExpr
method.comments:
Gets the expression used to compute the target of this 'goto'
statement.

For example, for
```
goto *ptr;
```
the result is `ptr`.

method.name: getGeneratingMacro
method.body:
// We only need the expression to be in the macro, not the semicolon.
    result.getAnExpandedElement() = this.getExpr()

method.name: getContinuable
method.comments: Gets the loop that this continue statement will jump to the beginning of.

method.name: getBreakable
method.comments: Gets the loop or switch statement that this break statement will exit.

method.name: getEnclosingTry
method.comments: Gets the `__try` statement that this `__leave` exits.

class.comments:
A C/C++ 'label' statement.

For example, the `somelabel:` statement in the following code:
```
goto someLabel;
...
somelabel:
```

method.name: getName
method.comments: Gets the name of this 'label' statement.

predicate.name: isNamed
predicate.comments: Holds if this 'label' statement is named.

class.comments:
A C/C++ `co_return` statement.

For example:
```
co_return 1+2;
```
or
```
co_return;
```

method.name: getOperand
method.comments:
Gets the operand of this `co_return` statement.

For example, for
```
co_return 1+2;
```
the operand is a function call `return_value(1+2)`, and for
```
co_return;
```
the operand is a function call `return_void()`.

method.name: getExpr
method.comments:
Gets the expression of this `co_return` statement, if any.

For example, for
```
co_return 1+2;
```
the result is `1+2`, and there is no result for
```
co_return;
```

predicate.name: hasExpr
predicate.comments:
Holds if this `co_return` statement has an expression.

For example, this holds for
```
co_return 1+2;
```
but not for
```
co_return;
```

class.comments:
A C/C++ 'return' statement.

For example:
```
return 1+2;
```
or
```
return;
```

method.name: getExpr
method.comments:
Gets the expression of this 'return' statement.

For example, for
```
return 1+2;
```
the result is `1+2`, and there is no result for
```
return;
```

predicate.name: hasExpr
predicate.comments:
Holds if this 'return' statement has an expression.

For example, this holds for
```
return 1+2;
```
but not for
```
return;
```

method.name: getInitialization
method.comments:
Gets the initialization statement of this 'for' statement, if any.

For example, for
```
for (int x = y; auto z : ... ) { }
```
the result is `int x = y;`.

Does not hold if the initialization statement is missing or an empty statement, as in
```
for (auto z : ...) { }
```
or
```
for (; auto z : ) { }
```

method.name: getStmt
method.comments:
Gets the 'body' statement of this range-based 'for' statement.

For example, for
```
for (int x : xs) { y += x; }
```
the result is the `BlockStmt` `{ y += x; }`.

method.name: getVariable
method.comments:
Gets the variable introduced by the for-range-declaration.

For example, for
```
for (int x : xs) { y += x; }
```
the result is `int x`.

method.name: getRange
method.comments:
Gets the expression giving the range to iterate over.

For example, for
```
for (int x : xs) { y += x; }
```
the result is `xs`.

method.name: getRangeVariable
method.comments: Gets the compiler-generated `__range` variable after desugaring.

method.name: getCondition
method.comments:
Gets the compiler-generated `__begin != __end` which is the
condition expression of this for statement after desugaring.
It will be either an `NEExpr` or a call to a user-defined
`operator!=`.

method.name: getBeginEndDeclaration
method.comments:
Gets a declaration statement that declares first `__begin` and then
`__end`, initializing them to the values they have before entering the
desugared loop.

method.name: getBeginVariable
method.comments: Gets the compiler-generated `__begin` variable after desugaring.

method.name: getEndVariable
method.comments: Gets the compiler-generated `__end` variable after desugaring.

method.name: getUpdate
method.comments:
Gets the compiler-generated `++__begin` which is the update
expression of this for statement after desugaring. It will
be either a `PrefixIncrExpr` or a call to a user-defined
`operator++`.

method.name: getAnIterationVariable
method.comments: Gets the compiler-generated `__begin` variable after desugaring.

class.comments:
A C/C++ 'for' statement.

This only represents "traditional" 'for' statements and not C++11
range-based 'for' statements or Objective C 'for-in' statements.

For example, the `for` statement in:
```
for (i = 0; i < 10; i++) { j++; }
```

method.name: getInitialization
method.comments:
Gets the initialization statement of this 'for' statement.

For example, for
```
for (i = 0; i < 10; i++) { j++; }
```
the result is `i = 0;`.

Does not hold if the initialization statement is an empty statement, as in
```
for (; i < 10; i++) { j++; }
```

method.name: getCondition
method.comments:
Gets the condition expression of this 'for' statement.

For example, for
```
for (i = 0; i < 10; i++) { j++; }
```
the result is `i < 10`.

Does not hold if the condition expression is omitted, as in
```
for (i = 0;; i++) { if (i >= 10) break; }
```

method.name: getUpdate
method.comments:
Gets the update expression of this 'for' statement.

For example, for
```
for (i = 0; i < 10; i++) { j++; }
```
the result is `i++`.

Does not hold if the update expression is omitted, as in
```
for (i = 0; i < 10;) { i++; }
```

method.name: getAnIterationVariable
method.body:
this instanceof ForStmt and
    // check that it is assigned to, incremented or decremented in the update
    exists(Expr updateOpRoot, Expr updateOp |
      updateOpRoot = this.getUpdate() and
      inForUpdate(updateOpRoot, updateOp)
    |
      exists(CrementOperation op, VariableAccess va |
        op = updateOp and
        op instanceof CrementOperation and
        op.getOperand() = va and
        va = result.getAnAccess()
      )
      or
      updateOp = result.getAnAssignedValue()
    ) and
    result instanceof Variable and
    // checked or used in the condition
    exists(Expr e, VariableAccess va |
      va = result.getAnAccess() and
      inForCondition(e, va) and
      e = this.getCondition()
    )
method.comments:
Gets a variable that is used as an iteration variable. That is, a
variables that is defined, updated or tested in the head of this
for statement.

This only has results that are quite certainly loop variables: for
complex iterations, it may not return anything.

For example, for
```
for (i = 0; i < 10; i++) { j++; }
```
the result is `i`.

method.name: getADeclaration
method.comments:
Gets a declaration from the initialization statement of this 'for'
statement.

For example, for
```
for(int x = 0, y = 10; x != y; ++x) { sum += x; }
```
the results are `x` and `y`, while for
```
for (i = 0; i < 10; i++) { j++; }
```
there are no results.

method.name: getGeneratingMacro
method.body:
(
      exists(this.getInitialization())
      implies
      result = this.getInitialization().getGeneratingMacro()
    ) and
    (exists(this.getCondition()) implies this.getCondition() = result.getAnExpandedElement()) and
    (exists(this.getUpdate()) implies this.getUpdate() = result.getAnExpandedElement()) and
    this.getStmt().getGeneratingMacro() = result

predicate.name: mayBeImpure
predicate.body:
this.getInitialization().mayBeImpure() or
    this.getCondition().mayBeImpure() or
    this.getUpdate().mayBeImpure() or
    this.getStmt().mayBeImpure()

predicate.name: mayBeGloballyImpure
predicate.body:
this.getInitialization().mayBeGloballyImpure() or
    this.getCondition().mayBeGloballyImpure() or
    this.getUpdate().mayBeGloballyImpure() or
    this.getStmt().mayBeGloballyImpure()

predicate.name: conditionAlwaysTrue
predicate.comments:
Holds if the loop condition is provably `true`.

For example, this holds for
```
for(x = 0; 1; ++x) { sum += x; }
```

predicate.name: conditionAlwaysFalse
predicate.comments:
Holds if the loop condition is provably `false`.

For example, this holds for
```
for(x = 0; 0; ++x) { sum += x; }
```

predicate.name: conditionAlwaysTrueUponEntry
predicate.comments:
Holds if the loop condition is provably `true` upon entry,
that is, at least one iteration of the loop is guaranteed.

For example, with
```
for (int i = 0; i < 10; i++) { ... }
```
the condition `i < 10` always evaluates to `true` upon entry since
`i = 0`, but the condition will evaluate to `false` after 10
iterations.

class.comments:
A C/C++ 'switch case' statement.

For example, the `case` and `default` statements in:
```
switch (i)
{
case 5:
...
default:
...
}
```

method.name: getExpr
method.comments:
Gets the expression of this 'switch case' statement (or the start of
the range if there is a GNU case range). Does not exist for a
`DefaultCase`.

For example, for
```
case 5:
```
the result is `5`, for
```
case 6 ... 7:
```
the result is 6, and there is no result for
```
default:
```

method.name: getEndExpr
method.comments:
Gets the end of the range, if this is a GNU case range. Otherwise
has no result.

For example, for
```
case 6 ... 7:
```
the result is `7`, while for
```
case 5:
```
and
```
default:
```
there is no result.

method.name: getSwitchStmt
getChildNum
method.comments:
Gets the 0-based index of this 'switch case' statement within its
'switch' statement.

For example, for
```
switch(i) {
case 5:
case 6:
default:
}
```
the `case 5:` has result 0, `case 6:` has result 1, and `default:`
has result 2.

method.name: getNextSwitchCase
getPreviousSwitchCase
getAStmt
method.body:
exists(BlockStmt b, int rnk, int i |
      b.getStmt(i) = this and
      i = indexOfSwitchCaseRank(b, rnk)
    |
      pragma[only_bind_into](b).getStmt([i + 1 .. indexOfSwitchCaseRank(b, rnk + 1) - 1]) = result
      or
      not exists(indexOfSwitchCaseRank(b, rnk + 1)) and
      b.getStmt([i + 1 .. b.getNumStmt() + 1]) = result
    )

method.name: getLastStmt
method.body:
exists(Stmt lastStmt |
      lastStmt = this.getAStmt() and
      not lastStmt.getFollowingStmt() = this.getAStmt() and
      if lastStmt instanceof BlockStmt
      then result = lastStmt.(BlockStmt).getLastStmtIn()
      else result = lastStmt
    )

predicate.name: terminatesInBreakStmt
terminatesInReturnStmt
terminatesInThrowStmt
isDefault
predicate.comments:
Holds if this 'switch case' statement is a 'default' statement.

For example, for
```
switch (i) {
case 5:
case 6:
case 7:
default:
}
```
this holds for `default:`, but not for `case 5:`, `case 6:`,
or `case 7:`.

class.comments:
A C/C++ 'default case' statement.

For example, the `default` statement in:
```
switch (i)
{
case 5:
...
default:
...
}
```

A C/C++ 'switch' statement.

For example, the `switch` statement in:
```
switch (i)
{
case 5:
...
default:
...
}
```

method.name: getInitialization
method.comments:
Gets the initialization statement of this 'switch' statement, if any.

For example, for
```
switch (int x = y; b) { }
```
the result is `int x = y;`.

Does not hold if the initialization statement is missing or an empty statement, as in
```
switch (b) { }
```
or
```
switch (; b) { }
```

method.name: getExpr
getStmt
getASwitchCase
getDefaultCase
getGeneratingMacro
method.body:
result.getAnExpandedElement() = this.getExpr() and
    forall(SwitchCase c | c = this.getASwitchCase() | exists(c.getGeneratingMacro()))

predicate.name: hasDefaultCase

method.name: getAMissingCase
method.body:
exists(Enum et |
      et = this.getExpr().getUnderlyingType() and
      result = et.getAnEnumConstant() and
      not this.matchesValue(result.getInitializer().getExpr().getValue())
    )

method.name: getBlock
method.comments: Gets the block containing the implementation of this handler.

method.name: getTryStmt
method.comments: Gets the 'try' statement corresponding to this 'catch block'.

method.name: getParameter
method.comments:
Gets the parameter introduced by this 'catch block', if any.

For example, `catch(std::exception& e)` introduces a
parameter `e`, whereas `catch(...)` does not introduce a parameter.

method.name: getStmt
method.comments:
Gets the 'body' statement of this 'try' statement.

For example, for
```
try { f(); } catch (...) { g(); }
```
the result is `{ f(); }`.

method.name: getCatchClause
method.comments:
Gets the `n`th 'catch block' of this 'try' statement.

For example, for
```
try { f(); } catch (...) { g(); }
```
the result of `getCatchClause(0)` is `{ g(); }`.

method.name: getACatchClause
method.comments:
Gets a 'catch block' of this 'try' statement.

For example, for
```
try { f(); } catch (...) { g(); }
```
the result is `{ g(); }`.

method.name: getNumberOfCatchClauses
method.comments:
Gets the number of 'catch block's of this 'try' statement.

For example, for
```
try { f(); } catch (...) { g(); }
```
the result is 1.

method.name: getParameter
method.comments:
Gets the parameter introduced by this 'catch block', if any.

For example, `catch(std::exception& e)` introduces a parameter
`e`, whereas `catch(...)` does not introduce a parameter.

method.name: getTryStmt
method.comments: Gets the try statement corresponding to this 'catch block'.

class.comments:
A structured exception handling 'try' statement, that is, a
`__try __except` or `__try __finally` statement. This is a Microsoft
C/C++ extension.

method.name: getStmt
method.comments: Gets the body statement of this __try statement.

method.name: getCondition
method.comments: Gets the expression guarding the `__except` statement.

method.name: getExcept
method.comments: Gets the `__except` statement (usually a `BlockStmt`).

method.name: getFinally
method.comments: Gets the `__finally` statement (usually a `BlockStmt`).

class.comments:
A C/C++ 'declaration' statement.

For example, the following statement is a declaration statement:
```
int i, j;
```

method.name: getDeclarationEntry
method.comments:
Gets the `i`th declaration entry declared by this 'declaration' statement.

For example, for
```
int i, j;
```
the result of `getDeclarationEntry(0)` is `i`.

method.name: getADeclarationEntry
method.comments:
Gets a declaration entry declared by this 'declaration' statement.

For example, for
```
int i, j;
```
the results are `i` and `j`.

method.name: getNumDeclarations
method.comments:
Gets the number of declarations declared by this 'declaration' statement.

For example, for
```
int i, j;
```
the result of `getNumDeclarations()` is `2`.

method.name: getDeclaration
method.comments:
Gets the `i`th declaration declared by this 'declaration' statement.

For example, for
```
int i, j;
```
the result of `getDeclaration(0)` is `i`.

method.name: getADeclaration
method.comments:
Gets a declaration declared by this 'declaration' statement.

For example, for
```
int i, j;
```
the results are `i` and `j`.

class.comments:
A C/C++ 'empty' statement.

For example, the following statement is an empty statement:
```
;
```

A C/C++ 'asm' statement.

For example, the `__asm__` statement in the following code:
```
__asm__("movb %bh (%eax)");
```

A C99 statement which computes the size of a single dimension of a
variable length array. For example the variable length array dimension
(`x`) in the following code:
```
int myArray[x];
```

Each `VlaDeclStmt` is preceded by one `VlaDimensionStmt` for each
variable length dimension of the array.

method.name: getDimensionExpr
method.comments: Gets the expression which gives the size.

class.comments:
A C99 statement which declares a variable length array. For example
the variable length array declaration in the following code:
```
int myArray[x];
```

Each `VlaDeclStmt` is preceded by one `VlaDimensionStmt` for each
variable length dimension of the array.

method.name: getNumberOfVlaDimensionStmts
method.body:
exists(BlockStmt b, int j |
      this = b.getStmt(j) and
      result =
        j - 1 -
          max(int i |
            i in [0 .. j - 1] and
            not b.getStmt(i) instanceof VlaDimensionStmt
          )
    )
method.comments:
Gets the number of VLA dimension statements in this VLA
declaration statement.

method.name: getTransitiveNumberOfVlaDimensionStmts
method.body:
not exists(this.getParentVlaDecl()) and
    result = this.getNumberOfVlaDimensionStmts()
    or
    result =
      this.getNumberOfVlaDimensionStmts() +
        this.getParentVlaDecl().getTransitiveNumberOfVlaDimensionStmts()
method.comments:
Gets the number of VLA dimension statements in this VLA declaration
statement and transitively of the VLA declaration used to define its
base type. if any.

method.name: getVlaDimensionStmt
method.body:
i in [0 .. this.getNumberOfVlaDimensionStmts() - 1] and
    exists(BlockStmt b, int j |
      this = b.getStmt(j) and
      result = b.getStmt(j - this.getNumberOfVlaDimensionStmts() + i)
    )
method.comments:
Gets the `i`th VLA dimension statement in this VLA
declaration statement.

method.name: getTransitiveVlaDimensionStmt
method.body:
i < this.getNumberOfVlaDimensionStmts() and
    result = this.getVlaDimensionStmt(i)
    or
    result =
      this.getParentVlaDecl().getTransitiveVlaDimensionStmt(i - this.getNumberOfVlaDimensionStmts())
method.comments:
Gets the `i`th VLA dimension statement in this VLA declaration
statement or transitively of the VLA declaration used to define
its base type.

method.name: getType
method.comments:
Gets the type that this VLA declaration statement relates to,
if any.

method.name: getVariable
method.comments:
Gets the variable that this VLA declaration statement relates to,
if any.

method.name: getParentVlaDecl
method.body:
exists(Variable v, Type baseType |
      v = this.getVariable() and
      baseType = this.getBaseType(v.getType(), this.getNumberOfVlaDimensionStmts())
    |
      result.getType() = baseType
    )
    or
    exists(Type t, Type baseType |
      t = this.getType().(TypedefType).getBaseType() and
      baseType = this.getBaseType(t, this.getNumberOfVlaDimensionStmts())
    |
      result.getType() = baseType
    )
method.comments:
Get the VLA declaration used to define the base type of
this VLA declaration, if any.

class.comments:
HashCons is the hash-cons of an expression. The relationship between `Expr`
and `HC` is many-to-one: every `Expr` has exactly one `HC`, but multiple
expressions can have the same `HC`. If two expressions have the same
`HC`, it means that they are structurally equal. The `HC` is an opaque
value. The only use for the `HC` of an expression is to find other
expressions that are structurally equal to it. Use the predicate
`hashCons` to get the `HC` for an `Expr`.

Note: `HC` has `toString` and `getLocation` methods, so that it can be
displayed in a results list. These work by picking an arbitrary
expression with this `HC` and using its `toString` and `getLocation`
methods.

method.name: getAnExpr
method.comments: Gets an expression that has this HC.

method.name: getKind
method.body:
result = this.getKind0()
    or
    not exists(this.getKind0()) and result = "error"
method.comments: Gets the kind of the HC. This can be useful for debugging.

method.name: toString
method.comments: Gets a textual representation of this element.

method.name: getLocation
method.comments: Gets the primary location of this element.
